For comparison, we also derive an optimal multi hypothesis LRT assuming perfect CSI and present an energy detector (ED) without CSI. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed solution approaches near optimal performance and significantly outperforms naive detection strategies without requiring CSI.

title: Optimizing Oil Palm Plantation Monitoring Using YOLOv8 and YOLOv12: Detection, Counting, and Multiclass
authors: Dule Abera, Indra Hermawan, Riza Rumayanti Dewi, Harry Imantho
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Accurate identification, counting, and health assessment of oil palm trees are essential for sustainable plantation management and yield optimization. Traditional manual inventory methods are labor-intensive, timeconsuming, and impractical for large-scale operations. Leveraging highresolution aerial imagery, this study conducts the first head-tohead benchmark of YOLOv8 and the newly released YOLOv12 architectures for automated oil palm detection, counting, and multiclass classification. Three model variants Nano, Small, and Medium were evaluated to balance accuracy, computational efficiency, and deployment feasibility. The dataset comprises $\mathbf{3, 4 9 9}$ aerial images ($\mathbf{2, 8 0 8}$ training, 461 validations, 230 testing) annotated into four health categories: Healthy, Yellowing, Small, and Dead. Results demonstrate that all models achieved strong detection performance, with YOLOv12-Medium attaining mAP@0.5:0.95 = 0.978 and YOLOv8-Small achieving $\mathbf{F 1}=0.997$ and recall $=1.000$. In terms of efficiency, YOLOv8 models achieved up to $\mathbf{2 8 \%}$ faster inference and 35% shorter training time than YOLOv12 counterparts, confirming their suitability for real-time UAVbased applications. These findings highlight the complementary strengths of both architectures-YOLOv12 for peak accuracy and YOLOv8 for real-time efficiencyproviding a practical foundation for scalable, data-driven precision agriculture and intelligent plantation management.

title: AGERS 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)

title: AGERS 2025 Editorial Note
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)

title: Hardware Distortion Aware Precoding for ISAC Systems
authors: Murat Babek Salman, Emil Björnson, Özlem Tuğfe Demir
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The impact of hardware impairments on the spectral efficiency of communication systems is well studied, but their effect on sensing performance remains unexplored. In this paper, we analyze the influence of hardware impairments on integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems in cluttered environments. We derive the sensing signal-to-clutter- plus-noise ratio (SCNR) and show that hardware distortions significantly degrade sensing performance by enhancing clutter- induced noise, which masks target echoes. The isotropic nature of transmit distortion due to multiple stream transmission further complicates clutter suppression. To address this, we propose a distortion- and clutter-aware precoding strategy that minimizes the deviation from the communication-optimized precoder while improving sensing robustness. We also propose an alternative power allocation-based approach that reduces computational complexity. Numerical results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed approaches in overcoming hardware- and clutter- induced limitations, demonstrating significant performance gains over distortion-unaware designs.

title: Quantile Randomized Kaczmarz with Time-Varying Noise in Signal
authors: Emeric Battaglia, Anna Ma
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: When solving a linear system of equations, the signal vector may be corrupted by time-varying noise. When the corruption is sparse, quantile-based methods such as the quantile Randomized Kaczmarz method (qRK) can recover the underlying solution to the system. However, when the corruption is insufficiently sparse, convergence is only guaranteed up to an error horizon. In the setting of time-varying noise, current theory also suggests convergence up to an error horizon. However, if the noise decays over time, one would hope that qRK can recover the solution to the system. In this work, we present an improved error horizon bound and demonstrate that it improves the previously known bounds under mild constraints. We also demonstrate that, in the time-varying case, the horizon decays as well, and qRK converges to the unique solution of the system.

title: Progress in the Application of Optical Fiber in Quench Detection on CFETR CSMC
authors: Qicai Ni, Jincheng Jiang, Longgui Zheng, Yanlan Hu, Yu Chen, Yezheng Xiao, Huaichao Liu, Qing Yan, Jilin Zhang, Xinxin Zhu
abstract: The CFETR project is a key node in China's efforts to achieve nuclear fusion to solve the energy crisis. CSMC is a large-scale hybrid (Nb3Sn/NbTi) superconducting coil designed for the CFETR project's central solenoid magnet. It is used to obtain the scientific goals and physical characteristics of CFETR, laying a solid foundation for the future development of CFETR CS superconducting magnets. At present, the quench detection system of the superconducting magnet system is mainly based on the detection of terminal voltage changes, combined with the temperature data of the thermometer as an auxiliary means. Although this solution can meet the basic quench detection needs, it has significant defects in the quench warning and positioning functions, and the high-voltage penetrating thermometer can only be installed at the back of the insulator, resulting in a deviation between the temperature measurement data and the actual value. This paper selects the distributed optical fiber based on Rayleigh scattering and the fiber Bragg grating (FBG) temperature measurement technology as a countermeasure. This technology has the advantages of naturally avoiding electromagnetic interference, fast temperature response speed, and continuous detection of temperature changes in space. Seamless steel tubes are used to encapsulate distributed optical fibers to improve their mechanical properties. We arrange distributed optical fibers in the coil at the highest magnetic field and the helium tube at the thermal disturbance trigger point to detect the cooling of CSMC. The trend of the spectral offset obtained from the curve is consistent with that of the thermometer, with some deviations in details. The distributed optical fiber successfully captured a 3 K temperature fluctuation based on the 20 K cooling baseline at the 14th turn of the inner Nb3Sn coil (high-field region) around the 80 h mark, demonstrating its sensitivity to thermal anomalies near the warning threshold. For the ultra-low temperature range where CSMC works normally, the temperature rise of 3 K in the 20 K range serves as a high-confidence indicator for thermal runaway confirmation and fault localization, which complements the instantaneous but non-localized voltage-based detection. The distributed optical fiber responds to this more obviously, providing a reliable basis for optical fiber quench detection. In order to improve the response of the optical fiber to the temperature details around 20 K, we use a Bragg grating packaged with PEEK with higher sensitivity at low temperatures for corresponding supplementation.

title: Codeword-Based Auto-Correlation Receiver for Ultrasonic Time-Code-Indexed Modulation Systems
authors: Qianqian Wang, Baiqiang Long, Xinyue Pei, Yuankun Tang, Haoyue Qu, Xiangdong Jia
abstract: Ultrasonic index modulation can effectively improve the spectral efficiency and energy efficiency of intra-body communication systems. This letter proposes a codeword-based auto-correlation (C-AC) receiver for ultrasonic code-indexed modulation (UCIM) and ultrasonic time-code-indexed modulation (UTCIM) transmitters, employing Hadamard codewords. The core idea is to utilize the auto-correlation properties of the Hadamard codewords to demodulate the code index bits, where the demodulation of each code index bit is based on the sum of the dot products of every two sub-sequences, dispensing with precise synchronization and channel state information. The theoretical bit error rate (BER) is derived and verified by extensive Monte Carlo simulations over intra-body fading channels, revealing that the proposed non-coherent low-complexity C-AC receiver can achieve lower BER compared with the conventional transmitted-reference receiver for both the UCIM and UTCIM transmitters.

title: Nuclear Power Project Construction Resource Allocation Model Based on Multi-Objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yuan Li, Songlin Wan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In the construction of nuclear power projects, resource allocation is complex and limited. How to efficiently allocate construction resources while meeting multiple requirements such as construction period and safety has become an important problem that affects the smooth progress of the project. To this end, this paper proposes a nuclear power project construction resource allocation model based on a multi-objective optimization algorithm. Firstly, a multi-objective mathematical model of resource allocation is established, and objectives such as cost minimization, construction period shortening and resource utilization maximization are incorporated into the optimization framework. Secondly, a method based on an improved genetic algorithm (GA) is used to solve the multi-objective problem, so as to ensure that the model can find the optimal solution that takes into account the needs of all parties under multiple constraints. Finally, multiple rounds of iterations and parameter adjustments are used to improve the convergence efficiency of the algorithm and the quality of the solution. Experimental results show that the model based on the improved GA achieves a 15 % cost savings and shortens the construction period by 10.8 % compared to particle swarm optimization (PSO). In terms of human resources, the average resource utilization rate is increased to 76 %. The overall results verify the efficiency and practicability of this method in nuclear power engineering construction, and effectively solve the problem of resource allocation under multi-objective optimization.

title: Automated Arthritis Detection from X-Ray Images Using Efficientnet B3 and Image Processing
authors: Prannov Shabari. N, Prarthana. K, Rahul Chiranjeevi. V
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Deep learning algorithms have recently received substantial interest for detecting arthritis automatically in medical imaging context. The proposed research introduces a solid methodology to identify arthritis by applying deep learning algorithms to analyzed X-ray scans of bones. The processing method starts with an image preparation process consisting of histogram normalization and noise reduction alongside normalization along with segmentation which improves image quality. The EfficientNet-B3 model analyzes preprocessed images as its input because it represents a state-of-the-art CNN implementation that delivers high efficiency in medical image classification tasks. Using blocks with different convolutional kernel dimensions and resolutions the model learns complex representations of arthritis-pattern distribution in its deep feature extraction process. The feature maps proceed to a classification section that generates predictions for arthritis existence or non-existence. The classification process forms two output groups which include “Arthritis” and “No Arthritis.” The proposed model uses deep learning technology together with optimal image preprocessing capabilities to enhance arthritis detection accuracy and thus help doctors with early diagnosis and medical decision support. EfficientNet-B3 provides the perfect equilibrium between processing speed and diagnostic outcomes which leads to its suitability in medical equipment handling biological data in real time. The proposed detection method demonstrates its ability to accurately identify arthritic conditions compared to normal cases according to experimental findings. Experiments were performed on various datasets where the proposed method achieved Based on the experimental results proposed method achieved a significant amount of accuracy compared to state of art methods.

title: Hybrid EfficientNet–BiLSTM Model for Psoriasis Classification
authors: Parthasarathi Pattnayak, Arpeeta Mohanty, Tulip Das, Sanghamitra Patnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: In dermatological disease diagnosis, the precise classification of psoriasis is important, as it can have a variety of clinical presentation and severity. The similarity of multiple subtypes of psoriasis to other skin diseases complicate the diagnostic process, and expert medical opinion is not always available. However, it is important to recognize psoriasis and its subtypes to avoid delay in treatment for the patient. The proposed hybrid deep learning model combines EfficientNet (a convolutional neural network) with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to automate the classification of psoriasis derived from dermoscopic imaging. The hybrid representation allows the model to learn spatial features using EfficientNet while also capturing sequential or contextual information via the LSTM units for improved classification accuracy. The model was trained and evaluated on a public benchmark dataset of dermoscopic images with 7 classes. According to the results the suggested approach outperforms existing models VGG16 and ResNet50 with an accuracy of 89.8%, an F 1 -score of 88.4% and an ROC of 97.6%. Therefore, due to the smaller size and number of parameters of the model architecture, the model has lower computational costs, low memory usage and would work well using mobile or portable devices for dermatological or other applications. This advancement brings us closer to reliable, on-the-go skin disease diagnosis using deep learning technologies.

title: Secrecy Capacity of Airy Beams
authors: Chloe Stults, Kevin K. Mutai, Zhaoji Fang, Daniel M. Mittleman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: Communication in the terahertz (THz) frequency band is a key focus for the next generation of wireless systems, in part because of the possibility for enhanced security in combination with ultra-high data rates. To enable communication links in the presence of blockage of a directional line-of-sight beam, self-accelerating terahertz beams have recently been proposed. For such beams, the main lobe radiated from an aperture follows some engineered curved trajectory in the electromagnetic near field. Self-accelerating beams enable advantages unlike traditional beams by curving around potential eavesdroppers located in the vicinity of the radiating aperture. Here, we theoretically and experimentally analyze the security implications of such beams by evaluating their secrecy capacity. We also explore the implications of blockage and self-healing on this security metric.

title: Carbon Footprint Efficiency Ratio: A Unified Indicator for Green Evaluation of Evolutionary Algorithms
authors: Mingyang Yu, Haorui Yang, Xinjian Wei, Shengwei Fu, Jing Xu, Jun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Evolutionary algorithms (EAs) are widely adopted for black-box optimization, yet evaluation protocols remain largely accuracy-centric and often omit computational energy use, hindering carbon-aware deployment in green computing settings. We propose the Carbon Footprint Efficiency Ratio (CFER) to quantify optimization benefit per unit carbon emission and enable a unified assessment of carbon efficiency. To compute CFER without dedicated power-monitoring hardware, we develop a device-independent energy estimation model based on the Multimodal Energy Consumption Factor (MECF). Using a dataset constructed from 13 benchmark functions and 8 classical EAs, MECF is identified via outlier removal and Huber regression, achieving a mean estimation error of 1.80% and a maximum error of 2.51%. On the CEC2020 test suite, CFER-based comparisons reveal rankings that differ substantially from accuracy-only evaluations, highlighting algorithms that improve carbon efficiency with only marginal accuracy loss. Beyond metric-based assessment, we introduce a CFER-aware green optimization mechanism as an algorithm-agnostic plug-in control layer. Driven by real-time carbon estimation and marginal CFER feedback, it adaptively budgets computation through carbon-budget termination and population shrinking under low marginal carbon efficiency. Integrated into GA, DE, and PSO for uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) three-dimensional path planning, the resulting CFER-aware variants reduce CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> emissions by 45.91%, 28.28%, and 59.14%, respectively, while increasing CFER by 78.59%, 32.45%, and 133.70%, respectively, yielding an improved performance–carbon trade-off with minor performance-score degradation. Overall, the proposed framework supports carbon-aware evaluation, algorithm selection, and adaptive computation budgeting for green AI systems.

title: Dynamic Virtual Heat Fields for Void-Avoidance Opportunistic Routing in UASNs
authors: Shen’ao Tu, Yougan Chen, Yihao Zhao, Xuchen Wang, Yanhan Dong, Yi Tao, Xiaomei Xu
abstract: Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks (UASNs) have attracted considerable attention due to their broad application prospects. However, the challenges of limited battery replacement and unstable communication in underwater environments make it difficult for traditional routing protocols to balance stability and energy consumption under dynamic topologies. To address these challenges, we propose a Dynamic Virtual Heat Field-based Void-Avoidance Opportunistic Routing (DVHVOR) protocol. Inspired by the diffusion of heat from sources to cooler regions in nature, the protocol constructs a virtual heat field to guide efficient data forwarding without relying on precise three-dimensional localization. Specifically, it integrates hop count, residual energy, and depth information into a heat-value metric. The Sink periodically broadcasts packets, forming a dynamic and adaptive virtual heat field across the network. During data transmission, each node selects the neighbor with the highest heat value as the relay and employs a concurrent forwarding mechanism to improve both energy efficiency and adaptability to topology changes. Compared with conventional greedy routing strategies, the virtual heat field provides stable and tunable multi-path guidance.Simulation results demonstrate that, relative to existing protocols, the proposed approach reduces the variance of residual energy by an average of 35.9%, decreases transmission delay by approximately 10.9%, and achieves superior performance across multiple metrics including network lifetime, thereby validating its feasibility and advantages in dynamic and network sparse scenarios.

title: Fully-distributed Prescribed-time Consensus of High-order Integrator Multi-agent Systems: An Adaptive Protocol with Matching Gain Convergence
authors: Tao Yu, Yong Chen, Xin Gong, Zhiguang Feng, Tingwen Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This work focuses on the design of fully-distributed prescribed-time consensus protocols for first-order to higher order integrator multi-agent systems. An adaptive gain with a convergence rate that matches the prescribed-time protocol is proposed. Then, first-order to higher-order zero-error fully distributed prescribed-time consensus protocols are designed by replacing the parameters that need to be large enough in the traditional prescribed-time protocol with this adaptive gain. Considering different application scenarios, the edge-based version of the above adaptive gain is further designed, which can ensure that the graph is still balanced after adding adaptive gains to the weights. A special Lyapunov function is constructed to prove that the state and the adaptive gain have a prescribed time convergence. Finally, the effectiveness of the above protocols is verified by a UAV formation experiment.

title: Instance-Conditioned Adaptation for Large-Scale Generalization of Neural Routing Solver
authors: Changliang Zhou, Xi Lin, Zhenkun Wang, Xialiang Tong, Mingxuan Yuan, Qingfu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In modern intelligent transportation systems (ITS), particularly in freight transportation and logistics, real-time route planning is crucial. It presents unique challenges driven by high uncertainty in service requests, where the number of service customers can vary drastically, ranging from hundreds to thousands. Existing neural methods struggle to maintain performance under such significant variations, which severely limits their practical applicability. To address this crucial shortcoming, this work proposes a novel Instance-Conditioned Adaptation Model (ICAM) designed for better large-scale generalization. In particular, we design a simple yet efficient instance-conditioned adaptation function that adjusts the policy based on the specific geometry and density of the current traffic scenario to improve model adaptability with minimal computational overhead. Furthermore, we propose a powerful yet low-complexity instance-conditioned adaptation module to generate better solutions for instances across various scales. Extensive experiments on synthetic, benchmark, and real-world instances demonstrate that ICAM can consistently achieve promising generalization performance across four widely studied large-scale route planning scenarios. Notably, our proposed method delivers high-quality solutions with remarkably fast inference speed, providing a scalable and efficient solution for real-time intelligent transportation operations. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/CIAM-Group/ICAM</uri>

title: Optimal Experimental Design for Fuel Cell Model Parameter Identification
authors: Luis M. Winkler, Michael A. Danzer, Herbert Palm
abstract: Model-based development provides a systematic approach for investigating fuel cell (FC) and fuel cell system (FCS) operation, with particular emphasis on understanding reaction kinetics, transport phenomena, and aging mechanisms as functions of operating conditions. Characterizing these processes by physical models requires effective and efficient parameter identification, i.e., the derivation of precise parameter information with minimal experimental effort. Conventional strategies for parameter identification in FC models have so far suffered in particular from high time and resource requirements, leading to considerable bottlenecks in FC model development and system characterization. Therefore, this paper proposes a generic, systematic workflow for equally effective and efficient parameter identification in nonlinear, parametric models through structural and practical identifiability analysis based on the Fisher information matrix. The approach takes into account existing prior knowledge about parameter values, thereby enabling the calculation of optimal experimental designs. The methodology is universally applicable to differentiable parametric models. We demonstrate its practical applicability for a proton exchange membrane FC under realistic measurement conditions using a zero-dimensional model with six unknown parameters. Compared to conventional Latin Hypercube Sampling, the proposed approach achieves parameter identification with a 74% reduction in uncertainty or, conversely, saves 84% of the experimental effort required to achieve the same accuracy. The framework enables experimenters to conduct measurement campaigns that are both maximally effective and efficient. All methodological implementations, data, and demonstration results are made publicly available to facilitate reproducibility and community adoption.

title: Flying Vehicle Detection Under Complex Conditions With RGB–Infrared Imagery: A Large-Scale Open-Source Suite and Benchmark Approach
authors: Xunkuai Zhou, Yijun Huang, Li Li, Jie Chen, Ben M. Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: While coordination among multiple flying vehicles improves aerial logistics efficiency, safe and orderly operation requires robust detection and collision-avoidance capabilities. These requirements apply to passenger aircraft as well as to urban traffic and maritime environments, including emerging underwater flying vehicles. However, existing detection methods often fail under challenging conditions such as low illumination or cluttered backgrounds. Their progress is further constrained by the lack of large-scale benchmarks and the high computational and memory costs required to achieve high accuracy, which limits their deployment in resource-constrained scenarios, such as air-to-air collision avoidance in aerial vehicles. To address this gap, we introduce FT55k, an open-source benchmark comprising over 55,000 annotated RGB and infrared images across diverse environments. We further provide baseline approaches tailored for platforms with different computational demands. Extensive experiments on FT55k and three public datasets demonstrate the superior accuracy and efficiency of our methods compared with state-of-the-art approaches. Notably, our approach is the first flying vehicle detection method with a computational cost below 0.5 BFLOPs, achieving real-time performance at 62.3 FPS on an edge-computing device. This work presents the first comprehensive benchmark for flying vehicle detection in complex environments, establishing a practical and scalable foundation for future research and deployment in intelligent transportation safety. Our datasets is publicly accessible at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chriszxk/Flying-Vehicle-Detection</uri>

title: On the Importance of Tactile Sensing for Imitation Learning: A Case Study on Robotic Match Lighting_supp1-3677716.mp4
authors: Niklas Funk
abstract: The field of robotic manipulation has advanced significantly in recent years. At the sensing level, several novel tactile sensors have been developed, capable of providing accurate contact information. On a methodological level, learning from demonstrations has proven an efficient paradigm to obtain performant robotic manipulation policies. The combination of both holds the promise to extract crucial contact-related information from the demonstration data and actively exploit it during policy rollouts. However, this integration has so far been underexplored, most notably in dynamic, contact-rich manipulation tasks where precision and reactivity are essential. This work therefore proposes a multimodal, visuotactile imitation learning framework that integrates a modular transformer architecture with a flow-based generative model, enabling efficient learning of fast and dexterous manipulation policies. We evaluate our framework on the dynamic, contact-rich task of robotic match lighting - a task in which tactile feedback influences human manipulation performance. The experimental results highlight the effectiveness of our approach and show that adding tactile information improves policy performance, thereby underlining their combined potential for learning dynamic manipulation from few demonstrations.

title: Sampling Augmented Bilevel Trajectory Optimization for Constrained Quadrotor Flights_supp1-3677754.mp4
authors: Qiang Li
abstract: Planning smooth trajectories through a sequence of waypoints under nonconvex constraints is challenging due to the coupling between coefficient optimization and time allocation. Existing gradient-based spline trajectory optimization methods tend to be susceptible to local minima and poor initializations, or restrained by complicated gradient computations. We propose a sampling augmented bilevel optimization (SABO) approach that integrates gradient-based optimization with correlated spatio-temporal sampling for improved robustness and optimality. Through temporal normalization, the closed-form solution of coefficient optimization becomes an explicit function of segment durations, while the Hessian becomes linear in their powers, enabling analytic bilevel gradient computation without using finite differences or linearized constraints. Correlated mutations are subsequently performed around the gradient-induced solution to further explore the constrained spatio-temporal space, with sample projection and covariance matrix adaptation to guide sampling towards low-cost, feasible regions. Simulations show that SABO outperforms existing methods in terms of optimality and robustness. We validate SABO in flight experiments conducted on a quadrotor.

title: LED Pouch Motor: Wavelength Selective Wireless Actuation of Dyed Liquid-to-Gas Phase Change Actuators Using LEDs
authors: Kazuki Yamaura, Hiroki Ishizuka, Takefumi Hiraki
abstract: Untethered soft actuators that operate without physical connections to external power sources or control system cables are an active area of research for their potential applications in human-robot interaction, haptic devices, and 4D printing due to their wide range of motion and high degrees of freedom. Among these actuators, liquid-to-gas phase change actuators have a simple structure and can generate higher output force using photothermal conversion. In this study, we propose a novel control method for untethered soft actuators by dyeing the low-boiling-point liquids used in liquid-to-gas phase change actuators. The dyed low-boiling-point liquids exhibit different light absorption spectra matching their color, enabling wavelength-selective actuation using light sources with corresponding wavelengths. We evaluated and discussed this control method in terms of the relationship between irradiance and output, actuation speed, and continuous operation. Additionally, we prototyped a robotic arm to demonstrate selective motion according to the light color.

title: Efficient Cluster-Based Knowledge Distillation for Deep Face Recognition
authors: Xianwei Lv, Haibo Mi, Xin Niu, Wang Chen, Kun Wang, Chen Yu
abstract: Knowledge distillation has been widely used to improve the performance of small compact models for face recognition. However, selecting key knowledge and effectively transferring it from teacher to student remains a challenging problem. In this work, we propose an efficient Cluster-based Knowledge Distillation (CKD) dedicated to aligning the student model with the teacher model in terms of both sample relations and class centers. Specifically, CKD first determines the key sample relations based on the similarities between the sample features extracted by the teacher and their cluster centers generated by existing clustering algorithms. Then, CKD effectively transfers the knowledge of the above relations from the teacher to the student by designing a cluster-based relation distillation loss. Finally, CKD further improves the quality of the student's class centers by constructing a center loss between the above representative cluster centers and the student's class centers. We validate the proposed CKD on multiple face benchmarks. For example, CKD improves the baseline student performance from 91.95% to 94.20% on MegaFace and consistently outperforms recent competitive distillation methods on multiple benchmarks. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of CKD.

title: PNProRL: Self-Supervised Neural Relighting via Photometric Perception and Progressive Optimization_supp1-3677109.pdf
authors: You Yang
abstract: Portrait relighting shows great potential in photography, film, and AR by simulating diverse lighting effects. Existing state-of-the-art methods often rely on expensive paired OLAT or synthetic data, which limits scalability. Moreover, accurately modeling the interaction between physics-guided rendering, neural rendering, and real-world remains challenging. To address these issues, we propose a novel multi-stage self-supervised relighting framework. It progressively refines intrinsic scene properties via a simple-to-complex training strategy, removing the need for expensive paired data while adapting to various lighting conditions. One core design introduces a novel pre-training method approach using diverse shading-based masking for self-reconstruction, which improves the model's perception of complex lighting variations. Furthermore, we introduce two perceptual modules that leverage the linear superposition of light to narrow the gap between physics-guided and neural rendering, and better align relit results with real-world observations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our unified framework achieves new state-of-the-art performance in portrait relighting, surpassing recent methods in photorealism, synthesis quality, and identity preservation. It provides a practical paradigm for high-fidelity relighting under diverse lighting.

title: A Multi-Source Data-Driven Hybrid Model for Predicting Crop Yield under Varying Climatic Conditions
authors: Premkumar Duraisamy, Sriharani R, Harishh D V, Jones Martin J
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Crop yield prediction is essential for enhancing food security and promoting sustainable agriculture, especially under varying climatic conditions. This work introduces a multi-source datadriven hybrid model that integrates structured soilweather parameters with unstructured satellite imagery to achieve superior prediction accuracy. Structured data comprising soil nutrients, rainfall, temperature, and humidity are analyzed using the XGBoost algorithm, known for its robustness in handling complex non-linear interactions and feature importance analysis. Simultaneously, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are employed to extract spatial and vegetation features from NDVI and RGB satellite images, capturing crop health variations across regions. Experimental datasets were obtained from FAO, MOSPI, and NASA Harvest (Radiant Earth MLHub). The hybrid XGBoost-CNN framework demonstrated outstanding predictive performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 96.5%, outperforming individual models such as Random Forest (94.1 %) and LSTM (93.7 %). This work highlights the effectiveness of combining multi-source agricultural data with hybrid AI techniques to enhance yield forecasting accuracy, strengthen climate resilience, and support informed decision-making in precision agriculture.

title: Design of an Efficient Tobacco Foreign Object Detection Model Based on YOLOv3 Architecture
authors: Tianhou Yang, Mengshi Fan, Chengshun Sun, Maozhe Qin, Chengkun Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: A tobacco foreign object detection model based on the YOLOv3 architecture was designed, integrating multiscale feature extraction and real-time inference mechanisms. Experiments conducted across multiple industrial scenarios achieved detection accuracy exceeding 99% and inference speeds reaching 41 FPS. This solution is suitable for real-time production line inspection, significantly enhancing quality control efficiency.

title: Real-time Explainable Deep Learning for Intrusion Detection System in Wireless Sensor Network using Edge Computing
authors: Vinod Kumar, Amit Kumar, Saurabh Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) have become a key element of edge computing systems today and can be used in healthcare and industrial automation, as well as smart cities, but due to their limited resources, they have become a major target of advanced cyberattacks. The scalability and flexibility of traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) can be a challenge, whereas deep learning (DL)-based IDSs, even though more accurate, are often considered black-box models, making it difficult to trust and use them. This paper will resolve these constraints by proposing XEdge-IDS, an explainable deep learning-based IDS that can be deployed as edges in WSNs. Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN-1D) were trained and tested on the UNSW-NB15 dataset using the hyperparameter optimization to guarantee efficiency in the conditions of resource allocation limitation. The CNN-1D performed the best among them with 94.38% accuracy and better precision, recall, and F1-score than MLP and LSTM. To mitigate the problem of the lack of interpretability, Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) and SHapley Additive explanations (SHAP) were used, which identified packet- and protocol-level characteristics as strong features of malicious traffic, including sttl, dttl, and ct_state_ttl. These insights increase the model transparency and the confidence of the administrator and make the IDS operational in real-time in limited WSN settings. The results support the claim that explainable deep learning is not merely an accuracy-improving detection method, but also a practice that offers applicability in a real-world context, and a potential route toward ensuring the safety of future-generation WSNs in edge computing environments.

title: Research on the Optimization of Engineering Task Based on the Assignment Model
authors: Bo Zhong, Yingchang Duan, Yuxin Bai, Weikang Peng
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In response to the practical needs of multi-equipment and multi-personnel collaboration for multi-task stages in engineering operations, this study establishes an assignment optimization design model targeting task completion efficiency. The model comprehensively considers factors such as multi-skilled personnel, multi-functional equipment, and different rest periods of human and equipment, provides corresponding constraint conditions. Given the characteristics of large feasible-solution space and long computation time for this optimization problem, the Virtual Equipment Method is adopted to decompose multi-functional equipment into combinations of single-functional equipment. Additionally, a rapid model-solving approach based on task cycles is designed. The results of the case analysis demonstrate that this assignment model can optimize and achieve the best personnel and equipment combinations in multi-stage tasks. Furthermore, by decomposing multi-functional equipment and incorporating task cycles, the feasible solution space can be significantly reduced, thus greatly improves computational efficiency.

title: Research on Innovative Software Architecture Design and System Performance Improvement Based on Intelligent Optimization Algorithm
authors: Zhu Lijin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This study focuses on designing innovative intelligent optimization algorithms and applying them to software architecture design to improve system performance. The algorithm is inspired by the cooperative behavior of biological groups and integrates dynamic adaptive search strategies and multi-dimensional information interaction mechanisms. By building a mathematical model, its operating principle is explained in detail. In the software architecture design model, the performance goals are clearly defined as the system response time is controlled within 5 milliseconds and the throughput is 10,000 transactions per second, while considering the 8GB limit of hardware memory, CPU computing power constraints and compatibility with existing systems. The experiment was built in a hardware environment equipped with an Intel Core i7 processor and 16GB of memory, using Python language and related frameworks, and using a data set containing tens of millions of transaction records collected from the e-commerce business system. Small, medium and large e-commerce system architecture scenarios are set for comparative experiments, and the architecture design scheme based on the innovative algorithm is compared with traditional algorithms and classic design methods. The results show that the innovative algorithm shortens the average system response time by 30%, increases the throughput by 2.5 times, and improves resource utilization by 20%.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Artificial Intelligence-Based Drug Side Effect Prediction Techniques
authors: Naga Padmaja Yenireddy, G Sri Lakshmi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Predicting drug side effects before they reach patients is one of the most important challenges in modern healthcare. Many recent studies have used different artificial intelligence methods to identify possible harmful reactions, but their performance varies widely because they rely on different types of data, learning techniques, and evaluation settings. This work presents a detailed review of these approaches to understand how well they perform, where they succeed, and where they fall short. The analysis covers models based on chemical structures, similarity networks, machine-learning classifiers, deep-learning architectures, and graph-based frameworks. The review shows that while these methods can detect common side effects with reasonable accuracy, they often struggle with rare or complex reactions due to limited data, narrow feature representation, and high computational demands. The study also highlights gaps in coverage across different disorder groups and points out the need for models that combine chemical, biological, and clinical information. This comprehensive assessment helps outline the strengths and weaknesses of current techniques and offers guidance for building more reliable and efficient drug side-effect prediction systems in the future.

title: Decoding Optimization of Spatially Coupled Polar Codes Based on Improved State Monitoring
authors: Mingqiang Su, Shiyao Li, Qiufang Lian, Miao Wu, Zijun Lu, Lin Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Polar codes face challenges of insufficient polarization and limited decoding performance in short code-length applications, and existing decoding algorithms struggle to achieve an ideal balance between performance and complexity. To address this issue, this paper proposes a decoding optimization scheme for spatially coupled polar codes based on an improved state monitoring decoding (ISMD). This scheme adopts a spatially coupled structure to enhance the polarization effect and achieves efficient decoding by combining fixed-iteration soft cancellation (SCAN) decoding. The ISMD mechanism adaptively saves the optimal soft information state by monitoring the variation of the average probability of errors in real time during the decoding process. It then makes a hard decision based on this optimal state, which effectively avoids the performance degradation problem in iterative decoding. The simulation results show that the proposed scheme can effectively improve the decoding performance of spatially coupled polar codes with short code lengths.

title: A FedAvg-Based Federated Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Recognition Using Multi-Modal MRI Data
authors: Jixuan Le
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Brain tumor poses a severe global healthcare challenge, with high mortality and low cure rates primarily attributed to diagnostic delays and high misdiagnosis rates in Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)-based detection. While artificial intelligence (AI) techniques—such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), transformer models, and hybrid architectures like VSMU-Net—have achieved promising results (e.g., Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.98 in 3D tumor segmentation), their real-world clinical application is hindered by “data silos” caused by privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR) and uneven data quality. Federated Learning (FL) emerges as a potential solution, but existing FL studies in brain tumor recognition either use small-scale simulated data or ignore the 3D structural characteristics of MRI sequences. To address this gap, this study proposes a FedAvg-based FL framework for brain tumor recognition using multi-modal MRI data. The framework enables collaborative training across 5 simulated medical centers without sharing raw data, adopting a CNN model optimized for medical image features. Experimental results show the global model converges steadily over 10 communication rounds, achieving 89.7% accuracy and 0.2536 loss on the test set, with 90.2% tumor-specific accuracy and 89.1% normal-specific accuracy. This work validates FL's feasibility in balancing privacy preservation and detection performance, providing a practical framework for collaborative medical AI research.

title: Design and Implementation of a Wav2Lip-based Digital Human Framework for Intelligent Power Customer Service
authors: Qiao Xueming, Qiao Linfei, Du Nianjiang, Gong Baoning, Xiao Linwei, Yu Anqi, Han Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the customer service domain of the power industry, we propose an intelligent digital human framework developed on an optimized Wav2Lip model. The framework integrates multimodal interaction, speech-driven lip synchronization, semantic understanding, and dynamic decision-making technologies to transform traditional customer service processes. By leveraging these capabilities, the digital human not only achieves accurate user intent recognition but also exhibits natural and human-like interaction behaviors, thereby improving user experience and engagement. Experimental evaluations on multiple key indicators demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed system. Results show that the framework achieves high speech recognition accuracy, fast response times, and timely feedback generation, highlighting its potential for practical deployment in intelligent power customer service scenarios.

title: Three Pooling Strategies for ViT → GPT-2 Image Captioning
authors: Qi Zhuo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper compares CLS, mean, and attention pooling within a unified ViT-GPT-2 image captioning pipeline. A Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder produces patch embeddings; each pooling variant compresses them into a single conditioning vector mapped to GPT-2 through a linear layer. All other components, training hyperparameters, and decoding are held constant to isolate pooling effects. Experiments on a Flickr30k subset use AdamW, schedules up to 40 epochs, and greedy decoding. We report BLEU-1-4, ROUGE-L, METEOR, and CIDEr. Results show CLS converges fastest and performs best under short training (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15$</tex> epochs), but overfits with longer schedules, decreasing test scores. Attention pooling improves and generalizes better, yielding the strongest higher-order BLEU and CIDEr. Mean pooling remains consistently weaker across metrics. Ablations confirm that gains are attributable to the pooling stage rather than decoder tuning. We conclude that attention pooling is preferable when robustness matters, whereas CLS is attractive for tight budgets; simple mean pooling is discouraged.

title: Enhancing Secondary-Level Polymer Chemistry Learning with Gamified Augmented Reality: Development and Evaluation of Gameful Experience and Usability
authors: Fadhlina Izzah Saman, Fadilah Ezlina Shahbudin, Muhammad Syaril Rusmizi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Polymer chemistry, a topic in the Malaysian secondary school curriculum, is often taught using traditional 2D methods that might hinder student understanding because of its abstract nature. Many students find it difficult to visualize complex molecular structures, leading to low interest and engagement. This paper presents the development of Poly-AR, a gamified augmented reality mobile application designed to enhance the learning experience of polymer chemistry for Form 5 students, and its usability and gameful experience evaluations by the target users. The application utilizes 3D AR models and also gamification elements to assist students in visualizing the polymer structures and also in their interaction with the learning material. A preliminary study involving 42 respondents highlights the challenges faced by learners and teachers, which Poly-AR aims to address. The usability and gameful experience of the application were evaluated, with results of 5.78 out of 7 point average mean value for gameful experience, and 4.60 out of 5 point average mean value for usability, indicating high levels of efficiency, effectiveness, satisfaction, and engagement, providing a modern educational tool to support ubiquitous, engaging learning.

title: Comparative Analysis of Bayesian Optimization and Neural Network-Based Adaptive PID Tuning for Complex Control Systems
authors: Carson Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Although Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) controllers are often seen as the gold standard for control engineering, providing robust and efficient control for many scenarios, they hinge on classical tuning processes such as the Ziegler-Nichols method and often struggle with nonlinear, time varying, and multi-loop environments. As a result, recent research has seen the rise of adaptive tuning techniques that result in enhanced performance and added situational robustness. This paper analyzes two such proposals: the first proposes a Bayesian Optimization (BO) based framework for automatic tuning in industrial processes with multiple inputs and multiple outputs and takes advantage of an iterative approach, while the second proposes a back propagation trained neural networks to tune a PID controller continuously in real time for motion control in intelligent vehicles. Both papers analyze the limits of using classical PID tuning methods, the performance metrics used within each respective experiment, experimental results achieved, as well as real world implications of the findings. While both proposals showed considerable improvements in their respective metrics in comparison to the controls tuned using classical methods, it is notable that they are each focused on different situations and thus have different strengths and weaknesses as a result. Ultimately, the study's comparison results in the suggestion for a hybrid of the two, yielding a combination of the advantages within each strategy.

title: Performance Analysis of GDOP-Based Satellite Selection Methods for LEO Constellations
authors: Bohan Zhang, Aijun Liu, Kegang Pan, Xiaohu Liang, Xin Lin, Fan Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Low earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation systems possess the potential to deliver integrated communication and navigation (ICAN) services with wider coverage, higher accuracy, and stronger reliability. However, the increase in visible satellites elevates observational data redundancy and substantially increases the computational load. In this context, efficient satellite selection algorithms serve to mitigate this issue and enhance overall system performance. This paper systematically elaborates on the principles of various traditional satellite selection algorithms, integrates the advantages of different approaches to propose an improved heuristic selection strategy, and conducts a comprehensive comparative performance analysis of five algorithms in a LEO navigation environment. Simulation results clearly reveal the strengths and weaknesses of each method. The proposed algorithm achieves a geometric dilution of precision (GDOP) value comparable to that of the exhaustive search method, while significantly outperforming it in computational efficiency, making it more suitable for addressing satellite selection challenges in LEO navigation constellation systems.

title: Application of dual-objective optimization algorithm in logistics route planning
authors: Fangziqiang Zhang, Tao Jiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This paper analyzes the core conflict between delivery timeliness and operating costs in logistics route planning. Drawing on the principles of constrained dual-objective optimization models, this paper constructs and implements a dual-objective optimization model for minimizing delivery time and cost, using multi-delivery point routing within an urban area as a scenario. The model is then solved in Python. It uses itertools to enumerate routes, calculate objective function values, and verify constraints. This model quantifies the trade-off between time and cost, providing flexible and actionable decision support for logistics scheduling. It is scalable to accommodate practical operational requirements such as multiple delivery points and dynamic constraints.

title: IRS Deployment Under Practical Channel Model: Joint Position and Rotation Optimization
authors: De Xu, Tao Wang, Nuocheng Yang, Xinxin He, Changchuan Yin, Changsheng You, Haonan Tong
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In this paper, we study the deployment strategy of intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) for coverage extension, where the IRS is used to establish a cascaded link between the access point and users. Existing works have mostly adopted simplified channel models that consider only propagation path loss, overlooking critical IRS reception and reflection factors. To address this issue, we propose a practical channel model where the reception and reflection factors are explicitly derived based on electromagnetic field theory. Based on this channel model, we formulate an average achievable rate maximization problem over multiple users by jointly optimizing the position and rotation of the IRS. Due to the high-dimensional and coupled variable space of the formulated problem, we adopt a particle swarm optimization (PSO)-based algorithm, which efficiently obtains a near-optimal solution with significantly reduced computational complexity compared to exhaustive search. Numerical results demonstrate that our proposed scheme improves the achievable rate by at least 34.2% compared to conventional deployment strategies.

title: Real Time IoT and Machine Learning Based Monitoring System for Bedridden Patients
authors: Sarika J, Sanjana N, Rakshitha S, Praveen Kumar KC
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Monitoring bedridden patients during their recovery often requires constant attention to notice any significant changes in their health and provide the necessary action on time. Traditional monitoring relies almost entirely on manual observation and any deficiencies in staff could delay necessary actions and reduce their effectiveness. This paper describes the development of a Real-Time Continuous Health Monitoring System for bedridden patients utilizing IoT and Machine Learning. The monitoring system is designed with multiple biomedical sensors, including heart rate, temperature, oxygen saturation and motion sensors, interfaced with an IoT driven microcontroller allowing real-time collection and transmission of data to a cloud platform. Then, the collected data is analyzed using machine learning algorithms to detect anomalies and predict risk for health deterioration. In the event of an abnormal finding, alerts are automatically forwarded to the clinicians or family members of the patient mobile applications. This intelligent system will eliminate the possibility of human error and allow for 24/7 monitoring of bedridden patients which will allow for early detection of health deterioration and shortening of response times.

title: AI Powered Network Intrusion Detection System Using Machine Learning
authors: M Prasanna Venkatesh, Nithish Kumar V, S Saraswathi, K Abinaya
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This study presents a machine-learning-based Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) designed to overcome the limitations of traditional signature-based IDS, such as low accuracy and poor real-time performance. The proposed system evaluates multiple classification algorithms, including Random Forest, AdaBoost, XGBoost, and Logistic Regression, for identifying abnormal network traffic patterns. Real-time testing shows an accuracy of 0.92 with a false alarm rate of 0.05 between 08:00-09:00, and an accuracy of 0.90 with a false alarm rate of 0.06 between 09:00-10:00. The average detection latency remained below 55 ms across all intervals. Keywords- network intrusion detection, machine learning, real-time monitoring, anomaly detection.

title: Defect Diagnosis and Analysis Method of Sewage System Based on SWMM and Flow Monitoring Data
authors: Yangjun Zhou, Yufei Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: It is established in the article that a method for identifying and locating the defects of the sewage system based on flow monitoring. An optimization locating model is established to optimize the distribution of groundwater infiltration at the nodes in the sewage system and realize the locating and diagnosis of the defects in the sewage system. The content is to establish the optimization and locating model of the sewage system defects by coupling secondary development technology and scientific algorithm with SWMM. Taking the minimum error of RMSE between the measured flow values and the simulated flow values of the target nodes as the objective function, the PSO optimization algorithm is used to find the most suitable node groundwater infiltration scheme, so as to realize the localization and diagnosis of defects in the sewage system under the limited flow monitoring data.

title: YOLOv11n-RDF: A Detail-Enhanced YOLOv11n with Low-Level Feature Fusion for Damper Detection
authors: Weiwei Zou, Can Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the challenge of detecting vibration dampers on transmission lines during UAV inspections, where complex backgrounds and their small size impede performance, this paper proposes an improved detection model, YOLOv11nRDF. The model introduces a Receptive Field-based Coordinated Attention (RFCA) mechanism to suppress background noise. It also integrates a new P2 detection head with a Detail Guided Block (DGB) and a Feature Refine Module (FRM) to significantly enhance the capture of high-resolution details and the lossless fusion of multi-scale features, thereby effectively boosting perception accuracy for these dampers. To validate our model, we first constructed a dataset of transmission line dampers covering diverse and complex real-world scenarios. Experimental results show that YOLOv11n-RDF outperforms existing mainstream models, achieving a remarkable 8.6 percentage point increase on the mAP@.5:.95 metric, which measures precise localization. The model strikes an excellent balance between accuracy and efficiency, offering a robust technical solution for achieving highly reliable automated monitoring of damper conditions.

title: Graph Neural Network-Based Method for Demand-Side Response Resource Regulation in Power Systems
authors: Yafei Ji, Ling Ling, Ting Zhang, Runming Dai, Xuming Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: Industrial enterprises consume substantial amounts of electricity and possess significant load regulation potential. Load-side demand response is a key approach for flexibility adjustment in distribution networks and optimal dispatch of demand-side resources. To fully leverage the adjustable capacity of these resources, a power demand-side response resource control method based on a graph neural network model is proposed. First, an uncertainty model for renewable energy output is constructed using fuzzy sets based on electrical distance, and a demand response baseline model that accounts for renewable energy forecast errors is reconstructed. Similar-day data are selected for calibration, and a neural network model is employed to rapidly estimate load power. By relying on the small dataset generated from calibration, the data required for neural network training is reduced, effectively lowering the structural complexity and training time of the neural network, thereby enabling rapid model response. A hierarchical clustering algorithm is applied to cluster the load curves of industrial enterprises, and the minimum safe load for each enterprise is calculated and compared within time windows to identify their adjustable load capacity. Finally, using electricity consumption data from industrial enterprises in a specific region, it is verified that the proposed strategy can enhance and optimize the regulation of power demand-side response resources.

title: A New Energy Benchmark Electricity Price Model Based on System Cost Optimization
authors: Xudong Jia, Xiaodong Chen, Xuyao Xiao, Zhe Xu, Jiang Wang, Zhaoyuan Wu, Ming Zhou
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: In response to the demand for market-oriented pricing reform of new energy electricity, this paper proposes a benchmark electricity price model based on system cost optimization. The model quantifies the overall system cost savings resulting from the integration of new energy sources—including thermal power generation costs, environmental costs, and energy storage costs—to establish a dynamic pricing mechanism. Specifically, the benchmark price is determined by the reduction in system cost per unit of newly installed capacity. Using a provincial power grid as a case study, empirical results show that the benchmark price for wind power is 0.308 ¥/kWh, higher than that of solar PV, which stands at 0.21 ¥/kWh. The primary reason for this difference is that wind power can replace high-cost power generation units and alleviate supply shortages during critical periods, whereas solar output coincides with midday peak load, limiting its marginal substitution effect.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Early Diagnosis of Chronic Liver Disease
authors: T V N L Aswini, Kamesh Sonti, Purnima K Sharma, A V N Kumara Swamy, T J V Subrahmanyeswara Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Chronic liver disease (CLD) is a severe global health concern that is frequently recognized only in the late stages due to limitations in standard diagnostic approaches. Reducing mortality and improving patient outcomes require early, accurate, and non-invasive CLD prediction. For the early diagnosis of CLD, this study offers a novel hybrid machine learning (ML) framework that combines Extreme Gradient Boosting and K-Nearest Neighbors. A dataset with demographic, lifestyle, and biochemical information for 1,700 people was examined. With an accuracy of 98%, precision of 0.96, recall of 0.95, and an AUC of 0.99, the suggested hybrid model performed better than any other. These results demonstrate how hybrid ensemble models can improve prediction accuracy, provide a dependable non-invasive method for early CLD screening, and open the door for sophisticated healthcare decision-support systems. Notably, the suggested paradigm shows potential for early-age screening as well, considering the rising prevalence of pediatric and adolescent CLD associated with obesity and lifestyle modifications.

title: Research on Contour Error Prediction of Five-Axis CNC Machine Tools Based on CLA-Net
authors: Xiyang Zhang, Juntao Li, Yi Hu, Yanqing Zhao, Di Wu, Zeng Tao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Contour error, primarily induced by the coupling of multi-axis dynamics and servo lag, is a critical factor limiting the machining accuracy of computer numerical control (CNC) machine tools during complex surface machining. Traditional theoretical modeling methods based on dynamics and parameter identification are difficult to cope with complex nonlinear errors. To address the challenges, this study proposes a data-driven deep learning method to achieve high-precision prediction of contour errors. In this paper, the VMC2100B vertical machining center is used as the experimental platform, and the high-speed dynamic coordinate data acquisition research is carried out with the help of dynamic coordinate acquisition technology, which provides strong data support for the training of deep learning model. A hybrid deep learning prediction model named CLA-Net (CNN-LSTMAttention Network) was constructed: 1D Convolutional Neural Networks (1D-CNN) were employed to extract local spatial features, Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTM) were used to learn long-term temporal dependencies, and an Attention was introduced to dynamically focus on critical historical information. The experimental results show that the proposed CLA-Net model can control the mean square error (MSE) at about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 ~ \mu m}$</tex>, which verifies the effectiveness and superiority of the model in the prediction task. This study provides a new solution for the contour error prediction task of five-axis machine tools.

title: Data Science Approaches for Global Renewable Energy Forecasting: Analysis of Sustainable Energy Data (2000–2020)
authors: Shivam Chaurasiya
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The transition to renewable energy is vital to mitigating climate change and achieving Sustainable Development Goal 7 (SDG 7) on affordable and clean energy. Accurate forecasting of renewable energy adoption is critical for policymakers, researchers, and energy planners to design effective strategies for sustainable development. This study presents a comprehensive data science framework that benchmarks statistical, machine learning (ML), and deep learning (DL) models using the Global Data on Sustainable Energy (2000–2020) dataset. Baseline models, including Linear Regression (LR) and AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA), provide interpretable foundations but demonstrate limited forecasting accuracy. Classical ML models such as Random Forest (RF) and XGBoost significantly improve performance by capturing non-linear feature interactions. DL approaches, particularly Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and CNN–LSTM hybrids, further enhance predictive capacity by modeling temporal dependencies across two decades of global data. To introduce novelty, hybrid ARIMA–XGBoost and Transformer-based architectures are employed, yielding superior results with an overall accuracy of 96.7% and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) below 5%. Comparative analysis demonstrates that while statistical models offer interpretability, hybrid DL approaches deliver state-of-the-art forecasting accuracy. The findings underscore the importance of advanced data-driven methods for global energy forecasting, providing actionable insights to guide renewable energy transitions, carbon reduction strategies, and evidence-based energy policy formulation.

title: RBD-Net: Enhancing Binocular Depth Accuracy with Robust Multi-Scale Neural Networks
authors: Xin Zhang, Yuqi Song
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Depth estimation is critical for applications such as autonomous driving and robotics, enabling these systems to perceive and interact with their environments effectively. Binocular depth estimation, which utilizes stereo image pairs to derive depth information, is a popular approach due to its potential for accuracy. However, the performance of existing methods is highly dependent on ideal conditions, focusing predominantly on scenarios involving perfectly aligned devices and optimal imaging conditions. These methods often fail to address real-world challenges like variable lighting, camera misalignments, and environmental noise, limiting their practical utility in non-ideal or dynamic settings. To address these limitations, this paper introduces RBD-Net (Robust Binocular Depth-Net), a sophisticated convolutional neural network designed to robustly estimate depth from stereo images under diverse and challenging conditions. RBD-Net integrates a noise-reduction module that proactively mitigates the impact of noise and disturbances, thereby enhancing the reliability of depth predictions. The model employs a multi-scale approach to capture detailed depth features across various resolutions and incorporates a novel loss function tailored to improve precision and robustness in varying environments. Extensive testing on the KITTI 2015 and KITTI 2012 datasets demonstrates that RBD-Net not only maintains but also significantly enhances the robustness and accuracy of depth estimations compared to existing methods. Our findings confirm RBD-Net’s superior performance, establishing it as a formidable tool for achieving high-fidelity depth perception across a broad range of operational scenarios, making it highly suitable for advanced computer vision applications where environmental adaptability and accuracy are paramount.

title: Deep CNN-Based U-Net Framework for Accurate Human Detection in Complex Through-the-Wall Scenarios
authors: Zakeen Ahmad, Chen Peng
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Through-the-wall radar imaging (TWRI) enables critical tasks such as search and rescue, surveillance, and health monitoring. However, strong wall reflections often obscure weak human signatures. This work presents a simulation-driven framework for detecting stationary and breathing individuals at low frequencies behind walls. Synthetic radar datasets were generated in MATLAB, modeling multiple wall types and clutter conditions. A convolutional neural network (CNN)-based U-Net was developed, trained, and validated to suppress wall clutter and enhance target visibility. The model effectively distinguishes human responses from dominant wall echoes, yielding over 30 dB improvement in signal-to-clutter ratio. Tests on synthetic and emulated radar data further confirm its ability to recover weak target echoes while maintaining real-time throughput within FPGA power limits. Results demonstrate the robustness and efficiency of the proposed deep learning approach, establishing a strong foundation for embedded implementation and practical field deployment in complex through-wall scenarios.

title: A Prediction Framework Incorporating Support Vector Regression and Random Forest Models
authors: Yiyang Wang, Tingyu Lin, Zihang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In this study, a hybrid prediction framework integrating support vector regression (SVR) model and random forest model is constructed. Firstly, the support vector regression (SVR) model is used to transform the problem of seeking optimal regression hyperplane into a quadratic convex programming problem solution, capturing the nonlinear distribution law of the data, and realising the prediction of continuous-type variables in small-sample and high-dimension scenarios. Meanwhile, the Random Forest model is used to select the characteristic quantities such as quantity and change trend for specific outcome prediction, and the Spearman correlation coefficient is applied to carry out two correlation analyses in order to explore the intrinsic connection between different categories of data. With the multi-model collaboration and multi-method comprehensive analysis, the framework effectively enhances the analysis and prediction ability of data, and shows good universality and robustness in related data processing tasks, providing an effective methodological reference for similar researches.

title: Inter-Turn Short Circuit Fault Diagnosis in PMSM Using a Temporal Convolutional Network with Attention Mechanism and Parametric ReLU
authors: Na Wu, Yixiao Zhang, Yao Meng, Xingzhen Bai
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are key components in modern industrial systems, and their operational reliability is critical to overall system stability— particularly in demanding applications such as mining roadheaders. Among early-stage electrical failures, inter-turn short circuit (ITSC) faults are common and can lead to significant motor degradation or catastrophic failures if not detected promptly. To address the challenges of detecting weak fault signatures under harsh conditions and varying fault parameters, this paper designs a fault diagnosis method based on a temporal convolutional network with attention mechanism and parametric ReLU (TCN-AP). A PMSM fault simulation model is developed to emulate various fault scenarios and generate corresponding training data. The TCN-AP model is then used to identify the fault phase and shorted turn ratio. Simulation results demonstrate that, under varying fault resistance conditions, the designed TCN-AP method achieves a diagnostic accuracy of 97.41% and enables more efficient early fault detection compared with other deep learning methods.

title: Advanced Machine Learning with Feature Aggregation based Detection and Classification of Cardiac Arrhythmia on Electrocardiograms
authors: Majed Balkheer, Reda Salama, Mahmoud Ragab, Ashis Kumer Biswas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: As we age, our blood vessels exhibit signs of deterioration, often leading to cardiovascular diseases. Arrhythmia is a common early symptom, and its timely detection is vital for reducing mortality. Automatic arrhythmia detection, heavily reliant on machine learning (ML), has gained significant attention. Using sensors, it captures real-time electrocardiogram (ECG) data, which ML models analyze to classify arrhythmias. However, body movements and environmental factors often disturb ECG signals, introducing noise, missing samples, and unbalanced data distributions that hinder detection. This study introduces a novel Classification of Cardiac Arrhythmia Using Multi-Technique Feature Fusion and Deep Hybrid Neural Networks (CCAMFF-DHNN) approach for ECG-based clinical diagnosis. The main objective is to establish the CCAMFF-DHNN system as a reliable tool for real-time cardiac arrhythmia analysis in clinical settings. Initially, the CCAMFF-DHNN method applies comprehensive preprocessing, including interpolation for signal regularization, noise removal, standardization for consistent scaling, and data oversampling. Subsequently, it employs a fusion of feature selection techniques—principal component analysis (PCA), recursive feature elimination (RFE), chi-square test, and mutual information (MI)—to extract the most informative features from the high-dimensional ECG data. Finally, a hybrid deep neural network combining a convolutional neural network with a long short-term memory (LSTM)-driven denoising autoencoder (CL-DAE) performs arrhythmia classification. The proposed system undergoes extensive experimental evaluation, with outcomes assessed under various performance metrics. Simulation results highlight the superiority of the CCAMFF-DHNN approach compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Weakly Supervised and Knowledge-Constrained Multi-Source Learning for Building Fire Safety Risk Assessment
authors: Ao Xing, Di Zhang, Zhenpeng An
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of limited labeled samples and heterogeneous multi-source data fusion in building fire safety risk assessment. A weakly supervised and knowledge-constrained multi-source learning framework is proposed to enable label-efficient and interpretable risk modeling. The framework employs a Feature-Level Completion Model (FLCM) to impute missing attributes across buildings based on similarity relationships at the feature level and integrates an LSTM - MLP hybrid architecture to jointly fuse temporal and static features. Rule-based soft labels and self-supervised deviation detection form a unified learning objective, while knowledge constraints enhance model interpretability and stability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms traditional machine learning methods in expert-ranking consistency, providing a scalable and practical approach for intelligent building fire safety assessment and decision support.

title: MCP-YOLO: Lightweight Detection Method for Pointer Instruments
authors: Feiqing Zhang, Longqing Fang, Zhenyu Yin, Fulong Xu, Weibin Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In order to solve the deployment difficulties caused by the high computational complexity and large number of parameters of traditional models in the detection task of pointer instruments, a lightweight improvement scheme MCP-YOLO based on the YOLOv8s network is proposed. First, the lightweight backbone MobileNetV4 is used to reduce the number of parameters of the model. Secondly, a Lightweight Token Mixer(LTM) is designed. By integrating the new interaction mode of spatial and channel attention mechanisms, complex operations such as matrix multiplication and Softmax in traditional methods are avoided. Finally, a pointer instrument special dataset DTS1500 containing 1500 samples is constructed. Experimental data show that compared with the baseline model YOLOv8s, the GFLOPs and Parameters of the improved model on the validation set are reduced by 31.0% and 10.0% respectively, and the mAP50: 95 is improved by 1.3 percentage points.
