This lightweight design significantly improves the applicability of the model on mobile terminals and edge computing devices, and can effectively meet the engineering needs of real-time detection of pointer instruments in industrial sites.

title: Threshold-Based Memory Resource Allocation for Entanglement Distribution in Quantum Communication Networks
authors: Zihao Wang, Yuhang Liu, Xiaosong Yu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Efficient quantum memory management is a critical challenge for large-scale entanglement distribution networks, where the probabilistic nature of entanglement generation and finite memory coherence time often cause asynchronous link completion and severe resource blocking. To address this problem, we propose a Threshold-Driven Dual-Mode Allocation (TDDMA) scheme that adaptively switches between pre-allocation and ondemand allocation according to a configurable success-probability threshold. The algorithm dynamically classifies links into highreliability and low-reliability categories. High-success-probability links are pre-allocated at the beginning of each attempt to guarantee continuity, while soft links allocate memory only when entanglement generation succeeds, releasing unused memories immediately after failure. This dual-mode mechanism effectively balances reliability and utilization by minimizing idle occupation without compromising end-to-end success probability. We develop a simulation framework on 64 -node grid and ring topologies to evaluate the proposed scheme. Results show that TDDMA achieves up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5. 7 \%}$</tex> lower blocking probability and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 - 8 \%}$</tex> higher effective memory utilization compared with the static End-to-End Reservation Allocation (EERA) baseline. Moreover, TDDMA demonstrates robust adaptability under varying traffic loads and link success distributions, maintaining stable performance even near network saturation.

title: Smart Jamming via Downlink Control Information Sniffing and its Countermeasures
authors: Shao-Di Wang, Heng-Wei Lou, Chang-Long Wang, Feng Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the susceptibility of the physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) to a novel smart jamming threat in fifth-generation (5G) new radio (NR). In this attack, an intelligent adversary first acquires resource allocation details by monitoring the downlink control information (DCI), and subsequently launches a targeted and stealthy jamming that severely undermines PUSCH data transmission. To defend against such smart jamming via DCI sniffing (SJ-DCIS), we propose an innovative suppression method by leveraging the DCI-scheduled subset identification and PUSCH resource reconstruction. The proposed method exploits distinguishable spatial characteristics specifically, variations in control channel element utilization and resource block group allocation patterns between legitimate users and the SJ-DCIS attacker. By identifying and filtering out malicious components while preserving the integrity of targeted transmissions, our method effectively mitigates the impact of SJ-DCIS. Simulation results are presented to validate the performance and demonstrate the efficacy of the developed defense method.

title: AI-Driven Analysis of Social Media Influence on Stock Market Trends
authors: Arpit Bhatia, Lakshay Khatri, Sagar Bhandari, Parul Gandhi
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The fast emergence of social media sites has changed the way financial information is conveyed, and this could affect the trends of the stock markets, though the exact effect is under-researched. This paper examines the connection between sentiment in social media and the stock market patterns, that is, the influence of online discussions on investor actions. We analyse a large amount of social media data through the application of AI-based natural language processing and sentiment analysis, and juxtapose this with historical stock market trends. In our approach, we combine machine learning to discover the sentiment trends and match them to the market changes. The results indicate that the social media sentiment has a substantial impact on short-term market volatility, especially in high-profile corporate events, but the impact of social media sentiment on longer-term trends is not as strong. Positive sentiment rises can be the forerunners of short-lived price increases, whereas short-term price drops can be aggravated by negative sentiment. We come to the conclusion that social media is a strong yet short-lived force in market dynamics, and we should not underestimate the fact that investors and regulators must evaluate the role of social media in the formation of financial markets.

title: Resource Optimization in Computer Networks using Differential Evolution Algorithm
authors: Sandeep Kumar Sunori, Chitresh Bhatia, Pradeep Juneja
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The intensive development of computer networks has made the necessity of effective and intelligent resource allocation systems extremely high. Conventional optimization techniques very often fail to deal with the nonlinear relation and multiobjective trade-offs between the network resources including bandwidth, CPU allocation, memory usage, power consumption, and buffer capacity. To solve these issues, this paper outlines a Differential Evolution (DE)-based resource optimization model to computer networks. The model designed uses MATLAB to allow the dynamic interaction of network parameters and the allocation to maximize throughput and minimize the operational cost. The algorithm will make repeated adjustments to a population of solutions by using mutation, crossover and selection to arrive at an optimal resource configuration. The analysis of the simulation reveals that this algorithm provides a balanced use of the resources, and an enhanced optimization efficiency. MATLAB has been used in the simulation, visualization and quantitative assessment of the algorithm performance.

title: Transfer Learning-based Culvert Silting Condition Classification
authors: Hemanth R. Madduri, Jun Zheng, Claudia Wilson, Isabel Morris, Wesley Cook
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Culverts are essential structures that channel water across or alongside roads and other infrastructure. Their primary function is to facilitate proper drainage, preventing water accumulation, stagnation, erosion, and potential flooding that could compromise structural stability and longevity. Regular inspections and maintenance are crucial as culverts deteriorate over time. However, traditional manual inspections often involve stringent conditions, making them hazardous, time-consuming and labor-intensive, especially in culverts with poor structural integrity, limited visibility, or confined spaces. These challenges highlight the need for predictive models to automate the inspection process, improving efficiency, accuracy, and safety. In this paper, we investigated the use of transfer learning to automatically classify culvert silting conditions based on image data. Transfer learning leverages a model pretrained with a large dataset for a specific task and applies it to a new, related task, significantly reducing training time and computational resources. Our approach employs pretrained convolutional neural network (CNN) models, including DenseNet-121, EfficientNetV2-Small, ResNet-50, and VGG11, to extract features from culvert images. The extracted features are then fed into a custom classification head to predict silting conditions. We evaluated model performance using a culvert image dataset collected in the state of New Mexico, categorizing silting conditions into three levels: low (<10%), medium (10%-60%), and high (>60%). Our results show that the model using EfficientNet as the feature extractor achieved the highest classification performance. Additionally, the customized classification head significantly outperformed traditional machine learning classifiers, including Support Vector Classifier (SVC), Random Forest, and XGBoost.

title: Bridging Visual and Linguistic Intelligence for Chest X-rays: A Comprehensive Review of ViTs and LLM Synergies
authors: Ismail Hossain, Mridul Banik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: The integration of Vision Transformers (ViTs) and Large Language Models (LLMs) in chest X-ray analysis has emerged as a promising solution to address the growing challenges in radiology, including increasing diagnostic workloads and the need for timely, accurate interpretations. This systematic review examines the recent advancements in ViTs-LLM hybrid systems, exploring their architectural innovations, multimodal fusion strategies, and application in automated report generation. A comprehensive search of databases such as Google Scholar, PubMed, and IEEE Xplore was conducted to identify studies published between 2018 and 2025, focusing on ViTs-LLM integration, performance metrics, and clinical validation. Key findings highlight that ViTs-LLM models significantly improve diagnostic accuracy, with a 15% improvement in pneumonia detection compared to traditional CNN-based models. These systems also excel at producing clinically relevant reports, achieving a 93% alignment rate with clinician-generated reports. Research demonstrates that ViTs-LLM hybrid models reduce diagnostic errors, enhance radiology workflow efficiency, and support clinical decision-making by offering real-time assistance. However, challenges related to computational complexity, data biases, and regulatory approval remain, posing barriers to widespread clinical adoption. Future directions include optimizing these models for real-time deployment, addressing ethical concerns, and integrating them into clinical settings with minimal disruption to existing workflows. The review points out the opportunity for ViTs-LLM systems to enhance both diagnostic performance and patient care, offering a transformative tool for the future of radiology.

title: Prenatal Heart Diagnosis Using Convolutional Neural Networks: A Web-Integrated Approach (PrenatalNet)
authors: Surabhi Kakade, Meghraj Ranaware, Shreyash Jadhav, Ishika Kale, Roshan Madake, Saurav Maghade
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Congenital heart disease (CHD) is one of the most common birth defects, and pre-prenatal diagnosis plays an important role in the planning of timely medical intervention. This article presents PrenatalNet, a fixed nervous network (CNN) based video processing framework designed to help with automatic identification of CHD from the fetal ultrasound video. The system integrates an intensive teaching model into a user-friendly web application, able to upload ultrasound videos to doctors and receive real-time clinical predictions. Backend achieves inputs from each video frame using advance exercise models, predicting overlay notes, and the highest frequency provides an anotat output video with predicted results. The system was built with OpenCV and Flush and originally ran with Google Colab. The system indicates how to use learning on diagnosis before delivery can be used effectively. This work provides a scalable and cheap solution for A-nhen's foster heart evaluation, especially with low resolution or distance applications in the health care system.

title: Asymmetric Protection-Based S-SFC Deployment for LLM-Inference-Enabled Networks
authors: Hengyu Jin, Danyang Zheng, Huanlai Xing, Xiaojun Cao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In emerging LLM-driven edge computing scenarios (e.g., edge robotics), service function chains (SFCs) must be deployed with both security and reliability guarantees. To enhance security, security-aware service functions (S-SFs) are employed, forming a security-aware SFC (S-SFC). To ensure reliability, service providers typically use dedicated backup SFCs to protect the primary one. However, no existing works jointly consider SFC security and reliability in the deployment process. To address this gap, we propose the concept of asymmetric protection for S-SFCs, allowing the primary and backup chains to differ in terms of order, version, and SF types to reduce resource overhead. We formally define the problem of Securityaware Service Function Chaining, Embedding, and Protecting (SSFCEP) and prove its NP-hardness. To efficiently solve S-SFCEP, we propose the Near-Source SFC Deployment (NS-SD) algorithm. Extensive simulations demonstrate that NS-SD reduces resource cost by up to 56% (1.8× savings) compared to state-of-the-art benchmarks, while achieving faster runtime.

title: ElbowHack: Video-Assisted PIN Inference Through Elbow-Forearm Dynamics Analysis
authors: Bo Yin, Yuefeng Chen, Hongbo Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Personal Identification Number(PIN), as a mainstream method for identity verification and payment authorization, has become a prime target for malicious attacks, leading to significant economic and privacy losses. However, with the increasing awareness of security, people now deliberately cover their hand movements when entering PIN to protect their privacy, leaving attackers with minimal side-channel information to exploit. In this paper, we present a novel video-assisted PIN inference system, ElbowHack, which leverages video captured from behind the victim to launch the attack. Specifically, to address scenarios where attackers cannot capture hand or wrist data because of visual occlusion, we utilize the subtle spatiotemporal information of elbow joints and the upper forearm contours to infer the entered PIN code. We develop an attack system that identifies the click time of each keystrokes, reconstructs fingertip coordinates using elbow joint positions and arm contour dynamics, and infers the entered PIN with inferred fingertip coordinates. with extensive experiments. ElbowHack demonstrates an average accuracy of over 73 % on the first attempt and over 81.3 % after 3 attempts.

title: DETU-Net: An Improved TransUNet with Dual-Branch for Right Ventricle MRI Segmentation
authors: Junchi Lu, Yu Fu, Bing Xu, Qingdi Li, Changhao Li, Haibo Yang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases related to the right ventricle have a high mortality and disability rate worldwide. Accurately segmenting the right ventricle from cardiac magnetic resonance imaging is essential for the diagnosis and analysis of cardiac diseases. However, owing to the intricate crescentshaped geometry of the structure, blurry boundary features, and significant shape variation from the apex to the base, automatic segmentation of the RV still faces tremendous challenges. Existing methods still lack accuracy in dealing with the complexity and deformability of such structures. Therefore, we propose a DETUNet model to segment right ventrilce, which mainly consists of two components: the dual-branch module and the ExTransformer module. The Dual-branch module employs a parallel branch structure to capture detailed information, thus enabling the extraction of the edge features of RV. ExTransformer enhances the long-range dependencies of the model, not only to focus on its own global features, but also to enhance the modeling of RV regions with structural discontinuities or large morphological variations in the form of cross-slices. Comparative experiments are conducted on both RVSC and ACDC datasets. The results on the RVSC dataset show that DETU-Net achieved a mean Dice on the epicardium and endocardium of 0.88 and 0.91, which demonstrates that our segmentation approach outperforms representative approaches.

title: A Stackelberg Game Model for Incentivizing Civil Aviation Data Sharing
authors: Meng Yue, Bolin Chen, Yuze Tian
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of civil aviation has generated vast and complex data, making accurate valuation and effective sharing difficult. Traditional single-dimensional methods are inadequate in multi-source environments, reducing data owners' willingness to share. To address this, we propose a Stackelberg game-based framework with data owners as leaders to maximize their utility. A multidimensional valuation approach replaces traditional intermediaries, enabling precise assessment. The framework consists of two stages: the first optimizes data source selection under complex multi-source scenarios, while the second applies dynamic pricing strategies to incentivize bulk transactions. This dual-stage design improves both valuation accuracy and sharing efficiency. Simulation results show that, compared with the bargaining model, the Stackelberg model increases data owners' utility by 26.11% and overall system efficiency by 52.88%, demonstrating enhanced willingness to share and improved system performance in the civil aviation data ecosystem.

title: Erasure Code Enhanced UAV Swarm Mission Planning for Critical Data Collection
authors: Haitao Wang, Zhuyu Liu, Zheng Dong, Zhi Liu, Yong Zhou, Jinhua Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: We consider a critical data collection mission undertaken by an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarm in a complex environment where each UAV is subject to the risk of being randomly damaged. Generally, to enhance data collection efficiency, each UAV is responsible for collecting data in a specific area with minimum or even no overlap and then carrying each copy back to the data center. Once a UAV is destroyed, the information collected by this particular UAV cannot be recovered by the data center. To deal with this problem, we employ erasure codes to combat random UAV loss. More specifically, the original information such as the remote sensing data for a certain area is encoded through the utilization of erasure codes, and then the UAV swarm collects data in accordance with the encoded data to achieve fault tolerance. We propose three distinct encoding schemes corresponding to the three scenarios of fault tolerance of 1, fault tolerance of 2, and fault tolerance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 3$</tex>, and design an optimized erasure code in the case of fault tolerance of 1, which significantly reduces the data collection duration. Simulation results demonstrate that with the aid of erasure codes, the probability of completing the data collection task has been substantially enhanced. Under the same fault-tolerance requirements, the number of UAVs required by the proposed method is much smaller than that of the backup-based method.

title: Predictive Modeling Framework for Early Identification of Hypothyroidism among Women
authors: Akshita Sharma, Vijeta Sharma, Rahul Sachdeva, Deeptanshu Jha
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: The rising cases of Hypothyroidism, especially among Women, have generated an urgent need for the effective detection of hypothyroidism using various personal and clinical thyroid data of patients. Hypothyroidism is a common disorder directly linked to the functioning of the thyroid gland, which is necessary for production of hormones that control the metabolism of body, its growth, and overall development. It is often under-diagnosed due to its non-specific symptoms. Early and accurate detection is essential to prevent long-term complications, yet conventional diagnostic practices face limitations in efficiency and scalability. The present paper compares the effectiveness of several classification approaches using clinical thyroid function data in order to assess supervised machine learning (ML) models specifically tailored for the detection and of hypothyroidism in women. The results show that Random Forest outperforms the other approaches achieving an accuracy of 96.8%. The proposed model may be used by medical practitioners to enhance the decision making and for early medication of patients so as to avoid related complications of hypothyroidism among women.

title: Research Based on Machine Learning Technology for Enhancing Consumer Behavior Prediction Models
authors: Jing Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This article systematically evaluate machine-learning models for consumer-behavior prediction across retail, e-commerce and subscription platforms. On 14 industry datasets (2.3 M sessions), CatBoost attains 0.934 precision, 0.935 recall and 0.985 ROC-AUC with 23% fewer features than XGBoost; both outperform SVM (0.886 recall) and RF (0.787 accuracy). A deep-learning session-level model reaches 0.952 F1 but requires <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 \times$</tex> training time. Production A/B tests show 30% stock-out reduction, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 5}$</tex>% CTR uplift and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 8}$</tex>% churn decrease. Interpretable trees remain preferred for regulated domains, whereas gradient-boosting and neural nets scale best for high-dimensional multi-modal data.

title: Research on Intelligent Facility Visualization System Design and Emergency Management Based on Internet of Things Technology
authors: Yunjie Sun, Xuesong Wang, Lina Xue, Zhipeng Yao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: In the construction and development of modern cities, emergency management can complete the prevention and treatment work through the intelligent facility visualization system, effectively control the loss of personnel and property, and provide technical support for the creation of smart cities. With the continuous development of the Internet of Things technology and three-dimensional visualization technology, the design of intelligent facility visualization system and emergency management system has become possible, and domestic and foreign scholars have put forward a number of issues on these contents. Therefore, after understanding the current research status of intelligent emergency management system and measures, this paper deeply discusses the key technologies and application principles of intelligent facility visualization system, and proposes effective measures for emergency management combined with practical cases, in order to provide reference for the design of intelligent facility visualization system and emergency management research in China.

title: Advanced OTP Based EV Charging and Maintenance Assistive System
authors: Baby Shalini V, Dhanush K, Leakhana R, Sukanya G, S Balaji
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The rapidly accelerating global transition towards electric vehicles highlights the critical necessity for cutting-edge charging innovations that tackle issues of accessibility and safety. This paper introduces an OTP-based charging system that can offer vehicle-to-vehicle wireless charging, coupled with GPS tracking and smart fault detection. The system proposed facilitates EV users in low battery emergencies to call for help through a secure website where OTP authentication confirms system integrity. After being authenticated, the system employs geo-location information to locate the user's vehicle and sends out mobile charging vehicles. Charging automatically initiates as the EV reaches the point in question, reducing reliance on static infrastructure. At the same time, the system uses voltage and current sensors to check charging behavior in real time. By processing current-voltage characteristics, the system can check for anomalies like overcurrent, undervoltage, and abnormal charging cycles and prevents unsafe charging practices. This method increases the convenience of the user, facilitates charging ease, and provides safety to batteries, ultimately leading to the mass appeal and reliability of EVs.

title: Research on Elastic Protection Methods of Generalized End Nodes in Information Networks
authors: Yunjie Gu, Jing Wang, Shijie Wang, Yanbin Li, Jianxing Chen, Huaidong Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: The trend of modernization of information networks is in full swing, however the continuous enrichment of network extensions has also introduced new security risks. The current information network security protection methods have problems such as limited applicability, poor scalability, and high resource consumption, making it difficult to match the current scale of information development and construction. In response to the appeal issue, this article conducts research on elastic protection methods applicable to information networks. Adhering to the concept of “elastic security protection” of “adaptability to defense scenarios”, “scalability of defense capabilities”, and “efficiency of defense methods”, we have established a basic framework of protection technology that can cover the generalized end nodes of information networks. We propose a security protection elastic adaptation method for complex battlefield environments, integrating protection functions and means based on access control and extended by monitoring, warning, risk assessment, etc. Research has shown that the elastic protection method designed in this article has higher attack blocking rate and lower false alarm rate. Filling the blind spots of information network security protection, and endowing defense with elastic expansion capabilities.

title: An Intelligent Personalized Learning Recommendation Algorithm Based on Deep Learning for Student Learning Behavior
authors: Jiahui Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To address the challenges of traditional recommendation algorithms in educational settings, which often underutilize multi-source data and struggle to capture dynamic demand, this study proposes a personalized learning recommendation algorithm based on multimodal attention fusion and LSTM. First, a multi-source data input system is constructed, preprocessed with anomaly cleaning, feature normalization, and temporal reconstruction. A CNN is then used to extract multimodal features of learning resources. An LSTM is used to model students' temporal learning behaviors, and a dynamic attention mechanism is designed for adaptive feature fusion. Finally, a two-layer fully connected network is used to predict student resource preferences. Experiments were conducted on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5, 0 0 0}$</tex> junior high school students and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> resources from a domestic K12 platform (January to June 2024). The training/validation/test set was split in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7: 2: 1$</tex> ratio, and the algorithm was compared with six other algorithms, including traditional CF and a single LSTM. Results show that the proposed algorithm achieves Precision@5 of 0.82 and Recall@15 of 0.86, representing improvements of 13.9 % and 13.2 % respectively compared to the NCF. The MAE and RMSE are 0.085 and 0.108, respectively, reducing them by 24.1 % and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 5. 5 \%}$</tex> compared to a single LSTM algorithm. Training time is 8.5 hours, and the recommendation response time is 0.3 seconds, achieving a balance between accuracy and efficiency, meeting the needs of personalized recommendations in online education.

title: Calibration and uncertainty analysis of mode conversion loss parameters of balanced twisted pair wire
authors: Lei Yu, Yunling Wang, Yibin Wan
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Balanced twisted-pair transmission lines stand as critical media for data communication, finding widespread application in high-speed data transmission scenarios such as buildings, data centers, and factories. They enable the transmission of voice, data, images, and other forms of information to target nodes within a network, demonstrating exceptional anti-interference performance. With the rapid progress of information technology, high-speed data transmission has set more rigorous requirements for frequency bands and anti-interference capabilities. In noisy environments, the common-mode and differential-mode signals of balanced twisted-pair transmission lines are susceptible to interference from external sources such as industrial noise, resulting in imbalanced Ethernet transmission signals, which may give rise to potential bit errors and data retransmissions. This paper elaborates on the calibration method for the Equal Level Transverse Conversion Transfer Loss parameter, conducts a comparative analysis of measurement results obtained from network analyzers and cable analyzer, and assesses the uncertainty of these measurements, thereby providing technical support for relevant metrological technology research.

title: Intelligent Dispatching Resource Optimization Configuration for Power Equipment Production Command
authors: Bei Li, Yuhuan Jiang, Haixin Luo, Siyuan Liu, Junfeng Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper proposes a solution based on an improved multi-objective sparrow search algorithm for the problem of optimal configuration of power equipment production dispatching resources. The algorithm balances the multi-objective relationship through a dynamic weight adjustment mechanism, and uses an adaptive step size strategy and an elite reverse learning strategy to improve search efficiency and accuracy. In the experimental design, based on actual production data and simulation data, traditional genetic algorithms and particle swarm optimization algorithms are selected as comparison algorithms, and the optimal solution quality, convergence speed, solution set diversity and other indicators are used for evaluation. The experimental results show that the improved algorithm has a 32<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">%</sup> increase in convergence speed compared with the traditional genetic algorithm and a 28% increase compared with the particle swarm optimization algorithm; in terms of optimal solution quality, the production cycle is shortened by an average of 18%, and the resource utilization rate is increased by an average of 22%; in terms of solution set diversity, the hypervolume index is increased by an average of 25% compared with the comparison algorithm. This algorithm provides an effective way to optimize the configuration of intelligent dispatching resources for power equipment production command.

title: DiffFreq: Emphasizing Important Frequencies and Equal Learning of All Frequencies for Time Series Generation
authors: Xuncan Xiao, Yi Zhu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Seasonal-trend decomposition is pivotal for time series analysis, yet existing frequency-domain learning methods face significant limitations. Approaches focusing solely on high-amplitude frequencies fail to capture subtle but critical variations encoded in low-amplitude components. Conversely, methods treating all frequencies equally often under-learn high-amplitude patterns, leading to suboptimal decomposition and generation quality. To overcome issues, we propose DiffFreq, a novel diffusion-based framework for time series generation. DiffFreq uniquely combines equal learning of all frequencies with adaptive emphasis on important frequencies, enabling a more comprehensive and balanced seasonal decomposition. This dual strategy is realized through specially designed components including Patch Attention and Adaptive Weighting Model, integrated within Transformer layers enhanced with rotational position encodings. Extensive experiments on eight challenging benchmarks demonstrate that DiffFreq consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly enhancing generation quality. Specifically, DiffFreq reduces the average correlation score metric by 41% and the average Context-FID metric by 19% compared to existing methods, confirming its superior fidelity to real data patterns.

title: Research on OFDM Subcarrier Extraction and Reconstruction Method Based on Multi-Rate Filtering for 5G Radar
authors: Peiyuan Hui, Chenghu Cao, Haisheng Huang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the high-fidelity extraction of a single subcarrier from a wideband Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) signal, this paper proposes a multi-rate filtering chain comprising down-conversion, anti-alias filtering, decimation, narrowband low-pass filtering, and up-conversion, combined with normalized cross-correlation for delay estimation. Under a high sampling rate of 122.88 MHz, two stages of Kaiser-window Finite Impulse Response (FIR) filters are designed to reduce computational complexity while effectively suppressing adjacent-channel interference (ACS). The method preserves the inherent delay and Doppler characteristics of the target subcarrier and enables high-precision delay measurement. Simulation results show that, compared with direct full-rate processing, the proposed scheme reduces the back-end computational load by more than an order of magnitude while keeping the delay-estimation error below 5% of one full-rate sampling interval. Furthermore, extensive simulations under various signal - to - noise ratio (SNR) conditions confirm the robustness of the proposed method. Even in the presence of strong adjacent - channel interference and random delay - Doppler shifts, the two-stage Kaiser - window filtering structure preserves the amplitude and phase integrity of the target subcarrier and provides stable delay estimates. The multi-rate implementation also facilitates real-time processing, making the technique well suited to high-bandwidth communication, passive radar sensing, and other applications that require accurate timing information while operating with limited computational resources. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach offers a practical and scalable solution for high-fidelity OFDM subcarrier extraction and precise delay estimation in wideband systems.

title: STM32-Based Smart Humidifier with Enhanced PID Algorithm
authors: Shimin Zhang, Xueting Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This study proposes an enhanced incremental PID algorithm for smart humidity control, employing a third-order recursive parameter self-tuning framework with sliding time window strategy. It dynamically adjusts PID coefficients, reducing steady-state error from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1 0 \% \text{RH}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2. 6 \% \text{RH}$</tex> (74%) improvement) at 50% RH target. Experimental results show 4.3% overshoot (vs 23.7%) and 84s settling time, maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 3.5 \% \text{RH}$</tex> accuracy under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10 \% \text{RH}$</tex> disturbances. A cloud-edge architecture enables 280 ms response via JavaScriptencapsulated mobile apps, forming closed-loop control. Statistical analysis confirms significant superiority over commercial products (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.01$</tex>) in precision and dynamics, offering a scalable solution for cost-effective smart environment devices through algorithm optimization and lightweight communication protocols.

title: A Comparative Study of Stacked Ensemble and SVM Models in Breast Cancer Classification
authors: S. Nithya Selvakumari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The research evaluates Support Vector Machine (SVM) alongside a Stacked Ensemble Model for their diagnostic use in breast cancer through direct comparison. The two models learned from classification data consisting of malignant and benign tumor patterns to determine their performance capabilities. An SVM model achieved successful detection of malignant cases with 90.69 % accuracy alongside precision at 1.00 and recall of 78.95 % combined with an F1-score value of 88.23 %. Layered / stacking an ensemble learning algorithm that uses meta-model to stack or layer-wise stack many classification, or regression models together. The level- 0 predictions given in base models are fed into a more comprehensive model (level-1 or meta-model) which produces the end prediction produced the best results which delivered 94.18 % accuracy along with 0.96 precision and 91.67 % recall and 93.75% F1-score. The experimental findings from these results demonstrate ensemble learning delivers improved performance through stable operation while using generalized algorithms than independent SVM classification models. These acquired data points validate that ensemble methods enhance diagnostic support systems for breast cancer detection through optimal optimization features. The enhanced performance of the stacked ensemble model directly supports global efforts to combat life-threatening diseases by providing reliable, technology-driven healthcare solutions.

title: A Multi-Label Fake News Detection Framework Using Undersampling and Transformer-Based Deep Learning Models
authors: Pratyush Ranjan Mohapatra, Jayadeep Pakala, Nagagopiraju Vullam, Vunnava Dinesh Babu, Naladi Ram Babu, P. Chandra Sekhar Reddy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The rapid spread of misleading and fabricated information across social media platforms has intensified the need for robust fake news detection systems. Traditional binary classification methods are insufficient for real-world scenarios where news items often receive multiple credibility judgments. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a multi-label fake news detection framework that integrates undersampling strategies with advanced transformer-based deep learning models. The dataset used in this study consists of tweets annotated with multiple credibility ratings, enabling multi-label classification through annotator-based label vectors. Two undersampling techniques—One-vs-Rest (OVR) and MultiLabel Random Undersampling (MLRU)—are employed to mitigate the effects of label imbalance and improve detection of minority credibility classes. Classical machine-learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, and transformer models including BERT and RoBERTa are evaluated on both undersampled datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that transformer models consistently outperform traditional approaches, with RoBERTa achieving the highest performance across all evaluation metrics. Furthermore, the application of MLRU significantly improves class-wise recall in minority categories. These findings highlight the effectiveness of combining undersampling with transformer architectures for multi-label fake news identification in highly imbalanced realworld settings.

title: A DC-Transformer Driven Multi-Source Dynamic Thresholding Algorithm for Spatiotemporal Pollution Early Warning
authors: BoLin Qi, Xiong Yang, Hongliang Wang, Jian Wu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Accurate early warning of air pollution is not only a key technical challenge in urban environmental management but also an important task for sustainable development. Traditional fixed-threshold methods, constrained by static national standards, fail to capture the spatiotemporal dynamics of environmental pollution and therefore often lead to false alarms and missed detections. To address this limitation, we propose a DC-Transformer-based dynamic thresholding framework that models threshold determination as an adaptive and evolving process. The framework integrates multi-source information, including real-time monitoring data, short-term sequence predictions, and long-term seasonal patterns, and generates context-aware dynamic warning thresholds through a weighted combination. Within this framework, diffusion convolution captures spatial dependencies among monitoring stations, while the Transformer network models complex nonlinear temporal evolution, jointly enabling fine-grained prediction of pollution dynamics. A tunable weighting mechanism further enhances flexibility while preserving interpretability. Experiments on multi-station hourly datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms fixed-threshold strategies in terms of accuracy, recall, and F1-score, effectively reducing missed detections while controlling false alarms. Overall, this study provides an efficient and scalable solution for dynamic pollutant early warning, offering both theoretical contributions and practical value for intelligent urban environmental management.

title: Construction of Japanese Text Mining Model Using Natural Language Processing Technology
authors: Xiaolu Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of complex grammar and semantic understanding of Japanese text, this paper proposes a Japanese text mining algorithm that integrates multimodal information with a dual attention mechanism and constructs a corresponding model. The algorithm captures the semantic association of Japanese sentences through the intra-text attention mechanism, realizes the interactive fusion of multimodal information such as text and image through the inter-modal attention mechanism, and optimizes information fusion with an adaptive weighting strategy. The experiment uses Japanese news, novels, academic papers and other types of data sets, and compares them with Text CNN, LSTM and other models with text classification and sentiment analysis as tasks. The results show that in the text classification task, the accuracy of the model in this paper is 92.3 % in the news corpus, which is 11.2 % higher than Text CNN and 8.7% higher than LSTM; the accuracy on the novel data set is 89.7 %, which is 6.8 % higher than the average of the comparison model. In the sentiment analysis task, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex> value of the model in the news corpus reaches 89.6 %, which is 7.5 % higher than the average of the comparison model. Ablation experiments show that removing the intra-text, inter-modal attention mechanism and fusion strategy will reduce the model performance by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.2 \%, 4.8 \%$</tex> and 3.3 % respectively, verifying the effectiveness of the algorithm and proving that the multimodal and dual attention mechanisms significantly improve the model's ability to process Japanese text.

title: Bridging the Age Gap: Do Privacy Literacy, Self-efficacy, and Concerns Explain the Effects of Age on Privacy Decisions?
authors: Reza Ghaiumy Anaraky, Kaileigh A. Byrne, Marten Risius, Bart Knijnenburg
abstract: None

title: Enhancing Building Change Detection in ChangeFormer by Integrating Deformable Attention Transformer
authors: Seung Bae Jeon, Myeong-Hun Jeong
abstract: Urban building change detection in remote sensing imagery supports planning, disaster response, and environmental monitoring; however, dense layouts, diverse building types, and varying imaging conditions still challenge existing models. We propose deformable attention transformer for enhanced building change detection (DA-ChangeFormer), which integrates a deformable attention transformer (DAT) into ChangeFormer. DAT adaptively focuses on regions with high change likelihood and enables flexible feature sampling, capturing subtle changes more effectively than fixed self-attention while suppressing pseudo-changes from illumination or seasonal shifts. We evaluate the proposed method on the LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and DSIFN-CD datasets. On LEVIR-CD, DA-ChangeFormer achieves 92.8% F1 and 87.3% IoU, outperforming our reimplementation of ChangeFormer by 1.06 and 1.62 points, respectively, and the originally reported ChangeFormer results by 2.40 and 4.80 points. Across datasets, the model demonstrates strong generalization in complex urban environments. In addition to performance gains, the proposed architecture reduces the parameter count relative to ChangeFormer under matched model configurations and shows modest FLOPs savings in the 256×256 setting while maintaining comparable FLOPs in the 512×512 setting. Overall, DA-ChangeFormer demonstrates that selectively integrating deformable attention into semantically rich encoder stages improves localization accuracy while maintaining competitive computational cost.

title: An Analysis of the Human Ability to Detect Deepfakes With Geopolitical Content
authors: Michele Brienza, Marta Golotta, Marco Romano, Daniele Nardi, Domenico D. Bloisi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The increasing diffusion of deepfakes has raised significant global concerns, especially due to their potential geopolitical implications. This concern relates to the spread of false information that can mislead people and have a serious impact on societies. However, identifying what can misinform people is not trivial. In this work, we present an experimental study that involves a sample of students of different backgrounds. Three different political deepfakes were created and shown to them. The perceived values were then analyzed using a specific questionnaire. The experimental results show significant differences in the ability to discern the authenticity of the proposed videos depending on the level of awareness of the contents viewed. This demonstrates the crucial role of education on deepfakes in countering the spread of misinformation.

title: Few-Shot Medical Image Segmentation With Hierarchical Hypercorrelation Vision State-Space Model
authors: Zheng Cao, Bang Du, Danqing Hu, Wei Zhou, Shangde Gao, Rong Tan, Jun Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Few-shot medical image segmentation (FSMS) seeks to delineate novel anatomical structures using limited annotated examples, thereby reducing dependence on extensive manual labelling. Conventional methods face challenges with computational inefficiency, overfitting, and information loss, particularly in high-ambiguity medical contexts. To overcome these limitations, we propose the hierarchical hypercorrelation vision state-space model (H2VSS). The novelty of H2VSS is twofold. First, we introduce a dedicated HyperCorrelation Block (HCB) designed to address the low-contrast and contextual ambiguity endemic to medical imaging. This module computes and fuses dense correlations derived simultaneously from both target-level (foreground-focused) and image-level (context-aware) support features, providing a robust feature basis that methods relying on target features alone cannot achieve. Second, H2VSS presents a novel architectural synergy by integrating vision state-space (VSS) blocks into a hierarchical, multiscale feature matching pipeline. This design leverages the linear-complexity and long-range dependency modeling of VSS to enhance matching consistency beyond prior Transformer-based hierarchical methods, while remaining architecturally distinct from recent sequential Mamba-based FSMS models. Evaluated on three public medical image datasets, H2VSS demonstrates superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) FSMS models, outperforming the prior SOTA by a significant margin.

title: A Phaseless Source Reconstruction Method Based on Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Network
authors: Zhao Feng, Shijian Zheng, Xiaoting Yuan, Shaoqiu Xiao, Hao Chen, Yin Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this work, a phaseless equivalent source reconstruction method based on a two-stage physics-informed neural network (PINN) is proposed. Unlike conventional dipole-based equivalent source artificial neural networks that rely on Green's functions, the proposed approach is capable of recovering the phase information of the equivalent sources while simultaneously predicting both near-field magnitude and phase. By leveraging the physical mapping relationship between equivalent sources and near-field magnitudes, the proposed framework enables the joint reconstruction of the magnitude and phase of the equivalent sources. Moreover, through a phase-information constraint, it effectively alleviates the multi-solution problem inherent in phase retrieval. Compared with traditional iterative phase-retrieval algorithm, the proposed method exhibits superior performance in both magnitude prediction accuracy and phase recovery capability. Two numerical examples are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Simulation results show that for a 2×2 patch antenna array and a microstrip line on glass-base substrate, the method achieves magnitude prediction errors of 0.7% and 2%, respectively, while recovering relatively accurate phase distributions. These results indicate that the proposed method is well suited for near-field measurement, as well as antenna analysis and design applications.

title: Women in Engineering
authors: N/A

title: Lightweight Learning from Actuation-Space Demonstrations via Flow Matching for Whole-Body Soft Robotic Grasping
authors: Liudi Yang, Yang Bai, Yuhao Wang, Ibrahim Alsarraj, Gitta Kutyniok, Zhanchi Wang, Ke Wu
abstract: Robotic grasping under uncertainty remains a fundamental challenge due to its uncertain and contact-rich nature. Traditional rigid robotic hands, with limited degrees of freedom and compliance, rely on complex model-based and heavy feedback controllers to manage such interactions. Soft robots, by contrast, exhibit embodied mechanical intelligence: their underactuated structures and passive flexibility of their whole body naturally accommodate uncertain contacts and enable adaptive behaviors. To harness this capability, we propose a lightweight actuation-space learning framework that infers distributional control representations for whole-body soft robotic grasping directly from deterministic demonstrations using a flow matching model (Rectified Flow), without requiring dense sensing or heavy control loops. Trained with only 30 demonstrations covering less than 8% of the reachable workspace, the learned policy achieved a 97.5% grasp success rate over 1000 trials in simulation. In real-world experiments on 50 uniformly distributed targets, the policy achieved a 100% success rate, generalized to object size variations from -33% to +100%, and remained stable under execution-time scaling from 20% to 200%. These results demonstrate that actuation-space learning effectively embeds mechanical intelligence into control, significantly reducing reliance on centralized computation for grasping under uncertainty.

title: A Planning-Oriented Autonomous Driving Framework: From Image to Trajectory With Intent-Aware Prediction
authors: Hong-Yi Kang, Jun-Guo Lu, Ming Wei, Qing-Hao Zhang, Zhen Zhu, YaFei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Autonomous driving requires accurate prediction of surrounding agents and reliable planning of ego-vehicle trajectories, yet integrating these components into a coherent pipeline remains challenging. This paper proposes a planning-oriented framework that maps bird’s-eye-view images directly into executable ego-vehicle trajectories, balancing an end-to-end formulation with modular components. The system operates in three sequential stages: multi-agent intent-aware prediction, where intent embeddings are injected into agent encodings together with static environment structures to enhance joint feature fusion, followed by a trajectory decoding stage that further exploits intent to guide sequential prediction; goal-conditioned trajectory generation via an imitation-learning planner with cross-attention and a transformer decoder; and geometric post-processing to enforce obstacle clearance and trajectory smoothness. Throughout the framework, road structure features can be obtained from coarse semantic layers in the bird’s-eye-view domain, which provides sufficient abstraction for prediction and planning while preserving flexibility and avoiding reliance on high-definition maps. Evaluations on the nuPlan benchmark show that the proposed method achieves strong and well-balanced performance across standard non-reactive and reactive planning metrics, while maintaining real-time efficiency with an average overall pipeline latency of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$164{\,}\mathrm {ms}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. In addition, a preliminary deployment on an Intelligent Guided Vehicle in a mixed-traffic container terminal demonstrates the practical deployability of the proposed framework in real-world industrial scenarios. Although not fully differentiable, the design intentionally balances end-to-end learning with structural modularity to ensure interpretability, flexibility, and robustness, ensuring both practical feasibility and strong closed-loop performance.

title: Learning Based Online Tracking Algorithms for Marine Litter in Multibeam Water Column Images
authors: Pedro Alves Guedes, Hugo Miguel Silva, Sen Wang
abstract: Marine litter is a growing environmental threat, with severe ecological and socio-economic impacts. Most monitoring strategies rely on optical sensors to detect surface pollution, however these approaches fail to capture submerged plastics dispersed throughout the water column. Multibeam acoustic imaging offers a complementary solution, but the scarcity of annotated sonar datasets and the high noise levels of acoustic imagery make automated detection and tracking particularly challenging. This study presents a comparative evaluation of deep learning based multi-object tracking (MOT) algorithms applied to water column acoustic data. Pre-trained YOLOv8 detectors were integrated with tracking-by-detection frameworks including BoT-SORT, OC-SORT, ByteTrack, and DeepOC-SORT. Performance was assessed across acoustic frequencies and preprocessing strategies using standard MOT metrics. Results show that adaptive Gaussian thresholding and opening morphology improved robustness at lower frequencies (950 kHz and 1200 kHz), while unprocessed inputs proved more resilient to severe clutter at 1400 kHz. BoostTrack and ByteTrack achieved the most consistent tracking, effectively managing intermittent detections to maximise MOTA and IDF1. In contrast, OC-SORT underperformed, struggling with fragmented sonar trajectories. Furthermore, while efficient Nano models dominated at lower frequencies, Medium models were required under higher noise. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of applying MOT methods to sonar-based litter monitoring. Future work will explore unsupervised learning approaches to leverage intrinsic sonar data structure, reduce annotation needs, and enable scalable marine litter tracking.

title: A Simulation Platform for MARL Training and Evaluation in Swarm Confrontation_supp1-3677714.pdf
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: In swarm confrontation, robots must swiftly formulate strategies in transient environments, a challenge well-suited for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL). However, existing platforms suffer from the lack of comprehensive confrontation scenario modeling and scalable frameworks, hindering MARL's widespread applications. We introduce a novel platform for training, simulating, and evaluating MARL algorithms in swarm confrontation tasks. It constructs a holistic simulation framework by integrating robot, environment, and rule models for complex confrontation scenarios. Equipped with a decentralized task allocator and path planner for each robot, the platform enables scalable cooperation across dynamic environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our platform simulates confrontations involving up to twenty agents per side, providing empirical guidance for algorithm selection in various settings.

title: Efficient Resource Allocation for Integrated Sensing and Communication Towards 6G
authors: Jiaying Ren, Yahia Shabara, Shawn Tsai, Doru Calin

title: Spatio-Temporal Series Prediction Based on Multi-Objective Evolutionary Deep Convolutional Echo State Networks
authors: Meiling Xu, Lixin Tang, Lu Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Accurate prediction of spatio-temporal series remains a challenging task due to complex spatial dependencies, temporal dynamics, and factors such as data sparsity, nonlinearity, and high dimensionality. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework which integrates a spatio-temporal interpolated deep convolutional echo state network with an improved multi-objective evolutionary algorithm. The model integrates cubic spline interpolation to enhance temporal resolution, a spatial attention mechanism to capture inter-location correlations, and a convolution-based deep echo state network to efficiently model spatio-temporal patterns. A multi-objective evolutionary algorithm is then employed to jointly optimize model hyper-parameters with two objectives of maximizing prediction accuracy and minimizing model complexity. To further improve efficiency, a topological data analysis-based strategy is used to select a representative and diverse subset of candidate solutions, reducing computational cost without compromising optimization quality. Experiments on two real-world meteorological datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior prediction accuracy across single and multiple horizons. Ablation study further confirms the importance of each architecture component and the effectiveness of the proposed optimization strategy. The results validate the potential of the proposed model as an efficient tool for real-world spatio-temporal forecasting tasks.

title: A High-sensitive and Fast-response MXene/GO Pressure Sensor for Low Pressure Sensing and Dynamic Threshold Voltage Regulation
authors: Siwei Sun, Ye Zheng, Debo Wang
abstract: In order to achieve the higher sensitivity and faster response, a MXene/GO piezoresistive pressure sensor with bilayer PDMS microstructures is proposed, in which MXene (Ti<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>) and GO dispersion are mixed in a volume ratio of 1:4. The composite exhibits a synergistic effect, ensuring high conductivity and excellent mechanical strength. Moreover, the bilayer hemisphere microstructures effectively increase the contact area between pressure-sensitive layers and establish abundant conductive paths under pressure. The measurement results show that the sensor achieves extraordinarily high sensitivity (21.65 kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>) within the low-pressure range (0~1.25 kPa), remarkably fast response time (4.9 ms) and recovery time (7.3 ms), low hysteresis (3.6%), and minimal repeatability error (3.9%). Furthermore, it is innovatively integrated into the voltage comparison circuit as an adjustable resistance. By switching three loading conditions, dynamic threshold voltage regulation is completed, resulting in the output signal demonstrating different duty cycles. Therefore, this work provides a novel sensing module for voltage comparison circuits, enabling immediate and easier-to-access threshold voltage regulation.

title: Model-Driven Engineering
authors: N/A
abstract: None

title: RISCAM: A 576-Element 2-D Scalable Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface With Multibeam RF Camera at X-Band for ISAC
authors: Tyler James Blundo, Zijian Shao, Juho Park, Emir Ali Karahan, Alex Stepko, Kaushik Sengupta
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) mitigate non-line-of-sight (NLOS) challenges but remain limited in 5G wireless systems due to poor scalability and the absence of integrated user localization needed for low-latency beam management and joint sensing–communication. Conventional AP-driven beam management adds significant delay and signaling overhead. This work introduces the first RIS with built-in user localization using a shared aperture integrating an RF camera forming 13 simultaneous beams. The 576-element (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$24\times 24$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) surface forms reconfigurable beams scanning –50° to 50°, achieving ~36 dB directivity and ~3° beamwidth. Thirteen sensing subarrays (208 elements) enable dual-purpose sensing and reflection, realizing an RF camera with <2° RMSE localization accuracy. By combining multibeam sensing with reconfigurable reflection, the system can estimate the angle of arrival (AoA) and dynamically retro-reflect or steer wavefronts. Measurements validate precise AoA estimation, effective beam steering, and strong agreement with full-wave simulations for future 6G integrated sensing and communications (ISACs).

title: New Input-to-State Stability Results by Revisiting Vector Halanay's Inequality
authors: Frederic Mazenc, Michael Malisoff
abstract: We study vector versions of Halanay's inequality. We provide an input-to-state stability result. Even in the scalar case, we provide tighter bounds than those that were available in the literature. Our key new techniques involve finding componentwise positive vectors in the null spaces of suitable matrices. These matrices are expressible as weighted sums involving the coefficient matrices that occur in the vector versions of Halanay's inequality. We allow time-varying coefficients in our Halanay decay conditions. Other key tools to obtain our tighter bounds include novel applications of the Perron-Frobenius Theorem.

title: A Novel Vector Control Integrated for Grid-Connected Kite-Based Airborne Wind Energy Conversion
authors: Pankaj Kumar, Yashwant Kashyap, K. Manjunatha Sharma
publish: IEEE Canadian Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering
abstract: Kite-based airborne wind energy conversion system (KAWECS) provides an innovative and interesting concept to exploit high-altitude winds for efficient power generation. The framework of this system utilizes a flying kite connected to the generator through a power electronics interface for grid integration. However, maintaining stable generator speed and maximum power extraction is very challenging due to the dynamic and periodic nature of the kite motion. This study presents a novel vector control integrated to enhance the performance of a grid-connected kite-based airborne wind energy conversion. The vector control scheme is designed to control the speed and torque of the permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG), ensuring optimal power extraction under varying wind conditions. A novel optimal symmetrical method is used to design the controller gain of the vector control scheme. Furthermore, a grid-side converter (GSC) controller is designed to regulate the DC-link voltage and synchronize the current and voltage with the grid. The LCL filter is designed to improve the quality of the grid voltage and current. The proposed system is implemented in MATLAB Simulink and validated hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) using a field programmable gate array (FPGA)-based OPAL-RT OP-4520 platform. The results demonstrated stable generator speed tracking, regulated DC-link voltage at 800 V, and distortion-free grid currents with a total harmonic distortion (THD) of 5.95%. Furthermore, the proposed control strategy improved system performance by achieving a 45.6% capacity factor, compared to 31.4% for the conventional wind turbine system (CWTS). These quantitative results confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed controller for real-time grid-connected airborne wind energy applications.

title: Self-Adapting Federated Continual Learning With Gradient Causal Constraint for Fault Propagation Path Identification
authors: Kai Zhong, Xiang Peng, Zhenhua Fan, Darong Huang, Shixiang Lu
abstract: In modern industrial scenarios, federated learning (FL) has been widely adopted due to its advantages in privacy preservation and distributed modeling. However, existing FL approaches rely on a single update paradigm, which significantly hampers communication efficiency. Moreover, the server preserves historical aggregation states to model causal propagation across training rounds, instead of performing memoryless per-round aggregation. For this end, we propose a self-adapting federated continual learning with gradient causal constraint (GCC-SFCL). First, the selection of synchronous or asynchronous communication across FL rounds is reformulated as continual learning task, where an experience accumulation mechanism records historical aggregation states to model long-term temporal dependencies among client updates. Based on the aggregated context, we further design a self-adapting scheduling strategy driven by multidimensional client state information, enabling the server to dynamically select optimal aggregation. Furthermore, a causality-steering gradient constraint is introduced to explicitly regulate gradient updates, improving the interpretability of the causal model and allowing the capture of fault propagation paths. Experimental results of a simulation case and the real-world case demonstrate that GCC-SFCL significantly improves not only communication efficiency but also the accuracy of fault propagation path identification.

title: Toward Generalizable Forgery Detection and Reasoning
authors: Yueying Gao, Dongliang Chang, Bingyao Yu, Haotian Qin, Muxi Diao, Lei Chen, Kongming Liang, Zhanyu Ma
abstract: Accurate and interpretable detection of AI-generated images is essential for mitigating risks associated with AI misuse. However, the substantial domain gap among generative models makes it challenging to develop a generalizable forgery detection model. Moreover, since every pixel in an AI-generated image is synthesized, traditional saliency-based forgery explanation methods are not well suited for this task. To address these challenges, we formulate detection and explanation as a unified Forgery Detection and Reasoning task (FDR-Task)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>, leveraging Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to provide accurate detection through reliable reasoning over forgery attributes. To facilitate this task, we introduce the Multi-Modal Forgery Reasoning dataset (MMFR-Dataset), a large-scale dataset containing 120K images across 10 generative models, with 378K reasoning annotations on forgery attributes, enabling comprehensive evaluation of the FDR-Task. Furthermore, we propose FakeReasoning, a forgery detection and reasoning framework with three key components: 1) a dual-branch visual encoder that integrates CLIP and DINO to capture both high-level semantics and low-level artifacts; 2) a Forgery-Aware Feature Fusion Module that leverages DINO’s attention attention maps and cross-attention mechanisms to guide MLLMs toward forgery-related clues; 3) a Classification Probability Mapper that couples language modeling and forgery detection, enhancing overall performance. Experiments across multiple generative models demonstrate that FakeReasoning not only achieves robust generalization but also outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both detection and reasoning tasks.

title: Secure performance of STAR-RIS aided networks with a full-duplex active eavesdropper
authors: Ping Yang, Liang Yang, Weichao Kuang, Shanjin Wang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Unlike prior works assuming perfect attack detection, this paper investigates the physical layer security of a simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) aided network under a full-duplex active eavesdropper in the more realistic scenario where its jamming remains undetected by the legitimate transmitter. Assuming Nakagami-m fading channels, we first derive the probability density functions (PDFs) of the signal-to-interference ratios (SIRs) at both the legitimate user and the eavesdropper. Then, using the PDFs, we obtain novel closed-form expressions for key secrecy metrics, including the probability of nonzero secrecy capacity (PNSC), secrecy outage probability (SOP), and average secrecy capacity (ASC). Moreover, the asymptotic PNSC, SOP, and ASC at high signal-to noise ratio (SNR) are also derived. Based on those asymptotic expressions, we present compact expressions for several key performance indicators: 1) the secrecy diversity order and the secrecy array gain of the SOP, and 2) the high SNR slope and the high SNR power offset of the ASC. Results demonstrate that, compared to the passive eavesdropper scheme, the active jamming attack carried out by Eve will not cause any loss in secrecy diversity gain and high SNR slope, but only result in the loss of secrecy array gain and high SNR power offset. Finally, we verify our analytical results via simulations.

title: Corrections to “Ultra-Wideband Tightly Coupled Dipole Array Fed by a Tapering Meandered Balun”
authors: Matthew W. Nichols, Michail O. Anastasiadis, Malcolm E. Taaffe, Elias A. Alwan, John L. Volakis
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Presents corrections to the paper, Corrections to “Ultra-Wideband Tightly Coupled Dipole Array Fed by a Tapering Meandered Balun”.

title: Smart Predict and Optimize: A Tutorial on End-to-End Optimization Resource Allocation for IoT Networks
authors: Xiaoyu Song, Kunsheng Wang, Kwan-Wu Chin, Changlin Yang
abstract: The operators and nodes of Internet of things (IoT) networks often perform resource allocation, where they may solve a mathematical program to compute the optimal allocation of resources or decision(s), e.g., energy usage of devices, using sensed data as parameter values. Further, these parameter values may be stored/predicted using a neural network given one or more features, e.g., historical energy arrival rates at nodes. In this respect, to date, there are no IoT specific tutorials or works that focus on the smart predict and optimize (SPO) resource allocation framework. This paper thus fills this gap. Briefly, it shows the workflow of SPO, which involves using a machine learning model and a mathematical program to compute decisions. A key feature of SPO is that unlike prior approaches that only aim to predict the parameter values of a mathematical program, it maps the features of a system to decisions in an end-to-end manner. This paper also outlines various potential IoT applications that would benefit from SPO. It then presents a case study where SPO is used to decide task offloading from a device that has to contend with other users in a WiFi network. Lastly, it outlines some future research directions.

title: ReSage AI: An AI-Enhanced Resume Screening Framework with Intelligent Content Scoring and Suggestive Feedback Mechanism
authors: Mukil Ganesh C, Prasanna Sai S, Lokesh Raj J, K.Regin Bose
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: While recruiters are overloaded with applications candidates frequently find it difficult to modify their resumes to match employer expectations in today’s competitive job market. In order to evaluate and enhance resume quality this paper proposes an intelligent resume analysis and recommendation system that makes use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Based on industry- standard hiring criteria, the system extracts, evaluates and generates feedback from resumes (in PDF format) using sophisticated computer vision and natural language processing techniques. In order to contextualize the resume content and provide focused recommendations regarding grammar, formatting, content clarity and professional completeness, the suggested solution incorporates Google’s Gemini Application Programming Interface (API). In order to determine whether a profile photo is included in the resume, a deep learning- based image recognition module driven by OpenCV is also used.

title: Nonsingular Fixed Time Integral Sliding Mode Trajectory Tracking Control Design for Quadrotor
authors: Madhumita Pal, Sanjoy Mondal, Santosh Sonar, Debabrata Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper proposes a nonsingular Fixed-Time Integral Sliding Mode Control (FxT-ISMC) scheme based on a State-Dependent Variable Exponent (SDVE) framework for trajectory tracking control of quadrotor Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). The proposed approach addresses the challenges of achieving precise and robust trajectory tracking under external disturbances and system uncertainties while ensuring efficient use of control. Unlike conventional terminal sliding mode controllers (TSMC) that rely on constant exponent terms, the proposed method introduces variable exponents in the sliding surface and control design to achieve fixed-time stability (FxTS) of both the sliding variables and the system states, independent of initial conditions. The integral sliding mode formulation ensures non-singular implementation of control law. Moreover, SDVE framework offers simple structure in constructing sliding surface, thus reducing computational complexity, and improved implementation feasibility. Rigorous stability analysis using Lyapunov techniques confirms the FxTS and robustness of the control system. Simulation results on a quadrotor UAV demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed FxT-ISMC approach in achieving accurate, and robust trajectory tracking in fixed time, even in the presence of disturbances and uncertainties.

title: AI-Driven Observability for Real-Time Incident Management
authors: Priyanka Verma, Shivam Ashokbhai Lalakiya, Rashmi Bharathan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The more complicated character of cloud-native and micro services-based infrastructures has led to the inability of the former-monitoring systems to respond to an incident in real-time. The contribution of an artificial intelligence (AI-based) observability framework, with multi-modal telemetry pipeline, machine learning-based anomaly detectors workflow, predictive incident correlation and machine learning-based automated remediation workflow will be presented to improve the reliability and effectiveness of the system in the current paper. The model was evaluated based on the benchmark data and enterprise observability space simulation. The results show that it had high level of performance with a lower Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) by 63.6 percent, lower Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR) by 60 percent and higher detecting accuracy by 16 percent and reduction of false-positive rates by 72 percent compared to traditional monitoring. These results suggest that the possibility of the framework can be based on the provision of proactive information, the accelerated recovery of the incident and less alert fatigue. Recent progress in smart incident management systems development could become the future of the study itself regarding explainable AI models, multicloud architecture, and self-healing observability pipeline.

title: Solving the Goal-Oriented Communication Problem through Policy-Level Constrained Programming
authors: Federico Chiariotti, Pietro Talli, Federico Mason, Andrea Zanella
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: A prototypal goal-oriented communication system consists of a transmitter agent that observes the state of a process and communicates it to a receiver agent that takes actions based on the information received to affect the behavior of the process itself. If the process is Markovian, this system can be modeled as a remote Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP). Despite the simplicity of the scenario, the complexity of finding optimal transmission and control policies grows exponentially with the size of the state space, calling for heuristic solutions. This is due to the interplay of the agents’ strategies, which need to consider the implicit information acquired by the receiver when the transmitter stays silent. In this paper, we propose a new way to optimize remote POMDPs, posing them as single-agent problems by introducing policy-level constraints. The proposed formulation allows us to adopt standard optimization tools for quadratically-constrained linear programming, significantly reducing the computational complexity of the task. In particular, we show that a Disciplined Convex-Concave Programming (DCCP) approach can perform near optimally while maintaining a manageable computational burden in a scenario in which policy iteration-based heuristics have a wide optimality gap.

title: Smart Grid Compatible Multiport Converter System for Renewable-Integrated EV Charging with V2G
authors: Magesh T, Sabari L Umamaheswari, L. Annie Isabella, Supriya S, Monisha K, Nandhini R
