abstract: Cardiovascular Diseases (CVDs) are the leading global cause of death and affect women disproportionately due to sex-specific risk factors, atypical symptoms, and frequent underdiagnosis. Despite advances in AI and ML that improve prediction and diagnosis, most models are male-biased, lowering accuracy for women. This paper presents the review of AI/ML Techniques for CVD Detection Techniques including the Deep learning methods, including CNN–LSTM and transformer architectures enhance ECG analysis and plaque detection but face issues with data imbalance, heterogeneity, and explainability. Bridging these gaps requires sex-balanced datasets, fairness-aware algorithms, and inclusion of female biomarkers. Future research should focus on sex-stratified validation, transparent reporting frameworks like TRIPOD-AI and CONSORT-AI, and prospective clinical evaluation in women with clinical and ethical implementation issues.

title: Refining Data Fusion and Production Process Optimization Based on Multimodal Large Models
authors: Tao Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This article focuses on the fusion of refining data and intelligent optimization of production processes based on multimodal large models. The refining production process is highly complex and strongly coupled, and abnormal working conditions are often sudden and diffuse. If not identified and intervened in a timely manner, it can easily trigger a chain reaction and cause serious losses. Therefore, this article proposes a deep learning (DL) anomaly monitoring framework that integrates multimodal data. In response to the bottleneck of short prediction cycle and low precision in traditional methods for abnormal data recovery, this paper constructs a time-series recovery model based on optimized long short-term memory network (LSTM). Firstly, principal component analysis (PCA) is used to reduce the dimensionality of high-dimensional data. Then, an LSTM network is constructed to capture temporal dependencies and integrate elastic network regularization to globally optimize hyperparameters, significantly improving the model's generalization ability and prediction stability. At the same time, in order to effectively process multi-source heterogeneous instrument image data on site, a lightweight YOLOv5s (You Only Look Once) network is introduced to achieve real-time visual monitoring and anomaly localization. The results indicate that the model can achieve high-precision and low latency recognition of abnormal states.

title: Deepseek-R1 Enhanced Adaptive RAG Framework for Intelligent Optimization of Titanium Alloy Melting-Casting Processes
authors: Hongliang Wang, Binglong Ji, Haoran Wang, Jing Xu, Pengfei Xiu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework integrated with domain-tuned DeepSeek-R1 (14B) for optimizing titanium alloy melting-casting parameters. Addressing critical industrial challenges including nonlinear thermal-stress coupling and realtime process control, our system implements a closed-loop “retrieve-generate-verify” workflow. Key innovations include: 1) Physics-constrained generation embedding metallurgical principles as differentiable loss functions; 2) Multi-scale knowledge retrieval combining ChromaDB-based case matching and knowledge graph reasoning; 3) Ray-accelerated finite element verification achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.7 \times$</tex> simulation speedup. Validated on TC4/TC11 titanium alloys, the framework reduces optimization cycles by 37.6% while maintaining 92.4% parameter accuracy against physical constraints. Industrial trials demonstrate porosity reduction from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 2 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 1 \%}$</tex> and dimensional accuracy improvement to IT6 tolerance class.

title: Predictive Analytics for Sales Forecasting at Ono Cafe Using Linear Regression and Business Intelligence Dashboard
authors: Danial Shah Iman Bin Mohamad Noor Khairul Azhillah, Arifah Fasha Rosmani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: This study addresses the lack of systematic, datadriven sales forecasting practices among small food and beverage (F&B) enterprises, using Ono Café as the case context. Many SMEs continue to rely on manual judgment and intuition, resulting in inconsistent inventory planning and limited operational foresight. To mitigate these challenges, this study develops a predictive analytics framework using linear regression to forecast monthly sales based on historical data from 2023–2024. The research fills a gap in existing literature by exploring underexplored advanced forecasting tools in small food and beverage (F&B) businesses, despite their proven benefits in larger retail environments. Using RapidMiner, multiple regression models were tested across three data split configurations (70/30, 80/20, 90/10), with the 80/20 split demonstrating the strongest predictive performance (RMSE = 172.679, MAE = 125.279, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.991). A Power BI dashboard was subsequently deployed to provide managers with actionable insights through interactive visualizations. The findings demonstrate the practical value of integrating predictive modeling and business intelligence to enhance decision-making, operational efficiency, and strategic planning in F&B SMEs.

title: Applications and Study of DenseNet-121 in Multi-class Chest X-ray Diagnosis
authors: Ling-Feng Chiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This study presents a systematic comparison of deep learning models for multi-class chest X-ray diagnosis, followed by a finding-level analysis. We adopt DenseNet-121 as a strong baseline and select the most comparable alternative— Swin+DenseNet+CrossAttention—as the primary comparator. Under an identical data split, we establish a train-validation-test pipeline with class-imbalance mitigation via weighted sampling. Training proceeds by freezing the backbone to learn the classification head, followed by full-model fine-tuning. We apply temperature scaling for probability calibration and report both overall and finding-level metrics, including Accuracy, Precision, Sensitivity, Specificity, F1, and AUC. To assess uncertainty and practical robustness, we define the T95 confidence threshold and Acc@95% coverage, and evaluate performance under brightness/contrast shifts, small rotations, Gaussian noise, and mild central cropping. Results show that DenseNet-121 achieves high accuracy and AUC overall and across most findings. Cross- attention fusion reaches comparable aggregate performance while exhibiting modest advantages in calibration and selective accuracy (T95 ≈ 0.59–0.63; Acc@95 ≈ 0.92–0.94), and it maintains slightly better median performance under several corruption settings. Finding-level analysis reveals different error profiles on harder classes, offering clinically meaningful guidance for model selection. Overall, DenseNet-121 balances effectiveness and efficiency; where clinical workflows demand tighter confidence thresholding and stronger shift robustness, cross-attention fusion provides added practical value. The proposed evaluation pipeline is fully reproducible and is intended to advance reliability and transparency in automated chest X-ray interpretation.

title: Instagram as a Digital Media Platform for the Supreme Court's Public Relations: Public Reach, Comprehension, and Strategic Challenges
authors: Felicia Kora, Muslikhin Muslikhin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This study explores the role of Instagram as a digital media platform for public communication by the Public Relations Division of the Supreme Court of the Republic of Indonesia (Humas MA-RI). As digital transformation reshapes institutional communication, Instagram has become a strategic tool to enhance transparency and deliver legal information in a visual and accessible format. Using Kent and Taylor's (1998) dialogic communication theory as an analytical lens, this research investigates how Instagram facilitates public reach, comprehension of legal content, and strategic interaction with the public. A qualitative case study method was used, involving semi-structured interviews with five relevant respondents from within and around the Supreme Court's public communication scope. Thematic analysis, based on Miles and Huberman's model, revealed that while Instagram is effective in disseminating information, two-way communication remains limited. Challenges include bureaucratic validation processes, content accuracy, and maintaining institutional neutrality. However, the potential for interactive and dialogic engagement exists, especially through features like comments, live sessions, and infographics. The study concludes that Instagram can serve not only as an information channel but also as a space for building public trust and participatory communication, provided it is managed with dialogic principles.

title: Instrument Development for Measuring the Effectiveness of Government Campaigns in Scam and Fraud Prevention: An Extension of Protection Motivation Theory
authors: Akhyari Nasir, Mohd Shah Shafie Idris, Muriati Muda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: The growing frequency of online scams and fraud has caused governments all over the world to undertake public awareness programs to encourage internet users to be more cautious. However, there is a major gap in assessing the impact of these programs. This study fills that gap by creating a theorybased and empirically informed tool for assessing the impact of government-led scam and fraud prevention programs on individuals' intentions to participate in protective behavior. Based on Protection Motivation Theory, the measure includes two fundamental cognitive appraisal constructs: perceived severity and perceived vulnerability. These are supported by three communication-related variables: campaign exposure, message understanding, and message credibility. A systematic approach was followed to operationalize each construct, using established definitions and adapting items to suit the context of scam and fraud prevention. The final measurement model consists of six constructs and eighteen items, reflecting the complex relationship between threat appraisal and communication in shaping protective behavior. The proposed instrument positions campaign exposure, credibility, and message clarity as key antecedents to threat appraisal, offering a structured basis for evaluating the effectiveness of scam prevention messages within a PMT-based research model. This study provides a robust tool for researchers and policymakers to assess the impact of public campaigns and offers practical insights into the design of effective communication strategies for cyber fraud prevention.

title: Blockchain-Based Voting System with OTP Authentication and Off-Chain Privacy Preservation
authors: Rahul Aravindh M, Prasannavelan R M, Pradeep N, K. Malathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: A secure and transparent blockchain-based voting system is proposed, designed to preserve voter anonymity, prevent tampering, and ensure one-vote-per-user compliance in decentralized digital elections. The system introduces a lightweight voter authentication layer using one-time password (OTP) verification, with off-chain hashed identity storage to prevent exposure of personal data. Unlike traditional models that rely solely on smart contract logic, this approach strengthens the end-to-end security boundary by validating user eligibility before on-chain interaction. Votes are cast through smart contracts deployed on a public blockchain, ensuring immutability and auditability, while maintaining voter anonymity by detaching authentication logic from vote recording. To address performance bottlenecks and storage limitations, non-critical identity data is excluded from the blockchain, with hashed authentication tokens acting as cryptographic proofs of voter legitimacy. The proposed method was validated through simulation of small-scale voting rounds, demonstrating secure vote casting with a rejection rate of 100% for duplicate or invalid attempts. Average authentication time remained under 200 milliseconds per session. The modular design facilitates integration with government or institutional ID systems and supports anonymous and verified voting modes, making it adaptable for educational, corporate, or civic deployment. Future iterations will explore zero-knowledge proofs to further enhance privacy guarantees while preserving voter eligibility validation.

title: Circular Patch Antenna with Corrugated Slot for Millimeter Wave Wireless Links
authors: Anslin Star R, Anitha G
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: A circular patch antenna operating at 60 GHz has been designed and simulated using Rogers RT/duroid 5870 as the substrate material. The initial model based on a conventional circular patch exhibited only moderate performance. To improve the performance two corrugated slots were incorporated into the patch structure, which enhanced energy coupling and reduced signal reflection. The optimized antenna achieved a return loss of −77.26 dB at a resonant frequency of 57.45 GHz and a peak gain of 7.53 dB. These results confirm that slot integration is an effective technique for boosting antenna efficiency, making the proposed design well suited for advanced wireless applications such as 5 G networks and short-range high-speed communication technologies.

title: Token-Invariant Contrastive Pretraining for Robust Domain Adaptation in Text Classification
authors: Aadhish C, Aravinthkumar P, Naagadiyanesh. N, Santhosh R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Domain adaptation is still an important issue in text classification, since models often break when faced with new words and semantic drifting. Current methods often only align high-level sentence or document embeddings while overlooking the evolution of individual token-level behavior, which forms the basic units of representation. This paper presents a lightweight, domain-aware Token Sorting Contrastive Pretraining (TSCP) framework to filter and prioritize domain-invariant tokens to facilitate representation learning to address distributional shifts. TSCP calculates the variance of token frequency between the source and target domains, emphasizing transferable and stable vocabulary via contrastive learning with a de-emphasis on domain-specific or noisy words. There is no modification to the architecture, nor the addition of parameters, making the approach feasible and efficient. We adopt two benchmarking datasets to evaluate TSCP: the Amazon Review Polarity dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3.6 \mathrm{m}$</tex> labeled sentences collected over 18 years) and the AG's News Topic Classification dataset (having more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 m}$</tex> news passages). TSCP improves cross-domain sentiment classification accuracy on Amazon from 85.0% to 87.2% and on AG's News from 88.0% to 89.3%, compared with baselines including fine-tuned BERT and SimCSE. We conducted all experiments on an average consumer laptop (Ryzen 55500 U, 16 GB RAM) for accessibility purposes. TSCP outperforms the state-of-the-art models and emerging applications in sentiment analysis, news filtering, and content moderation. In the future, we plan to study dynamic token weighting, multilingual adaptability, and apply it to generative pretraining frameworks.

title: Implementation of Hybrid model for Enhanced Crop Yield Prediction using Explainable AI for Precision Agriculture
authors: Sheetal Mavi, Neetu Mittal, Pradeepta Kumar Sarangi
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Crop yield prediction is important for global food security, sustainable farming, and efficient resource management in agriculture. Inaccuracy and inherent opacity are the main issue for accurate prediction. To solve the problems of deep learning models a hybrid model has been developed for better prediction. This research works to improve agricultural decision-making through advanced predictive modelling. In this research work SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping), have been employed. SHAP analysis helps us to understand the input feature impact of soil moisture at middle level growth and helps us to analyse the over fertilization that is no longer increasing the crop yield rather reducing it. This work shows integration of deep learning and explainable AI for data-driven decisions on irrigation process, usage of fertilizers, and strategies to improve the decision making of farmers and thus heading towards precision farming. A hybrid model that considers dynamic and static characteristics to forecast crop yields. The model employs Conv1D layers for finding short-term patterns and Bidirectional LSTM layers for long-term dependencies, along with dedicated dense layers for static features. The overall $\mathbf{R}^{\boldsymbol{\wedge}} \mathbf{2}$ is $\mathbf{0. 8 1}$ and $\mathbf{R}^{\boldsymbol{\wedge}} \mathbf{2}$ for specific crop types (wheat, soybean, corn, and rice) is always 0.91 and above, therefore outperforming models based on exclusive use of dynamic or static characteristics by a wide margin.

title: An Individual Depression Detection Method based on Bi-LSTM and Attention Mechanism in Sina Weibo
authors: Haichao Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Research to date indicates that information on social networking websites can be used to predict the early stages of depression. There is still considerable scope for further advancement and development of associated research methodologies. This is evidenced by the fact that studies using machine learning and other methods for identifying depression still lack satisfactory classification performance. The Weibo User Depression Detection Dataset, which comprises over 10,000 depressed users and 20,000 healthy users, was examined in this study. A deep neural network classification model, designated BBA, was constructed using the pre-trained model Bert to extract features from textual data. The aforementioned representations were then passed through a bidirectional long short-term memory (Bi-LSTM) layer, which was employed to capture the sequence information present within the input. Subsequently, an attention mechanism was applied to the output generated by the Bi-LSTM. The experimental results demonstrate that the performance of the model can be significantly improved by introducing a Bi-LSTM and an attention mechanism, as well as pre-training on a large-scale sentiment classification dataset, resulting in an F1 value of 97.83% and a recall value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 7. 8 2 \%}$</tex>.

title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Autism Spectrum Disorder Diagnosis and Therapeutic Insights: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: V. Chithra, J. I. Sheeba, S. Pradeep Devaneyan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: It is crucial to manage autism spectrum disorder (ASD) through accurate diagnosis, followed by efficient rehabilitation. AI methodologies include conventional machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL). DL approaches are concentrating on neuroimaging-based methods. ML and DL are cutting-edge methodologies that researchers have advanced for recognising ASD using neural network (NN)-based approaches. This survey paper provides a comprehensive overview of ASD, covering screening methods, key features, underlying causes, and treatment options. Numerous screening models, comprising standardised diagnostic tools and behavioural evaluations, are inspected, together with their efficacy in early identification. The paper also addresses the multifactorial causes of ASD, encompassing genetic, neurological, and environmental impacts, and emphasises how these factors shape individualized therapeutic strategies. Existing classification and identification approaches are reviewed, concentrating on both conventional methodologies and developing AI-based models. A comparative study of these approaches shows that the AI-based method offers greater scalability and precision; however, challenges such as limited data and model interpretability remain. This survey concludes by highlighting the importance of integrating clinical expertise with sophisticated computational models to improve ASD diagnosis and treatment, thereby enhancing outcomes and quality of life for affected individuals. Ultimately, it presents significant challenges for automated recognition of ASD and proposes some future work.

title: Pediatric T1D Copilot: LSTM-Driven Glucose Forecasting and Conversational Decision Support from Real-World CGM, Insulin, and Meal Streams
authors: Krishna Karan Ghantasala
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning pipeline for pediatric type 1 diabetes that fuses continuous glucose monitoring, insulin delivery, and meal inputs to forecast short-term glycemia and deliver actionable recommendations through a safety-aware conversational interface. A stacked LSTM model trained on the AZT1D real-world dataset learns 12-step temporal contexts over 62 engineered features, with regularization, early stopping, and patient-wise validation; clinical evaluation reports MAE, RMSE, MARD, time-in-range, and error-grid analyses to align with bedside utility. The system operationalizes predictions into individualized guidance for dosing, nutrition timing, and activity via a pediatric-oriented chat UI, illustrating how sequence models can translate multi-modal diabetes telemetry into interpretable, point-of-care decision support in ML-enabled healthcare.

title: Smart Classroom Assistant Based on Raspberry PI
authors: Huijie Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In today's increasingly digital education background, traditional classroom management is faced with many challenges, including teachers' difficulty in effective management, students' low learning efficiency and low utilization of classroom resources. The advancement of science and technology and the improvement of the performance of various intelligent devices have provided great support for the realization of smart classroom solutions. In this project, we integrate Android Studio platform, SQLite database, TCP/IP communication technology and other advanced technologies to create intelligent classroom assistants for teachers and students. This paper aims to design a set of intelligent solutions, with Raspberry PI as the core, a variety of sensors and mobile application software, including real-time environmental parameter monitoring of temperature and humidity sensors, real-time monitoring of classroom seat use by infrared sensor components and user seat selection services. In the future, we will further expand and optimize the system functions, explore intelligent application scenarios to ensure cross-platform compatibility of the system, strengthen system security and privacy protection, ensure better support for teachers and students' daily learning, and achieve intelligence and efficiency. Contribute to better classroom utilization, more effective classroom management and more targeted instructional activity design.

title: Weather Forecasting using Supervised Learning Techniques LSTM and XGBoost
authors: Pranali Kosamkar, Shambhavee Gune
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Weather prediction is a crucial aspect in many fields. It impacts the decisions made in day-to-day life. From farming to aviation, it is important to take into consideration meteorological factors and insights for accurate weather forecasting. Currently, AI, ML, and DL models are used alongside the traditional NWP (Numerical Weather Prediction), in order to increase the accuracy of prediction. Traditional NWP exhibits a low accuracy as compared to models with ML integration (0.1% increase in accuracy). This paper focuses on implementation of Deep Learning Model – LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) with different number of layers (1, 5, 10, 15) and Machine Learning Model - XGBoost to forecast certain factors of overall weather. Also, 2 different optimizers and activation functions (Adam, SGD, tanh, ReLu) are implemented in order to achieve a clear insight on which variation of LSTM yields more accuracy. It is observed that XGBoost (~73%) and one certain variation of LSTM (~72%) give the maximum accuracy. Even though many models are implemented till date for accurate weather forecasting, further research is required to analyze and create models that give high accuracy and perfect predictions.

title: Predictive Analytics for Vehicle Utilization and Fleet Decision Support in Higher Education
authors: Sharifah Masyitah Binti Syed Mohamad, Norulhidayah Binti Isa, Syahrul Hezrin Mahmud
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Efficient vehicle utilization is vital for higher education institutions (HEIs) that rely on transportation for academic, administrative, and operational needs. Traditional manual fleet management methods often poor resource allocation and limited visibility. This study analyzes vehicle utilization and travel patterns in a HEI to support datadriven fleet management. Predictive analytics techniques were applied to historical vehicle logbook data (2023–2024) using machine learning models, including Random Forest, Decision Tree, and Support Vector Machine. Two experiments were conducted; classification of vehicles based on utilization trends and prediction of daily trip counts. Results demonstrated that Random Forest achieved the best performance, with an accuracy of 77.86% in the classification task and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.773 in the regression task. An interactive dashboard was developed using Power BI to visualize vehicle mileage, driver activity, and underutilized resources. The findings highlight the potential of integrating predictive analytics and dashboard visualization to improve fleet planning, optimize resource allocation, and enhance operational efficiency in higher education environments. Future work may include real-time tracking, automated data collection, and predictive fuel monitoring.

title: Color Image Enhancement Using Ripple-Carry Approximate Adders
authors: Y. S. S Sriramam, Bala Sindhuri Kandula, Triveni Chintha, Dasari M N K Shankar, Vaddipalli Vinesh, Thoram Sai Ram Durga Ganesh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: From medical diagnostics to embedded vision, the improved quality of images is vital as they have a direct effect on decision-making. The present work analyzes a histogram equalization algorithm that uses Ripple-Carry Adder (RCA) and Approximate Ripple-Carry Adders (ARCAs) using different approximate variants such as using ARCAs (A1-A12). The findings indicate that the ARCA using A2, A9 and A11, gives the best compromise between computational performance and perceptual quality, with good PSNR and SSIM values. Apart from this, the Probability Density Function (PDF) and Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF) indicate the effect of approximation on the intensity distribution. Comprehensively, the results suggest that approximation when carefully applied to LSB positions reduces hardware metrics with no apparent quality reduction and thus approximate computing is a feasible option when the application demands real-time color image enhancement under power constrained operations.

title: End-to-End Advanced Visual Speech Recognition Using 3D-CNN and BiLSTM with Beam Search Decoding
authors: Medha Mummigatti, Pankaj Saini, Burungale Swapnil Birudev
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced visual speech recognition, focusing on Beam Search decoding for sentence-level performance improving the accuracy. The proposed architecture combines 3D-CNN and BiLSTM layers along which is optimised for CTC loss function that employs an end-to-end learning on the GRID dataset. Experimental results indicate that Beam Search decoding significantly reduces the number of transcription errors, achieving a CER of 2.4 % and a WER of 9.0 %, outperforming traditional greedy decoding with accuracy of 96 %. Improved contextual understanding and robustness allow for more accurate lip reading in silent and noise-prone environments. Furthermore, Beam Search also enables the model to assess several possible decoding paths, which helps it maintain consistent sentencelevel predictions and recover from unclear visual cues. This is particularly helpful for phonemes that are visually similar, like m, b, and p, where greedy decoding frequently fails. Additionally, the system adapts to differences in lip motion and articulation across speakers in the GRID dataset. Overall, the integration of Beam Search strengthens contextual understanding and improves robustness without modifying the core architecture, making the approach suitable for silent communication, assistive technologies, and noise-resilient speech interfaces.

title: XG-MSTA Machine Tool Anomaly Detection Method Based Multi-Modal Signals
authors: Xiyang Zhang, Feiyang Li, Yu Dong, Yi Hu, Yanqing Zhao, Yongze Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Computer Numerical Control (CNC) machine tools are pivotal in modern manufacturing, yet their machining performance, precision, and service life are frequently compromised by anomalies in axis motion arising from assembly quality issues or wear due to prolonged use. To address the challenge of accurate anomaly detection in CNC machine tools, this study proposes a novel Cross-Modal Gated Multi-Scale Transformer (XG-MST) model, leveraging multi-axis vibration and current signals for supervised learning. Evaluated on a self-collected dataset derived from real-world CNC machine tool operations, the model achieves a detection accuracy of over 95%. Further validation through small-batch multi-axis simulation experiments on the open-source Paderborn Bearing Dataset yields an accuracy of over 90%. These results underscore the XG-MST algorithm's superior performance and significant practical value for enhancing machine tool quality inspection and predictive maintenance in manufacturing.

title: Research on the Sentiment Analysis Algorithm of Works based on Deep Learning
authors: Muyang Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the context of information explosion, sentiment analysis of works is crucial to content processing and value mining. This paper proposes a sentiment analysis algorithm based on multimodal fusion and dynamic attention. It extracts text semantic features by improving the Transformer architecture, obtains image visual features by using lightweight convolutional neural networks, extracts audio acoustic features by combining Mel-spectrograms and recurrent neural networks, and introduces a dynamic attention mechanism to adaptively focus on key sentiment information. Experiments were conducted on a public multimodal sentiment analysis dataset. Compared with six mainstream algorithms such as LSTM, BERT, and Text CNN, the algorithm in this paper achieved an accuracy of 93.2% in sentiment classification, which is 4.7 percentage points higher than the suboptimal algorithm, the F1 value is 92.1% and the recall rate is 91.8%, which are significantly better than the comparison algorithms. Ablation experiments show that multimodal fusion and dynamic attention mechanism increase 3.5% accuracy and 2.8% respectively, which proves the effectiveness of this module. Experimental results show that this algorithm is effective in integrating multimodal information and capturing sentiment features accurately.

title: Research on Spiral Trajectory Modeling and Collision Control Based on Runge-Kutta Method and Genetic Algorithm
authors: Mingzhi Liu, Siyao Lu, Xiushan Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In this paper, a spiral motion model and related optimization methods are proposed, focusing on the motion laws of the head, body and tail of the spiral, the collision detection mechanism and the pitch optimization problem. Firstly, the polar coordinate equations of the spiral are established and converted into the right-angle coordinate parametric equations, and the position change of the spiral head under variable angular velocity is calculated by the integral method combined with the Runge-Kutta numerical solution method, so as to clarify the recursive position updating method of the spiral body and the tail segments. Secondly, the collision model is constructed, and the relevant parts are abstracted as quadrilaterals, and the collision between non-adjacent parts is detected by geometric boundary analysis, and the earliest collision time and the corresponding state parameters are determined by gradual iteration. Finally, a genetic algorithm is used to optimize the pitch parameter, taking the minimum pitch as the target, combining collision, spatial boundary and velocity constraints, and solving the optimal solution through selection, crossover and mutation operations. The model can accurately describe the spiral trajectory motion, and realize the motion simulation, collision detection and parameter optimization through numerical methods and intelligent algorithms, which are both accurate and efficient, and can effectively guide the design and optimization of spiral-like motion systems.

title: AI-Enhanced Prediction of Respiratory Irritation From Biomass Combustion Byproducts: An Integrative Machine Learning Framework for Sustainable Bioenergy Systems
authors: Shehabul Alam, Ayush Biswas, Rehnuma Islam, Shah Samadur Rahman, Hasebul Hassan Chowdhury, Sudoy Kumer Ghosh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Biomass burning has continued to be a part of renewable energy infrastructure, but due to incomplete oxidation, they produce fine particulate matter and volatile organic compounds that cause pulmonary oxidative stress in the exposed populations. The study is a dual-paradigm machine learning model that combines the frameworks of both the Random Forest and the Long Short-Term Memory to forecast respiratory health risks of bioaerosol exposure in biomassdependent rural areas. Environmental monitoring data (PM2.5 concentrations, nitrogen oxides, and volatile organic compounds) in time synchrony with the clinical data (inflammatory biomarkers) were integrated to predict against clinical biomarkers to create real-time risk stratification predictive models. Nitrogen oxides were the most important feature according to the importance analysis, which confirmed mechanistic connections between inefficiency in combustion and respiratory inflammation. Random Forest model was the best in the interpretability with low prediction bias whereas LSTM networks performed high-fidelity in their ability to model temporal exposure dynamics. This integrative framework closes the environmental engineering, medical diagnostics, and optimization of renewable energy, provides evidence-based opportunities in the implementation of clean cooking technology and feedstock processing strategies to balance energy access and respiratory health care in the sustainable changes of bioenergy.

title: CogRec: Neural Cognitive-Style Modeling for Adaptive Learning Path Recommendation in Communication-Centric Environments
authors: Huiying Wan, Yanan Gong
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: In online learning platforms, intelligent recommendation algorithms are now an essential tool for boosting individualized learning experiences and increasing learning effectiveness. However, current approaches frequently ignore the cognitive distinctions across learners, which leads in recommendation outcomes that are not sufficiently flexible. This study suggests the CogRec (Cognitive Style-Aware Neural Recommendation) model as a solution to this problem. This model uses adaptive path optimization and multimodal cognitive style modeling to dynamically suggest learning paths. Eye movement signals, electroencephalogram (EEG) patterns, microexpression features, and learning behavior records are all used by CogRec to create a cognitive style vector. Additionally, it uses a self-attention technique to combine multimodal elements. At the modeling level, the model combines an extended restricted Boltzmann machine (RBM) with an attention-based heterogeneous graph neural network (A-HGNN). This approach captures the complex relationship between users and learning resources. Then, a cognitive style adaptation function is introduced to dynamically adjust the recommendation list based on task difficulty and individual processing preferences. This allows for the generation of personalized learning paths that are highly tailored to the learner's characteristics. The experimental part is verified based on two open education datasets (EdNet, MOOCCube) and a self-built multimodal dataset (CogLearn). At the same time, it is compared with four baseline methods: BPRMF, GRU4Rec, LightGCN and DGSR. Multi-dimensional indicators such as Precision@K, Recall@K, NDCG@K, ItemCoverage and Average-Popularity are used for comprehensive evaluation. The results show that CogRec outperforms the baseline model in accuracy and diversity indicators on all datasets. On the CogLearn dataset, CogRec's NDCG@10 is improved by about 36.5 % compared with DGSR, and it achieves the best performance in diversity and novelty indicators. The results of the ablation experiment further show that removing the cognitive style encoding module will result in a 21.7 % reduction in NDCG@10.

title: Analysis on the 3D Electromagnetic Characteristics of a Permanent Magnet Governor with a Double-Layer Permanent Magnet Rotor
authors: Ke Mei, Mengmeng Tian, Wenliang Zhao, Hailiang Cai, Pu Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Based on a permanent magnet governor (PMG) with a double-layer permanent magnet rotor, the three-dimensional finite element method (3D FEM) is adopted to investigate its electromagnetic characteristics in this paper. And comparing the results with those of the 2D finite element simulation. Firstly, the topology and operating principle of the studied PMG are described comprehensively. Then, a 3D model is established, and 3D FEM is adopted to analyze its electromagnetic characteristics of the two-layer PMRs at different relative positions, including magnetic flux density characteristics and mechanical characteristics. And comparing the results with those of the 2D finite element simulation, the results show that a certain deviation is observed due to the influence of end magnetic leakage.

title: Web Spam Detection for the Evolving Web Graph Based on Inductive Learning
authors: Yuanyi Ma, Siyang Chen, Shuangwu Chen, Dong Jin, Feng Yang, Jian Tan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The dynamic and adversarial nature of the web presents significant challenges for web spam detection, as spammers continuously evolve their tactics in both content deception and link manipulation. To address this, we propose a novel Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based framework for robust web spam detection. A key innovation of our work is an inductive learning model that operates on subgraphs, enabling it to generalize effectively to previously unseen nodes and dynamically evolving web graphs. We evaluate our framework extensively on both fixed and dynamic web graph datasets. The results demonstrate its strong practical viability, high accuracy in spam identification, and superior ability to adapt to the continuously changing topology of the web. Our approach integrates the web page's content features with the topological information from link structures within a model. The results demonstrate its strong practical viability, high accuracy in spam identification, and superior ability to adapt to the continuous changes of the web graph.

title: MaskDiff-TAD: Masked Diffusion Trajectory Imputation for Anomaly Detection
authors: Qi Ouyang, Shuxin Liu, Ran Li, Junjie Zhang, Hongchang Chen, YanCheng Mu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Spatio-temporal trajectory anomaly detection is critical for applications like autonomous driving and traffic monitoring, yet faces challenges in capturing complex dependencies and localized anomalies. To address this, we proposes the MaskDiff-TAD framework. Its core innovation lies in introducing an alternating masking strategy that compels the model to learn deep spatio-temporal dependencies between trajectory segments and their context under partial observation conditions. Building upon this, the framework combines spatio-temporal Transformer layers with a conditional diffusion imputation mechanism to achieve robust reconstruction of missing trajectory segments, while exhibiting high sensitivity to subtle differences between normal and anomalous patterns. Ultimately, a multi-step voting function integrates reconstruction errors from different denoising steps during diffusion, enabling stable and adaptive anomaly inference. Experiments demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms existing baselines on real-world datasets, achieving F1 scores of 0.933 and 0.945, thereby substantially enhancing the detection accuracy of fine-grained local anomalies.

title: Optimization of Operations in Industry 4.0 Settings with Real-Time Predictive Maintenance Working with Machine Learning
authors: S.B. Prakalya, V. Seethalakshmi, Murthoty Satheesh Babu, Zakaria Azzam, Ginni Nijhawan, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Since Industry 4.0 introduced cutting-edge technologies like IoT, Big Data, and AI into operational processes, the industrial and manufacturing sectors have changed drastically. Predictive maintenance with these technologies is promising. It reduces maintenance costs and downtime by predicting equipment failure using real-time data. In Industry 4.0, this project studies real-time predictive maintenance utilizing ML techniques to optimize industrial operations. IoT sensor data can be used to build predictive models to detect wear and malfunction in machines and tools. This makes procedures short and effective, allowing rapid action. Deep learning models, decision trees, regression models, and machine learning methods are used to analyze massive amounts of time-series data produced by industrial machinery. By finding new sensor data patterns and correlations, these systems may be able to predict equipment failure. Another essential part of the project is integrating predictive models with IoT systems. It tracks equipment health and operation in real time. Technology’s continual machine data analysis improves maintenance plans and reduces unscheduled downtime. It also sends out real-time alerts to users. Data quality, interpretability of models, and scalability are three more issues that this study attempts to resolve for predictive maintenance systems operating in complicated industrial settings. The findings show that in an Industry 4.0 environment, predictive maintenance driven by machine learning may greatly increase operational efficiency while decreasing costs and boosting total production.

title: Design of Intelligent Report Automatic Generation System and Optimization of Generative Algorithm in Power Business Scenarios
authors: Li Chen, Hao Yang, Juntai Shi, Yixuan Chen, Xunquan Lu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The digital transformation of power industry challenges the value mining of massive data, and the traditional report generation mode is inefficient and difficult to meet the professional and real-time requirements. In this paper, an intelligent automatic report generation system driven by business rules and optimized by generative algorithm is proposed to solve the problems of low efficiency, poor accuracy and poor readability of report generation in power scene. The system architecture is divided into a data fusion layer, a business rule layer, an intelligent generation layer, and an application interaction layer. A dynamic template engine is constructed through a knowledge graph, and combined with a rule engine and a domain fine tuned Large Language Model (LLM) to achieve automatic generation, verification, and optimization of reports. The experimental results show that the system can significantly improve the efficiency of report generation, reduce error rates, and support human-machine collaboration, providing an effective tool for the digital transformation of the power industry.

title: CBBA-Based Task Planning for Heterogeneous Unmanned Surface Vehicles in Area Search Missions
authors: Yue Yang, Binyi Xia, Nanxuan Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis and mathematical modelling of the cooperative area search problem employing heterogeneous unmanned surface vessels (USVs). By incorporating practical constraints such as communication range and communication quality during task allocation, the study establishes clear principles for task assignment and defines specific objectives for search trajectory planning. Subsequently, an enhanced Consensus-Based Bundle Algorithm (CBBA) with dynamic communication reallocation is proposed. This improved algorithm explicitly accounts for limited inter-platform communication ranges and incorporates a redefined objective function and an enhanced conflict resolution mechanism tailored to operational requirements. Finally, simulation results are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach under representative mission scenarios.

title: An Enhanced Lightweight Model for Real-time Pavement Condition Index Prediction
authors: Anthony Dontoh, Logan Sirbaugh, Andrews Danyo, Stephanie Ivey, Blessing Agyei Kyem, Armstrong Aboah, Jude Dontoh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: This study proposes a lightweight, attention-enhanced deep learning framework for real-time Pavement Condition Index (PCI) prediction from pavement imagery. The model integrates a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into an EfficientNet-B0 backbone to enhance feature representation without significantly increasing computational cost. Extensive experiments conducted on a publicly available urban pavement dataset demonstrate that the proposed EfficientNet-B0 + CBAM architecture outperforms seven standard CNN baselines, achieving a test MAE of 9.102 and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.738. Statistical analysis confirms that these improvements are significant. The model maintains a compact size and inference efficiency, making it suitable for deployment on edge devices and UAVs. Future work will assess the model's reliability at PCI class boundaries and reframe PCI prediction as a classification task to better align with transportation agency decision-making practices.

title: Evaluating Lightweight Vision Transformers for Chest Disease Detection in Low-Resource Clinical Settings
authors: Mridul Banik, Ismail Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of lightweight Vision Transformers (ViTs) for chest disease detection in low-resource clinical settings using the Indiana Chest X-ray Reports dataset. The research addresses the critical need for computationally efficient diagnostic models that can operate effectively in resource-constrained healthcare environments. Through systematic preprocessing and exploratory data analysis, 349 validated normal chest X-ray cases were analyzed across ten distinct radiological finding categories. The dataset demonstrated excellent characteristics for training lightweight ViTs, with balanced distribution (CV = 0.47) and strong clinical indication-finding correlations (r ¿ 0.6, p ¡ 0.05). Performance evaluation revealed that lightweight Vision Transformers achieved promising diagnostic accuracy with precision scores reaching 1.0, while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for deployment in low-resource settings. The keyword extraction algorithm successfully identified medical conditions with 100% data quality assurance following preprocessing. Statistical analysis confirmed dataset suitability for machine learning applications, with comprehensive terminological diversity (847 unique medical terms) and clinically meaningful correlations between indications and findings. The findings demonstrate that lightweight Vision Transformers represent a viable solution for addressing diagnostic challenges in resource-limited healthcare environments, offering an optimal balance between diagnostic accuracy and computational efficiency. This research contributes to the broader goal of democratizing AI-powered healthcare solutions and reducing diagnostic disparities in underserved populations worldwide.

title: Evaluating Artificial Intelligence Based Predictive Analytics for Improving Performance Management and Employee Satisfaction in Organizations
authors: T. Deepika, Srinivas. D
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This article analyses the contribution of Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based predictive analytics to both the performance management systems and employee satisfaction in the organization. Subjective performance assessment and regular reviews have been ineffective in the traditional performance management as they could not give timely and accurate performances of employees and their satisfaction. The use of AI models, based on the machine learning algorithms, is a potential game changer by providing real-time, datagenerating efforts that match organizational objectives with individual performance. This paper is a predictive analytics model derived with data of the organization, including past performance history, the training attendance history, and the employee engagement measurement. The findings prove that predictive analytics are much more effective in terms of accuracy of performance forecasting and even enables providing feedback to employees in a personalized manner. In addition, the integration of these predictive models in performance management processes can be found to have a positive impact on employee satisfaction and offer them more customized growth opportunities and less dissatisfaction. These findings stress the opportunities available to AI to enhance the operation of performance management systems and create an increasingly engaged and more satisfied workforce. Moral aspects of data privacy and fairness of algorithms are another issue. The next generation of research could be focused on examining the long-term effects of AI-based interventions on the performance of the organization, such as retention and productivity of employees.

title: Vectorized HD Map Construction Based on Efficient Kernelized Self-Attention
authors: Jiaming Liu, Yi Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Vectorized maps are crucial in autonomous driving, providing critical support for vehicle localization and planning. This paper aims to develop an efficient vectorized map construction architecture to enhance both accuracy and efficiency. To achieve this, we introduce an efficient SCC-kernel-based self-attention (ESSA) which exhibits antiinterference characteristics to address environmental disturbances in map data. ESSA module stabilizes feature extraction of map elements, expands receptive fields, and fully utilizes multi-scale spatial-spectral information, enabling precise capture of map element shapes and positions for improved vectorization accuracy. Simultaneously, ESSA module reduces computational complexity from quadratic to linear, significantly alleviating computational burdens and meeting real-time requirements for processing large-scale, high-resolution map data. Experiments on the nuScenes dataset demonstrate that the proposed architecture outperforms state-of-the-art methods in metrics such as average precision, achieving superior mapping performance. This framework holds significant application potential, offering more accurate and real-time map information for autonomous driving development.

title: Contextual Understanding in Retrieval Augmented Generation: A Comparative Evaluation of BLOOM-560M and Pythia-410M on Bhagavad Gita Interpretation
authors: Divyansh Tuli, Hardik Yadav, Aryan Rawat
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Large Language Models have advanced a lot in natural language understanding and generation. But still their implementation in specialized fields needs further contextual understanding. Retrieval Augmented Generation has proven to be an important model for integrating external knowledge sources and generative models for generating more accurate and specialized responses. In this study, we evaluate the behavior of medium scale LLMs through a RAG pipeline for contextual question answering from the Bhagavad Gita. We curate a dataset from the Bhagavad Gita translations, interpretations and commentaries and build knowledge retrieval using FAISS vector databases and embeddings. We also test the models under different prompting techniques. Our study offers a benchmark for domain specific reasoning for RAG systems, studying trade offs between model selection and contextual accuracy. It also lays a foundation for further work using LLMs for spiritual and philosophical writing.

title: AI-Powered Embedded Systems for Predictive Maintenance in Industrial IoT Devices
authors: Dr.G.Ashok Kumar, K.B. Shobana, Dr.Harikarthik S K, T. Venkatesh, B Chitra Lekha, Vasukidevi G
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The research develops an AI-backed embedded framework which operates for predictive maintenance tasks in Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) applications. Through its combination of lightweight machine learning models and edge-computing platforms the system facilitates immediate fault detection together with performance forecasting in industrial equipment. The approach operates with minimal delay while decreasing reliance on cloud services and supports maintenance planning before failures happen which results in longer equipment uptime and decreased costs. Testing of the proposed architecture commences by deploying it to limited-power devices which proved effective anomaly detection while using minimal power. This research establishes the capability of edge AI technologies to revolutionize current maintenance standards in smart manufacturing operations.

title: BERT-TFPAM: An Improved Attention Mechanism-Based Approach to Telecommunication Fraud Event Argument Recognition
authors: Wen Zhou, Qian Wang, Fumin Wang, Anying Chai
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In telecommunication fraud prevention, information technology has helped reduce crime rates and eased the burden on police. However, the unstructured and colloquial nature of case texts still requires significant manual effort. Existing event extraction models often fail to capture fine-grained semantics, leading to incomplete extractions. To address this, we propose BERT-TFPAM, a model optimized for telecommunication fraud detection. It enhances role-trigger interaction with expanded BERT embeddings, introduces a multi-granularity attention layer to aggregate semantics across levels, and uses a BiLSTM module for improved contextual understanding. A CRF layer is applied for optimal label sequence generation. Experiments on a custom TFEE dataset show that BERT-TFPAM achieves an F1 score of 73.02, outperforming the Doc2EDAG model by 3.08 percentage points, demonstrating its effectiveness in extracting key elements from fraud case texts.

title: A Systematic Mapping Study on Accessibility Design Guidelines of Assistive Technology for Students with Autism
authors: Muhammad Mu'izzuddin Mohd Yusri, Nor Shahida Mohamad Yusop
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Designing for accessibility is an important aspect in the development of assistive technology (AT) for individuals with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). Although technology continues to experience accessibility problems that are generally accepted without in society, especially in educational intitutions, these problems need more attention and systematic intervention. This research presents a systematic mapping study (SMS) that summarizes design recommendations aimed at improving the accessibility of assistive technology (AT) for autistic students. The research is important for the field of inclusive interaction design because it finds important accessibility factors that might make the user experience better and reduce cognitive load for students with autism to learn. This study seeks to enhance inclusive educational practices and facilitate the creation of functional, accessible technology designed to accommodate varied learning requirements by synthesising existing evidence. The results present six main categories of accessibility design guidelines and seven types of AT devices. The results are useful for teachers, therapists, and developers who work on creating, testing, and putting into use accessible assistive technology in education center.

title: Cross-platform digital economy data security sharing and modeling method integrating federated learning and privacy computing
authors: Yu Zhu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In the digital economy, cross-platform data sharing is the key to release data value. However, due to the difficulties of privacy protection regulations and heterogeneous data collaboration, the traditional centralized model faces compliance and efficiency challenges. Therefore, this paper proposes a cross-platform data security sharing and modeling method that integrates federated learning (FL) and privacy computing. This method constructs a hierarchical FL architecture, and solves the problem of heterogeneous data collaboration in financial, e-commerce, logistics and other platforms through the three-layer structure of local feature coding, cross-domain integration of graph attention network (GAT) and global prediction model, avoiding the limitation of forcing data schema unification and high-precision ID alignment. A dynamic differential privacy mechanism based on sensitivity evaluation is introduced, and the privacy budget is dynamically adjusted according to data types and training rounds, so as to balance the protection of highly sensitive data and the utility of low sensitive data. The lightweight mechanism of "knowledge distillation+sparse update" is designed. By screening the important parameters of Top-k and the knowledge transfer of teacher-student model, the communication overhead is reduced and the response speed of dynamic environment is improved. The experimental results show that the AUC of this method is 0.832 in the cross-platform user purchase prediction and credit risk assessment task, which is 23.1% higher than that of independent training. The single-round parameter transmission capacity is reduced to 14.2MB, the update time is shortened to 31s, and the convergence speed is increased by 171% in the scenario of sudden change of user preferences in the promotion season, which effectively supports the demand for safe data circulation and efficient modeling in the digital economy scenario.

title: Improved YOLOv8-Based Surface Defect Detection for Aluminum Alloy Profiles
authors: Xiaoyu Li, Xiaojun Xia, Ying Gao, Hejian Sun
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Aluminum alloy profiles are critical in industries such as aerospace and automotive manufacturing due to their lightweight, high strength, and corrosion resistance. However, detecting small-sized and low-contrast surface defects remains challenging, with traditional methods suffering from high false detection rates 15%–20% and limited adaptability to multi-scale defects. This paper proposes an enhanced YOLOv8-based algorithm for aluminum alloy profile defect detection, addressing issues like small defect detection, class imbalance, and discrimination of similar defects. Three key improvements are introduced: (1) a C2f-SE module integrating Squeeze-and-Excitation attention to enhance fine defect feature extraction, (2) an Asymptotic Feature Pyramid Network (AFPN) for improved multi-scale feature fusion, and (3) a multi-component joint loss function incorporating category-adaptive focal loss, scale-aware IoU loss, and contrastive classification loss to optimize training. Experiments on the Tianchi Aluminum Profile Surface Defect Dataset demonstrate significant improvements, with the proposed algorithm achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 72.45% (up from 56.74%), a 43.9% increase in small defect recall, and a 24.13% AP for challenging defects like bruises. These results highlight the algorithm's potential for high-precision, real-time industrial defect detection.

title: AI-Powered Labor Demand Forecasting: Advanced Deep Learning Architectures for Workforce Management
authors: Hao Zhang, Linjun Wei, Yangchun Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper systematically evaluates the performance of deep learning models-including LSTM, CNN, Transformer, and XGBoost-in workforce demand forecasting. LSTM achieves a 1.5% MAPE and 26.5 RMSE in hourly forecasting, significantly outperforming traditional methods. The CNN-LSTM hybrid model reduces MAE by up to 70% in multi-period tasks. XGBoost optimized with TPE achieves 91% prediction accuracy in retail scenarios while reducing labor costs by 18%. Across healthcare, retail, and logistics cases, hybrid architectures incorporating attention mechanisms boost prediction accuracy by over 25% on average. The study confirms that deep learning-based AI forecasting systems demonstrate significant advantages in handling nonlinear, nonstationary labor data, providing a scalable technical pathway for intelligent workforce scheduling.

title: An Improved Force-Directed Layout for Interactive Visualization of Medical Knowledge Graphs
authors: Junqiang Zhao, Meng Chen, Zezhou Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Medical knowledge graphs contain heterogeneous entities and complex relationships, making visualization crucial for learning in medical education. Traditional force-directed algorithms struggle with large-scale graphs, leading to congestion, edge crossings, and slow convergence. This paper proposes an improved force-directed layout with three enhancements: (1) semantic weighting to cluster domain-specific entities, (2) damping and dynamic step-size control for faster, stable convergence, and (3) parallel computation with Web Workers to improve efficiency. Experiments on graphs of up to 10,000 nodes show significant improvements over Fruchterman-Reingold and ForceAtlas2 in frame rate, convergence time, and layout quality. A user study further confirms clearer structures and smoother interaction. The approach demonstrates both technical advantages and practical value for building effective medical education visualization systems.

title: Traffic Flow Monitoring and Congestion Control Using VANETs
authors: Sheenam Middha, Bobbinpreet Kaur, Narinder Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: VANETs, or vehicular ad-hoc networks, are a new way to think about Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). They make it possible for automobiles and infrastructure to talk to each other quickly, which makes the roads safer, speeds up traffic, and makes people more aware of what's going on around them. This paper lays out a complete plan for employing VANETs to find and lessen traffic jams in a wide range of settings, such as cities, rural areas, and highways. We use SUMO and NS2 to assess how well vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V), vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I), and infrastructure-to-infrastructure (I2I) communication models work in real-world traffic situations. Greedy +BFS uses AODV routing technology because it is flexible and performs well in networks that change all the time. The findings of the simulation show that VANETs can minimize traffic jams by sending more packets and lowering end-to-end latency in all scenarios. The research looks into a new way to employ VANET technologies to make it easier for boats to talk to each other, which will make fishing safer. The suggested paradigm lays the groundwork for smart, connected transportation systems on land and sea.

title: Transforming LLM Two-Stage Dialogue Summarization Techniques
authors: Vijay Kumar Joshi, Manish Motghare, Basudeb Dey, Nilesh Shelke, Nilesh Ashtankar, Syamprasad Guda
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Dialogue summarization is essential for distilling key insights from conversations in domains like customer service, education, and healthcare. This paper introduces the Dual Refinement Paradigm, a two-stage framework that enhances large language model performance through scene-aware context extraction, iterative text revision, and multi-task learning. Evaluated on a benchmark dataset, our approach achieves a ROUGE-L score of 48.2 and a novel composite M score of 0.84, outperforming established baselines. Key contributions include a scalable low-resource adaptation strategy, a new evaluation metric blending content fidelity and coherence, and an open-source implementation to foster further research in conversational AI.

title: BioMuse – Living Art and Wellness: A Fusion of Technology and Bio-Art for Sustainability and Identity
authors: Rohini Sengupta, Rajanikanth Aluvalu, Mallellu Sai Prashanth
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: BioMuse is the name for a futuristic startup idea that re-explores art as a living, evolving system at the intersection of biology, technology and design. Unlike static décor, BioMuse art pieces use living organisms, generative processes, and bio-inspired eco friendly sustainable systems to create a dynamic artwork that can grow, adapt and interact with environment. Its domain expands across art, sustainability and education, translating experimental bio-art practices, once confined to labs, into accessible, everyday experience. BioMuse merges biology with design enabling customers to curate personalized [1]"living museums" in their homes, and offices making life itself a part of artwork. The main reason of project BioMuse is to stimulate dialogue and reflection around identity, ecology, and technology while contributing to promote sustainability and well-being through interactive living art pieces that connects people with living systems. BioMuse ultimately positions itself as a [2]"21<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup>-century model of living art", transforming usual everyday spaces into interactive, sustainable, and a reflective environment.

title: Efficient SOMP Algorithm Implement on FPGA for DOA Estimation
authors: Xiang Li, Mengjiang Sun, Peng Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Due to the parallel characteristics and high-speed computing features, field programmable gate array (FPGA) is widely used in prototype development, with direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation being an important application area. However, existing high-precision DOA estimation algorithms suffer from high computational complexity, and thus direct implementation on FPGA often faces challenges such as insufficient resources and failure to meet real-time requirements. In this paper, an improved DOA estimation algorithm is proposed, which significantly reduces computational complexity compared to traditional algorithms without compromising estimation accuracy. Especially in the residual update part, the computational complexity of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-th iteration is reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}\left(n^{3}+n^{2}+n\right)$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}(n)$</tex>. Then the parallel processing characteristics of FPGA are fully utilized to reduce the overall algorithm computation time, while the reusability of data in the algorithm is thoroughly explored to minimize resource usage and computational time. Finally, the data flow in FPGA is shown and test results are given to demonstrate the proposed algorithm can operate effectively after implemented on FPGA, and the usage of look-up tables (LUTs), digital signal processing blocks (DSPs), flip-flops (FFs) and block random access memory (BRAMs) are reduced <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$34.64 \%, 32.27 {\%}$</tex>, 30.50% and 38.71% respectively.

title: Research on Data Mining and Analysis Algorithm of Personnel Files in Big Data Environment
authors: Hong Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the context of big data, the mining and analysis of personnel file data have emerged as crucial factors in enhancing the efficiency of human resource management. This treatise unveils an algorithm leveraging the Stacking-based ensemble learning approach to augment the prognostic precision of personnel file data analysis. This method combines XGBoost, random forest and logistic regression as the base classifier, and uses convolutional neural network as the meta-classifier to reduce the overfitting risk of a single model by learning the prediction pattern of the base classifier. To bolster the model's capacity for generalization, cross-validation is employed during the training phase, thereby ensuring the independence of the base classifier's training and validation processes.

title: Optimization of Concrete Crack Feature Extraction in YOLO for High-Precision Crack Detection
authors: Yingbo Weng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Crack identification in concrete structures plays a vital role in maintaining infrastructure safety. Nevertheless, current detection approaches struggle to accurately identify fine cracks in intricate backgrounds. To overcome this challenge, we introduce an enhanced YOLOv8-CRC series algorithm aimed at improving both detection accuracy and efficiency for concrete cracks. Integrating the CBAM attention mechanism strengthens the model's focus on crack-damaged areas, enhancing feature extraction in challenging environments. The backbone network incorporates ResNet50, utilizing depthwise separable convolutions and residual connections to strengthen the detection of fine cracks and mitigate information loss. Moreover, the CSPPC module is incorporated into the neck network to enhance feature fusion while minimizing redundant computations and memory usage. The experimental findings indicate that the YOLOv8-CRC algorithm exhibits outstanding performance in detecting concrete cracks. Compared to the standard YOLOv8, our model achieves enhancements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.6 \%, 5.6 \%, 6.1 \%$</tex> and 6.3% in precision, recall, mAP50, and mAP50-95, respectively. These findings confirm that the YOLOv8-CRC algorithm provides a reliable solution for automated concrete crack detection and lays a solid foundation for structural health monitoring systems.

title: A Cybersecurity Event Reconstruction Framework Considering Source Accuracy and Conflict Validation
authors: Shuaichen Ye, Zhe Bu, Yuetao Ge
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the continuously increasing of the cybersecurity event frequency and intensity, requirements for the timeliness response and loss mitigation are growing. Generally, event extracting and reconstructing is regarded as the basis of the response process, its accuracy and completeness determines the efficiency of the response. Current literature major focuses on using neural network-based algorithms to improve the performance of event content extraction. Research on multisource information fusion and event reconstruction remains insufficient. To address this issue, this paper proposes a cybersecurity event reconstruction framework to aggregate the most credible information for generating an integrated event. Four reconstruction scenarios are selected and conducted on a dataset with 1352 identified events to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Comparison results indicate the accuracy and completeness enhancing of the reconstruction framework.

title: Gamified Cognitive Interaction Design for Zakat Education: Usability and Learning Evaluation of a Digital Board Game
authors: Alias bin Deraman, Mohd Shukor Bin Abd Rahman, Nor Intan Shafini Nasaruddin, Mohd Ali bin Mohd Isa, Syahbudin Bin Senin, Nur Alya Batrisyia Ahmad Suhairi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: This study investigates the potential of gamified cognitive interaction design to enhance zakat education. A prototype board game was developed following the ADDIE instructional design framework and used as a research instrument to test learning outcomes and usability. Thirty students participated in experimental (gamified) and control (lecture-based) groups, with data collected through pre/posttests, the System Usability Scale (SUS), and classroom observations. The results showed a significant improvement in learning outcomes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{t}(\mathbf{1 4})=\mathbf{8. 1 2}, \mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 0 1})$</tex>, high usability (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SUS}=88.2, \text{SD}=3.45$</tex>), and strong reliability (Cronbach's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.89$</tex>). These findings suggest that gamified interaction design can effectively facilitate complex religious financial concepts and support sustainable, technologyenhanced learning.
