title: Voltage Oriented Controller (VOC) Based Vienna Rectifier For Electric Vehicle Charging Stations
authors: Kandakatla. Vishnu, M.Malavika, Shaik.Khaisar, K. Ashwini, A. Siddharth Reddy, G. Srinivas
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) charging systems that are not only dependable and efficient but also compliant with current power grid regulations are becoming more and more necessary as EVs continue to gain popularity. The Vienna Rectifier, a three-phase, three-level converter renowned for its high efficiency, small size, and capacity to reduce power quality problems like harmonic distortion, is a viable option for transforming AC grid power into the DC power needed by EVs. Voltage Oriented Control (VOC) is frequently employed in such systems to guarantee accurate control and stable functioning. Active and reactive power components can be independently controlled by VOC, usually using proportional-integral (PI) controls. Despite being straightforward and popular, PI controllers frequently have trouble with abrupt load variations, non-linear behaviors, and system uncertainties.To address these challenges, this study looks at taking the place of the traditional PI controller with a Fuzzy Logic Controller (FLC) in a VOC-based Vienna Rectifier setup. With its more adaptable, rule-based methodology and lack of reliance on a precise mathematical model, FLCs are more appropriate for dynamic and complicated settings. Under the same conditions, both control strategies are examined using MATLAB/Simulink. Total harmonic distortion (THD), dynamic stability, transient response, and voltage regulation are used to compare their performance. With lower THD, faster time response, less overshoot, and better voltage management, the FLC dramatically enhances system performance, according to the results. According to these findings, FLCs may be crucial in the development of future EV charging stations that are more resilient and intelligent.

title: Graph Contrastive Learning for Connectome Classification
authors: Martín Schmidt, Sara Silva, Federico Larroca, Gonzalo Mateos, Pablo Musé
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: With recent advancements in non-invasive techniques for measuring brain activity, such as magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), the study of structural and functional brain networks through graph signal processing (GSP) has gained notable prominence. GSP stands as a key tool in unraveling the interplay between the brain’s function and structure, enabling the analysis of graphs defined by the connections between regions of interest—referred to as connectomes in this context. Our work represents a further step in this direction by exploring supervised contrastive learning methods within the realm of graph representation learning. The main objective of this approach is to generate subject-level (i.e., graph-level) vector representations that bring together subjects sharing the same label while separating those with different labels. These connectome embeddings are derived from a graph neural network Encoder-Decoder architecture, which jointly considers structural and functional connectivity. By leveraging data augmentation techniques, the proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in a gender classification task using Human Connectome Project data. More broadly, our connectome-centric methodological advances support the promising prospect of using GSP to discover more about brain function, with potential impact to understanding heterogeneity in the neurodegeneration for precision medicine and diagnosis.

title: Enhancing Seismic DAS Data Through Multi-Stage Signal Denoising and SNR Analysis
authors: Noor Izni Zafirah Mohd Radzi, Norashikin Yahya, Ismailalwali Alobaid Magzoub Babikir, Jafreezal Jaafar
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) offers a costeffective solution for seismic monitoring by transforming standard fiber optic cables into dense arrays of virtual sensors. However, DAS data are often contaminated by diverse noise sources from system and environmental factors, complicating seismic interpretation. Effective preprocessing is crucial for enhancing signal clarity while preserving seismic event integrity. This paper proposes a multi-stage denoising framework that combines spike removal, amplitude clipping, notch filtering, frequency-wavenumber (FK) filtering, bandpass filtering, and automatic gain control (AGC) to address the distinct noise characteristics in DAS data. The approach is quantitatively evaluated using Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) analysis on field datasets from the Pyrenees dataset, France. Results show that the proposed framework provides robust noise suppression and improved signal quality compared to single-stage approaches, offering a practical and interpretable preprocessing strategy for DAS-based seismic applications.

title: MIMO Communications with 1-bit RIS: Asymptotic Analysis and Over-the-Air Channel Diagonalization
authors: Panagiotis Gavriilidis, Kyriakos Stylianopoulos, George C. Alexandropoulos
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper presents an asymptotic analysis of Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) systems assisted by a 1-bit Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) under Ricean fading conditions. Using random matrix theory, we show that, in the asymptotic regime, the dominant singular values and vectors of the transmitter–RIS and RIS–receiver channels converge to their deterministic Line-of-Sight (LoS) components, almost irrespective of the Ricean factors. This enables RIS phase configuration using only LoS information through a closed-form Sign Alignment (SA) rule that maximizes the channel gain. Furthermore, when the RIS is asymptotically larger than the transceiver arrays, proper RIS configuration can render the end-to-end MIMO channel in the capacity formula asymptotically diagonal, thereby eliminating inter-stream interference and enabling Over-The-Air (OTA) spatial multiplexing without channel knowledge at the transmitter. Building on this result, a waterfilling-inspired SA algorithm that allocates RIS elements to spatial streams, based on the asymptotic singular values and statistical channel parameters, is proposed. Simulation results validate the theoretical analyses, demonstrating that the proposed schemes achieve performance comparable to conventional Riemannian manifold optimization, but with orders of magnitude lower runtime.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Intelligent System Design for IoT Security using Stacking Ensemble Meta-Learning
authors: N. V. Dhandapani, Tannmay Gupta, Sakthi Prabha Ramachandran, Suma N G, Deepu B, Teena Vats
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Rising usage of IoT devices in different areas has caused security issues, including the chance for hacks like DoS, spoofing and malware. To solve these problems, this research suggests using the Stacking Ensemble Meta-learning (SEM) framework for detecting anomalies in IoT systems. The intended structure has five basic classifiers—DT, KNN, SGD, SVM and NB—organized in several layers and a meta-learner that enhances prediction accuracy by blending their outputs. The suggested approach outperformed others in experimental runs using a simulated DS2OS dataset which had <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> samples and eight different types of activity. SEM performed best with an accuracy of 96.5% as well as top precision and ROC-AUC scores out of all the models tested. The Friedman test added more proof that it is a reliable method. By using such an approach, IoT security is strengthened by detecting anomalies efficiently.

title: Blockchain-Driven Decentralized Finance (DeFi): Transforming the Architecture of Traditional Financial Ecosystems
authors: Nilaish, Farhatnida Unnisa, Bhupendra Bahadur Tiwari
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Blockchain and Decentralized Finance (DeFi) are transforming the financial world by redefining how financial transactions and services operate. This paper explores the convergence of these technologies, emphasizing their potential to disintermediate traditional banking, enhance financial inclusivity, and increase transparency and efficiency. While these innovations hold significant promise, they are also met with challenges, including regulatory uncertainty, security vulnerabilities, and technological limitations. This study analyzes the underlying technologies, key platforms, and comparative frameworks that highlight how DeFi is challenging conventional financial systems, while suggesting avenues for sustainable adoption and global impact

title: MolBioART: Molecular Biological ART for Drug Discovery
authors: Sourav Singh Sehgal, Rahul Shrivastava, Trasha Bansal, Rohit Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This work presents a hybrid framework combining Adaptive Resonance Theory (ART) clustering with deep neural network–based molecular restoration for efficient, target-specific drug optimization. Drug–protein pairs are first encoded into vector representations and clustered using ART to form interpretable prototype classes. For a new protein target, the nearest ART prototype serves as an initial candidate. A Protein-Guided Drug Restoration (PGDR) module then refines these candidates by learning to reconstruct optimal drug vectors from perturbed, protein-conditioned inputs. The restored molecules are further optimized in latent space under efficacy and pharmacological constraints. Experiments on benchmark DTI datasets show that the framework enhances interpretability and candidate quality, providing a scalable and biologically grounded approach to drug repurposing and de novo design.

title: Person Re-Identification in Complex Environments Based on Multimodal Feature Extraction and Data Augmentation Methodology Research
authors: Yan Hao, Zhang Ming, Tongzhou Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Occlusion and viewpoint variation are core challenges in image-text cross-modal person re-identification (ReID). To enhance recognition robustness in occluded scenes, this study proposes a CLIP-driven data augmentation method, which improves system performance through a dynamic part decoder and a three-level fusion module based on SeNetV2. The dynamic part decoder employs learnable query vectors to adaptively locate discriminative regions, combined with a visibility predictor to suppress interference from occluded areas. The three-level fusion module constructs a hierarchical optimization architecture (Spatial Enhancement → Part Enhancement → Fusion Enhancement), achieving data augmentation by reinforcing spatial details, optimizing part discriminability, and enhancing feature compatibility. Experiments on the Market1501, DukeMTMC-reID, and Occluded-Duke datasets show that the method achieves improvements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+0.4 \%,+0.4 \%$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 2. 0 \%}$</tex> in mAP, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{0. 5 \%}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{1. 4 \%}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{0. 5 \%}$</tex> in Rank-1 accuracy, respectively. The results verify the method's effectiveness in occluded scenarios, with particularly significant performance gains on the heavily occluded dataset (Occluded-Duke).

title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Malware Classification in Cybersecurity Applications
authors: Indumati R Nagesh, A. Daniel Das, Prateek Garg, Sanjay Bhatnagar, Dharani. V, Pushpalatha M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The proposed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture in our research paper is required to analyze malware in the field of cyber safety. The method combines opcode frequency mappings, control-flow graph embeddings and entropy-derived texture-related features into a common multi-channel Convolutional Neural Network, which allows to extract complementary spatial and structural features. The convolutional processing of each feature type is independent, which is then merged by residual fusion and convolutional attention to improve discrimination ability. Using a hybrid loss criterion that is a combination of cross-entropy and center loss augments intra-class condensation and inter-class separability. Experimental tests using various malware datasets were done to show the proposed method was better than current methods and was found to have 97.8 percent accuracy, 97.2 precision, 96.9 recall, and a 97.0 F1-score with less computational expenditure. Such findings confirm the scalability of the framework with respect to deployments on large scale, as well as the robustness against obfuscation and polymorphism. The solution suggested opens a gap between the static, and the dynamic analysis, and presents efficient and flexible solution to detecting emerging threats in dynamic cybersecurity environment.

title: Scaling Wideband Hybrid Beamforming for sub-THz Communication
authors: Amirmohammad Haddad, Oveys Delafrooz Noroozi, Canan Cebeci, Mark J. W. Rodwell, Upamanyu Madhow
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We investigate the capacity attainable for a multiuser MIMO uplink as we scale both array size and bandwidth for regimes in which all-digital arrays incur excessive hardware complexity and power consumption. We consider a tiled hybrid beamforming architecture in which each tile, or subarray, is a phased array performing analog (or RF) beamforming, followed by DSP on the tile outputs. For parameters compatible with sub-THz fixed access links, we discuss hardware and power consumption considerations for choosing tile size and number of tiles. Noting that the problem of optimal multiuser MIMO in our wideband regime is open even for the simplest possible channel models, we compare the spectral efficiencies attainable by a number of reasonable strategies for tile-level RF beamforming, assuming flexibility in the digital signal processing (DSP) of the tile outputs. We consider a number of beam broadening approaches for addressing the "beam squint" incurred by RF beamforming in our wideband regime, along with strategies for sharing tiles among users. Information-theoretic benchmarks are computed for an idealized MIMO-OFDM system, with linear per-subcarrier multiuser detection compared against an unconstrained complexity receiver.

title: Medical Image Synthesis and Multi-View Reconstruction Using CGAN and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Nirmalrani V, D P School of Managementashekar, Arghya Das Dev, Divyanshi Rajvanshi, P. Mayilvel Kumar, Nittin Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In this work, a high-fidelity 3D view is generated by presenting a sophisticated Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN) model to convert 2D radiographic images into 3D upon which medical visualization can be improved. The model allows full multi-angle reconstruction from 0 to 360 with the possibility of focusing on specific bones like the knee, elbow, and lower limbs, similar to the result of a CT and MRI scan. The method involves a lot of pre-processing noise removal, resampling, rescaling, normalization, and augmentation to align heterogeneous DICOM datasets acquired through various sources in different hospitals. Utilizing procurement, bottleneck, and extending routes, Leaky ReLU, tanh, and sigmoid activations, our U-Netbased network successfully accomplishes 3D composition. Training is done with a hybrid loss function that uses L1, MSE, BCE and Wasserstein metrics, which is optimized with Adam optimizer. SSIM, PSNR, MSE and entropy-based evaluation show high visual quality and similarity to ground-truth data. The model provides a cheap, radiation-free option over conventional imaging, enhanced diagnostic performance, and accessibility in resource-limited medical care environment.

title: Leveraging FPGAs for Homomorphic Matrix-Vector Multiplication in Oblivious Message Retrieval
authors: Grant Bosworth, Keewoo Lee, Sunwoong Kim
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: While end-to-end encryption protects the content of messages, it does not secure metadata, which exposes sender and receiver information through traffic analysis. A plausible approach to protecting this metadata is to have senders post encrypted messages on a public bulletin board and receivers scan it for relevant messages. Oblivious message retrieval (OMR) leverages homomorphic encryption (HE) to improve user experience in this solution by delegating the scan to a resource-rich server while preserving privacy. A key process in OMR is the homomorphic detection of pertinent messages for the receiver from the bulletin board. It relies on a specialized matrix-vector multiplication algorithm, which involves extensive multiplications between ciphertext vectors and plaintext matrices, as well as homomorphic rotations. The computationally intensive nature of this process limits the practicality of OMR. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a hardware architecture to accelerate the matrix-vector multiplication algorithm. The building homomorphic operators in this algorithm are implemented using high-level synthesis, with design parameters for different parallelism levels. These operators are then deployed on a field-programmable gate array platform using an efficient design space exploration strategy to accelerate homomorphic matrix-vector multiplication. Compared to a software implementation, the proposed hardware accelerator achieves a ×13.86 speedup.

title: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
authors: N/A

title: Enhancing Network Traffic Analysis with a Unique Imbalanced Data Algorithm in Deep Learning
authors: P Ashwini, Deepthi Potlakayala, Vivekanand Aelgani, Rajuladev Sridhar, Karthick K, Vishwa Priya V
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Cyber security depends on network traffic analysis to identify anomalies and stop intrusions. Vast network data contains seldom occurring harmful activities which traditional models struggle to detect traffic patterns in. The research created an innovative deep learning system to solve class imbalance problems while producing superior network traffic classification results. The proposed framework combines adaptive data augmentation with deep learning approaches together with cost-sensitive learning and feature optimization methods to boost detection capabilities. Results show that our proposed model surpasses conventional models through more precise metrics and improved performance measures including F1-score and AUC-ROC metrics when tested against benchmark datasets. Testing outcomes demonstrate that our system effectively detects minority class occurrences while maintaining high classification achievements. The research presents a scalable monitoring solution for real traffic inspection which enhances network security analytics technologies.

title: SmartCloud AI/ML-Infused Architectures: Sustainable Cloud Computing Architectures Environments Optimization
authors: Jagadeesh Varrey
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The exponential growth of cloud computing has significantly increased its environmental impact, with data centers now accounting for approximately 1% of global electricity consumption. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for integrating artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) techniques into cloud computing architectures to enhance sustainability. Propose a novel carbon-aware computing algorithm that dynamically optimizes workload scheduling based on renewable energy availability and thermal management constraints. Our architecture leverages edge computing integration, federated learning, and AI-driven resource optimization to minimize energy consumption while maintaining performance benchmarks. The proposed solution demonstrates potential reductions in carbon emissions by up to 30% compared to conventional cloud architectures through intelligent workload distribution and predictive scaling. This research contributes to the emerging field of sustainable computing by providing a structured approach to balancing computational demands with environmental responsibility.

title: EEG and Forehead EOG-Based Driver Fatigue Classification Using Sparse-Deep Belief Networks
authors: Jie Zhang, Nanfeng Xiao
publish: 2021 IEEE 23rd Int Conf on High Performance Computing & Communications; 7th Int Conf on Data Science & Systems; 19th Int Conf on Smart City; 7th Int Conf on Dependability in Sensor, Cloud & Big Data Systems & Application (HPCC/DSS/SmartCity/DependSys)
abstract: In this study, we fuse electroencephalography (EEG) signals and forehead electrooculogram (EOG) signals to classify the driver's fatigue and alert states. Based on time-varying power spectral density (PSD) and Hilbert-Huang spectrum entropy extracted from EEG, 36 eye movement features from EOG, we develop novel sparse-deep belief networks (Sparse-DBN) for detecting driver drowsiness. PSD, four modalities, and support vector machine (SVM) classifier are used for comparison. The experimental results show that EEG and forehead EOG feature fusion can improve performance compared with two single modalities. Furthermore, the results show that the classification performance achieves an improved classification performance using two feature fusions and Sparse-DBN classifier with the sensitivity, specificity, accuracy,and area under the curve of 97.99% /96.72%, 94.73% /95.51%, 97.93% /97.91%, and 0.96273/ 0.96376, respectively. Additionally, EEG and forehead EOG features can be collected simultaneously using only four shared forehead sites and achieve comparative performance compared with traditional sites, which is the preferred approach for practical applications.

title: Advanced MPPT Technique with High-Gain Modified SEPIC Converter for Solar Powered PMSM Motor Fed Electric Vehicle
authors: A. Jeyaraj, S. L. Sreedevi, M. Baskar, R. Vijayalakshmi, K. Sushita, Ananthan Nagarajan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: With the global emphasis on sustainable transportation and the growing demand for clean energy, Electric Vehicles (EVs) powered by renewable sources, particularly solar energy, have gained significant attention. Integrating Photovoltaic (PV) system with EV propulsion introduces the need for efficient power management techniques that adapt to environmental variations such as irradiance and temperature. This study proposes, an advanced Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) technique with a High-Gain Modified Single-Ended Primary Inductor Converter (SEPIC) (HGMS) Converter is proposed for enhancing the performance of a solar-powered Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM). White Shark Optimized (WSO) Artificial Neural Network (ANN) MPPT algorithms become crucial to confirm that PV array works and tracks maximum amount of output power at its optimal power point under all conditions. The use of a Three-Phase Voltage Source Inverter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \varnothing$</tex> VSI) results in improved power quality by producing output voltage and current that are more balanced and steadier also, the speed of PMSM motor is highly controlled with the aid of Proportional Integral (PI) controller. Simulation outcomes obtained from MATLAB validation demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms with a THD of 2.2% and tracking efficiency of 98%. Overall, it enhances tracking speed, improves the overall system efficiency, and ensures efficient power flow to the drivetrain of the EVs.

title: User Side Fault Diagnosis and Intelligent Management Integrating Big Data and Intelligent Systems
authors: Xiaoxuan Guo, Yasai Wang, Leping Sun, Min Guo, Tonghe Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Agricultural parks consist of many functional parts for crops cultivation, including irrigation systems, environmental monitoring equipment, and other important infrastructure. In order to prevent and respond to various technical failures, accurate and real-time monitoring needs to be carried out. This article is based on big data and artificial intelligent technology, and conducted in-depth mining on it. It adopts an intelligent fault diagnosis and management system based on a combination of machine learning and big data analysis. By combining discrete data with intelligent algorithms, the fault diagnosis process is optimized to improve the real-time and accuracy of the system. By automatically collecting and analyzing various information during the production process, scientific decision-making basis is provided for enterprise management to reduce human errors and improve operational efficiency. This article also optimizes and improves existing user side fault diagnosis and intelligent management technologies. Our results indicate that the fault prediction accuracy achieved in this article is significantly improved. The research results can provide theoretical basis and practical guidance for the transformation, upgrading, and development model innovation of agricultural parks in China.

title: A Bilingual Retrieval Augmented Generative AI Chatbot Architecture for Inclusive Retail Banking in Egypt
authors: Marwan El-Baily, Fares El-Sayed Mahmoud, Fathy Mahfouz, Yousef Ayman, Ahmed Yasser, Yara Ibrahim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents Cash Compass, a bilingual Modern Standard Arabic - English generative AI chatbot designed to advance ethical financial inclusion in Egyptian retail banking. The system provides real-time, context-aware guidance on deposits, credit, cards, savings, and investment products while scaffolding financial literacy for underserved users. Ethical design principles are embedded across privacy, fairness, transparency, accountability, accessibility, and user protection. The modular architecture integrates intent and entity parsing, a curated banking knowledge base, retrievalaugmented generation, compliance filtering, and adaptive dialogue management optimized for literacy and bandwidth constraints. Evaluation demonstrates improved accuracy, reduced latency, enhanced bilingual clarity, and increased user satisfaction, indicating that Cash Compass can enable trustworthy, inclusive, and scalable digital financial advisory in emerging markets.

title: Blinding the Wiretapper: RIS-Enabled User Occultation in the ISAC Era
authors: Getuar Rexhepi, Hyeon Seok Rou, Giuseppe Thadeu Freitas de Abreu, George C. Alexandropoulos
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: An undesirable consequence of the foreseeable proliferation of sophisticated integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) technologies is the enabling of spoofing, by malicious agents, of situational information (such as proximity, direction or location) of legitimate users of wireless systems. In order to mitigate this threat, we present a novel ISAC scheme that, aided by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS), enables the occultation of the positions of user equipment (UE) from wiretappers, while maintaining both sensing and desired communication performance between the UEs and a legitimate base station (BS). To that end, we first formulate an RIS phase-shift optimization problem that jointly maximizes the sum-rate performance of the UEs (communication objective), while minimizing the projection of the wiretapper’s effective channel onto the legitimate channel (hiding objective), thereby disrupting the attempts by a wiretapper of localizing the UEs. Then, in order to efficiently solve the resulting non-convex joint optimization problem, a novel manifold optimization algorithm is derived, whose effectiveness is validated by numerical results, which demonstrate that the proposed approach preserves legitimate ISAC performance while significantly degrading the wiretapper’s sensing capability.

title: Design of an Intelligent Home–School Communication Mini-Program Powered by Large Language Models: A Case Study of an AI Teaching Assistant
authors: Yuan Yao, Xu Tu, Chang Liu, Yaming Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: This study explores the application mechanisms and interaction design innovations of Artificial Intelligence (AI) teaching assistants in home–school communication under the context of large language models. Based on a review of existing research and intelligent communication tools, it addresses challenges such as information fragmentation, heavy teacher workloads, and low feedback efficiency by leveraging the natural language understanding and generation capabilities of large language models. Using the “AI Teaching Assistant” mini-program as a case study, the research analyzes interaction processes, information integration, and intelligent response strategies. The findings indicate that, compared with traditional communication tools, AI teaching assistants based on large language models offer clear advantages in semantic understanding, contextualized feedback, and content generation, effectively reducing teachers’ cognitive and time burdens while improving the efficiency and quality of home–school communication.

title: Collective Resonance in Dielectric Resonators and Application to Microfluidic Dielectric Spectroscopy
authors: Nikhil Kumar, Utpal Dey
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a novel technique to engineer collective resonance in a 2-D periodic array of spherical dielectric resonators sandwiched within a parallel metal plate configuration. To do so, a favorable choice of the Mie electric quadrupole (EQ) resonance mode in alumina dielectric spheres is used to generate a tightly coupled resonating array featuring a high quality factor and intense electric fields in the air-gaps between consecutive array elements. A microcapillary tube carrying the test fluid can be conveniently placed in the nonradiating field hotspots, thereby resulting in high-sensitivity dielectric spectroscopy. The microfluidic-based dielectric spectroscopy of water and commonly available chemicals is performed around 40 GHz. The collective resonance-based microfluidic sensor design is robust, easy-to-assemble, low-cost, features self-alignment, and is frequency-scalable up to sub-terahertz (THz) frequencies.

title: Electromagnetic Scattering of Uniformly Moving PEC Objects-Part I: Integral Equation Formulations
authors: Guomin Liu, Gaobiao Xiao, Mengxia Hu, Junwei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A novel method is proposed for analyzing the electromagnetic scattering by an arbitrarily shaped perfectly electrically conducting (PEC) object in uniform motion in the vacuum. In Part I, the jump boundary conditions and the current continuity relation are simplified for PEC objects. A set of time-domain electromagnetic field integral equations on the surface of the moving PEC objects are formulated based on the jump boundary conditions. The relativity effect is properly addressed with the kinematic theorems. The hyper singularities associated with the Green’s function are analyzed and properly treated. The electromagnetic scattered fields generated by the induced current are determined according to the theory of special relativity (TSR) and the causality principle. All calculations are performed in the laboratory frame without explicitly applying Lorentz transformations. Consequently, it is also valid for analyzing systems including multiple objects uniformly moving with different velocities. The method is numerically verified using the marching-on in time algorithm that will be developed in Part II. It will be demonstrated that, for a PEC sphere with velocity near to the light velocity in the vacuum, the scattered fields calculated with the proposed method agree very well with the Lorentz-Mie analytical results obtained using the frame hopping technique.

title: Transformation-Driven Hellinger Distance Framework for Electricity Theft Detection in Advanced Metering Infrastructure
authors: Sandeep Kumar Singh
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: The integration of sensing, computing, and communication technologies has improved the efficiency and flexibility of modern power grids. However, it has also increased the vulnerability of advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) to cyber-physical threats, particularly energy theft. This paper proposes a novel, transformation-driven detection framework based on the Hellinger Distance (HD) to identify anomalies in daily electricity consumption at the individual smart meter level. By applying log and antilog transformations to measurement variations, the method enhances distribution separability, improving detection accuracy. The HD metric is then used to quantify statistical deviations between historical and real-time consumption patterns. The proposed scheme operates without requiring system-wide observability or state estimation, making it lightweight and scalable. Experimental validation using real-world datasets from the Irish Social Science Data Archive (ISSDA) and the State Grid Corporation of China (SGCC) smart meter database demonstrates detection rates between 83% and 100% across multiple attack scenarios. Compared with existing statistical and ML-based methods, the proposed approach achieves higher detection rates while remaining computationally efficient, confirming its effectiveness and suitability for practical AMI deployments.

title: A Novel Ultrahigh-Resolution and Wide-Swath Sliding Spotlight SAR Imaging approach Based on Poisson Disk Sampling Strategy
authors: Xinchang Hu, Di Yin, Fei Teng, Lihua Zhong, Jitong Duan, Lijia Huang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Since multichannel sliding spotlight synthetic aperture radar (SAR) can simultaneously achieve high-resolution and wide-swath (HRWS) remote sensing observations, the large volume of data collected from multiple channels increases the burden on the data link, while the estimation and compensation of channel errors further complicate the signal processing. To reduce hardware costs and algorithm complexity while ensuring HRWS capabilities, this letter proposes a novel single-channel ultrahigh-resolution and wide-swath sliding spotlight SAR imaging approach based on a Poisson disk sampling strategy. First, the proposed method adopts a Poisson disk sampling strategy to collect nonuniform azimuth sampling echo data, which has well anti-aliasing effects for the sparsity-driven reconstruction. Second, a modified Chirp Scaling algorithm, which integrates a fourth-order range model (FORM4) to precisely account for the spaceborne SAR curved orbit, is presented to achieve full-aperture focusing of ultrahigh resolution sliding spotlight SAR data across the entire scene. Then, the complete imaging reconstruction process is given, which mainly involves the operation of Deramp preprocessing based on nonuniform sampling sliding spotlight SAR data, as well as threshold iterative processing. Simulation results powerfully demonstrate the capability of the proposed MCSA to achieve a 0.3m resolution over a 4km×4km swath in non-uniformly sampled sliding spotlight SAR, with a 20% reduction in data acquisitiion compared to traditional approaches.

title:
BR
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    L: Rethinking Bilateral Reweighted Reconstruction Representation Learning for Unsupervised Hyperspectral Band Selection
authors: Guoxia Xu, Qi Wu, Yulong Sun, Lizhen Deng, Hu Zhu
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Unsupervised band selection aims to identify the most informative spectral bands from hyperspectral images, thereby reducing spectral redundancy while preserving essential spatial–spectral structures. However, existing methods often ignore global or local structural information and noise, leading to suboptimal band subsets. In this paper, we propose a bilateral reweighted reconstruction representation model that jointly leverages spatial and spectral priors for unsupervised band selection. To simultaneously exploit both spectral correlation and spatial structure, a reconstruction residual consistency constraint is introduced into superpixels, forcing residuals of pixels inside the same superpixel to converge toward the regional mean. Additionally, spatial adaptive weight matrices W<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</sub> and spectral adaptive weight matrices W<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">b</sub> are designed to coordinate the weight consistency of pixels and band dimensions through bilateral weighting terms, suppress noise and maintain edge structure. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several representative algorithms in terms of noise suppression and edge preservation.

title: Quad-Radiator MIMO with Asymmetric Feed Line for (n77/n78/n79/WLAN) 5G-Wireless Applications
authors: Manasa R, Trupti Shripad Tagare, B Lakshmi Narayana Reddy, S. Thulasi Prasad, Kolli Venkatrao, T. Saravanan, N. Karuppiah, Ajay Sudhir Bale, Deepak Kapila
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This article explores advanced antenna techniques specifically within the sub-6GHz frequency range, focusing on compact Quad port antennas tailored for 5G bands that offer extensive bandwidth capabilities. The primary aim of this research is to achieve a broader bandwidth and elevated data rates while preserving a nearly omnidirectional radiation pattern through a unique design methodology. To improve the necessary mutual coupling, the quad patch elements are arranged face-to-face with adequate spacing between the antenna elements in the MIMO (Multiple Input Multiple Output) system. The enhanced design features an inverted T-shaped partial ground and square patches equipped with stub elements on both sides to optimize impedance matching. Additionally, the role of the twin stub and the rectangular notch positioned at the top right corner of the patch is thoroughly examined. An asymmetrical feed line structure is incorporated for the operation of the Quad port antenna, which measures (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{W} \times \mathrm{L}$</tex>) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$31 \times 40 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. This compact configuration achieves an impedance bandwidth (-10 dB) spanning from 2.27 to 5.34 GHz. The assessment of key antenna performance metrics, including correlation coefficient, diversity gain, and isolation—crucial in the current wireless environment—has been thoroughly addressed. Furthermore, the paper presents a highly effective design approach for n77/n78/n79 band antennas that enables wideband coverage, appropriate correlation, and high isolation for wireless 5G communications.

title: Advancing Plant Health: Leveraging Machine Learning for Precision Leaf Detection in Agriculture
authors: Shiplu Das, Panchami Das, Nipashree Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In agricultural and botanical applications, accurate leaf detection underpins plant classification, disease identification, and yield estimation. Conventional image processing pipelines face performance degradation under intra-class shape variation, illumination change, occlusion, and complex backgrounds. We present a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)–driven framework that augments scarce and imbalanced plant-leaf datasets with diverse, high-fidelity synthetic imagery, thereby strengthening downstream machine learning performance. Our system integrates Progressive Growing GANs (PGGAN), transfer learning with state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN; ResNet, EfficientNet), Faster R-CNN for lesion localization, hybrid K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)+Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for low-resource inference contexts, and custom loss formulations (weighted cross-entropy, focal loss) to prioritize severe disease classes. Experimental evaluation demonstrates consistent performance gains across accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and mean Intersection-over-Union (mIoU) when GAN-augmented data are incorporated. Notably, Faster R-CNN with GAN augmentation achieved 97.1% accuracy and 92.3 mIoU, while CNN+GAN achieved 96.7% accuracy. These results highlight the promise of synthetic-data–driven, multi-model pipelines for robust, scalable, and early plant disease detection in precision agriculture.

title: A Theoretical Model Explaining the Impact on Satisfaction, Engagement, and Repurchase Intention in Modern Retailing
authors: Do Bui Xuan Cuong, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study aims to test a theoretical model that explains how an omnichannel customer experience can improve satisfaction, engagement, and repurchase intention in modern retail. Using a quantitative research approach, a survey of 533 customers was conducted, and the data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The findings confirm that all proposed hypotheses were substantiated with a high degree of statistical significance, demonstrating a strong model fit. The results indicate that an omnichannel customer experience has a positive impact on satisfaction (SAT), engagement (CE), and repurchase intention (RI). Additionally, SAT and CE both positively influence RI and act as partial mediators between the omnichannel experience and repurchase behavior. These findings underscore the importance of delivering seamless experiences across all channels to foster customer loyalty and encourage repeat purchases. The study strengthens the theoretical foundation of the Stimulus-Organism-Response framework and offers practical insights for firms undergoing digital transformation.

title: The Architecture of Silence
authors: Fahim Kawsar

title: A Polyaniline-SDS Based Flexible Sensor for Glyphosate Detection in Aqueous media
authors: Lazar Milić, Mitar Simić, Radmila Tomovska, Jadranka Blaževska-Gilev, Goran M. Stojanović
abstract: Glyphosate is one of the most widely used herbicides worldwide, and its extensive application has raised growing concerns regarding environmental persistence. Conventional analytical techniques for glyphosate detection, such as chromatographic and spectrometric methods, offer high accuracy but require complex instrumentation, extensive sample preparation, and skilled personnel, limiting their suitability for rapid and on-site monitoring. In this work, a flexible electrochemical sensor, based on a screen-printed electrode functionalized with a polyaniline/sodium dodecyl sulfate (PANI/SDS) composite is presented for glyphosate detection in aqueous media. Electrochemical characterization was performed using open-circuit potential, electrochemical impedance spectroscopy, and differential pulse voltammetry. The sensor exhibited a linear response to glyphosate concentrations in the range of 0.0013–0.02%wt, with a sensitivity of 134.0 μA/%wt. The detection mechanism was supported by mathematical modeling based on the Randles–Ševčík relation, confirming diffusion-controlled charge-transfer kinetics. Analytical figures of merit include a limit of detection of 6.7 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> %wt and a limit of quantification of 2.25⋅ 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> %wt. Selectivity was demonstrated against potassium. Owing to its simple fabrication, low-cost materials, and flexible format, the proposed PANI/SDS-based sensor represents a promising platform for scalable and field-deployable glyphosate monitoring in environmental applications.

title: A Novel Model Predictive Control-Based Energy Management for Stable and Efficient Operation of Renewable Integrated DC Microgrids
authors: Narendra Babu Kampa, Sandeep Vuddanti, S. V. K. Naresh
abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical model predictive control (HMPC)–based energy management system (EMS) for a DC microgrid integrating photovoltaic (PV) generation, wind energy conversion, battery energy storage, and dynamic DC loads. The proposed framework coordinates multi-source power flows while ensuring robust DC bus voltage regulation, efficient renewable energy utilization, and battery-friendly operation. A detailed MATLAB/Simulink model of the DC microgrid is developed and evaluated under realistic operating conditions, including variable solar irradiance, fluctuating wind speeds, bidirectional battery operation, and dynamic electric vehicle and industrial load demands. The performance of the proposed HMPC is quantitatively compared with an existing adaptive model predictive control (AMPC) strategy. Numerical results demonstrate that HMPC significantly improves DC bus voltage stability, reducing voltage deviation from ±1.67% under AMPC to below ±0.83% during renewable power variations and to ±0.083% under battery power fluctuations. Voltage standard deviation is reduced by up to 70–90%, indicating enhanced damping and faster convergence. Furthermore, HMPC achieves smoother battery power transitions and superior state-of-charge regulation, limiting SoC ripples to below ±0.005 and reducing electrochemical stress by approximately 60–70%. These improvements confirm stronger stability margins, improved disturbance rejection, and higher overall EMS efficiency. The results validate HMPC as a robust and scalable control strategy for reliable and stable operation of modern DC microgrids with high renewable penetration.

title: Dynamic Performance of Parallel Heterogeneous Grid-Forming Inverters During Islanding and Grid Resynchronization
authors: Zahra Ahmadimonfared, Stefan Eichner
abstract: Modern power systems with high inverter penetration face reduced inertia and increased frequency instability during disturbances, necessitating grid-forming inverters (GFMs) that emulate synchronous-machine dynamics. This study examines the parallel operation of a Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) controlled and a synchronverter-controlled GFM in a low-inertia microgrid across the full operational cycle of grid-connected, islanded, and resynchronization phases.Aunified simulation framework was developed to systematically evaluate the impact of coordinated variations in virtual inertia (H) and damping coefficient (Dp) on frequency dynamics, power sharing, and stability under power imbalance and grid-code-compliant reconnection conditions. The results demonstrate that a higher virtual inertia limits the rate of change of frequency and nadir after islanding, while increased damping enhances oscillation suppression and settling performance. During resynchronization, both GFM satisfy IEEE Std 1547–2018 [1] limits on voltage, frequency, and phase-angle differences, confirming stable interaction and seamless transition between the operating modes. These findings reveal the complementary strengths of heterogeneous VSM–synchronverter operation and the critical role of unified parameter tuning for robust microgrid performance in low-inertia environments.

title: All-Fiber Multimodal Sensor for Dual-Gas and Spectral Co-Detection Based on Hollow-Core Photonic Bandgap Fiber
authors: Junhao Lin, Jifeng Li, Yujing Wu
abstract: Accurate, non-invasive measurement of greenhouse gases is crucial for environmental monitoring and sensor networks. Traditional gas sensors are limited in terms of sensitivity and spatial resolution, and thus accurate spatiotemporal mapping of gas concentrations is difficult. In this work, we present a fully fiber-integrated multimodal sensing system combining a HC-PBF gas detection module with a UV-excited spectral analysis module. This arrangement allows for simultaneous spatially resolved monitoring of vegetation spectral signals and greenhouse gases. The HC-PBF sensor with side-hole diffusion structure provides non-invasive gas sampling, and time-division multiplexed dual-wavelength detection measures methane and carbon dioxide with 1 s integration detection limits of 0.15 ppm and 14 ppm, respectively. The system provides high sensitivity, low interference, and spatial resolution, allowing real-time integration of gas concentration and spatial data. Rice paddy field experiments demonstrate the robustness and stability of the system under complex environmental conditions and indicate a potential correlation between vegetation fluorescence ratio parameters (F735/480 and F780/480) and gas emissions. These results demonstrate that the fully fiber-integrated approach is suitable for high-precision multimodal environmental sensing and can be applied to ecological monitoring and distributed fiber-optic sensor networks.

title: Cross-Layer Task Scheduling for NOMA-Assisted Satellite Edge Computing_supp1-3677654.pdf
authors: Guangming CUI
abstract: Ubiquitous, low-latency intelligence at the network edge is central to large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) deployments, yet effectively coordinating communication, computing, and backhaul operations across heterogeneous layers remains challenging. This paper presents a unified cross-layer framework for terrestrial–satellite edge computing IoT systems that integrates Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA)-based terrestrial access with local, satellite, and cloud execution. Unlike conventional terrestrial Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC)/edge– cloud scheduling, we jointly optimize partial offloading and path selection over a NOMA-coupled uplink and a multi-hop satellite edge/cloud execution chain under end-to-end latency coupling. The framework jointly determines path selection and partial offloading to minimize a latency–energy objective using accurate end-to-end models. Within this framework, two complementary scheduling algorithms are developed. The Centralized Optimal Cross-Layer Scheduler (COCS) formulates the joint scheduling problem as a mixed-integer nonlinear program (MINLP) with logarithmic and bilinear terms. It employs the spatial branch-and-bound (sBB) method within a commercial solver to obtain a global solution, serving as a performance benchmark. The Decentralized Game-Theoretic Scheduler (DGTS) models user decisions as an ordinal potential game (OPG) and achieves distributed convergence via best-response dynamics (BRD), enabling scalability and adaptability to large networks. Extensive simulations demonstrate that COCS achieves the global optimum while DGTS attains near-optimal performance with much lower complexity. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed cross-layer framework and highlight the importance of coordinated communication–computation–backhaul design for terrestrial–satellite integrated edge computing.

title: Assessment of Contrast-Enhanced Ultrasound Detection Limit for Abdominal Hemorrhage_supp2-3677094.mp4
authors: Brian Telfer
abstract: Hemorrhage is the leading cause of preventable death from trauma, with most fatalities occurring in pre-hospital settings. The current standard for point-of-care hemorrhage detection is the focused assessment with sonography in trauma (FAST) exam, or computed tomography (CT) angiography after hospital arrival. Contrast-enhanced ultrasound (CEUS) has been proposed as a next-generation FAST exam, due to potential for (1) differentiating active vs. inactive bleeding, (2) localizing bleeding sources, and (3) guiding percutaneous and/or endovascular treatments to temporize bleeding for prolonged transport. While these advantages have been reported in human trauma case studies, characterization of the minimum hemorrhage rate detectable by CEUS remains a translational research need. Prior work using conventional B-mode imaging and microvascular CEUS algorithms showed detection of 5 mL/min flow in a generic hemorrhage phantom. Here, we apply nonlinear imaging waveforms (pulse inversion, PI, and amplitude modulation, AM) with an iterative spatiotemporal filter that further enhances contrast signals (fast iterative-shrinkage thresholding algorithm, FISTA) to detect significantly lower flow rates in an anatomically realistic abdominal hemorrhage phantom. We demonstrate detection of bleeding at a minimum flow rate of 0.5 mL/min, comparable to multidetector CT. We achieve this detection limit even with 10% of the standard contrast dose (area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.90 and 0.84 for PI+FISTA and AM+FISTA, respectively, compared to 0.38 for conventional B-mode), suggesting that CEUS examination is feasible throughout contrast wash-in / wash-out. Finally, to bridge towards in vivo characterization, we present a novel porcine liver hemorrhage model tailored to evaluating CEUS capabilities.

title: Longitudinal, non-invasive magnetocardiography of avian embryos with optically pumped magnetometers_supp1-3672704.pdf
authors: Xiang He
abstract: Optically pumped magnetometers (OPMs) provide high-sensitivity, contact-free detection of biomagnetic signals, yet their use in avian developmental monitoring has not been validated across full incubation or in conservation-relevant species. We present an OPM-based magnetocardiography (MCG) workflow that records embryonic heart activity throughout the 21-day incubation of White Leghorn (Gallus gallus domesticus) eggs. A rotating platform enabled four orientations per egg in under 2 minutes, yielding comprehensive MCG datasets with minimal handling. Quantitative analysis revealed a progressive increase in R-wave amplitude (from 0.70 pT at day 13 to 0.95 pT at day 20) and a systematic upward shift in dominant spectral frequency from 2–4 Hz to 3–5 Hz over the course of incubation, providing a detailed electrophysiological maturation profile. Extending beyond a model species, We demonstrate, for the first time, successful MCG recordings in a conservation-relevant, endangered species Crested Ibis (Nipponia nippon) embryo. Together, these results establish OPM-MCG as a rapid, longitudinal, and non-invasive approach for assessing avian cardiac development, with implications for developmental biology, hatchery monitoring, and species conservation.

title: A Significance Measure for Node Splits in C4.5
authors: Nicholas Bidler, Shawn X. Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Decision trees remain one of the most interpretable and computationally efficient classification methods. The ID3 (Iterative Dichotomiser 3) algorithm is a classic algorithm in machine learning used to construct decision trees from a dataset. C4.5 extends ID3 to handle numeric attributes using thresholding and information gain. The C4.5 algorithm recursively partitions data based on maximum information gain, producing trees that progress from the most informative attributes at the root to the least informative at the leaves. While inner nodes are chosen by optimizing entropy-based criteria, leaf nodes do not admit further entropy comparisons, leaving the importance of the last split ambiguous. We propose a simple, dataset-agnostic scoring scheme that quantifies the significance of a parent node’s final subdivision into its leaves, enabling a direct measure of the utility of the final split. After training a full tree, we record predictions and ground truth at each leaf on a held-out test set, compute per-leaf scores as accuracy × correct, and then "merge" sibling leaves back into their parent by concatenating their predicted/actual lists; the same score is computed for the merged node. The final-split significance is measured as the difference between the sum of leaf scores and the merged-parent score; zero indicates no benefit from splitting. Across several public datasets, the measure highlights when small but accurate leaves or imbalanced families meaningfully improve performance, and when splits add little value. The measure is simple to compute, compatible with standard pruning workflows, and can act as a practical heuristic for late-split evaluation. Our experiments on multiple real-world datasets show that this approach can identify and prune non-beneficial splits, improving model interpretability without compromising accuracy. This method offers a statistically aware pruning criterion that complements existing entropy-based splitting strategies.

title: A Privacy-Compliant Hybrid Framework for Real-Time Offensive Text Moderation in Dual-Mode Chat Systems
authors: Raghu Yashwanth T M, Parivel R, Priyan S, Santhosh R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: An automatic text moderation system that provides secure, synchronized, real-time communication in chat-style venues for anonymous and non-anonymous modes is described. It is constructed to prevent inappropriate content by targeting and blocking messages at the edge, forming a protective layer that ensures inappropriate content is intercepted before delivery. Any flagged message is blocked and never transmitted to the recipient and never be transported to the end user. A hybrid filtering approach was developed to combine the rule-based approach with AIinduced detection to handle phonetically changed and structurally modified word forms, which are often employed to bypass the regular filters. This manually compiled dataset was used to mimic those linguistic variants and train moderation models that are tailored towards them. Furthermore, generative language models were integrated to increase the dataset and generalize to unseen inputs. The test results indicate that the detection ratio was 96.4% with a false positive ratio of 2.1%, and the average processing time was 117 milliseconds, regardless of the message size. The modular design allows for a lightweight deployment on various digital platforms, such as educational, corporate, and public-based systems. Continual adjustment is supported through the use of dynamically updated wordlists and scalable moderation logic. The system emphasizes privacy-preserving incognito modes alongside policy-enforced permanent chat, enabling flexible deployment while ensuring regulatory compliance and communication safety. Further work will add deeper semantic models informed by a context-aware language model and improve the quality of the moderation by refining the user feedback loop.

title: Quantum Annealing Algorithm Based Power Optimization for Electric Vehicle Charging Using a Buck Converter
authors: B Sharmiladevi, T Bhuvanasundar, NP Bose, J Dharunkumar, M Gokul
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: DC-charging Electric Vehicle (EV) systems commonly use DC-DC buck converters to regulate voltage and manage power. Conventional control techniques, such as Proportional-Integral (PI) controllers or Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), often struggle with dynamic load and input variations, resulting in higher switching losses, voltage ripples, and reduced efficiency. This paper proposes a novel Quantum Annealing Algorithm (QAA)-based approach for real-time power optimization in EV charging using a buck converter. By leveraging quantum tunneling principles, the proposed method dynamically adjusts the converter duty cycle to minimize switching losses, reduce harmonic distortion, and stabilize voltage under varying load and input conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that the QAA-based controller converges faster, improves energy efficiency, and enhances robustness compared to conventional methods. This framework offers scalable and flexible solutions for modern EV charging infrastructure, combining quantum optimization with classical converter technology to achieve energy-efficient and highperformance operation.

title: Integrating Mosaic Loss of Y Chromosome, Clinical and Lifestyle Factors, and Heart Rate Zones for Early Cardiovascular Risk Prediction in Men
authors: M. Ayyadurai, Krithika R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: CVD (cardiovascular disease) continues to be one of the primary causes of death among men worldwide; thus, there is an urgent need for an established system for determining which men will develop CVD at an early age. Current traditional clinical scoring methods fail to capture the multifaceted ways in which genetic, physiological and lifestyle factors interact and/or influence cardiovascular health in men. An example of a newly identified genetic marker that is associated with CVD is the loss of the Y chromosome (mLOY), which occurs during the aging process of males; to date, the mLOY has not yet been incorporated into any predictive CVD models. The present study presents a combination of machine learning methodologies for estimating CVD risk in males by utilizing mLOY as a proxy for determining CVD risk along with lifestyle patterns and heart-rate dynamics (HRZ). The multisource database (10,175 participants) has been processed/merged/engineered to create clinically pertinent predictors based upon the most reliable CVD risk factors available (MHR, % MHR, Hypertension stage, BMI category).

title: The Hybrid Deep Learning Framework to Identify Real World Papaya Leaf to Detect Disease by Approximating the Severity
authors: Joan Yazhini J, Nikhita Sherlin Christopher, Sowmiya L
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Despite the fact that papaya is a staple of tropical farming, the growth of the crop is frequently crippled by viral infections which affect the foliage and loss of output is a norm. The need to detect and accurately quantify the severity of infection in time comes to the fore, so as to reduce the cost of loss, and to take corrective actions on time. A second problem of the real-world detection is clutter field scene, changeable illumination patterns and partial foliage coverage. To overcome this challenge, the disease classification and severity levels are combined to present the Hybrid Deep Learning Framework of intelligent papaya leaf disease management. It consists of a transfer learning-explained joint of both Xception and DenseNet201 backbones to learn representations of non-uniform vein patterns and surface textures. The mask-aware severity estimator applies the infection proportionally to the surfaces of the leaves after classification to provide actionable and transparent disease information. To enable the capacity to make decisions at a contextual level, a context sensitive feature such as climatic variations and geo-spatial tags are incorporated thus allowing a series of agro-ecological zones to adapt. This methodology opposes to exhaustive augmentation methods and generative approaches, which enables data augmentation of such challenging and uncontrolled real-field situations which require increasing ground truth data that impiles generalization directions, even though the data insufficiency problem exists. The framework should also offer AI-based treatment recommendations in the local languages, using an edge-computing-based mobile platform which can be implemented in rural settings with limited connectivity. The research paper builds a farmer friendly, interpretable solution, which not only helps in preserving sustainable farming, but also helps in precision agriculture, by implementing the deep learning models in practical contexts..

title: Iterative Floorplan Refinement for Efficient Area Utilization and Reduced Congestion in VLSI Physical Design
authors: P.Kishore, B.Haindavi, V.Priyanka
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) technology has enabled the creation of complex chips and System-on-Chip (SoCs) by integrating billions of transistors within a single integrated circuit. The design of such advanced chips is a lengthy process that involves multiple stages, particularly in the domain of physical design. Physical design converts the Register Transfer Level (RTL) netlist into a manufacturable layout, commonly referred to as a GDS. It involves Floorplan, Powerplan, Placement, Clock tree synthesis, and Routing stages. Among them, floorplanning is a crucial phase, as it influences design performance and manufacturability by addressing congestion and area utilization. This paper focuses on a block-level implementation of an ORCATOP block using 32nm technology. It employs Synopsys’ IC Compiler II tool, using nine metal layers for routing and operating at a maximum frequency of 200 MHz. This study focuses on optimizing the design from the initial floorplan to various placement stages. Initially, the design exhibits severe congestion issues. Upon modifying the floorplan, significant improvements were achieved, particularly in congestion reduction. The results indicate that the iterative approach and a well-optimized floorplan highlight the significant reduction in congestion while achieving better utilization of the area by improving the overall design efficiency.

title: Employ the Machine Learning Models for immediate Predictive Diagnostics: Anomaly detection of Industrial IoT
authors: Jubert R. Pagunuran, Khaled Alqawasmi, V. Samuthira Pandi, Navyatha Ravi, M. Dinesh, A.Rajalingam
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has transformed plants and factories, facilitating real-time monitoring, automation, and informed decision-making. Nevertheless, in such complex data-rich environments, identification of the fault in a timely manner, detection of the anomaly, has become one of the most important challenges. Unresolved anomalies in IIoT systems can cause unscheduled maintenance, slippage in quality, or even catastrophic failure. The conventional rule-based models are not capable of accommodating the complicated dynamics and the non-linear dependencies that widely exist in industrial systems. In this study, the mixture machine learning (ML) models are applied for real-time predictive diagnostics and anomaly detection in the IIoT networks. System Overview The proposed system combines both supervised and unsupervised ML models getting the best of each of them such as Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Autoencoders, Isolation Forest, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. These models are learned and tested over massive time-series sensor data derived from IIoT ready industrial machines (e.g., pumps, motors, turbines, and conveyors). Method of feature extraction and the detection of temporal patterns are applied to find the abnormal behavior in the operational parameters: temperature, vibration, pressure and energy consumption. One of the key contributions in our study is that models could be deployed in real time and this is made possible by edge computation. By moving ML inference to the edge, the system provides quick response times even under bandwidth-constrained conditions. Alongside, long-term analytics trend forecasting and cross-facility model improvement is made possible via cloud-based aggregation. The combined alerting and strategic system along with hybrid structure allows operators to avoid catastrophic failures before they occur. Experiments on benchmark IIoT datasets show that the proposed models achieved excellent detection performance, e.g., more than 95% precision and recall for the temporal based model-LSTM. The ML-based approach is shown to vastly outperform conventional threshold-based detections in the presence of noisy, multivariate and asynchronous data. In addition, machine behavior evolves, and with it the ability to learn adapts diagnostic accuracy. This work underlines the disruptive nature of the ML for predictive maintenance and real-time industry diagnosis. Through a transition from reactive to proactive approaches, the developed framework minimizes downtime, lowers maintenance costs, and extends asset life. Incorporating smart diagnostics into the IIoT networks not only promotes operational efficiency, but also provides an infrastructural building block for the next-generation autonomous industrial systems that are data, intelligence, and connectivity enabled.

title: Mind the Gap: The Laggards in a G2B Digital Service
authors: Apriasih
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: HM Land Registry for England and Wales introduced the Digital Registration Service (DRS) in 2021 to replace the Electronic Document Registration Service (EDRS). Despite its aim to accelerate property registration, many clients had not adopted it as of four months before the 2022 deadline. This study examined the cognitive barriers to adopting DRS using a qualitative approach. Five participants, three clients and two HM Land Registry staff, were interviewed, and the data were thematically analyzed using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). Although clients perceived DRS as easy to use, perceived incompleteness arising from temporal misalignment reduced its usefulness, leading obligated users to adopt laggard behavior driven by intertemporal cost considerations.

title: Integration of Multimodal Perception and Path Planning in Autonomous Driving
authors: Tianzuo Yuan, Zhanwei Wang, Xiaolong Jia, Chenhao Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To achieve safe, comfortable, and efficient driving of autonomous vehicles in complex dynamic environments, this paper proposes a model predictive control trajectory planning method that integrates multimodal perception information. Firstly, based on information such as environmental obstacles, road boundaries, and dynamic traffic participants acquired by the multimodal perception system, a “three-circle” inflated safety zone model is constructed. Environmental modeling driven by perception data simplifies the planning complexity and effectively avoids collision risks caused by deviations between the idealized vehicle model and the actual perceived environment. Secondly, a planning architecture with horizontal and vertical spatial decoupling is adopted. At the horizontal level, combined with the perceived road topology, model predictive control is used to generate a set of candidate trajectories that meet the constraints of continuous curvature and obstacle avoidance. At the vertical level, based on dynamic information such as the preceding vehicle's speed and traffic flow status obtained through perception, dynamic programming is applied to quickly solve the optimal speed profile, significantly improving planning efficiency and real-time performance. Finally, a multi-objective optimal trajectory evaluation function is constructed by synthesizing environmental constraints fed back by multimodal perception, vehicle dynamics limitations, and ride comfort requirements, realizing the coordinated optimization of path and speed planning. A closed-loop verification environment for multimodal perception-planning is built using the Matlab/Simulink, Prescan, and Carsim co-simulation platform. Experimental results show that the longitudinal acceleration change rate (comfort index), steering wheel angle smoothness, and trajectory tracking accuracy of the vehicle under the proposed method are all superior to traditional planning methods. The average deviation between the planned curve and the actual tracking curve is less than 0.15 m, which fully verifies the effectiveness and superiority of the fusion architecture of multimodal perception and path planning.

title: Ethernet-Based Inter-FPGA Data Transfer Using Softcore Processors for Kintex Ultrascale
authors: Harshvardhan Singh, Veer Mukadam, Hem Raj Shau, Durga Digdarsini, Mahesh Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: In this paper, high-speed data transfer is carried out between the two different FPGA boards using two different softcore processor configurations. First configuration involves, the MicroBlaze softcore processor with the lwIP stack to implement TCP/IP over Ethernet, enabling reliable, protocol-compliant data transfer while the second configuration comprise the LEON3 softcore processor with the GRETH MAC core to implement raw Ethernet communication, allowing low-latency, high-speed data exchange without any transport-layer protocol. The designs are implemented using Xilinx Kintex-7 and Xilinx Kintex Ultrascale FPGA based boards, both equipped with on-board Ethernet PHYs and support for embedded data processing.

title: Securing CAN Bus Transmissions with Lightweight AEAD Ciphers
authors: Joshua Copeland, Leonie Simpson, Geoff Walker
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Australasian Information Security Conference
abstract: None

title: Machine Learning-Based Load Forecasting for Vehicle-to-Grid Scheduling: A Case Study on the Italian Power System
authors: Zahid Ullah, Michele De Santis, Luigi Rubino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are emerging not only as a sustainable mode of transportation but also as a promising source of flexibility in modern power systems using vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology. This study investigated the integration of short-term load forecasting with V2G scheduling using open-access hourly data from the Italian grid. Multiple forecasting models, including linear regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and a hybrid ARIMA + XGBoost model, were evaluated for their accuracy using standard metrics. The hybrid model demonstrated superior performance, with an RMSE of 376.91 MW and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9969. This model was then used to simulate two V2G discharging strategies, forecast-driven discharging during the top-5 peak hours and fixed-window strategy. The fixed-window strategy resulted in 2320 MWh of energy discharge over the month and contributed to improved load curve flattening. These results demonstrate the potential of coupling accurate forecasting with well-timed V2G operations to support peak-load management and enhance grid flexibility. This study provides a standardized process for integrating forecasting into V2G planning, supporting smart grid development and policy design.

title: Llm-Fuzz: Adaptive, Structure-Aware Fuzzing of Javascript Engines with Large Language Models
authors: Kongyang Liu, Chengxiang Tan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Fuzzing modern JavaScript (JS) engines remains challenging due to the semantic complexity required to explore deep program states and advanced components such as Just-InTime (JIT) compilers. Traditional mutation-based fuzzers often generate invalid inputs, while existing structure-aware techniques still face a semantic wall, producing syntactically valid but semantically flawed programs. This paper presents LLM-Fuzz, a coverage-guided fuzzing framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform AST-based, semantics-aware mutations. Unlike prior fuzzers with fixed operators, LLM-Fuzz introduces an adaptive feedback loop that fine-tunes the mutation strategy during fuzzing via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). Experiments on V8 and ChakraCore show that LLM-Fuzz reduces runtime error rates and achieves higher edge coverage compared to state-of-the-art fuzzers. These results demonstrate that incampaign, LLM-driven adaptation can improve the effectiveness of fuzzing structured inputs.

title: Sky-ground Collaborative Perception and Image Recognition Algorithm for Transmission Lines Facing Complex Terrain
authors: Jinhu Liu, Shuntao Huang, Xiao Zeng, Songhao Shi, Dianhong Xu, Yadi Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low efficiency, high risk and data fragmentation of traditional manual and single unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) inspection in complex terrain such as mountains and forests, this paper proposes a three-layer cooperative sensing framework of "satellite -UAV- ground robot", and designs a cross-modal feature alignment network (CMFA-Net) and a lightweight YOLOv8 detection model. The system uses satellite remote sensing to generate global patrol heat map, and guides multi-UAVs to dynamically partition based on improved Voronoi diagram and energy consumption model, achieving 98% coverage in 3.9 h and shortening the range by 44%. CMFA-Net aligns the features of satellite, UAV and ground heterogeneous images with cross-correlation attention mechanism, and the accuracy of cross-view defect association is still 91.8% when the vegetation is blocked by 60%, and the mAP@0.5 is improved to 93.6%. At the same time, the dynamic safe distance model and improved A* path planning are proposed to reduce the collision risk of UAV to 2.3%.
