Through mixed quantization compression, the parameter quantity of YOLOv8 is reduced to 1.2 M, and real-time reasoning at 25 FPS is realized on Jetson AGX Orin, and the loss of mAP is only 0.3%. The experimental results of 83 km transmission line in Hengduan Mountains show that this method has the advantages of high efficiency, safety and accuracy under the conditions of complex terrain and strong wind and rain fog, and provides a feasible scheme for intelligent inspection of transmission lines.

title: Wearable HRV Stress Detection and Intervention for Sensory Overload in Children with Autism in the Philippines
authors: Berenice Medina, Miguel Villanueva, Norshuhani Zamin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) are often highly susceptible to sensory overload, triggered by factors such as excessive noise, unfamiliar environments, or challenges in emotional regulation. Sensory overload can result in intense reactions, including tantrums and meltdowns, which may escalate into heightened anxiety, withdrawal, self-stimulatory behaviors, or even impaired communication. These responses make it difficult for caregivers to manage and comfort the child effectively and can hinder their learning, social interactions, and emotional well-being. Existing assistive technologies, such as wearable devices, have been developed to detect sensory overload and provide real-time information to parents. This study proposes an innovative assistive technology system, designed to enhance the management of sensory overload in children with ASD. The system integrates a wearable device equipped with a DFRobot Gravity photoplethysmography (PPG) heart rate sensor, using Arduino with an ESP32 microcontroller. The wearable continuously measures heart rate and derives heart rate variability (HRV) metrics, which serve as physiological indicators of stress associated with sensory overload. Validation experiments were conducted with five high-functioning children with ASD, where heart rate and HRV stress scores were measured before and after controlled physical activity, using wrist and finger sensor placements. Comparative analysis with a commercial wearable band showed a strong correlation (r = 0.97) and a minimal mean difference in BPM of 2.00, highlighting the feasibility of our HRV-based stress wearable in real-world use. Our system aims to improve stress management and strengthen communication between children with ASD and their caregivers in educational and everyday settings.

title: Research on Small Lesion Detection in Medical Imaging Based on Improved YOLO
authors: Xingyi Zhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Aiming at the challenges of low accuracy, poor robustness and high hardware dependence in the detection of small lesions in medical images, this study proposes an attention-enhanced detection system based on the improved YOLOv8. This system embeds the ECAttention module into the backbone network based on the YOLOv8 benchmark framework, enhancing the global context feature aggregation ability through efficient one-dimensional convolution. Meanwhile, a multi-scale enhanced attention module (MultiSEAM) is designed and integrated into the detection head. By using a parallel multi-branch structure to fuse the features of different receptive fields and dynamically calibrate the channel response, the perception and positioning accuracy of the model for small lesions with variable scales and blurred boundaries is effectively improved. Experimental verification was conducted on a dataset containing 3064 brain MRI images. The results show that the comprehensive performance of this system is excellent, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> reaching 92.9%. Among them, the detection accuracy for meningioma and pituitary tumor is as high as 99.1% and 97.2% respectively, which is significantly better than the benchmark model.

title: Bridging AI and Practical Implementation: A Low-Cost, High-Accuracy Motion Capture System for Dynamic Environments
authors: Inder Pal Singh, Mahesh Chandrashekar, Jubert R. Pagunuran, D.Shanmugam, A.Rajalingam, C.Murugamani
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This research introduces a cost-effective, high-precision human motion capture system designed for real-time performance in dynamic and unconstrained environments. Leveraging lightweight artificial intelligence techniques, the proposed framework integrates the Continuously Adaptive Mean Shift (Camshift) algorithm with Kalman filtering to deliver robust tracking under conditions of variable lighting, rapid motion, and partial occlusion all while requiring only standard video cameras and minimal computational resources. A novel algorithm for motion data extraction from video sequences further enhances the system’s responsiveness, achieving an average processing time of just 54 milliseconds per instance significantly outperforming conventional approaches. The system’s modular, scalable architecture enables seamless deployment across a range of practical applications, including sports analytics, physical rehabilitation, interactive animation, and intelligent human-computer interfaces. This work bridges the gap between AI-driven theoretical innovation and real-world applicability, resulting in a scalable and versatile motion capture framework optimized for the complexities of modern intelligent systems.

title: Research on the Construction of Data Standard Management System
authors: Wei Wu, Liang Ge, Yaping Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Effective management of data standards is crucial for ensuring data quality and interoperability in cloud-native and distributed data environments. This paper proposes a data standard management system that enables integrated governance of both foundational and application-level standards through a standardized pedigree model and clause-level differential analysis. The system incorporates a unified database schema supporting version control, AI-enhanced feature extraction, and efficient retrieval mechanisms. Developed within a B/S architecture using Spring Boot and Vue.js, it provides granular permission management, real-time visualization of modifications, and external API integration. Results demonstrate that the framework significantly improves standard consistency, traceability, and operational adaptability, offering a scalable solution for complex data-driven organizations.

title: A Large Language Model for Hybrid Table-a nd-Text Question Answering Based on a Dynamic Chain Reasoning Framework
authors: Yongzeng Zhang, Bo Sun, Yilong Wen, Kang Han, Lei Zhang, Dianwen Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the challenges of question-answering with mixed table and text data in smart transportation scenarios, this paper proposes a large language model solution based on a dynamic chain reasoning framework. By constructing a multistage reasoning mechanism that includes situation analysis, information filtering, logical construction, intelligent validation, and engine execution, the solution effectively addresses the collaborative understanding issues between structured tables and unstructured text. Using the DeepSeek R1 model for instruction fine-tuning and combining it with the professional dataset RTTQA(Rail Transit Table Question Answering Dataset) in the rail transit domain, the solution achieves deep integration of domain-specific knowledge. Experiments on the HybridQA, TATQA, and RTTQA datasets demonstrate that the proposed 32B/72B models achieve significant improvements over generalpurpose large language models in Exact Match (EM) and F1 score metrics. Notably, the EM score on the RTTQA dataset in the rail transit domain reached 79.91, validating the method's superiority in complex business scenarios. This study provides an innovative solution for intelligent question-answering systems in the smart transportation domain, offering significant engineering application value.

title: CardioFusion: An Ensemble Machine Learning Approach to Cardiovascular Anomaly Detection
authors: Shambhavi Singh, Hari Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Electrocardiograms (ECGs) are essential for identifying cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) and abnormalities related to the circulatory system, but manual interpretation of ECG records is labor-intensive and error-prone. The existing research often relies on high-dimensional data which requires significant computational resources. The proposed framework, CardioFusion, provides a revolutionary approach to solve these challenges by combining medical imaging, artificial intelligence, and signal analysis for signal digitization. The core of the paper involves exploring and implementing data pre-processing and signal extraction techniques to convert ECG images into 1D signals, allowing for the rapid and accurate automatic diagnosis of cardiovascular abnormalities. CardioFusion aims to reduce medical workload, improve patient outcomes, and minimize diagnostic delays by automating ECG interpretation.

title: Fault-Tolerant Sliding Mode Leader-Follower Strategy for Multiple Charging of Electric Vehicles
authors: Desh Deepak Sharma, Jeremy Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates a reliable and efficient charging infrastructure. However, charging systems are susceptible to faults caused by power fluctuations, grid instability, cyber threats, and equipment failures. This paper explores fault-tolerant control (FTC) strategies for EV charging, focusing on adaptive fault detection, resilient leader-follower control, and decentralized architectures. This paper proposes a Fault-Tolerant Sliding Mode Leader-Follower strategy for multiple EV Charging. For obtaining the robustness in multiple charging of electric vehicles, the sliding mode control laws for leader and follower EVs incorporate sliding mode gain for the leader, reaching law gain, and the signum function. The reduction in charging power and overcharging faults are considered in multiple charging of EVs. The efficacy of the proposed strategy is tested with simulation work carried out using python software.

title: Transforming Guest Experiences in the Hotel Industry: Harnessing Generative AI for Personalized and Seamless Hospitality Solutions
authors: Soumit Roy, Rajarshi Roy, Mayank Agrawal, Jaya H. Dewan, Pallavi S. Kulkarni
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Generative AI is making hospitality frictionless by changing how hotels tailor and optimize guests' experiences. This article will address a new approach to bettering service customization through methods under the umbrella of Generative AI, which in turn strengthens operational efficiency and customer happiness. Each guest's preferences can be dynamically set, service requests can be automated, and real-time needs can be anticipated by employing machine learning algorithms and natural language processing. The ability to process large amounts of consumer data and render useful insights is the premise on which mathematical models such as customization frameworks and recommendation algorithms are developed and justified. This demonstrates how a number of use cases, from chatbots to tailored room configurations, can enhance guest experiences by examining them. After AI-driven solutions were implemented, operational KPIs and customer satisfaction both increased statistically significantly. This research illustrates the way Generative AI can transform the hotel sector by opening the door for more effective and adaptable service delivery.

title: Task-Aware Remote Sensing Image Semantic Segmentation via Multi-Layer Semantic Fields and Adaptive Decoding
authors: Tengfei Sun, Shixing Zhang, Peng Bei
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Remote sensing image segmentation can be vital for a variety of tasks including rescue, supply delivery, and military operations. Yet existing methods remain task-agnostic, focusing mainly on improving pixel-level accuracy while ignoring further functional and risk semantics, thus failing to produce missionsensitive outputs. We propose a task-adaptive framework that extends RemoteSAM with a lightweight Semantic Field Head (SF-Head) and a Task-Adaptive Decoder (TAD). The SF-Head introduces geometry, functionality, and risk layers, while a CLIPbased text encoder embeds mission-level descriptions into task vectors. TAD fuses task semantics with multi-layer features, enabling “same image, different tasks” segmentation. Experiments show that our method maintains competitive accuracy while uniquely generating task-aware results.

title: Deep Hybrid Networks for Early Detection of Breast Cancer from Multi-Modal Imaging Data
authors: Neha Nahariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Breast cancer remains a major global health challenge, which emphasizes the need for early and accurate diagnostic approaches. This study proposes a deep hybrid network that integrates convolutional and transformer architectures for early breast cancer detection using the MultiModel Breast Cancer MSI Dataset. The data set includes ultrasound, histopathology, and chest radiographs, which collectively capture diverse diagnostic perspectives. The proposed model employs modality-specific feature extraction followed by a late-fusion mechanism to combine spatial and contextual information from multiple modalities. The evaluation results demonstrate that the hybrid multimodal network achieves an overall precision of 94.5% and an ROC–AUC of 0.97, significantly outperforming conventional CNN and ensemble-based baselines. Furthermore, interpretability is improved using Grad-CAM visualization, which highlights clinically relevant regions corresponding to malignant features. The framework provides an interpretable, robust and scalable solution for computer-aided breast cancer diagnosis, demonstrating strong potential for integration into real-world healthcare systems to assist radiologists in early detection and decision support.

title: Efficient Sign Language Classification Utilizing Neural Networks
authors: Basel A. Dabwan, Majda O. Elbasheer, Arwa M. Eldhai, Nabila A. Saeed, Soad M. Almula, Yahya A. Ali
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The Translation System (TS) is a crucial innovation aimed at supporting people with disabilities by enhancing their ability to communicate effectively within society. As computers become increasingly embedded in everyday activities, progress in human-computer interaction has brought significant advantages to individuals who are mute or deaf. Gesture language serves as a fundamental medium for closing communication gaps faced by those with hearing impairments. Our proposed system presents an intelligent translation model capable of converting sign language gestures into written text, thereby enabling smooth interaction between individuals with speech or hearing difficulties and the wider community. The system is built on a RegNet-inspired neural architecture and trained using a comprehensive Sign Language Dataset A total of 27,455 instances are used for training, while 7,172 are allocated for testing. With an outstanding accuracy rate of 99.86%, as illustrated in the accuracy, confusion matrix, and class distribution figures, the model proves to be highly efficient and dependable in translating sign language gestures into text form.

title: An Efficient Deployment of Deep Learning LSTM Models using Docker and Kubernetes (Minikube)
authors: Kunal Rathor, Hari Singh, Prateek Thakral
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: The substantial growth in the intricacy of deep learning applications has heightened the necessity for scalable, efficient, and portable deployment environments. Conventional standalone systems frequently do not satisfy these criteria, especially for resource management, scalability, and reproducibility. This study illustrates the implementation of LSTM-based rainfall prediction models with Docker containers managed by a local Kubernetes cluster via Minikube. The experimental configuration facilitated a comparative assessment of standalone and containerized executions, emphasizing training performance, resource utilization, and deployment flexibility. The results underscore the efficacy of container orchestration in maintaining consistency across environments and facilitating model deployment and management. The paper highlights the significance of these designs in enhancing reproducible and maintainable machine learning workflows, with possible applications in cloud-native Kubernetes systems such as Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) and Amazon EKS for managing large-scale real-world applications.

title: Hybrid Knowledge Graph–Deep Learning Framework for Automated Exception Handling and Investigation in Complex Insurance Claims
authors: P.R. Sudha Rani, Keerthi Amistapuram, Vamsee Pamisetty, Sneha Singireddy, Dwaraka Nath Kummari, Goutham Kumar Sheelam
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The processing of insurance claims encounters difficulties in managing complex exceptions and fraudulent activities, frequently demanding manual investigation, which is labour-intensive and susceptible to errors. Current AI methodologies primarily concentrate on tabular data or text analysis, resulting in the absence of semantic context and interpretability. This study presents a hybrid system combining knowledge graphs and deep learning, which merges structured tabular data, textual adjuster notes, and knowledge graph embeddings to autonomously discover, classify, and analyse exception claims. The system develops a domain-specific knowledge graph, extracts multi-modal characteristics, and utilises transformer-based text encoders and graph neural networks to forecast exception types, risk severity, and root causes. A module for explainable reasoning produces actionable investigation reports based on subgraphs. Experimental evaluation on synthetic and real-world Kaggle datasets reveals exceptional performance: accuracy 0.94, recall 0.92, F1-score 0.93, ROC-AUC 0.96, RMSE 0.08, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.91, and explanation fidelity 0.92. Comparisons with baseline models, such as XGBoost, BERT+MLP, and GraphSAGE, demonstrate substantial enhancements across all measures. The hybrid approach delivers superior accuracy, interpretability, and efficiency, surpassing current methodologies by combining multi-modal inputs with semantic reasoning for automated exception management in insurance workflows.

title: A Comparative Study of YOLO and Deep Neural Network Models for Vehicle Number Plate Detection
authors: Ebai Geslen Muwa, Vaneet Kumar, Ruchika Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Due to the increasing number of commercial vehicles, fluctuating weather conditions, and long-existing cars with extremely noisy back-grounds, Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) is crucial in improving traffic management, optimizing transportation networks, and enhancing security zone access control. The accuracy of different license plate detection models trained on diverse datasets was analysed, compared, and evaluated to propose an improved system for the automatic license plate detection and recognition of characters. The objective was to achieve state-of-the-art license plate detection and character recognition accuracy, particularly in challenging scenarios. Several deep learning models were explicitly trained on manually collected and annotated datasets under various environmental conditions. It was shown that YOLOv5 (You Only Look Once model) outperformed other models, achieving a consistent average of 99.69% for detection and 98.37% for recognition across multiple conditions, including rainy, cloudy, and nighttime scenarios.

title: Incentive Mechanism-Based Coordinated Jamming Channel and Power Joint Decision-Making Method
authors: Xing He, Zhibin Feng, Yuping Gong, Kexin Liang, Hao Han, Wenqi Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In wireless communication confrontation under complex electromagnetic environments, users tend to be clustered, frequency pattern being unpredictable and the jamming energy of multi-jammer is limited. These makes it difficult for traditional jamming methods to achieve ideal results. Therefore, this paper proposed an Incentive Mechanism-based Coordinated Jamming Channel and Power Joint DecisionMaking Method for energy-constrained multi-jammer. Firstly, the method introduced an incentive mechanism to encourage jammers to jam as many users as possible while maintaining the jamming effectiveness. Secondly, jammers employed the Markov Decision Process (MDP) framework and Q-learning algorithm to select optimal jamming strategies based on Boltzmann probability. Finally, the simulation results showed that the proposed algorithm can effectively resolve the joint decisionmaking problem of multi-jammer under energy constraints, and compared with the jamming algorithm without incentive mechanism, it also improves the success rate of jamming.

title: Smart Community Health Monitoring and Early Warning System for Water-Borne Diseases in Rural
authors: Preethi P, Sri Vishruthi C, Revathi R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Water-borne diseases continue to be a major concern in rural regions, primarily due to inadequate monitoring of water quality and delayed identification of outbreaks. Recognizing the recurring nature of such diseases emphasizes the importance of proactive health surveillance in these communities. This study introduces an Intelligent Community Health Monitoring and Early Warning Framework that integrates Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks (ST-GNN) with advanced Boosting algorithms, including XGBoost and LightGBM. By analyzing both patient health records and environmental factors, the proposed model aims to predict potential outbreaks, thereby connecting data- driven research with practical public health applications. By integrating rural health surveillance, anomaly detection, and patient data prediction, the proposed framework can generate early warnings, allowing healthcare professionals to intervene before diseases become widespread. This proactive approach addresses a critical gap in low-resource settings. Ultimately, the aim of this system is to improve community health monitoring, reduce response times, and support health authorities in making data-driven decisions in resource-limited regions. From an academic perspective, it demonstrates how data-science techniques can be effectively applied to enhance the quality of life in rural communities. In addition to conventional monitoring approaches, the proposed framework integrates a dynamic AI-driven pipeline capable of adapting to evolving disease patterns over time. The Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (ST-GNN) component captures intricate spatial relationships among rural wards, while ensemble models such as XGBoost and LightGBM strengthen the temporal analysis, enabling accurate outbreak predictions. Incorporating real-time patient and environmental data enhances the system's responsiveness and interpretability. Experimentally, the model achieved an F1-score of 0.71 to 0.79 and a sensitivity of 0.69, demonstrating its effectiveness for real-world health surveillance in rural settings. This framework exemplifies how AI-based hybrid systems can transition from experimental research to practical early-warning applications in resource-limited regions

title: Hybrid Transformer–Federated Learning Model for Secure Release Engineering in Global Payment Networks
authors: K. Reddy Madhavi, Vijaya Rama Raju Gottimukkala, Lahari Pandiri, Harish Kumar Sriram, Murali Malempati, Balaji Adusupalli
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Global payment networks encounter significant difficulties in administering safe software releases owing to diverse systems, sensitive client information, and escalating deployment difficulty. Conventional centralised learning methods pose privacy risks and do not effectively identify cross-silo patterns. This study introduces a Hybrid Transformer– Federated Learning model for secure release engineering, utilising Transformer encoders to extract multi-modal features from CI/CD logs, release notes, and transaction monitoring, while ensuring data privacy through federated optimisation, secure collection, and differential privacy. Solid methods, such as Krum and reduced mean aggregation, counteract hostile client attacks and abnormal updates. Experimental assessment utilising datasets from Kaggle reveals exceptional performance with an Accuracy of 0.95, Accuracy of 0.93, Recall of 0.95, F1-score of 0.94, and AUC of 0.96, surpassing baseline models such as Random Forest, LSTM, and FedAvg Transformer. The proposed architecture facilitates cross-silo knowledge sharing, secure risk prediction for releases, and anomaly detection, all while preserving client data security. The model surpasses existing methods in predicting accuracy, robustness, and privacy protection, providing practical utility in the deployment pipelines of global payment networks. This study establishes a basis for privacy-conscious, scalable, and dependable release engineering systems within distributed financial contexts.

title: Research on Carbon Emission Allocation Methods in the Production Stage of Electrical Smart Meters
authors: Jun Yi, Bo Miao, Jie Wen, Haotian Zhang, Wanshui Yu, Ying Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Carbon footprint of products (CFP) accounting needs reasonable energy allocation, but existing studies lack methods for smart electricity meter production. This study proposed four co-produced product carbon allocation methods for power equipment, then applied three (output, mass, output value) to calculate production-stage carbon emissions of a DDZY242-Z Class A smart meter. Results showed its emissions were 0.43,0.26,0.25 kgCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> e via the three methods, with mass and output value results closer to the industry average (0.28 kgCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> e). Conclusion: Mass allocation is preferred, providing a reference for power equipment carbon accounting; future research will expand case studies.

title: Bayesian Belief Networks for Predicting Ecosystem Collapse Using Climate and Satellite Data
authors: Hrithvi D Salian
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Ecosystem collapse is an increasingly imminent global threat driven by accelerated climate change and environmental degradation. This study presents a data-driven framework using Bayesian Belief etworks (BB s) to predict ecological collapse by modeling the probabilistic relationships among critical environmental stressors. Real-world datasets from ASA's MODIS satellites—including DVI and land surface temperature (LST)—alongside IPCC CMIP6 climate model outputs are utilized to assess changes in vegetation cover, rainfall variability, and temperature anomalies. The BB structure integrates historical ecological data to infer collapse probabilities under various climate scenarios, particularly in vulnerable biomes such as the Amazon. Results reveal strong correlations between multi-year DVI degradation and extreme temperature and precipitation shifts, aligning with documented collapse events. The probabilistic nature of BB s allows for the quantification of uncertainty and enhances interpretability, making them a robust tool for risk assessment. This approach supports proactive decision-making for conservation planning, ecosystem resilience analysis, and policy design. By integrating climate models with remote sensing indicators, the framework facilitates timely identification of high-risk areas and provides a scalable methodology for global ecological monitoring. (Abstract)

title: Embedded Solar Energy Monitoring System Using Arduino for Stand-Alone Photovoltaic Applications
authors: Sugandan N, Jayaprakash D, Kannan R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: With the embedded energy monitoring system, recent developments on solar applications are aimed at making systems adequate in managing and analysing both the generation and consumption of power in stand-alone systems. The system combines photovoltaic energy to a DC-DC converter to provide regulated power flow, which in turn allows to charge and discharge a battery attached to the system. There are actual loads of various types like a fan and a DC motor, which are used to show real-time use of the stored solar energy. Current sensors and voltage sensors are used to measure input and output parameters so that the performance of the system can be properly tracked. An Arduino microcontroller receives the data sensor and manages the flow of power and displays the results on an LCD screen, where the user can interact with the results. This is a cost-effective, trusted, and scalable method of using solar energy in rural and urban settings that can allow maximum use of renewable energy and facilitate sustainable development.

title: Alt-Eye: A Smart Vision Assistance Device for the Visually Impaired Using Deep Learning
authors: K. Kartheeban, Thummala Nithin Reddy, Pamulapati Pooja Sri, Enjirapu Yuva Sekhar Nikhil Eswara Achari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper introduces Alt-Eye, a wearable smart vision assistance system that enables visually impaired individuals to recognize objects in real time, perform optical character recognition, detect currency, and understand contextual scenes. The system comprises a Raspberry Pi Zero 2W interfaced with a Pi Camera V1 (OV5647) mounted on custom 3D-printed spectacle frames, connected to Bluetooth earphones for audio feedback. Alt-Eye adopts an edge-cloud hybrid architecture: while images are captured at the edge device, inference is executed on the cloud infrastructure provided by Microsoft Azure running a deep learning multimodal fusion pipeline that merges the output of a visionlanguage model with large language model reasoning. The system has an end-to-end latency of 2.5-3 seconds and is able to identify objects, extract text from product labels including MRP and expiry dates, recognize Indian currency denominations, and generate human-understandable descriptions using speech synthesis. Power is supplied via a custom battery management system using a 3.7 V 1000 mAh lithium polymer battery integrated with a charging and 5 V boost circuit that enables portable operation for 8-10 hours. Experimental validation is performed by achieving reliable detection accuracy across common indoor scenarios and product recognition tasks and represents a significant advancement in assistive technology by delivering context-aware, multimodal intelligence in a lightweight, ergonomic wearable form factor.

title: A Zero-Knowledge-Based Approach to Resist Poisoning Attacks in Federated Learning
authors: Juhao Wang, Xiaosong Guan, Changxin Gao, Shijuan Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) network, numerous vehicle terminals are required to continuously upload local data to maintain the latest service models, which supports intelligent transportation and personalized services. However, the privacy risks posed by this continuous data uploading cannot be ignored. Federated learning, as a distributed ma-chine learning paradigm, enables global model training without sharing original data. The introduction of blockchain further supports decentralization and immutability. However, federated learning also faces the risk of poisoning attacks, where malicious clients may upload abnormal or tampered model updates, severely impacting global model performance. To address this, this paper proposes a security framework that combines zero-knowledge proofs, federated learning, and blockchain. Clients use zero-knowledge proofs to ensure the legitimacy of uploaded updates, while the blockchain is responsible for verification and storage. Ultimately, a robust global model is obtained through federated aggregation. Experimental results demonstrate that this scheme effectively resists poisoning attacks, significantly improving system security and reliability while protecting user privacy.

title: Research on a Large Model for Children's Multimodal Emotion Recognition Based on Multi-Feature Cross-Fusion
authors: Rui Zhao, Zhiyan Liu, Shuai Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To accurately identify children's emotional tendencies in multimodal data from daily and educational scenarios (supporting psychological needs understanding, educational strategy optimization, and mental health interventions), this study proposes a multi-feature cross-fusion-based children's multimodal emotion recognition model (BCVAFC) to solve existing issues of fragmented feature extraction and insufficient inter-modal fusion. The model enhances features by introducing CLIP alongside BERT (text) and ViT (image), mapping text (e.g., speech-to-text) and image (e.g., facial frames) to a shared semantic space via contrastive learning; it then captures inter-modal dependencies through a text-image dual-guided cross-attention mechanism fused with self-attention and Fourier convolution, and achieves deep fusion of low-level and high-level features via global attention and residual connections to boost recognition accuracy. Experiments on children's emotion datasets show BCVAFC outperforms traditional multimodal models in recognizing emotions like joy, anxiety, and grievance, with accuracy converging stably to 92.3% and good robustness; adapted to children's data characteristics, it can be expanded with more modalities (e.g., body movements, heart rate) for wider applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of Vision Transformer, DenseNet201, and InceptionResNetV2 for Accurate Skin Lesion Recognition
authors: Nandini MS, J.R. Arunkumar, Prakash Dhanavelu, Durairaj. M
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: It is only possible to find melanoma and other skin diseases early by carefully dividing and classifying skin lesions. This project aims to compare three advanced deep learning architectures for autonomous skin lesion detection based on dermoscopy: Vision Transformer (ViT), DenseNet201, and InceptionResNetV2. We used softmax entropy to see how uncertain the model was. ViT has the least amount of entropy (0.5530 nats), which means that its predictions are more accurate than those of DenseNet201 (0.6781 nats) and InceptionResNetV2 (0.6902 nats). We used standard measures like Intersection over Union (IoU), Pixel Accuracy (PA), Sensitivity, Specificity, and Precision to see how well the segmentation worked. The segmentation was rather well, with an IoU of 0.5084 and a pixel accuracy of 0.6355. In medical diagnostics, it is very important to be able to find lesions accurately, with a sensitivity of 0.7643, to avoid false negatives. The specificity of 0.5102 means that there are a lot of false positives and that the data is likely to be over-segmented. The precision of 0.6029 shows that the estimated lesion locations are somewhat reliable. The framework consistently identifies lesions; however, the findings suggest that enhancements in segmentation accuracy and background differentiation are necessary to elevate diagnostic precision.

title: ChunkGraph: Relationship-Driven Retrieval Through Progressive Complete Graphs
authors: Jiale Zhang, Kui Zhao, Hao Zhang, Fuzhe Zhang, Xu Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems focus on similarity-based retrieval, often failing to capture the relationships necessary for complex reasoning. We introduce ChunkGraph, a relationship-driven retrieval method that constructs progressive complete graphs from document chunks. Our approach progressively refines retrieval through three stages: (1) complete graph initialization where every chunk pair has potential relationships, (2) adaptive relationship discovery using BiModal distribution analysis, and (3) progressive pruning via Personalized PageRank to focus on the most relevant relationship paths. Unlike entity-based knowledge graphs, ChunkGraph treats chunks as nodes in a complete graph structure, enabling comprehensive relationship modeling without expensive entity extraction. Evaluation on HotpotQA and Quality datasets demonstrates that relationship-driven retrieval achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1=0.71$</tex>, matching state-of-the-art methods while providing superior computational efficiency compared to entity-based graph methods <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(60 \%)$</tex> faster indexing time). ChunkGraph proves that progressive complete graphs can effectively bridge the gap between similarity and relevance in retrieval tasks.

title: A Novel Constraint-Aware Fixed Time Stabilization Technique for Micro Grid Integrated Multi-Area Network
authors: Anamika Talukdar, Tapan Kumar Maiti
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This paper emphasizes on the mathematical analysis of load frequency control (LFC) of two interconnected areas where one is thermal area and another one is a micro grid. The modeling of the whole system is done with the help of state-space form with consideration of non-linearity such as governor dead-band, saturation and generation rate constraint. The proposed idea is novel in case of designing the sliding surface considering area control error (ACE) and governor command. The design of the new controller- a fixed-time integral sliding mode control (FTI-SMC) and the Lyapunov stability analysis, both make sure to converge the ACE to zero within a bounded time that is independent of initial conditions. The approach of this paper is to fill the gap present in the existing simulated versions by explaining the theoretical and mathematical solutions in case of novel designing of controllers as well as their stability analysis.

title: Dynamic Time-Stamped Network for Optimizing Project Approval Workflows
authors: Fan Shuxian, Wang Hao, Du Bin, Yuan Xiaoyong, Zhang Sisi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In the context of digital transformation driven by information technology, traditional project approval processes often fail to meet the efficiency demands of modern enterprises due to complex procedures, poor information flow, and excessive manual intervention. Existing studies show limitations in identifying critical nodes and coordinating resource and time optimization, and they lack effective mechanisms for dynamic optimization under uncertainty. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive optimization method for project approval processes based on critical node identification and resource optimization. The approval process is modeled as a directed acyclic graph (DAG) through the construction of a timelabeled network data structure. Forward and backward time calculations combined with float time analysis are applied to identify critical paths and nodes. A multi-dimensional evaluation model is developed to assess node importance for accurate critical node detection. Furthermore, a multi-level resource management strategy is designed to balance workloads by adjusting non-critical activities and optimizing resource utilization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively identifies critical paths and nodes, achieving a 10.1% reduction in total project duration and a 15.6% improvement in resource utilization, showing strong applicability in practical engineering project management.

title: Mitigating Congestion with Physics-Informed Neural Networks and Adaptive Traffic Signal Control
authors: Ntemi Masanja, Abdul Ngereza, Tumaini Sakaza, Philip Balyagati, Emmanuel Kidando
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Traffic congestion and delays at signalized intersections remain a challenge in urban mobility. To minimize congestion, adaptive traffic signal control systems have been adopted across the United States. However, for these systems to function at their best, systems require an accurate estimation of traffic states, such as traffic density and queue length. This paper implements a relatively novel method, Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs), for real-time traffic density estimation to enhance adaptive intersection signal control. The methodology leverages PINNs, which integrate both data-driven learning and physical constraints imposed by the Lighthill-Whitham-Richards (LWR) macroscopic traffic flow model. A simulation of a four-way intersection was used to generate realistic spatio-temporal traffic data to evaluate the performance of the model. Separate PINNs models, one trained for each incoming approach using simulation-based spatio-temporal density data, provide segment-level density predictions. These real-time density estimates are then employed within an adaptive traffic signal control algorithm designed to adjust green light phasing based on detected demand. The proposed PINNs control strategy was evaluated through simulation, comparing its performance against the Webster traffic timing method using key metrics such as average vehicle delay at the intersection and average vehicle travel time. The findings show an approximate 18% decrease in average vehicle delay and more than a 6% reduction in average travel time when utilizing adaptive traffic control based on the PINNs method for determining density values. These findings highlight the potential of leveraging PINNs for accurate, real-time traffic state estimation, which can effectively inform and improve the performance of adaptive traffic signal control systems, leading to reduced congestion and enhanced mobility at intersections.

title: Ghostden: Hybrid Framework for ECG Image Based Heart Disease Classification
authors: S. Hemalatha, D. Sivabalaselvamani, T. Kavitha, K. Kaliswary, V. Dhanush, J. Jaisapthagiri
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Heart disease is one of the major causes of death around the world today. Detecting heart disease early through electrocardiogram (ECG) analysis can significantly improve chances for timely medical treatment. This paper introduces GhostDen, a deep-learning-based framework that classifies ECG images into four main classes: Normal, Abnormal, History of Myocardial Infarction (MI) and Myocardial Infarction. The presented system employs GhostNet as a lightweight and efficient base for feature extraction. To cause the model to focus on critical patterns shown in ECG images, two attention mechanisms, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) and Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), are integrated into the architecture. Subsequently, dense layers and softmax activation are used to accomplish the classification. Experiment-based results show the SE model achieves a classification accuracy of 97.00 %, with a recall of 96.64 % and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 96.39 %. By comparison, the CBAM model has a reported accuracy of 95.34 %. These results indicate the successful integration of attention modules with GhostNet to support accuracy in diagnostic prediction; SE performs best with ECG image-based heart disease classification and prediction.

title: Neuro-Symbolic Dynamic Pricing for Intent-Driven Sustainable UAV Swarm Task Allocation
authors: Jianrui Fan, Lei Lei, Shengsuo Cai, Pan Cao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: We present a hierarchical Neuro-Symbolic Intent-Driven Networking (NS-IDN) framework for sustainable cooperative task allocation in UAV swarms. Our approach innovatively bridges the intent-to-action gap via a three-layered architecture: an LLM-based translator (L1) converts natural-language commands into structured tasks; a reinforcement learning (RL) commander (L2) optimizes the meta-parameters of a hybrid dynamic pricing formula, broadcasting strategic economic incentives rather than low-level actions; and a lightweight Gossip-Intent-Response (GIR) protocol (L3) drives distributed execution and incorporates a decentralized self-preservation logic for autonomous energy management. The L2 commander's policy is learned using a shared Transformer backbone to process variable-length state inputs, enabling sophisticated, contextaware decision-making. Simulation experiments reveal NS-IDN's fundamental advantages over state-of-the-art baselines. While market-based methods like CBBA suffer a performance collapse in large swarms due to quadratic communication explosion, and end-to-end MARL agents like MAPPO incur prohibitive bandwidth costs due to continuous vector exchange, NS-IDN maintains a linear overhead and sustains a near-perfect Task Completion Rate (TCR) of 0.99. Furthermore, unlike MAPPO which fails to generalize to novel intents, NS-IDN demonstrates instantaneous adaptability, re-tasking agents in under a second in response to novel, mid-mission intents. These advances, coupled with its modular design, establish NS-IDN as a robust and practical framework for scalable intent-driven swarm intelligence.

title: Dual-Attention Enhanced Diffusion-Based Medical Image Diagnosis
authors: Hang Zhou, Tao Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Medical image classification plays a crucial role in clinical diagnostic support, yet this task remains fraught with challenges. These include domain shifts arising from variations in imaging equipment, feature instability caused by noise and artifact interference, as well as issues such as variable lesion sizes, blurred boundaries, and uneven distribution. Recent novel approaches based on diffusion models have encountered certain issues, such as the lack of interconnectivity between extracted lesion regions. This paper proposes a novel framework named GLADiff based on diffusion models, aiming to address general medical image classification problems by eliminating unexpected noise and perturbations in medical images while robustly capturing semantic representations. We designed a novel Global-Local Attention Guidance (GLAG) strategy to finely regulate each diffusion step from two perspectives, thereby enhancing progressive regional attention. The global stream incorporates a channel attention mechanism, while the local stream innovatively introduces a Bi-Level Routing Attention (BRA) and Generalized Pooling Operator (GPO). This enables precise targeting of lesion regions while suppressing background noise. We evaluated the effectiveness of GLADiff on two medical classification tasks involving different image modalities: skin lesion classification using dermatoscopic images, and diabetic retinopathy grading using fundus images. Our experimental results demonstrate that GLADiff achieves highly competitive results across all tasks through its unique guidance mechanism, showcasing the proposed model's efficacy.

title: Smart Lung Health Analyzer: A Sensor-Based Device for Early Detection of Respiratory Diseases
authors: Kiruthika B, Manikandan L, Birundha Devi P, Jaisiva S, Prema S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Early detection and monitoring of respiratory and physiological illness are instrumental in enhancing the outcomes of patients and alleviating healthcare loads. This study presents an IoT-based health monitoring system that incorporates several biomedical and environmental sensors such as ESP8266, Flow Sensor, DHT11, MAX30100, PPG Sensor, pH Sensor, and MQ135. The system gathers real-time information on lung airflow, body temperature, heart rate, oxygen in blood, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> level, and parameters on air quality. The sensor values are sent through the ESP8266 to a computing system, where complex machine learning algorithms analyze and process the data. The main purpose is to detect and classify health conditions like pneumonia, tuberculosis, asthma, and Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), as well as detecting normal health conditions. Using sensor fusion and smart data processing, the presented system presents an inexpensive, non-invasive, and scalable method for disease detection and continuous health monitoring. This methodology not only increases diagnostic precision but also lays the groundwork for distant care applications, facilitating timely intervention and better patient care.

title: Object Recognition Method in Silicone-Encapsulated Structures Based on Tactile PSS-NET
authors: Xiaolong Guo, Yongkang Kong, Fubang Zhao, Tian Tang, Dapeng Wei
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: As progress is made in materials science and engineering, the significance of recognizing embedded objects within materials is on the increase. In various sectors, including manufacturing, medical diagnostics, and safety inspections, the precise detection and identification of embedded objects inside materials are crucial for ensuring product quality, enhancing diagnostic precision, and safeguard safety. Prevailing recognition techniques predominantly focus on classifying one-dimensional time-series signals, often neglecting the spatial features of these signals and the orientational attributes of embedded objects. This research introduces a novel approach to embedded model identification, which is based on the processing of time-series pressure data. Furthermore, a Convolutional Neural Networks named Pressure Signal Shape Net(PSS-Net), with the capacity to recognize spatial features and perform feature fusion, is devised to capture the rotational changes of the embedded model. The method attained a classification accuracy as high as 97.11% when applied to a time - series current signal dataset that was generated by pressing a silicone cube with nine embedded Arabic numerals.

title: Discovering Top 10 Risk Factors for Type 2 Diabetes through HNB and AODE with Methodological Insights
authors: Chavali Raja Sricharan, Jadi Meghana, Rishika Thakur, Yogash Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: T2DM is one of the most urgent health conditions on the planet that should be diagnosed earlier and analyzed to take the necessary measures. Based on an enormous publicly available health (Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System; n 2,53,680) dataset, we identify the top-10 risk factors of T2DM and determine the approximate explainable performance of the probabilities-based classifiers. Specifically, we compare and minimise variance respectively Hidden naivete bayes (HNB) the two variants of the classical Naivete Bayes model to learn conditional dependencies and minimise variance respectively. The SHAP values are ranked by the importance of the feature and the top ten risk factors of HighBP and GenHlth, HighChol, BMI, Age, DiffWalk, HeartDiseaseorAttack, PhysHlth, stroke and MentHlth. It provides experimental findings on stratified samples of the data, that AODE and ROC-AUC are 85% and 0.88 accurate relative to HNB (79% and 0.83). That positive case recall on default thresholds is not as good as positive case recall on non-default thresholds, and is subject to both threshold adjustments and class-weight adjustments. The evidence confirms the study hypothesis that AODE is a good interpretable model and risk factors modeled by AODE are consistent with the clinical data. Along with the methodological requirements according to which dependency-based probabilistic classifier must be applied in clustering diabetes, we also obtain in the paper the tradeoff between classification and explainability.

title: Practice of Blockchain Technology in Trusted Notarization for Emerging Contaminants Monitoring Data
authors: Shuang Shen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Emerging contaminants monitoring data face challenges such as susceptibility to tampering, difficulty in traceability, and insufficient mutual trust among multiple parties during collection, transmission, and storage. To address these issues, this study proposes and implements a blockchain-based trusted data notarization solution. A distributed notarization framework is designed and built using Hyperledger Fabric. A collaborative notarization model is then constructed to optimize on-chain storage load. Building on the conventional Kafka consensus, a voting verification mechanism weighted by monitoring task types and node reputation is introduced. Finally, core smart contracts are developed. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves 100% tamper detection accuracy in 1,350 simulated attacks, 95% of cross-institutional queries (5,000 tests) are responded to within 3.17 seconds, under 400 concurrent loads, the dynamic consensus mechanism achieves 205 TPS with an average latency below 2.5 seconds. This study confirms the solution's effectiveness in resolving trusted notarization challenges for emerging contaminants monitoring data, providing innovative technical support for transparent and reliable environmental data management systems.

title: An Integrated Learning Framework for Hypertension Risk Prediction Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Jiasuo Xu, Siwei Liao, Shuxing Luo, Xipei Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In this study, a high-precision classification prediction framework fusing Random Forest algorithm and XGBoost algorithm is constructed. First, four single models, including logistic regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree and Random Forest, were constructed and evaluated, and the evaluation results showed that logistic regression, SVM and Random Forest performed better in terms of interpretability and accuracy, while the Decision Tree was relatively poor; second, in order to break through the bottleneck of the performance of a single model, Random Forest was fused with XGBoost to construct a stacked integrated model, which adopts a two-layer architecture. Secondly, in order to break the performance bottleneck of a single model, Random Forest and XGBoost are fused together to construct a stacked integrated model, which adopts a two-layer architecture: the first layer trains the two base models independently and records the prediction probabilities, and the second layer splices these prediction probabilities with the original features to train the meta-model, which gives full play to the complementary advantages of the two features - Random Forest is based on the idea of Bagging, which can This architecture gives full play to the complementary advantages of the two features - Random Forest is based on the idea of Bagging, which can enhance the generalisation ability of the model and reduce the variance, and XGBoost is based on the idea of Boosting, which can effectively reduce the bias and deal with complex relationships. Through this stacking integration strategy, the models are deeply integrated to form a high-precision classification prediction system. Through multi-level model construction and integration optimisation, the framework significantly improves the accuracy, stability and generalisation ability of classification prediction, and shows excellent performance in classification prediction tasks, providing innovative prediction methods and technical paths for related fields.

title: Deep Learning-Based Automated Identification of Date Fruit Varieties Using CNNs
authors: Raghavendra Sridhar, Jhulan Kumar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Premlata Chopra, Gaurav Sharma, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: The classification of dates is a way of approaching these three facets of agriculture: Research, Quality Control, and Market Distribution. This paper presents a possible Convolution Neural Networks (CNN) application to classify 9 different types of date fruits (Ajwah, Galaxy, Medjool, Menafi, Nabtat Ali, Rutab, Shaishah, Sokari, and Sugaey). Deep learning provides the ability to utilize CNNs accurately for this type of work. The accuracy achieved by this model illustrates the future of sustainable agriculture, food security, and responsible production and consumption with deep learning technologies. CNN performance is evaluated via Accuracy, Recall, F1 score, and Confusion Matrix; results show excellent methods of classification with low misclassifications and high classification capability. Training and validation loss analyses reflect adequate learning and balanced performance, while the confusion matrix reveals areas for improvement. Another purpose of this research is to illustrate the role of CNN models in improving resource use efficiency, developing climate-smart agricultural practices, and boosting economies by providing enhanced quality control of agriculture and market analysis for sustainable development.

title: Advancement in Supply Chains using Blockchain in Tracking Goods and Transactions
authors: Saurav Singh, Rajbala Simon, Kamini Sharma
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Supply chains means to transfer the goods from one place to another place and the transaction done from one end to another. This process needs to be more secure and transparent owner and client side. The proposed system component-based architecture comprises the following modules: Product Registration, Order Management, Shipment Tracking, and Payment Processing. All of them are based on smart contracts that streamline validation, authorization, and transaction compliance processes. Chain code that serves as smart contracts is programmed in Go, implemented and deployed on a permissioned blockchain network to facilitate data integrity and process automation across all parties. The framework is prototyped in a simulated environment through Dockers containers and its performance in terms of latency and data immutability across multiple nodes is measured. Experimental results show that the traceability is enhanced, the process latency is reduced and the resistance against tampering is increased. This study provides an efficient and scalable architecture for blockchain-based supply chain management, Upgrades will add AI-based predictive analytics and dynamic

title: CAGNet - Comprehensive Node-Labeled Graph Analysis of Mobile Malware Using Force Atlas 2 and Deep Learning
authors: Nur Khairani Kamarudin, Ahmad Firdaus, Mohd Faris Mohd Fuzi, Nurul Hidayah Ahmad Zukri, Mohammad Hafiz bin Ismail, Muhamad Arif Hashim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Mobile malware continues to pose a significant threat as the use of Android-based devices expands across personal, financial, and professional domains. The constant evolution of malware techniques makes early and accurate detection a critical requirement to safeguard users. Traditional detection approaches, which rely mainly on static statistical features, often fail to capture deeper semantic patterns of malicious behavior. In this study, we propose CAGNet, a graphbased detection framework that utilizes fully labeled call graphs to preserve detailed semantic and structural information of applications. By applying the Force Atlas 2 layout to model graph relationships and employing a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) as the detection engine, CAGNet effectively leverages node-level information for robust analysis. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that this approach achieves a highest detection accuracy of 95.89 %, surpassing conventional static analysis methods and highlighting the advantage of full node labeling in mobile malware detection.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Edge Data Caching with Robust Optimization and Approximate Linear Models
authors: P Srinivasarao, Vamsi Kumar Arava, V. Margasahachary, J. Syam Babu, Ch Mandakini, Swapna Raghunath
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Edge computing has become essential for supporting low-latency applications by storing popular data closer to users. However, conventional edge data caching techniques often assume predictable demand patterns and overlook real-world uncertainties such as user mobility, abrupt shifts in content popularity, and edge server failures. These assumptions limit their practical effectiveness, especially in dynamic environments where user behavior and system conditions fluctuate unpredictably. In this work, we present an uncertainty-aware edge data caching framework that explicitly accounts for both data popularity variations and potential failures in edge servers. The proposed methodology formulates the caching problem as a two-stage robust optimization task, ensuring resilient performance even under volatile network conditions. To address computational challenges in large-scale scenarios, we further introduce an approximate solution, uEDC-L, based on linear decision rules, significantly reducing overhead while maintaining strong performance. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate that our methods outperform established caching strategies, including MagNet and ARO. Notably, the optimal algorithm uEDC-B reduces data retrieval latency by up to 68.4% and lowers storage costs by over 50%, compared to baseline approaches. The approximate method uEDC-L achieves comparable performance with much faster computation, making it a practical choice for real-world deployments. The results confirm that incorporating uncertainty handling into edge caching design can significantly improve both efficiency and system robustness. These contributions advance the reliability of mobile edge computing, especially for applications demanding high data availability under dynamic conditions.

title: FPGA-Based Visual Reminder System for Dementia Care
authors: Divya Vani Gaddam, Nikhil Varma, Sai Anirudh V, Shafiya Tahreen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: In today’s world the Dementia cases are increasing primarily due to the growing global population of elderly people. This highlights the importance of interactive reminder systems, by which older people or anyone with dementia can manage their day-to-day tasks independent of others. Existing systems like mobile notifications or software based are lacking visually engaging features which leaves a major gap in user-friendly and hardware related notifications. This paper presents Visual Reminder System which is implemented on FPGA for time-to-time hardware-based reminders. The objective of our work is to develop a reliable time-controlled logic for reminder schedules, by using a 3*3*3 LED cube as visual alert and some on board LED’s for specifying the tasks. This system is developed using Verilog HDL in VIVADO XILINX and implemented on Nexys 4 DDR FPGA. Results demonstrate accurate time progression, reliable reminder activation at scheduled times, and effective user acknowledgment through a stop button.

title: Fast Optical Phased Array Calibration Using a Hybrid GS-SPGD Algorithm
authors: Yanlin Chen, Hao Lou, Xiaodong Xu, Xiaowei Qin
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Optical phased arrays (OPAs) offer a solid-state beam steering solution for Light detection and ranging (LiDAR) and free-space optical (FSO) communication systems. To achieve high-precision beam control, phase calibration of each element is essential. The stochastic parallel gradient descent (SPGD) algorithm is a mainstream model-free calibration method that enables simultaneous phase calibration for all array elements. However, it typically suffers from slow convergence, particularly in large-scale arrays. To address this problem, we first propose the adaptive-weight GS-SPGD (aGS-SPGD) algorithm. This method concurrently executes both the Gerchberg-Saxton (GS) and SPGD approaches, adaptively computing weighting coefficients based on the similarity of phase updates to achieve optimized phase calibration. To further reduce computational complexity, we propose the compact GS-SPGD algorithm (cGSSPGD) that alternatively executes GS and SPGD operations, with switching moments determined by monitoring the evolution of fitness function. Simulation results show that under different array scales and phase noise conditions, the proposed algorithms can efficiently calibrate the OPA output beams. Specifically, in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> arrays, the aGS-SPGD algorithm exhibits superior convergence speed, completing calibration <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 3 \%}$</tex> faster than SPGD. The cGS-SPGD algorithm maintains comparable convergence performance while delivering a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> improvement in computational efficiency.

title: Experimental Study on System-Level Performance Impact of Read Disturbance in Modern SSDs

authors: Ali Sedaghatgoo, Reza Salkhordeh, André Brinkmann, Hossein Asadi
