More importantly, after signal separation processing, the measuring accuracy of the acceleration shock and atmospheric pressure signal is up to 93.07% and 99.5% respectively. Macroscopic equivalent experiments were further carried out to verify the feasibility of a sensing mechanism. Experimental results indicate that both acceleration shock and atmospheric pressure can be detected with high linearities of 0.9993 and 0.9755, respectively. The mechanism proposed in this work is expected to provide a solution for synchronous high-sensitivity measurement of acceleration and air pressure in high dynamic environments.

title: Origami-Based Inner Channel Securing Mechanism for Soft Growing Robots
authors: Sanghun Lee, Nam Gyun Kim, Shinwoo Park, Dae-Young Lee, Jee-Hwan Ryu
abstract: Soft growing robots possess unique advantages, such as the ability to navigate confined and complex environments. Although securing a stable inner channel is essential for enabling a wide range of applications, such as sensor integration, tool passage, and material transfer, the pressure required for eversion based growth imposes compressive loading on the channel, causing it to collapse by constriction. Existing approaches for channel stabilization rely on auxiliary actuation or complex control, which introduce leakage, deformation, or limited scalability. To over come these limitations, this paper presents an origami-inspired mechanism embedded into the robot membrane that inherently forms a structurally rigid inner channel without additional actuation, while preserving the intrinsic benefits of soft growing robots. The design further enables user-defined customization of channel geometry to meet application-specific requirements. A comprehensive modeling framework is developed to characterize the geometric and mechanical behavior of the mechanism, and its validity is confirmed through simulations and comparative experiments against conventional soft growing robots. Demonstrations, including inner channel visualization, steering, growth through confined paths, and parameter-dependent scalability, validate the practicality and versatility of the proposed approach.

title: Efficient Joint Estimation of Optical Flow and Stereo Disparity with Event Cameras
authors: Muhammad Ahmed Humais, Sajid Javed, Yahya Zweiri
abstract: Optical flow and stereo disparity, both are fundamental in the perception pipeline of robotic systems, enabling 3D understanding and motion estimation of the robot itself and the dynamic objects around. In this context, event cameras offer great potential to reduce latency and improve the efficiency of the perception pipeline. However, existing event-based approaches often deal with flow and disparity estimation tasks separately, potentially leading to redundant computations and reduced efficiency. In this work, we developed a joint flow and depth estimation network, featuring shared feature encoders for high computational efficiency. Moreover, we introduce a novel Bidirectional Mamba module to enhance feature expressiveness by increasing the spatial receptive field to capture global context with significantly lower computational overhead than vision transformers. We further improve efficiency by incorporating informed priors to reduce the number of refinement iterations in the sequential estimation task. Additionally, we extend our framework to combine the estimated flow and disparity to predict 3D scene flow, an important task in many robotics applications.

title: Google Cloud Implementation of Deep Learning Models for Crop Health Classification
authors: Tushar Mishra, Shivansh Tiwari, Sarthak Yadav, Vinod Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This work presents an integrated framework for deploying and optimizing deep learning models for crop health classification that makes use of Google Cloud's computational infrastructure. The designed platform gets its input from the high-resolution agricultural images taken by drone and IoT sensors and processes them using Google Cloud Storage and TensorFlow APIs. Various convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, namely ResNet50, InceptionV3, and EfficientNet, were implemented and trained on the Google Cloud AI Platform to determine the health condition of crops and to distinguish between healthy, stressed, and diseased categories. The models were scored based on a set of criteria including accuracy, precision, recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score, and inference latency. It was found that EfficientNet not only achieved the highest classification accuracy of 96.8% but was also able to significantly reduce the computational cost and training time. Moreover, the use of Google Cloud's scalable services made it possible to perform data handling in an efficient manner, thus allowing for model deployment to be done at a faster pace and for classification to be carried out in real-time over a large area of agricultural monitoring.

title: Sparse Error Correction for Robust State Estimation: Vulnerability and Robustification
authors: Razeel Hassan Ibrahim Rasheed, Jinsub Kim
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Robust linear state estimation in the presence of adversarial sensor data falsification is considered. In the literature, the basis pursuit approaches have been proposed to nullify the impact of the falsified measurements on the state estimate by leveraging the sparsity assumption that only a small fraction of measurements are subject to falsification. It is first demonstrated that adversaries can design a successful sparse attack, which can mislead the basis pursuit approach while requiring falsification of only a small fraction of sensor measurements. To address the discovered vulnerability, a robust sparse error correction approach is developed by integrating randomized weighting into the basis pursuit approach. The proposed approach is experimentally evaluated using random measurement matrices as well as power system DC state estimation for IEEE 14-bus test case. Experimental evaluations show that the proposed approach offers much enhanced resilience against adversarial data falsification compared to the existing approaches.

title: SyndromeCode: Feedback-Assisted Concatenated Coding based on Transformer
authors: Jonggyu Jang, Hongjae Nam, Vishrant Tripathi, David J. Love
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Deep learning-based feedback coding has demonstrated superior performance over classical linear counterparts under specific channel conditions by learning robust non-linear mappings. To achieve better reliability, these neural codes are typically deployed as inner codes within a concatenated architecture, wrapped around an outer linear error-correcting codes (ECC). However, existing data-driven architectures typically treat the feedback coding as a black-box optimization, done independently of the selection of the outer ECC. Unfortunately, this means the data-driven architecture is not optimized to exploit the algebraic structure of the outer code, resulting in an inability to consider the underlying outer code structure when they are concatenated. To bridge this gap, we propose SyndromeCode, a novel feedback coding architecture that explicitly incorporates the outer code structure via syndrome inputs. Instead of relying solely on raw received signals, SyndromeCode leverages a soft syndrome vector—a differentiable indicator of parity violations derived from the outer code structure—and feeds it directly into the Transformer-based encoder and decoder. Furthermore, we demonstrate the versatility of this approach in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) multi-stream scenarios, as well as different spatial noise correlations. Our extensive results demonstrate that SyndromeCode outperforms the baselines by validating the power of injecting domain knowledge into deep learning-based feedback coding architecture.

title: A Conditional GAN Approach for Artwork Restoration Using Paired Image-to-Image Translation
authors: N Prabhakaran, Neeraj Das, D. Jebakumar Immanuel, Rahul Thakur, Jeberson Retna Raj, Y.N. Raja
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The proposed research is a conditional Generative Adversarial Network (cGAN) based framework that restores degraded artwork images through a paired image-toimage translation manner. The extent of the proposed architecture lies in using a U-Net-based generator with two attention blocks and a channel transformer amongst skip connections to maintain local and global contextual details in addition to the excellent fusion of multiscale features. The spatial and channel attention modules enhance capability of the network in capturing inter-spatial and inter-channel relations and the channel modernizer helps to bridge the semantic gap between the encoder and decoder layers. An image authenticity discriminator is implemented in a patch wise manner. Training of the model employs a multiloss function comprising adversarial loss, Perceptual loss (Smooth L1) and SSIM loss, which allows improved perceptual superiority and structural constancy of the restored images. Subensive experiments on three benchmarking datasets namely, the 'Art images,' 'Best artwork of all time, and offline information set prove that the suggested approach produces better performance with SSIM up to 0.95, MSE up to 0.31, and PSNR up to 53.92 than the existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Autoencoder–LSTM and Graph-Theory Hybrid Framework for Secure Clustering and Lifetime Maximization in WSNs
authors: Yuvaraj Durasiamy, Rangu Shirisha, Md. Chan Basha, M. Sugumaran, A. Kaliappan, Ananthi M.
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Smart sensors that are smaller, cheaper, and more energy efficient have been made possible by the fast evolution of MEMS technology, which has greatly increased the importance of WSN. Structured as networks of interconnected nodes, these sensors track a variety of environmental variables and relay that information wirelessly to a main BS. But problems like secure clustering and limited network lifetimes still exist. Secure Clustering and Lifetime Maximisation is a suggested framework that aims to tackle these concerns. To improve the quality of sensed data, the framework uses data preparation techniques. To make clustering and routing more efficient, it uses a graph-based learning methodology. A Graph-AE-LSTM is trained with graph sequences generated using random walks, breadth-first search, and shortest path algorithms to obtain continuous vector representations of network topologies. Through the use of the Lifetime Extension Factor, these representations make it possible to aggregate data in an efficient and safe manner, all while increasing the lifetime of nodes. The Graph-AE-LSTM model accomplished a remarkable 95.31% accuracy in comparison and classification tasks, according to the experimental data. The SCLM framework establishes a solid groundwork for smart and sustainable WSNs by improving their performance in three key areas: secure communication, balanced energy consumption, and extended network operation.

title: Electrophysiologic Biomarkers for Detection of Neurodegenerative Diseases
authors: K.G. Harindi, D.N.T. Lawrencehewa, S.L. Nandasiri, R.D. Ranaweera, V. Weerasinghe, T.L. Dassanayake
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) offers a noninvasive and cost-effective avenue for detecting early biomarkers of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer’s disease (AD) and Frontotemporal Dementia (FTD). We present a hybrid framework that integrates Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (GCNNs) with Random Forest classification to analyze brain connectivity patterns and differentiate disease states. The features used for classification were derived from the values of connectivity matrices, constructed using Phase Locking Value (PLV) and coherence measures, with alpha and theta frequency bands identified as the most discriminative for classification. To reduce noise and improve mapping of new raw EEG data points to the disease spectrum, a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)–based smoothing algorithm was employed. This approach enables robust differentiation between AD, FTD, and healthy controls (HC), while maintaining interpretability—a critical requirement for clinical translation. Our findings highlight the potential of EEG connectivity features combined with graph-based and interpretable machine learning methods as a pathway toward low-cost, clinically applicable diagnostic tools.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Phishing Detection: Enhancing Cyber security
authors: Punyaban Patel, Kilari Ram Priya, Shaheena Fatima, Ravi Kumar Chandu, S. Vasanthi, J.Jebathangam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Phishing attacks remain among the most regular cybersecurity threats since they exploit human weaknesses to penetrate systems unauthorized. Security procedures that exist today are unable to identify advanced cyberattacks effectively which leads to a requirement for updated protection measures. The combination of machine learning with deep learning and natural language processing methods within artificial intelligence creates an effective tool for detecting phishing threats. Algorithms based on artificial intelligence enable the analysis of large datasets for detecting anomalous email patterns and identifying dangerous URLs while recognizing unhonest website characteristics at a level of high precision. This research investigates how artificial intelligence (AI) detects phishing through its superior capability to identify threats in real time while improving accuracy by learning from experience and establishing fewer incorrect alerts. The text addresses main obstacles like adversarial attacks, data privacy challenges along with the requirement of continuous model maintenance. The incorporation of AI-based phishing detection systems into existing cyber security frameworks leads to defense improvements that lower both data breach possibilities and financial losses for organizations.

title: Hardware-in-the-Loop Simulation of a Robust Controller to Damp Low-Frequency Oscillations in Power Systems
authors: Nawanjana Y. Ranathunga, Vimukthi R. Liyanage, D. Prasad Wadduwage, Lahiru L. Premasiri
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Poorly damped low-frequency oscillations in multi-area power systems (0.1 Hz - 2 Hz) can create significant challenges to its security level. A properly-tuned robust damping controller can mitigate these adverse effects by improving the damping of such oscillations to acceptable values while ensuring the small signal stability of the power systems. Real-time simulations, performed using the Real Time Digital Simulator (RTDS), provide a state-of-the-art approach for testing and validating the performances of these damping controllers under realistic operating conditions. This paper presents the design and implementation of a robust damping controller using the RTDS facility via Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) approach. The implementation of the damping controller for 2-area test system using C2000 microcontrollers by Texas Instruments demonstrates its near-real-time, robust operation under different operating conditions.

title: Deep Learning for Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy Using Retinal Fundus Images
authors: Shwetha Nayak, Mounesh Arkachari, Shreeya G R, Nandini K A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) remains a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide, affecting approximately one-third of diabetic patients. Early detection through retinal fundus imaging is crucial for preventing vision loss, but manual screening presents challenges in scalability and inter-observer variability. This paper presents a novel hybrid deep learning architecture that integrates ResNet50 for hierarchical feature extraction with Capsule Networks for precise spatial relationship modeling. The proposed model addresses critical limitations of conventional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), particularly their inability to preserve spatial hierarchies and partwhole relationships essential for accurate DR severity grading. Our approach leverages ResNet50's powerful feature extraction capabilities to identify intricate retinal patterns, while Capsule Networks capture spatial dependencies through dynamic routing and vector outputs. Experimental results on retinal fundus images demonstrate superior performance compared to standalone CNN architectures, with significant improvements in detecting subtle pathological features such as microaneurysms, exudates, and hemorrhages. The hybrid model achieved an overall accuracy of 97.2 in five-class DR classification, showcasing strong potential as an automated screening tool for clinical deployment.

title: AGERS 2025 List Reviewer Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)

title: Active Learning for Joint AoA and CSI Acquisition in mmWave Systems
authors: Chi-Shiang Gau, Tara Javidi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) communication systems require accurate initial beam alignment to overcome high path loss and fully leverage its directional transmission. Joint estimation of the channel and angle of arrival (AoA) must respect hardware limitations, most notably the random phase distortions in analog phase arrays. In this paper, we propose a two-stage beam alignment strategy that operates without prior channel knowledge and relies solely on magnitude measurements. In the first stage, a coarse angular scan ensures that the true AoA is captured within one of the scanning beams, enabling a reliable estimate of the channel gain. The second stage refines the AoA estimate through a fine-grained active search algorithm guided by the marginal posterior probability. Simulation results show that our method outperforms existing approaches in scenarios with unknown CSI, achieving superior beamforming accuracy and channel estimation.

title: AI Copilots for Machine Learning Pipelines: A Comparative Study on Code Quality and Model Performance
authors: Nikos Flamourtzoglou, Eleni Vrochidou, George A. Papakostas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: As Artificial Intelligence (AI) continues to reshape, among others, the field of software development, its integration into coding workflows opens up both opportunities and critical questions. The rise of AI-powered coding assistants has introduced a paradigm shift in the way developers approximate programming tasks. This work aims to investigate the efficacy and quality of AI-powered coding in the development of end-to-end machine learning pipelines. More specifically, a comparative study of Amazon Q, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf and Gemini is conducted, by developing two machine learning pipelines, for customer churn prediction and Titanic survival prediction. Each assistant was prompted with the same instructions towards a fair evaluation, and their outputs were assessed using static and dynamic code analysis tools, such as Pylint, Radon, SonarQube and MLflow. The evaluation is focused on code quality, model performance, feature importance, explainability and fairness. Our findings provide practical insights into the coding assistants' strengths and limitations, offering guidance for developers and researchers for the selection of the appropriate tool for machine learning tasks.

title: Enhanced Algal Bloom Detection Using Three Novel Geometry-Based Spectral Indices in a Hybrid CNN-CatBoost Ensemble
authors: Manoranjan Dash, Sabita Misra, Atul Dash
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Accurate estimation of chlorophyll-a (Chl-a) concentration from multispectral satellite data is critical for early detection and monitoring of harmful algal blooms (HABs). Conventional indices such as the Normalized Difference Chlorophyll Index (NDCI) and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) often exhibit reduced sensitivity in optically complex inland and coastal waters. In this study, we propose an ensemble framework integrating a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and CatBoost to predict in-situ Chl-a concentrations from Sentinel-2 surface reflectance data provided by the GLORIA dataset. Three novel spectral indices-Angles@(650,704) capturing curvature characteristics of the red-region reflectance profile, and Polyfit Residual Sum (PRS) quantifying the deviation of observed spectra from fitted polynomial baselines-were developed and incorporated along with standard spectral bands and ancillary features in an ensemble model of CNN and CatBoost. Comparative and fair evaluation against seven existing indices (e.g., NDCI and NDVI) demonstrated that the proposed indices (Angles@(650, 704) and PRS) achieved higher accuracy in terms of $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ and RMSE. The inclusion of these proposed indices also showed a clear improvement in terms of MAE, CI(Confidence Interval) and bias across multiple water types. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining physically interpretable indices with deep and boosted learning architectures, paving the way for more robust satellite-based Chl-a retrieval and early HAB warning systems.

title: Enhanced Multi-model Online Conformal Prediction
authors: Erfan Hajihashemi, Yanning Shen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Conformal prediction is a framework for uncertainty quantification that constructs prediction sets for previously unseen data, guaranteeing coverage of the true label with a specified probability. However, the efficiency of these prediction sets, measured by their size, depends on the choice of the underlying learning model. Relying on a single fixed model may lead to suboptimal performance in online environments, as a single model may not consistently perform well across all time steps. To mitigate this, prior work has explored selecting a model from a set of candidates. However, this approach becomes computationally expensive as the number of candidate models increases. Moreover, poorly performing models in the set may also hinder the effectiveness. To tackle this challenge, this work develops a novel multi-model online conformal prediction algorithm that reduces computational complexity and improves prediction efficiency. At each time step, a bipartite graph is generated to identify a subset of effective models, from which a model is selected to construct the prediction set. Experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing multi-model conformal prediction techniques in terms of both prediction set size and computational efficiency.

title: Development and Performance Evaluation of an Open-Ring PET Prototype for Intraoperative Metabolic Monitoring_supp1-3676872.docx
authors: Bingxuan Li
abstract: This study presents an All-Digital PET prototype based on multi-voltage threshold (MVT) technology, featuring an open-ring geometry and adjustable ring diameters specifically designed for intraoperative metabolic monitoring. System performance was evaluated under three transaxial field of view (FOV) configurations following the relevant guidelines of NEMA NU 4-2008 and NU 2-2018 standards. In addition to phantom-based assessments, a dynamic imaging experiment was conducted on a rabbit bearing VX2 liver tumors to demonstrate the in vivo metabolic imaging capability. Across all configurations, the system achieved sub-3 mm spatial resolutions in the y- and z-directions at a 1 cm radial offset, while resolution in the x-direction was degraded due to limited angular coverage. The time-of-flight (TOF) resolution ranged from approximately 300 to 330 ps. System sensitivity and count-rate performance decreased with increasing FOV, reflecting the inherent trade-off between imaging performance and surgical workspace in open-ring PET designs. For the minimum 30 cm ring diameter, the system sensitivity was measured at 22.2 kcps/MBq, with a peak noise-equivalent count rate of 1282.7 kcps at 74.1kBq/mL (using a monkey-sized phantom). At 5.3 kBq/mL, the scatter fraction was 24.0%. Despite limited angular coverage, the image quality phantom test demonstrated good uniformity, while recovery coefficients and spill-over ratios were affected by incomplete angular sampling, particularly at larger FOVs. The animal study demonstrated clear metabolic contrast and tumor delineation, supporting the feasibility of using an open-geometry, All-Digital PET system for intraoperative imaging scenarios. These results establish a technical foundation for further development of dedicated PET solutions in image-guided surgical interventions.

title: CLAP: Cross-Layer Adaptive Pipelining Inference Scheduling for Resource-Efficient Edge-Cloud Vision Systems
authors: Zheming Yang, Wen Ji, Qi Guo, Jian Zhao, Chang Zhao, Xingzhou Zhang, Yangyu Zhang, Zhicheng Li, Yang You

title: Factors Influencing Employee Adoption of AI-Driven Valuation Tools in the Real-Estate Appraisal Industry
authors: Tang Chao, Wang Shan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This study aims to explore the factors that influence the willingness of employees in the real estate appraisal industry in the Greater Bay Area to adopt AI driven valuation tools. By using a qualitative method with 15 employees from leading companies in the Greater Bay Area, the results indicate that successful integration depends on resolving the fundamental contradiction between operational efficiency and human judgment validation. The results of this study contribute to the literature on technology acceptance by contextualizing adoption barriers in high-risk valuation environments, and provide the following clues: (1) combining artificial intelligence deployment with specialized evaluation workflows, (2) establishing accountability governance protocols, and (3) redesigning training for human AI collaboration. These practical contributions may enable the implementation of strategic artificial intelligence to enhance industry competitiveness in a downward trend.

title: An Intelligent Cross-Platform System for Indian Sign Language Translation
authors: K. Sakthivel, Pavithra K, Sonika N
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Effective communication through sign language is essential for people with hearing or speech challenges, yet its limited use among the broader population often creates obstacles to interaction. This project introduces a deep learningpowered system designed to identify static hand gestures from Indian Sign Language (ISL) using images. We built and trained three convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures InceptionV3, ResNet50, and DenseNet121 on a curated dataset of labeled ISL gestures. The incorporation of a user-friendly Streamlit web application allows users to upload images, select a model, and view the predicted gesture with confidence scores. The application also offers a comparative visualization of confidence scores across the models, showcasing DenseNet121's exceptional accuracy for ISL gesture recognition. This solution provides a reliable and adaptable framework for sign language recognition, fostering more inclusive communication and supporting the development of assistive technologies.

title: Design of Hardware Devices for Network Asset Discovery
authors: Bin Zhang, Yuguang Zhang, Dong Chen, Yan Chen, Nanyang Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Network asset discovery is a core method used in the management of network asset exposure surfaces. Most existing network asset discovery products are implemented in software form, which often suffer from limitations such as single detection approaches and constrained performance. This paper analyzes the principles of network asset discovery and its typical application scenarios, and proposes a hardware-based design method for network asset discovery devices. The method systematically details the selection of hardware platforms, the design of software architecture, and the construction of core modules such as the detection engine. Based on this, a system prototype integrating both active and passive detection mechanisms is designed. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed hardware device significantly outperforms software tools in terms of detection efficiency. The proposed approach provides a feasible pathway and technical foundation for building high-performance network asset discovery systems.

title: RPKT: Learning What You Don’t Know – Recursive Prerequisite Knowledge Tracing in Conversational AI Tutors for Personalized Learning
authors: Jinwen Tang, Qiming Guo, Zhicheng Tang, Yi Shang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Educational systems often assume learners can identify their knowledge gaps, yet research consistently shows that students struggle to recognize what they don’t know they need to learn—the "unknown unknowns" problem. This paper presents a novel Recursive Prerequisite Knowledge Tracing (RPKT) system that addresses this challenge through dynamic prerequisite discovery using large language models. Unlike existing adaptive learning systems that rely on pre-defined knowledge graphs, our approach recursively traces prerequisite concepts in real-time until reaching a learner’s actual knowledge boundary. The system employs LLMs for intelligent prerequisite extraction, implements binary assessment interfaces for cognitive load reduction, and provides personalized learning paths based on identified knowledge gaps. Demonstration across computer science domains shows the system can discover multiple nested levels of prerequisite dependencies, identify cross-domain mathematical foundations, and generate hierarchical learning sequences without requiring pre-built curricula. Our approach shows great potential for advancing personalized education technology by enabling truly adaptive learning across any academic domain.

title: Steel Surface Defect Detection Algorithm Based on Improved RT-DETR
authors: Xiyang Zhang, Youlin Chai, Yi Hu, Yuxuan Shang, Zeng Tao, Yongze Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Given that Steel surface defects generally exhibit characteristics such as small size, edge blur, irregular shape, complex background page, and variable light, Traditional detection methods and existing Deep learning models still have deficiencies in terms of Detection accuracy, Robustness, and Real-time performance. To address this, this paper proposes an optimization-oriented Lightweight surface defect detection model for Industrial grayscale images based on the RT-DETR framework. By introducing three Improved modules: SNI-FCA, CGFM, and GSConvE-GACW, the model's Perception ability for Subtle defects is enhanced through three key aspects: Feature reconstruction, Multi-scale fusion, and Input representation. The experiment results on the NEU-DET dataset show that the proposed method achieves a Mean Average Precision of 77.43 % without relying on a Bigger Backbone network, which is significantly superior to Mainstream models such as YOLOv5l, YOLOv8I, and YOLOv11I, achieving significant improvement in recall rate compared to other methods. Meanwhile, the Model parameters are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9. 7 2 M}$</tex>, and the Computational complexity is 56.4 Giga Floating - Point Operations Per Second, meeting the industrial Real-time performance requirement. Ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness and Synergistic gain of each module. This method achieves a good Balance among accuracy, velocity, and Generalization ability, providing an efficient and robust solution for Automated surface quality inspection in Industrial realms such as steel.

title: Innovative Development and Analysis of Key Technologies of the Relay Protection Automatic Testing Device
authors: Qiongliang Chen, Bingwei Gao, Susu Pan, Mingfeng Chen, Junqiang Li, Zhi'an Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the field of relay protection testing in power systems, problems such as inconsistent process standards, insufficient adaptability of test templates, and strong dependence on manual experience have long existed. This paper proposes an innovative development scheme for an automatic testing device for relay protection. By constructing a standardized test business model, a business framework covering all scenarios is established, by developing an intelligent configurable test template, flexible and efficient test configuration is achieved, and by researching technologies for intelligent judgment in the test process and human-machine collaboration, the accuracy and reliability of testing are ensured. This device has strong compatibility, which can seamlessly adapt to relay protection equipment from various manufacturers and of multiple models, meeting the needs of complex and changeable testing scenarios. Verified by practice, it has significantly improved the efficiency and quality of relay protection testing, providing technical support for the efficient inspection of relay protection devices in power systems.

title: Algorithm Optimization of Natural Language Processing in Automatic Review and Compliance Testing of Legal Documents
authors: Changshu Dai, Chao Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper focuses on optimizing NLP algorithms for automated legal document review and compliance testing, addressing the dual challenges of long-text processing and high-precision recognition. By incorporating sparse attention mechanisms (e.g., Longformer, BigBird), computational complexity is reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(n^{2}\right)$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(n \cdot w)$</tex>, enabling efficient processing of documents exceeding 4,096 tokens. Combined with Legal-BERT domain pre-training, this approach enhances legal entity recognition and compliance classification performance, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87-92 \%$</tex>. Experiments demonstrate that the optimized model maintains 95% accuracy while reducing computational resources by 80%, enabling real-time compliance detection. We propose a hierarchical sparse attention and overlapping chunking strategy that balances semantic integrity and computational efficiency, making it suitable for large-scale automated legal text processing.

title: Research on Cost-Benefit Estimation for Mechanized transformation in Power Construction Based on Improved Analytic Hierarchy Process
authors: Jiali Ding
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The rapid development of the power construction industry has imposed higher demands on efficiency, safety, and environmental benefits, driving its mechanized transformation. However, existing cost-benefit estimation methods struggle to quantify intangible benefits such as improved safety, environmental protection, and enhanced quality, leading to an incomplete assessment of the transformation's value by decision-makers. This study proposes an improved AHP cost-benefit estimation model integrating entropy weight correction and Monte Carlo simulation. The model establishes a full-cycle dynamic estimation system covering second-level indicators such as direct/indirect costs, direct economic benefits, safety benefits, environmental benefits, and long-term strategic benefits, further detailed into 13 third-level indicators like equipment purchase/leasing fees and reduction in accident losses, systematically monetizing intangible benefits. By introducing a dynamic combined weighting mechanism that combines subjective weights from Delphi expert surveys with objective corrections from the entropy weight method, and a Monte Carlo simulation module for uncertainty quantification, the model's adaptability and accuracy in complex decision-making environments are significantly enhanced.

title: A Hierarchical Graph-Language Model for Detection of Malicious Rumors in Social Media
authors: Jianbin Wang, Yujie Lin, Qitao Tan, Changxing Chen, Yue Su
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of social media, online rumors spread at an alarming rate, posing a serious threat to public perception and social stability. To address this, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Graph Language Model (HGLM) based on Graph Attention Networks (GAT) and Large Language Models (LLM) for early detection of online rumors. HGLM captures multi-layered semantic features of posts and their reply chains through hierarchical text modeling. It dynamically adjusts the influence between nodes in the propagation graph via an adaptive edge weight mechanism and employs multi-model expert fusion to achieve collaborative discrimination of text semantics and propagation structure. Experimental results demonstrate that on the Twitter15, Twitter16, and PHEME datasets, HGLM significantly outperforms traditional machine learning models and deep graph neural network models across metrics including Accuracy, F1 true, and F1 false. It exhibits outstanding stability, versatility, and cross-dataset transferability. This approach provides an efficient, scalable, and robust solution for early rumor identification within complex social network environments. Experimental results on Twitter15, Twitter16, and PHEME datasets demonstrate the model’s superiority. HGLM achieves accuracy rates of 89.2%, 89.1%, and 86.8% respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines by approximately 6–9%. These results validate HGLM as a robust solution for early rumor identification, significantly advancing the capability to maintain social stability in complex network environments.

title: Advancing AI-Driven Disease Diagnosis: A Hybrid MDCN and Transfer Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Diagnosis
authors: Neetu Settia, Pawan Kumar Singh, Monica Bhutani
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Healthcare sector is evolving in all fields with the introduction of Artificial Intelligence (AI). AI methods aid in precise health diagnosis, enables detection at early stages of disease and makes personalised treatment procedures hence resulting in overall better patient care. With the advancement in AI, their further incorporation in healthcare sector will speed up the accuracy of diagnosis and detection of diseases. This research has dual purposes: first, to demonstrate dual model AI architecture for diagnosis of brain tumor using MDCN and Transfer Learning and second, to address global challenges associated with integration of AI models with existing healthcare sector. It demonstrates that Transfer learning model achieved a higher accuracy of 97% as compared to MDCN for brain tumor detection. This dual model approach would prove beneficial to clinical staff due to enhanced accuracy and reliability in automated brain tumor detection in clinical field.

title: YOLO-Based and DCNN Fusion for Urban Traffic Congestion Prediction
authors: Yuxiang Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Heavy congestion in metropolitan areas is a major barrier to smooth traffic and city operation. This paper proposes a novel predicting method for urban traffic congestion based on You Only Look Once (YOLO) segmentation and a deep convolutional neural network (DCNN). Firstly, the YOLO segmentation model is employed to precisely detect and segment vehicles, pedestrians, and other targets in traffic scenes and acquire target-level information such as vehicle and pedestrian numbers. Then the DCNN is trained using this information and additional features such as traffic volume. The DCNN, based on the multilayer architecture of convolutional and fully connected features, can create discriminatory models and non-linear mapping based on heterogeneous pattern diversity and correlation from data. A cross-entropy loss function and Adam optimizer are used to optimize the parameters during training, and early stopping is implemented to avoid overfitting. On the test set, the experimental results with this model, which integrates YOLO with deep convolutional neural networks, demonstrate the strong capabilities of effectively predicting traffic congestion states, with a micro-average ROC area up to 0.661 on the test set, and accurately classifying urban traffic congestion states, which can provide robust support for urban traffic management.

title: Brain Tumor Detection in MRI images Using Hybrid Machine Learning Model
authors: Pradeep Kumar Gupta, Neha Gupta, Utkarsh Pandey, Parulpreet Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Medical imaging is very important for diagnosing and treating patients in healthcare. Oncologists examine images to identify the distinct features of malignant diseases, formulate treatment plans, and evaluate disease progression [25]-[27]. This study aims to develop an approach for the identification of brain tumors. Appropriate segmentation techniques can be used to find brain malignancies in Magnetic Resonance (MR) pictures. Recent improvements in brain radiographic imaging show that deep learning techniques can be used to find certain features of brain tumors, which can help doctors make better diagnoses. Consequently, the majority of data scientists and AI researchers focus on Machine Learning techniques to develop automated screening systems. An automated method would yield quicker segmentation results, delivering reliable insights into data source discrepancies, mostly attributable to varying data recording and storage techniques, hence facilitating more consistent brain tumor identification. This study proposes and assesses innovative structures to enhance the effectiveness of the segmentation method. We suggest using deep neural networks to find brain cancers by training them using MRI scans of patients' brains. The proposed architectures employ convolutional neural networks and inception modules for brain tumor segmentation.

title: Bridging the Gap Between Donors and Recipients: A Digital Application to Food Donation
authors: Poongodi K, Sathish Kumar M, Srinithi R, Devadharshini S, Mohana Swara Raj N
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: A digital food donation platform employing the MERN stack has been developed in response to the growing problem of food waste and hunger. The system links volunteers, donors, and receivers through an integrated platform featuring AI-powered food quality detection, secure authentication, and real-time coordination. Volunteers oversee the collecting and distribution of leftover food, donors list available items with geographical information, and recipients can access resources instantaneously. Trust, accountability, and transparency are guaranteed by features like role-based dashboards, location services, OTP verification, and AI-driven freshness analysis. The platform encourages fair food distribution, reduces waste, and increases community involvement by fusing technology with social responsibility.

title: Multi-Level Frame Preemption Mechanism for TSN Based Avionics Systems
authors: Nan Liu, Da Chai, Xin Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Avionics systems, the core of modern aviation, demand stringent real time performance, determinism, and reliability. TSN with deterministic capabilities like precise synchronization has become critical for next generation avionics networks. However, IEEE 802.1Qbu's single-level preemption, which divides frames into “Express” and “Preemptable”, struggles with complex avionics traffic. The same level frames cannot preempt each other, causing critical Preemptable traffic to suffer excessive delays due to lower priority frames. This paper proposes an Aviation Enhanced Multi-Level Frame Preemption mechanism. Based on IEEE 802.1Qbu, it dynamically subdivides preemptable frames by avionics standards with priority mapping to preemption categories. It extends the MAC Merge sublayer's SMD field to identify subclasses and adds logical interfaces with adjusted state machines for fine-grained scheduling. To further coordinate transmission among these subclasses, a credit-based mechanism is introduced to quantify blocking time, with credit accumulating in proportion to the duration of delay. This accumulated credit, combined with the PCP, forms a composite scheduling metric that enables higher-priority preemptable subclasses to preempt lower ones fairly and deterministically. Simulations in OMNeT++ show that the mechanism significantly reduces end-to-end delay for high-priority Preemptable traffic, ensures timely response to time-sensitive events, and maintains network determinism.

title: Self-Adaptive Vision Repair: Handling RGB Failures in Autonomous Driving Systems
authors: Yong Xie, Xiang Hao, Tianshuo Zhang, Li Fan, Yong Liu, Weiwei Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Autonomous driving algorithms rely on accurate and precise sensor data inputs. However, when RGB sensors fail, the performance of object detection and image segmentation can degrade significantly, potentially compromising the vehicle's ability to navigate safely. To address this issue, this paper first systematically analyzes and categorizes various potential RGB sensor failures and develops a Python library to simulate realistic RGB fault manifestations. Subsequently, we propose the Perception of Fault Adaptive Framework (PoFA), which employs an adaptive network to enhance the robustness of autonomous driving algorithms against faulty sensor inputs. Extensive experiments conducted in the Carla simulator validate the effectiveness of the PoFA framework, demonstrating its ability to mitigate driving performance degradation caused by most RGB faults.

title: Unified Predictive Maintenance with Generative Anomaly Injection, RL Scheduling in Data-Driven Digital Twins
authors: Raj Kumar Myakala, Sanjeev Kumar Pedhapally, Vinithya Reddy Podduturi, Akhil Reddy Jagirapu, Praveen Kumar Nagata, Sampath Lavudya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: In modern industrial systems, unplanned downtime leads to significant operational and financial losses. To address this, we propose a unified predictive maintenance pipeline that integrates generative anomaly injection, explainability, and reinforcement learning (RL) within a digital twin framework. Our system uses sensor data from real-world datasets (XJTU-SY, AI4I, and C-MAPSS) for Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction, fault simulation, and proactive maintenance policy generation. First, a GAN-based fault simulation module injects rare but realistic failure patterns using low discriminator confidence. Next, SHAP-based explainability is applied for feature attribution and drift monitoring. LSTM and Prophet models are employed to forecast RUL trends, which are fed into a Q-learning-based RL scheduler to optimize prescriptive actions like maintenance or load reduction. These actions are sent to a virtual command layer and closed-loop feedback is achieved via MQTT/OPC-UA to simulate interaction with the physical twin. We quantitatively evaluate the synthetic fault realism using t-SNE, PCA, and classifier generalization. The overall pipeline improves prediction accuracy by 12.6% and reduces failure downtime by 19.4% on average. SHAP analysis reveals interpretable patterns across synthetic and real failures. Our modular architecture demonstrates a self-optimizing, explainable, and simulation-augmented predictive maintenance system for Industry 4.0 environments. This work highlights the practical synergy of generative models, RL, and digital twins for scalable, real-time industrial reliability solutions.

title: QSE-QA: A Question Space Expansion Framework for Creative and Diverse Answer Generation
authors: Zhikai Mi, Bo Yu, Jianing Tong, Lijun Fu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In generative question answering (QA) systems, a key challenge in the field of natural language generation lies in enhancing the creativity and diversity of answers while maintaining semantic relevance. Traditional QA systems typically adopt a single question-single answer mapping mechanism, which results in limited answer diversity and restricts their ability to accommodate multi-dimensional information needs and user expressions in open-domain contexts. This paper proposes a novel QA generation framework called QSE-QA (Question Space Expansion for Question Answering), designed to improve semantic diversity and creative expressiveness of generated answers through expansion of the question space. The core idea is to generate semantically relevant question variants via an intermediate modeling mechanism, which guides large language models to activate different knowledge pathways and thereby produce more creative and multi-perspective responses. The proposed method consists of two main stages: Based on prompt engineering techniques, guiding the model to generate a diversified question set Q2 from the original QA pair (Q, A); Using a fine-tuned intermediate middleware model, the system automatically generates a set of diverse questions when encountering new queries, which serve as generative prompts to drive the answer generation process. Experiments on a medical QA dataset demonstrate that QSE-QA significantly outperforms traditional methods on evaluation metrics such as ROUGE, and shows enhanced multi-view and creative expressiveness in human evaluations. This research introduces a “human-like question construction” capability into generative QA systems, expanding the pathways for creative modeling in QA generation tasks. The proposed framework exhibits strong generalizability and potential for future extensions.

title: Hybrid Artificial Potential Field based UAV Patrol Inspection for Uncertain Target Events in Obstacle Environments
authors: Suyi Xue, Yongkang Zheng, Wenke Yuan, Huasen He, Yunpeng Hou, Xiaofeng Jiang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In this paper, we study the cooperative autonomous inspection by Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAV) swarms in dynamic and uncertain environments. Facing the challenges of spatio-temporally random target events and constrained onboard resources, we propose a distributed online path planning algorithm based on a Hybrid Artificial Potential Field (HAPF). In contrast to existing works relying on a single probability map, we construct a hybrid artificial potential field to integrate a probability map and an exploration map. The probability map, generated via Kernel Density Estimation from historical and real-time data, learns the distribution pattern of events, while the exploration map encourages coverage of unvisited areas. Virtual attractive forces guide UAVs to prioritize high-probability events and explore unknown regions, whereas virtual repulsive forces ensure collision-free cooperation. Moreover, a Hungarian algorithm based dynamic task allocation method is proposed to prevent resource redundancy. To tackle the well-known local minimum problem in potential field, a sticking release mechanism is designed to escape local optima. Simulation results demonstrate that, in comparison to existing algorithms, our method improving the task completion rate by 20 % and shortening the average completion time by 13 %, offering a practical solution for resource-constrained UAV swarms to achieve efficient and safe autonomous inspection in complex and dynamic environments.

title: Adaptive Lighting Control in Visible Light Systems: An Integrated Sensing, Communication, and Illumination Framework
authors: Xinyan Xie, Xuesong Wang, Xin Lai, Yongheng Wen, Fengrui Yang, Haoyang He, Lai Zhang, Dong Zhao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Indoor visible light communication (VLC) is a promising sixth-generation (6G) technology, as its directional and sensitive optical signals are naturally suited for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). However, current research mainly focuses on maximizing data rates and sensing accuracy, creating a conflict between high performance, high energy consumption, and user visual comfort. This paper proposes an adaptive integrated sensing, communication, and illumination (ISCI) framework that resolves this conflict by treating energy savings as a primary objective. The framework's mechanism first partitions the receiving plane using a geometric methodology, defining an activity area and a surrounding non-activity area to match distinct user requirements. User location, determined using non-line-ofsight (NLOS) sensing, then acts as a dynamic switch for the system's optimization objective. The system adaptively shifts between minimizing total transmit power while guaranteeing communication and illumination performance in the activity area and maximizing signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) uniformity in the non-activity area. Numerical results confirm that this adaptive ISCI approach achieves 53.59% energy savings over a nonadaptive system and improves SNR uniformity by 57.79%, while satisfying all illumination constraints and maintaining a mean localization error of 0.071 m.

title: DCLB: A Distributed Cooperative Load-Balancing Mechanism for Large-Scale Satellite Networks
authors: Ruiwei Yuan, Ran Zhang, Kaiqiao Ji, Pengfei Zhang, Jiang Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: As the scale of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks continues to expand, traditional inter-satellite routing models are becoming increasingly unsuitable due to their inability to handle dynamic topologies and complex traffic patterns. Recently, domain-based routing schemes have been proposed to enhance scalability and management efficiency in large-scale LEO satellite networks. However, the uneven distribution of ground hotspot areas leads to imbalanced traffic loads among different domains, causing congestion in certain regions and degrading overall network performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a distributed cooperative load-balancing mechanism (DCLB) for large-scale satellite networks. The strategy leverages Segment Routing (SR) for autonomous path planning and incorporates an on-demand scheduling mechanism to balance traffic loads globally, thereby mitigating congestion in hotspot regions. By utilizing localized information exchange, our approach enables distributed decision-making without requiring centralized control, making it highly scalable. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces congestion in hotspot areas, improves network throughput, and balances link utilization, making it well-suited for large-scale dynamic satellite networks.

title: Predictive Analysis of Olympic Medal Distribution Based on Deep Learning and Statistical Models: A Multi-Method Approach for the 2028 Summer Olympics
authors: Jiayi Yang, Jiaxiang Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: Understanding and predicting Olympic medal distribution has become increasingly important for national Olympic committees and sports policymakers. This study presents a comprehensive data-driven framework to forecast the 2028 Summer Olympics medal standings and identify key determinants of Olympic success. We employ multiple analytical approaches, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal pattern recognition, Back Propagation Neural Networks (BPNN) for identifying emerging medal-winning nations, and statistical methods such as Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression and Cumulative Sum (CUSUM) control charts to quantify coaching impacts. Our analysis of historical Olympic data reveals that sustained investment in sports infrastructure, strategic coaching programs, and systematic athlete development significantly influence medal outcomes. The LSTM model achieves strong predictive performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> values of 0.98531 on training data and 0.96068 on validation data, forecasting that USA, China, and Great Britain will dominate the 2028 medal standings. Additionally, we identify countries with high probability of winning their first medals and quantify the substantial impact of elite coaching on national performance. These findings provide actionable insights for optimizing resource allocation and strategic planning in Olympic preparation.

title: Research on Civil Aviation Flight Safety Management System Integrating QAR Data Preprocessing, Operational Quantitative Analysis, and Real-Time Early Warning
authors: Wu Ruoyuxuan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: With the rapid development of the civil aviation industry, flight safety has become a core concern, and Quick Access Recorder (QAR) data, as a key carrier of aviation safety big data, plays a crucial role in safety management. However, current QAR data research in China's civil aviation Flight Operations Quality Assurance (FOQA) mainly focuses on exceedance event analysis and non-exceedance data statistics, lacking in-depth exploration of exceedance causes, over-relying on exceedance data, and failing to form a complete technical chain from data preprocessing to risk early warning—making it difficult to support the transformation of safety management from “post-event analysis” to “pre-event early warning”. To address these issues, this study conducts in-depth mining of QAR data and constructs a full-chain flight safety management system integrating data preprocessing, operational quantitative analysis, technical evaluation, and real-time early warning. First, QAR data is divided into six flight phases (taxiing, takeoff, climb, cruise, approach, landing) based on altitude; outliers are screened and removed using the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{3} \sigma$</tex> criterion, and data gaps are filled with the average of adjacent data points to ensure data reliability. Second, through the Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) test, key indicators such as control column input and landing G-force are confirmed as weakly stationary sequences, and sudden changes in these indicators within 7 seconds before and after landing are identified as core causes of hard landings. Third, four machine learning algorithms (Random Forest (RF), Logistic Regression (LR), Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBM), Decision Tree (DT)) are used to build pilot technical proficiency prediction models; among them, the LGBM model achieves a test set accuracy of 0.656, enabling quantitative evaluation of pilot operational stability. Finally, an ILA+GAN-based real-time early warning mechanism is proposed, which reaches an accuracy of 92.55% in time-series prediction and outperforms comparative algorithms such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{OB}+\text{RNN}$</tex> and TF+RNN. This study innovatively integrates Principal Component Analysis (PCA) with flight experience and applies the ADF test to operational behavior traceability, providing a quantitative tool for civil aviation flight risk management. Future research will expand extreme scenario data coverage and explore deep learning models like Transformer to enhance early warning real-time performance and robustness.

title: GA-LSTM Based Method for PV Power, Wind Power and Electricity Load Forecasting
authors: Feng Zhou, Kunpeng Li, Weijie Tang, Yu Chen, Shanmao Gu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid model based on a Genetic Algorithm (GA) optimized Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, aiming to enhance the forecasting accuracy of photovoltaic (PV) power, wind power, and electricity load. This method innovatively utilizes GA to automatically fine-tune the crucial hyperparameters of the LSTM network, effectively overcoming the challenges of manual parameter selection inherent in traditional LSTM models. Case studies conducted on actual electricity load data from Australia, wind power data from a wind turbine, and generation data from a PV power plant validate the significant superiority of the proposed GA-LSTM model. Results indicate that, compared to standard LSTM models, the GA-LSTM model substantially improved the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value to 0.928 for electricity load forecasting, and to 0.889 and 0.979 for wind power and PV power forecasting, respectively, while also significantly reducing MAE and RMSE error metrics. These improvements demonstrate the GA-LSTM model's robust capability in handling highly volatile time series data, providing more reliable forecasting support for stable power system operation and efficient renewable energy utilization.

title: Using Machine Learning Algorithm to Predict Climate Change Pattern in Natural Geographical Environment
authors: Yu Lu, Yanxia Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Traditional climate prediction methods are limited by the processing capacity of nonlinear and complex climate systems, while machine learning algorithms show significant advantages in capturing complex patterns. In this paper, a hybrid model is adopted, which combines the Gradient Boosting Decision Tree (GBDT) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). By comprehensively applying the advantages of these two algorithms, the accuracy and stability of climate change prediction are improved. The research data comes from weather stations and satellite observations, covering key climate variables such as temperature, humidity and wind speed. In the data preprocessing stage, the abnormal values are removed by IQR rule, and the missing values are supplemented by time series interpolation method, and feature selection and standardization are carried out. The experimental results show that the hybrid model (GBDT+LSTM) significantly improves the prediction accuracy compared with the single GBDT and LSTM models in the prediction of climate variables such as temperature, humidity and wind speed. Specifically, the accuracy of temperature prediction reached 92.6%, and the accuracy of humidity prediction and wind speed prediction increased to 88.5% and 85.7% respectively. In addition, the predicted values of the hybrid model in different time periods are in good agreement with the actual observed values, which shows the stability and effectiveness of the model in dealing with complex climate data. The research in this paper not only verifies the effectiveness and potential of machine learning algorithm in climate change prediction, but also further improves the prediction performance through reasonable selection and combination of features and optimization of model structure. The research results provide scientific decision-making support for policy makers and help to better understand and cope with the challenges brought by climate change.

title: Optimizing the Online and Offline Hybrid Teaching Mode of Mechanical Drawing by Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Jing Liu, Jingjing Dong, Yanhong Liu, Kaifeng Zheng, Zheng Wang, Tianxu Cao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper focuses on optimizing the online and offline hybrid teaching mode of mechanical drawing by using reinforcement learning, aiming to achieve accurate learning situation analysis and character portrait through artificial intelligence, and break through the barriers of traditional teaching. First, a reinforcement learning model based on the improved Q-Learning algorithm is constructed. The model contains a state space that integrates mechanical drawing knowledge points and student learning status, an action space covering online and offline teaching links, and a reward function that integrates immediate and delayed feedback. The forgetting factor and hierarchical decision-making mechanism are innovatively introduced. Secondly, a precise learning situation analysis and character portrait system are designed to generate multi-dimensional student labels. The experiment selected 77 mechanical students. The experimental group used the optimization mode, and the control group used the traditional mode. The results show that the experimental group's average final score is 86.3 points, an increase of 18.1% compared with the control group's 73.1 points, the average mastery rate of core knowledge points is 91.1%, and the control group's is 76.2%, an increase of 14.9%, the total learning time is 52.3 hours, a decrease of 33.5% compared with the control group's 78.6 hours, the learning efficiency is 1.65 points/hour, an increase of 77.4%, the overall student satisfaction is 90.8%, and the control group is 72.5%. The study shows that reinforcement learning can effectively optimize the teaching mode and improve the teaching quality and learning effect.

title: Impact of Poisson Surface Reconstruction on Quality Assessment of Compressed Point Clouds
authors: Zhang Chen, Luqian Bai, Mengting Yu, Shuai Wan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Surface reconstruction is generally required for displaying point clouds in practice. To explore the influence of point cloud surface reconstruction on its perceptual quality regarding compression distortion, we apply Poisson surface reconstruction for point clouds with compression distortion and evaluate the subjective quality of the point clouds before and after surface reconstruction. The reconstruction process improves the overall subjective quality, but this improvement is not uniform for point clouds with different quantization parameters and compressed distortion. When there is significant geometric and attribute compression distortion, surface reconstruction enhances subjective quality. However, as the geometric and attribute quantization parameters decrease, the improvement from surface reconstruction gradually diminishes. Surface reconstruction can reduce the subjective quality when there is slight geometric and attribute compression distortion due to over-smoothing or incorrect surface fitting. The findings in this work are helpful for the design of point cloud compression technologies. A further contribution of this work is a mesh subjective dataset with 36 different compressed distortions. This dataset enriches the data for point cloud quality evaluation and provides a valuable reference for compression. Our subjective database is available at the following link: https://github.com/ZhangChen2022/ICCC2025.

title: An ENF Feature-based Machine Learning Framework for Audio Deepfake Detection and Forensic Authentication
authors: Soham Gangopadhyay, Inderpreet Singh, Prateek Pandya, Ashish Mani, Sumit Goswami
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Audio deepfakes pose a growing threat to the reliability of digital evidence in both legal and security settings. In order to distinguish synthetic audio from authentic recordings, the present work attempts to analyze the Electrical Network Frequency (ENF), a unique, stable, location-specific environmental trace left provided by the power grids. We extracted 27 targeted statistical, spectral, and derivative features from a dataset comprising 66,832 files available in the FakeOrReal (FoR) collection. The results have identified optimized XGBoost classifier as the most effective learning model providing a test accuracy of 96.11% and an Equal Error Rate (EER) of 0.0404. These metrics provide higher precision desired for forensic casework. Our assessment has indicated that out of the 27 indicators, ENF 25th percentile, spectral bandwidth, and interquartile range were found to be the best in terms of reliability and explainability. The method relies on a detectable grid signal, and a robust method to detect even in the presence of noise. This significantly enhances the reliability of the method enables it to be suitable in forensic settings.

title: Hybrid Plant Disease Detection and Treatment Prediction Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Bestley Joe S, Vanitha V, Aarav Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Agriculture is the backbone of every nation worldwide. It is the main prerequisite for the overall economic betterment and stability of any country as it provides raw material, food for people and employment to those who are connected to this sector. Traditional methods of disease identification are impractical, requires special knowledge and are time consuming. This research work aims to address these limitations by providing a system that leverages cloud-based tools like OpenAI vision and gemini vision wherein an image upload of a diseased agricultural specimen utilizes the pre-trained models to diagnose the issues. A simple client-server architecture using flask is developed that communicates with front end which are capable of managing posts like farmer community forum, chatbot that is offered with multi-language option. Six samples of agricultural specimens including one healthy sample was tested and the detection cum diagnosis results are obtained. Future opportunities for further enhancement of the system are also suggested.

title: Research on Performance Prediction Method Based on Unsupervised Feature Selection
authors: Jinsong Li, Hongyan Liu, Na Wang, Minghai Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Performance prediction can provide reference for teachers and students to improve teaching strategies and learning methods. Most of the existing prediction models are based on student achievement, ignoring the influence of other non-academic factors. Therefore, in order to construct a more accurate and efficient performance prediction model, a performance prediction model based on unsupervised feature selection is proposed. The performance prediction model based on the features selected by the Laplacian score and the sequential forward search algorithm not only considers the unsupervised attributes of student performance, but also reduces the computational complexity. A large number of random experiments were conducted on real data from different schools, and the proposed method was compared with mainstream prediction methods. The experimental results show that the proposed method has higher prediction performance and stability, and can provide more credible decision support for teachers and students to improve teaching and learning methods.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning Approaches for Crop Yield Prediction Using Agronomic and Meteorological Features
authors: Diksha, Simrandeep Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: The right prediction of crop yield is essential in planning agriculture sustainably and optimizing resources. The proposed research is about forecasting crop yield through agronomic and meteorological features like temperature, humidity, soil moisture, area, season, crop type, and location by using machine learning(ML) and deep learning(DL) ensemble models. The used dataset consists of the crop yield records of 7 states, 112 districts, and 80 crops over many years. The dataset got judged by the seven models mentioned, which are Random Forest, Gradient Boosting Regressor, AdaBoost, Random Subspace, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost along with a Feed Forward Neural Network (FFNN). The model was evaluated based on R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) metrics. Out of all the methods used, CatBoost gave the highest prediction accuracy, thus surpassing both traditional ensemble methods and the neural network model. The results emphasize the power of tree based boosting methods, particularly CatBoost, in forecasting crop yield, thus offering a digital agricultural future ready for data driven decision making.

title: Graph Attention on Hasse-Based Simplicial Complexes
authors: Hong Wu, Sheng Wu, Hui Feng, Bo Hu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In real-world systems, multi-way interactions beyond pairwise relations are pervasive and play a crucial role in uncovering the underlying structural and functional patterns. While Simplicial Neural Networks (SNNs) extend Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to model multi-way interactions via simplicial complexes, existing SNN methods struggle to capture explicit cross-order dependencies due to their reliance on the local propagation of the Hodge Laplacian. In this work, we propose HGSNN (Hasse-based Graph Attention Simplicial Neural Network), a novel framework that represents simplices of all orders as nodes in a unified Hasse diagram. Built upon this representation, our model employs graph attention mechanisms to adaptively weight the influence of neighboring simplices across different orders, enabling flexible and data-driven cross-order message passing. Moreover, HGSNN supports unified input and end-toend training of multi-order signals, providing a scalable and expressive framework for higher-order representation learning. Extensive experiments on a real-world dataset demonstrate that HGSNN consistently outperforms existing models.

title: Hybrid Ensemble Learning Framework for Multi-Class Brain Hemorrhage Classification
authors: Gayathri S, Monika R J, Monishaa S, Rishibalan M B
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Early identification and subtyping of intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) subtypes from CT scans is important for timely medical treatment and better patient outcome. This paper introduces a new hybrid ensemble learning approach that inte grates deep convolutional neural network feature extraction with machine learning meta-learners for multi-class brain hemorrhage subtyping. By rigorous assessment of various CNN architectures such as custom CNN (53 %), DenseNet (47 %), and EfficientNet (63 %), we have selected best feature extractors for our ensemble system. Our system combines EfficientNetB3 and a custom SEResNeXt architecture to extract hierarchical features followed by ensemble classification employing Deep Neural Networks with Monte Carlo dropout, Support Vector Machines, and XGBoost. A logistic regression meta-learner combines predictions optimally by stacking methodology. The model includes uncertainty quantification through MC-dropout to improve clinical reliability. Tested on a large dataset of 47,014 CT images over six classes (EDH, IPH, IVH, SAH, SDH, and Healthy), our model attains 91.7 % accuracy with strong performance across all subtypes of hemorrhage. The hybrid architecture outperforms single models and single CNN methods while delivering interpretable uncertainty estimates critical for clinical decision-making.

title: Bridging Temporal Reasoning and Language Planning for Vision-and-Language Navigation with Large-Scale Pretraining and LLMs
authors: Xingjian Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: We propose a two-stage framework for Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) that integrates temporal-aware pretraining with large language model (LLM) reasoning. In the first stage, we introduce RHTAVLN, a pretraining pipeline based on multiple proxy tasks–including trajectory order modeling and instruction matching–to improve instruction–trajectory alignment and temporal understanding. In the second stage, we design NavLLM, an interpretable planning agent that transforms visual observations into language prompts and uses LLMs to generate chain-of-thought reasoning for action selection. Experiments on the Room-to-Room dataset demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both seen and unseen environments. Ablation studies further validate the contributions of each component. Notably, NavLLM supports zero-shot deployment with strong generalization and transparent decision-making. Our results suggest that combining multimodal pretraining with LLM-based symbolic reasoning can lead to more robust, adaptive, and interpretable embodied agents.

title: Collaborative Decision-Making System for Drone Swarms in Dynamic Cross-Regional Pollution Source Inspections Based on Federated Reinforcement Learning (Federated RL)
authors: Zeli Cui, Shanlei Xin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To address the challenges of dynamic cross-regional pollution source inspections, such as regional data silos, high privacy and security requirements, dynamic source migration, and low drone collaboration efficiency, this paper proposes a collaborative decision-making system for drone swarms based on federated reinforcement learning.
