The system first constructs a hierarchical federated architecture, then designs a federated experience replay sharing pool, and finally employs multi-agent proximal policy optimization to achieve distributed task allocation and conflict resolution. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves inspection coverage by 24.6% compared to independent learning strategies, reduces the attack inference success rate to 51.7%, and reduces communication overhead by 85.7%. The results validate the proposed framework's comprehensive advantages in coverage, privacy protection, communication efficiency, and real-time performance, providing an effective technical path for building a secure, efficient, and adaptive wide-area dynamic environmental monitoring system.

title: Hybrid Learning-based Demographic Estimation and Nutrition Recommendation using Dental Panoramic X-Ray Images
authors: J. Ponnudurai, M. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Dental radiography is essential to the diagnosis and treatment planning process in the modern oral healthcare setting. Using dental panoramic X-ray images, this study suggests a hybrid computational architecture that might not only estimate age and gender but also offer personalized food recommendations. Although the Convolutional Neural Networks are used to extract precise radiographic information from the dento-maxillofacial region that classical machinelearning classifiers are used to improve prediction accuracy and generalization across a range of populations. A rule-based nutrition recommendation system recommends appropriate dietary practices to improve oral health and lower the risk of dental conditions including caries and enamel erosion based on the anticipated demographic profile. By lowering the amount of manual interpretation work and facilitating effective decision assistance in both clinic-based and remote dental care settings, the technology is intended to help dental professionals. During the expert review, the experimental assessment shows high acceptability of dietary recommendations and great performance in the demographic prediction. For better clinical applicability, future improvements will involve extending the dataset, adding multimodal imaging and implementing the model in real-time scenarios.

title: Cnn-Bilstm Based Cross-Condition Fault Diagnosis for Crane Gearboxes with Attention and Residual Enhancement
authors: Feiqing Zhang, Shihua Chen, Zhenyu Yin, Qingfang Zhang, Leile Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Gearbox fault diagnosis is vital for crane safety and reliability. However, existing intelligent models often show weak generalization and poor noise robustness. This paper proposes a CNN-BiLSTM-based approach enhanced with Efficient Channel Attention and residual structures. CNN captures spatial features, BiLSTM models bidirectional temporal dependencies, Efficient Channel Attention highlights fault-sensitive channels, and residual connections improve gradient flow. Experiments on the Southeast University gearbox dataset verify superior accuracy and robustness compared with CNN-LSTM and CNN-GRU, demonstrating strong adaptability under varying loads and noisy conditions.

title: AI-Driven Waste Segregation Using Convolutional and Deep Neural Networks
authors: Chandragandhi S, Dharanidharan S, Koteeswarar MM, Shahanas M, Ajay Christo P
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Managing waste efficiently has become a serious challenge due to the growing amount and variety of garbage produced every day. Manual waste segregation is slow, costly, and often inaccurate. To address this problem, this work explores an AI-based approach for automatic waste segregation using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Deep Neural Networks (DNN). Waste images are classified into multiple categories such as glass, plastic, metal, organic, and hazardous using popular benchmark datasets like TrashNet, TrashBox, and a Kaggle waste dataset. The system applies image preprocessing techniques (resizing, normalization, and augmentation) and uses transfer learning with advanced architectures including ResNet, DenseNet, and ResNeXt, implemented in TensorFlow and PyTorch on GPU platforms. Our experiments show that CNN models deliver high classification accuracy. For example, a fine-tuned ResNet50 achieved 87% accuracy on TrashNet, DenseNet121 reached 95%, and ResNeXt-101 obtained 89.6% on TrashBox. A hybrid CNN model used in a smart-bin prototype achieved up to 98.9 % accuracy. These results highlight strong performance not only in accuracy but also in precision, recall, and F1-score. The proposed AI system can be applied in real-world environments such as smart waste bins, automated sorting conveyors in recycling plants, and IoT-enabled municipal systems. This demonstrates that CNN/DNN-based waste segregation can significantly improve waste handling, making recycling faster, more reliable, and environmentally sustainable.

title: An Integrated Transformer Learning Based Framework for Multi-Stage Feature Optimization in Naval Intention Detection
authors: Md Azizul Hakim, Monica Nicolescu, Mircea Nicolescu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: To enhance naval security systems, accurate detection of intentions in naval domain plays a vital role. A set of important predictive features can strengthen the strategy of decision making and reduce the likelihood of possible incidents. In this paper, we present a transformer learning based multi-stage feature optimization framework that enables multi-class classification of seven different behaviors in the naval environment to minimize potential threats. Our framework involves several key steps: first, important features are identified based on a machine learning technique; second we introduce a multi-headed transformer learning based deep neural network to extract the optimal subset of features; and finally the intentions are predicted by using a fully connected multi-class feedforward network. The experimental results demonstrate that our proposed model can effectively classify the seven different naval intentions with an overall accuracy of 96%, which outperforms baseline deep learning models such as fully connected deep neural network and LSTM.

title: Topology Optimization of Motor Bracket and its 3D Printing
authors: Zhifeng Nie, Kai Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The traditional design of motor brackets is often constrained by empirical construction patterns, leading to some problems such as structural redundancy and material waste. In contrast, topology optimization technology systematically reconstructs the spatial distribution of materials through intelligent algorithms, promoting the evolution of electromechanical equipment towards greater efficiency and energy savings while meeting core indicators such as stiffness, strength, and safety factors. This paper employs topology optimization algorithms to design a lightweight solution for the motor bracket, geometric reconstruction is performed based on the preliminary optimization scheme, and the final optimized scheme for the motor bracket is determined through comparison and strength analysis. In the final, a physical model of motor bracket is obtained using 3D printing.

title: AI-Driven CV Analysis and Job Matching in HRM Using a Hybrid Fuzzy Logic-Deep Neural Network Approach for Higher Education
authors: Naveen Nandal, Kartik Tyagi, Love Kumar, Anil Lokesh Gadi, Rita, Sathiyanathan S
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Companies can't function without efficient human resource management, especially when it comes to matching open positions with qualified applicants. Nevertheless, in the modern HRM environment, recruiters have obstacles like managing a high number of resumes, catering to candidates' varied interests, and satisfying employers' growing need for specialised skills. Intelligent CV analysis and job matching solutions are necessary since these time-sensitive judgements frequently degrade selection quality. The research methodology used in this study is a hybrid approach that combines machine learning, fuzzy logic, and NLP. Tokenisation, phrase splitting, named entity recognition, and part-of-speech tagging are preprocessing stages that are carried out before BERT is used for training, engineering, and feature extraction. Combining ANNs with fuzzy regression models improves predicted accuracy by taking advantage of both the adaptability of ANNs and the partial data handling capabilities of fuzzy algorithms. The results demonstrate that the suggested Fuzzy ANN model outperforms the competition, reaching a 98.38% analytical accuracy rate. Based on these results, it's clear that the model can make HRM more efficient, less taxing on recruiters, and better at connecting candidates with jobs.

title: A Hybrid Self-Supervised Framework for Intelligent Data Imputation Using Causal Graphs, Diffusion Models, and Generative World Learning
authors: Shivam Kumar Singh, Paras Kumar Yadav, Pragati Kumar, Abhishek Kumar, Sharik Ahmad, Himani Tyagi
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Dealing with missing data remains a key challenge in machine learning because incomplete data leads to poor model performance and trustworthiness. Most traditional options, such as mean imputation, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE), do not sufficiently account for the dynamics of dependencies, and while deep generative models (like Variational Autoencoders (VAE) and Generative Adversarial Imputation Networks (GAIN)) excel in modeling complex, high-dimensional datasets, they poorly model cases of diverse missingness mechanisms. Even recently proposed self-supervised (self-supervised contrastive learning) and diffusion (denoising score matching) based representations improve representation learning but also have limitations under Missing at Random (MAR) or Missing Not at Random (MNAR). We introduce a hybrid self-supervised framework for missing data imputation called HyImpute that takes the first principles idea of causal graph discovery, generative modeling, diffusion-based process reconstruction, and an adaptable hypernetwork (model) to generate representations and provide an adaptive process for robust imputation to better account for dependency. HyImpute utilizes pretext tasks to learn the representations, employs casual reasoning to retain the association-dependencies, and supports the process of denoising via diffusion for adaptive imputability. Experiments across healthcare, finance, and Internet of Things (IoT) datasets show a consistent advantage for HyImpute over traditional models (mean imputation, KNN, MICE), generative models (GAN-based and VAE), and based diffusion (denoising) representations. The HyImpute results for reconstruction error (RMSE) are outperformed consistently in all datasets, and downstream classification tasks improved (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1). Overall, HyImpute is an efficient and scalable alternative to imputation for tabular datasets in real-world applications.

title: Spectral Graph Filtering for Long-Term Multivariate Time Series Forecasting
authors: Zongyao Feng, Konstantin Markov
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Long-term multivariate time series forecasting requires modeling complex spatiotemporal dependencies, yet existing approaches tend to focus on addressing the problem from the temporal perspective using channel-independent modeling, with few works exploring effective spatial modeling for long-term horizons. While recent methods use attention mechanisms to capture spatial interactions, they suffer from producing diffuse patterns when the number of variables increases. We propose a spectral graph filtering approach that transforms learned attention weights into graph Laplacians, enabling frequency-aware spatial modeling through Chebyshev polynomial approximations. This design treats the multivariate time series as signals over a graph, allowing the model to adaptively learn spatial patterns most critical for long-term forecasting through learnable spectral filters. Our method first projects temporal sequences into a latent space, computes attention-based adjacency matrices, then applies multi-scale spectral filtering to extract predictive spatial patterns. Experiments on three benchmarks, Electricity, Solar and Weather, demonstrate consistent improvements over the representative baselines, validate that frequency-domain spatial modeling offers a principled and scalable alternative to conventional attention mechanisms for long-term multivariate forecasting.

title: DEFF: A Distributed Edge Fusion Framework for Cybersecurity Incident Prediction
authors: Victor Chang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: Enterprise security operations centers evaluate millions of events daily, yet <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85-95 \%$</tex> are false positives or innocuous activities, causing analyst overload and delayed threat identification and response. Endpoint Detection identifies device vulnerabilities, while User Behavior Analytics detects anomalies in user behavior. They operate independently with limited detection capabilities. The Distributed Edge Fusion Framework (DEFF) enables network-edge agents to correlate various signals without centralizing raw data. Edge processors standardize vulnerability counts, patch age, and operating system risk and behavioral indicators (logon velocity, geographic disparities, and temporal entropy) before sending aggregated scores to a central system using weighted sigmoid activation functions. Central ensemble classifiers, utilizing Random Forest, XGBoost, and logistic regression, outperform community benchmarks <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.84-0.87)$</tex> by 6 % to 10 %. DEFF reduces false positives by 72 %, data transmission volume by 87 %, and detection delay by 92 %. GDPR and CCPA-compliant architecture protects privacy and enables precise detection. 13.6 million event reports demonstrate business system capability using real class distributions. DEFF shows that decentralized edge-level integration of diverse security signals meets operational, latency, and legal constraints better than centralized ensemble learning.

title: Eco-Pulse: an IoT-Based Multi-Sensor Environmental Monitoring and Air Quality Prediction System
authors: K. U. Gayathri, J. Kavipriya, S. Karthick
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Environmental surveillance has also become a crucial issue into the study and alleviation of the effects of urbanization and climate change on the quality of the air and ecological wellbeing. In this paper, the author introduces EcoPulse, a system of integrated environmental monitoring relying on IoT to combine the real-time collection of sensor data with a machine-based air quality forecasting. Arduino UNO R3 microcontroller with several environmental sensors (mq-135, MQ-2, DHT22) and ESP8266 NodeMCU is used to transmit the data wirelessly. The developed model was a Random Forest regression model, which predicted the values of Air Quality Index (AQI) with Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 5.38 and R2 of 0.220. Comparison of Eco-Pulse data with the reference data of Chennai revealed high correlation between temperature (r > 0.95) and humidity (r > 0.92) with the AQI monitoring approach revealing the mean expense of variation amounting to 3.2 units. The system was able to classify environmental conditions into four different zones with the use of K-means clustering with an accuracy of 0.81 with Gradient Boosting classification. The analysis of feature importance indicated that temperature was the most important predictor (importance=0.424), then humidity (0.341) and noise level (0.208). This was proved by continuous 24-hour monitoring, where the system could have constant environmental surveillance with a noise level reading of 48 to 78 dB.

title: A Multi-Object Tracking Framework Integrating Gait Features
authors: You Zhai, Minghua Li, Jianhua Xie, Kaicheng Yu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional MOT algorithms rely solely on appearance or motion features, and their robustness is insufficient due to constraints such as target occlusion and appearance changes. To address this issue, a multi-target tracking framework that integrates gait features to enhance tracking stability in complex scenarios is proposed. The framework constructs a multifeature fusion model of “appearance + motion + gait”. In the framework, YOLOv11 provides high-precision target detection boxes, DeepSORT realizes cross-frame target association through Kalman filtering and Hungarian algorithm, and GaitSet extracts gait features with strong scene adaptability, making up for the defects of traditional features in scenarios such as occlusion and clothing changes. The ReID appearance features and gait features are fused through dynamic weight adjustment. Short-term tracking mainly relies on appearance features, and long-term tracking mainly relies on gait features, forming a spatiotemporal complementary recognition system. Experimental results show that after fusing GaitSet, MOTA is further improved to 3.1%, and the number of identity switches (IDs) is reduced by 9.2%, verifying that gait information can enhance MOT performance in complex scenarios.

title: Lightweight CP-ABE Scheme with Puncturable Revocation for Smart Civil Aviation
authors: Lizhe Zhang, Yiao Ma, Zhijun Wu, Jin Lei, Lihan Yang, Ruiqi Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart civil aviation, the digital transformation in the civil aviation field has raised higher requirements for the security and efficiency of data sharing. However, the current access control mechanisms in civil aviation data sharing are still inadequate. Ciphertext-Policy AttributeBased Encryption (CP-ABE), as a flexible and efficient access control technique, provides effective security guarantees for data sharing. Nevertheless, traditional CP-ABE schemes based on bilinear pairing operations involve high computational complexity, making them unsuitable for resource-constrained terminals used in smart civil aviation. To address this problem, we proposed a lightweight CP-ABE scheme with puncturable revocation. This scheme uses the scalar multiplication operation on the elliptic curve to replace the complex bilinear pairing calculation, and the decryption operation is performed by the edge node, thereby reducing the computational overhead. At the same time, we use puncturable revocation to achieve fine-grained aviation data access control without requiring ciphertext re-encryption, thus reducing the revocation overhead. Through performance evaluation and security analysis, we verified that our scheme can meet the security and lightweight requirements of smart civil aviation.

title: Deep Learning and Explainable AI for Knee Osteoarthritis Diagnosis: A Comprehensive Review of Multitask Models, Fairness, and Diagnostic Interpretability
authors: Naveen V, Manivannan E, Munirathinam T, Velusamy A, Prabhu R, Vasudevan I
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Osteoarthritis severely affects the individual quality of life at a large scale of the disease through its extensive disease of the knee. This is because by combining Deep learning (DL) systems with explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques, it is possible to generate automatic interpretations of accurate diagnostic models in the classification of knee osteoarthritis. Current evaluation of knee OA analytic development introduces evaluations of the way in which DL and XAI approaches to knee evaluation assignments are carried out as well as assessing the datasets and significant research results applied. The paper investigates key factors, which are both related to multitask learning and feature selection and deep neural networks and fairness considerations in orthopedic imaging AI applications. The section discusses how different data types such as medical scans with gait patterns and medical records of the patient help in resolving the diagnosis issues and problems with disease classifications. The review draws an assessment of the current problems in AIbased knee OA evaluation procedures since it addresses the existing concerns on bias prevention and clarity of interpretation and then outlines possible ways to improve AI evaluation systems in terms of reliability and fairness.

title: VGGMobiNet: A Unified VGG16-MobileNetV2 Model for Brinjal Leaf Disease Analysis and Management
authors: Gogineni Manju Sri, Akavarapu Kavya Sri, Palaparthi Samarpan, Vangalla Veera Babu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This study describes a complete, automated, deep learning-based approach for diagnosing and recommending treatments for brinjal (eggplant) leaf diseases. A carefully designed hybrid architecture using VGG16 for feature extraction and MobileNetV2 for classification is employed as the backbone of the present method. The model has been trained using a dataset containing brinjal leaf images of both healthy and diseased categories, and it can detect with precision six diseases along with the healthy state: Insect Pest, Small Leaf, Leaf Spot, Mosaic Virus, White Mold, and Wilt Leaf. The system exhibits remarkable classification accuracy by making use of transfer learning and data augmentation techniques and at the same time reducing the requirement of manual feature engineering and computation overhead to a great extent, the model achieves a classification accuracy of 97 %. The small size of the hybrid model provides the advantage of fast inference and easy deployment in terms of resources, thereby making it suitable for real-time field applications and mobile-based agricultural systems. The entire agricultural sector, in general, and the smart agriculture sub-sector, in particular, will benefit from the proposed system through supporting early disease diagnosis, sustainable crop management, and increased agricultural productivity.

title: Simulation of Autonomous Navigation and Obstacle Avoidance Method for Smart Car based on SLAM Algorithm
authors: Yupeng Yin, Zhi Yang, Yunchang Xiao, Zixuan Long, Aoxiang Wang, Tengda Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Aiming at the autonomous navigation challenges of smart cars in narrow static environment and dynamic complex environment, this paper proposes an autonomous navigation and obstacle avoidance method based on SLAM algorithm. The improved A* algorithm is adopted, the dynamic heuristic function is introduced, and the B-spline curve post-processing is combined to significantly optimize the smoothness of the path; the improved IRRT* method integrates the target offset sampling and the surrounding sampling of obstacles, and embeds the DWA dynamic window mechanism to improve the dynamic obstacle avoidance ability. The experimental results show that the improved A* algorithm reduces the inflection point by 45%; the convergence speed of the improved IRRT* algorithm is increased by 30%, and the success rate of dynamic obstacle avoidance is 92.3%. The system is significantly superior to traditional methods in path quality and real-time performance, and provides reliable technical support for intelligent storage, emergency rescue and other scenarios.

title: Enhancing Channel Randomness with Dynamic Metal Plate Scattering: A Computational and Simulational Study
authors: Boqian Liu, Jiabao Yu, Aiqun Hu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper presents a hardware-based approach for improving wireless channel randomness through vibratile metallic scatterings. Parametric modeling in SolidWorks enabled the creation of geometrically diverse metallic scattering designs, which were then computationally assembled in MATLAB for electromagnetic wave interaction analysis. The investigation combined radar cross section (RCS) theory with deterministic raytracing models to characterize wave refraction and scattering behaviors across the randomized metallic structures. Quantitative evaluation using the Hilbert-Schmidt Independence Criterion (HSIC) demonstrated that the number and shape of metallic scatterings critically affect channel independence. The simulation results verified that this approach enhances the randomness of wireless channels through the non-stationary scattering effect induced by mechanical vibration, therefore achieving improved cryptographic security and robustness against eavesdropping for the communication system.

title: Improvised HPR Multiplier Using Fast Bipartitioned Hybrid Adder by Approximate Computing
authors: K. S. Neelu Kumari, P. Agniraj, I. Azarudeen, P. Chandraprakash
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The triple modular redundancy (TMR) scheme is generally used to build a fault-tolerant arithmetic unit in critical applications that require higher reliability. While TMR is effective, it also incurs a significant area and power/energy overhead. The primary voter in most TMR designs can also be highly susceptible to soft errors and may result in an error for only a small difference between the modules' outputs. There is also a wide spectrum of applications deployed in critical systems that are inherently error-resilient. In particular, they can tolerate some degree of inexactness at their output while still meeting some reliability level. In this paper, propose a high-precision redundancy multiplier (HPR-Mul) architecture, implementing principles of approximate computing, augmented with a Fast Bipartitioned Hybrid Adder (FBHA) to achieve higher performance while still meeting the prescribed reliability level. The HPR-Multiplier consists of one full-precision (FP) and two reduced-precision (RP) multipliers, together with a simple voter to arrive at an output. Unlike most current <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R P}$</tex> redundancy multipliers (RPR-Multiplier) that require a complicated voter, the voter of the present HPR-Mul architecture relies on mathematical formulas resulting in a much simpler structure. In addition, the intermediate signals of the FP multiplier are employed as inputs to the RP multipliers thus greatly improving HPR-Mul accuracy. The main contribution of this paper is to replace the HPR-Mul's traditional summation block that suffers from delay, with a Fast Binary Hierarchical Adder (FBHA), a high-speed adder and architecture that utilizes parallel carry-lookahead and carryselect logic. This change is to target the multiplier's critical path to reduce delay and improve performance overall.

title: Contrastive-Enhanced Multi-Task Learning for Chip Pin Defect Detection under Low-Light Conditions
authors: Gongsheng Zhu, Shuo Wang, Dongqin Hu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Chip pin structural information provides detailed insights into chip pin defect detection in electronic manufacturing. However, the pin data associated with defects is often compromised by suboptimal lighting conditions and noise in low-light industrial environments, which impedes the accurate detection of chip pin defects due to degraded visual quality. Additionally, traditional methods that separate image enhancement from defect detection struggle because they fail to preserve defect-discriminative features after enhancement. As a result, some defective chips go undetected, while others are incorrectly identified as non-defective (false positives). This study introduces a contrastive enhanced multi-task learning framework to address these challenges. Specifically, we first extract more robust feature representations using a contrastive learning mechanism, which are then utilized as structural priors for identifying defective chips. Concurrently, multiple task optimization constraints are incorporated. Experimental results on benchmark and industrial datasets demonstrate excellent performance and generalization capabilities, with detection accuracy increasing by an average of 5.2% and the F1 score rising by 3.8%. The results further indicate that this approach significantly outperforms existing solutions when tested on the latest state-of-the-art chip pin evaluation dataset.

title: Federated Deep Learning for Intrusion Detection in Vehicle-to-Vehicle Networks: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Ranjima P, Devi Kannan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication functions as a core technology for Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) because it allows vehicles to exchange safety data in real-time for enhanced traffic control and improved road safety. The open nature of V2V networks together with their ability to move freely creates significant security risks because they can be easily targeted by cyber-attacks which include spoofing and denial-of-service and new adversarial machine learning threats. The operation of traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) becomes difficult because they experience scalability issues and high latency and limited adaptability in dynamic environments. Deep learning (DL) shows excellent ability to detect intricate and changing intrusion patterns. The decentralized structure of federated learning (FL) maintains user privacy protection through its distributed operation which matches the characteristics of vehicular networks. The survey provides an extensive evaluation of DL- and FL-based IDS systems which operate in V2V networks. The paper introduces a systematic classification system which organizes security threats and IDS architectures and federated aggregation techniques and performs an evaluation of available datasets and tools and robustness approaches. The research paper outlines future study paths to create explainable IDS systems which will scale and remain resilient for future vehicular networks.

title: A Gesture-Based Virtual Typing Interface
authors: Gannoji Durga Prasad, Amireddy SreeLasya, Sowmith Sharan Vallaji, Ch. Sreenivasa Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Human-computer interaction (HCI) systems that are both inventive and intuitive are in high demand in today’s rapidly changing digital world. Even though physical keyboards and touchscreens are efficient, they frequently have drawbacks in situations that call for contactless or hands-free interaction. This project presents the idea and execution of a virtual keyboard using hand gestures, a system intended to replicate keyboard input using real-time recognition and interpretation of human hand movements. The system uses deep learning models such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and computer vision and machine learning algorithms, including Mediapipe, to track and detect the location and movement of hand joints. Users can input data in a smooth manner by making hand movements in front of a camera by mapping these gestures to particular characters or commands. The system’s main components are gesture classification, hand landmark detection, and a virtual interface that looks like a keyboard. Particularly useful applications for this virtual keyboard include augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), assistive technology for people with limited mobility, and public terminals where touch-based input presents hygienic concerns. To ensure practical usability in a variety of environments, the project places a strong emphasis on high accuracy, low latency, and user adaptability. To evaluate the robustness and efficacy of the created system, it was tested in various lighting scenarios and hand orientations. With precise gesture recognition and seamless user interaction, the results indicate encouraging possibilities for real-world deployment. Multilingual support, gesture customisation, and integration with current operating systems are possible future enhancements.

title: A Review of Deep Learning-Based Video Analysis Techniques for Quantitative Performance Monitoring
authors: Gang Xu, Hongli Yu, Qiang Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The expansion of video data and progress in deep learning have transformed quantitative performance monitoring in various sectors, including industry, healthcare, and surveillance. This study offers an extensive examination of deep learning methodologies for video analysis employed in quantitative performance assessment. The research categorizes current methodologies according to their fundamental techniques, including CNNs, RNNs, 3D CNNs, transformers, and multimodal fusion. We analyze their applications, performance benchmarks, and assessment metrics. Additionally, we address prevalent problems, including real-time processing limitations, interpretability, domain adaption, and data privacy concerns. It culminates with rising trends and prospective research avenues to augment the efficacy and implementation of video-based performance monitoring systems.

title: Research on Optimization of AIGC Technology for Enhanced Short Video Creation Processes
authors: Zhiyong Wang, Kewen Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This research present an AIGC-accelerated pipeline that reduces 60-s video production time from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8\text{-}12~\mathrm{h}$</tex> to 45–90 min (85 %) and cost from $150–300 to $15–40 (90%). A modular API couples GAN-based frame synthesis, ViT temporal modeling and diffusion post-processing; reinforcement-learning policy gradients optimize engagement proxies while actor-critic training stabilizes convergence. Progressive resolution and knowledge distillation cut GPU memory by 40 %. Cloud rendering with VMAF-guided QA delivers 87/100 quality at 94 % human-level retention and 94 % interaction rate. A/B tests across 10 k TikTok clips show <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times$</tex> more variants (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0}-\text{5 0}$</tex> vs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1}-\text{3}$</tex>) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 0} \times$</tex> language coverage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$120+$</tex> vs 1-2). The system embeds into Premiere/DaVinci via plugins and supports federated fine-tuning for cultural or brand customization within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 - 1 0} \%$</tex> of original training cycles. Ethical safeguards include blockchain watermarking and EU AI Act compliance.

title: Multi Agent Economic Operation of Hydrogen Integrated Energy System Taking into Account Carbon Trading
authors: Luming Ci
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: With the increasingly significant challenges of environmental pollution and energy consumption, integrated energy systems have become a key area of research because of their ability to couple multiple energy sources and flexibly coordinate various energy consumption. In order to further reduce the carbon emission of the integrated energy system and optimize the system structure, this paper introduces hydrogen energy as a clean energy source and guides the system to operate in a low-carbon manner by means of the carbon trading mechanism. This study establishes a mathematical model by analyzing the structure of the hydrogen-containing integrated energy system, applies the idea of target cascade method, builds a two-tier structure of "hydrogen storage station - park", and proposes a multi agent optimal dispatch strategy of the hydrogen-containing integrated energy system taking into account the carbon trading mechanism with the optimization objectives of l ow carbon and operational cost. An example analysis is carried out to study the influence of carbon trading mechanism on the low-carbon nature of the hydrogen-containing integrated energy system, and the effectiveness and feasibility of the operation strategy are verified.

title: An AI Ensemble Framework for Cross-Layer State Identification and Throughput Prediction in 5G/6G Wireless Networks
authors: Naween Kumar, Aarthi Sivasankaran, Ankit Dubey, Balamurugan Balusamy, Rose Bindu Joseph P
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The 5G, 6G, and IoT have resulted in modern wireless networks that are complex and non-linear in interactions between cross-layer parameters that could not be sufficiently captured in traditional analytical models. This paper discusses these issues through the lens of unsupervised and supervised learning in the form of a data-driven AI framework. Exploratory Data Analysis showed weak linear relationships, which led to applying PCA and the K-Means clustering to identify three different states of operation networks. We subsequently analyze several regression models in the throughput prediction, with the results indicating that the traditional models, such as Linear Regression and SVR, do not provide strong results (RMSE 11, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.76-0.84). On the contrary, more sophisticated ensemble techniques, such as Random Forest, Histogram-based Gradient Boosting, and Stacking, perform almost perfectly (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> =1.000, RMSE 0.028). Our findings prove that AI-based ensemble models are better at predicting network performance accurately and proactively to facilitate smart and proactive governance of resources.

title: Hybrid CNN–BiLSTM Model for Mental Health State Recognition from Audio-Visual Signals
authors: Nikita Thakur, Rohit Bajaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Mental health prediction plays a crucial role in early diagnosis and preventive care. The proposed study develops a multimodal Artificial Intelligence (AI) framework that predicts emotional and psychological states using both speech and facial cues. The model integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks to capture spatial and temporal dependencies in audio–visual data. Speech features such as Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC), spectral centroid, and chroma components are fused with CNN-derived facial features to form a comprehensive feature representation. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the hybrid CNN–BiLSTM model achieves an accuracy of 95.3%, surpassing unimodal architectures. Performance metrics including precision, recall, and F1-score confirm the reliability and generalization capability of the proposed approach. Explainability tools such as Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) are incorporated to highlight critical facial regions and influential acoustic features contributing to prediction outcomes. The study establishes an interpretable, real-time, and non-invasive mental health prediction framework that supports clinical applications and enhances psychological well-being through intelligent human–computer interaction.

title: Higher-order Monte Carlo cluster dynamics for community detection in Euclidean graphs
authors: Louis Hauseux, Nahuel Soprano-Loto, Konstantin Avrachenkov
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We study Gibbs distributions with competing interactions and propose a higher-order extension of the Swendsen-Wang dynamics that incorporates triangular bonds. The new dynamics preserves the same stationary distribution, alleviates frustration, and yields markedly better sampling. When applied to a synthetic Euclidean-graph community-detection benchmark, our algorithm outperforms existing methods.

title: TID Impact on 30nm NC FinFET with PZT oxide
authors: Suman Lata Tripathi, Neeraj Nayan Prakash, Balwinder Raj
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper presents a detailed work on the influence of Total Ionizing Dose (TID) radiation on the electrical behaviour of both conventional FinFET and Negative Capacitance FinFET structures. The NC FinFET configuration integrates a ferroelectric PZT (lead zirconate titanate) layer while benchmark comparisons were carried out using SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> as standard gate oxides. All simulations were executed using advanced 3D TCAD environments to capture the degradation in essential device metric including threshold voltage, subthreshold slope (SS) and off-state leakage current under increasing radiation exposure. The simulated structures were defined with a gate length of 30nm fin width of 10nm fin height of 70nm and oxide thickness of 3nm. Precise doping concentrations were implemented with 2 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> in the source/drain regions and 1 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">16</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> in the channel to replicate practical device behaviour. This analysis emphasizes the importance of material selection and structural tuning to enhance device resilience under ionizing radiation. The incorporation of PZT in NC FinFET is shown to improve stability against radiation-induced defects due to its inherent polarization characteristics, effectively reducing charge trapping and threshold voltage shifts compared to FinFET with conventional high-k dielectrics.

title: High Level Synthesis Design for Soft-Input GRAND
authors: Tuomas Aaltonen, Mikko Valkama, Syed Mohsin Abbas
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Guessing Random Additive Noise Decoding (GRAND) is a universal channel code decoder for short linear block codes. GRAND can be implemented efficiently on hardware and features both hard-input and soft-input variants. Hardware implementations based on GRAND range from those that employ sequential error pattern generation to reduce resource consumption at the expense of higher decoding latency to those that employ parallel error pattern generation to reduce decoding latency at the expense of higher resource utilization. In this work, we present a soft-input Ordered Reliability Bits GRAND (ORBGRAND) hardware implementation framework based on High-Level Synthesis (HLS). The proposed HLS-based methodology simplifies Design Space Exploration (DSE) for ORBGRAND hardware implementations targeted at applications with varying constraints on decoding latency and resource utilization. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first-of-its-kind HLS-based soft-input ORBGRAND hardware implementation framework, and the source code for the proposed framework is provided under a permissive open-source license.

title: Omnidirectional Recognition of Medical Emergency Situations Through Body Gesture Using 360-Degree Camera
authors: Cherry Joy L. Escoña, Anne Jeline R. Lazaga, Ma. Aubrey D. Mensote, Lea Grace C. Obtinalla, Sherwin Vasquez, Engr. Roselito E. Tolentino
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents an omnidirectional system for recognizing medical emergency situations through body gestures. This research addresses the significant limitations of previous systems which often relies on traditional cameras with a restricted field-of-view. A 360-degree camera has been utilized by the system for omnidirectional gesture recognition of body gestures. The researchers constructed MediaPipe for real-time pose estimation and a Random Forest classifier trained on five emergency gestures: headache, cough, chest pain, vomiting, and fall. The system achieved a gesture recognition reliability of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 3 7 \%}$</tex> across multiple angles and distances, and a combined recognition-response success rate of 96.2 % in robot-assisted medicine dispensing and emergency alert triggering. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of omnidirectional vision in enabling robust, angle-independent gesture recognition suitable for medical monitoring applications.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy-Neural AI Models for Dynamic Environment Control
authors: Vamsi Krishna Velpuri, Mayank Atreya, Prashanthi Pala, Ponnarasan Krishnan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Adaptive control strategies are required in dynamic systems, like smart buildings, autonomous vehicles, and industrial IoT systems that need to be able to deal with uncertainty, nonlinearity, and fast contextual variation. In these conditions, the traditional methods that are rule-based or neural approaches in general do not tend to be robust and interpretable. The paper suggests Adaptive Fuzzy-Neural AI Models which integrate the power of reasoning of fuzzy logic with that of neural networks to adaptively and in real-time make decisions. The suggested models dynamically adapt fuzzy membership functions using neural adaptation and could finetune them in highly volatile and unstructured environments. It proposes a hybrid framework that combines fuzzy reasoning and deep reinforcement learning to achieve context-dependent adaptation. The experimental tests which are done on benchmark dynamic control situations such as climate regulation, swarm robotics navigation and smart grid load balancing, have shown faster convergence speeds and stability and accuracy over traditional fuzzy systems or neural systems. The findings indicate that the adaptive fuzzy-neural structure can decrease the error rates to a maximum of 18, increase resilience to noisy inputs and can effectively scale to more complex systems. The results define the possibility of the fuzzyneural AI as a powerful paradigm of intelligent control in dynamic, safety-critical, and resource-constrained settings.

title: Commitee
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)

title: Mapping of Habitat Suitability for Endemic and Vulnerable Flora in Caraga Region using Ensemble Species Distribution Modelling
authors: Richielyn May G. Soso, Dominique D. Magpatoc, Arturo G. Cauba, Romell A. Seronay
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Shorea contorta and Shorea negrosensis are Philippine endemic species classified as vulnerable under DAO 2017-11. This study modeled their habitat suitability and potential distribution in the Caraga Region under current climate conditions using ensemble species distribution modeling. Georeferenced presence points (37 for S. contorta and 79 for S. negrosensis) and 29 initial predictor variables were used in two approaches: (1) environmental variables only and (2) both environmental and anthropogenic variables. The models showed excellent performance, with AUC and TSS scores of $\mathbf{1. 0}$ for $\mathbf{S}$. contorta across both approaches, and 0.97 and 0.86 (approach 1) and 0.98 and 0.88 (approach 2) for S. negrosensis. The results indicate that both species exhibit distinct habitat suitability patterns with differing proportions of highly, moderately, lowly, and not suitable, influenced by anthropogenic factors with a decreasing trend of highly suitable areas as anthropogenic variables are included, highlighting the detrimental effect of human-induced factors.

title: Bias-Enhanced Successive Cancellation List Decoder for Polar Codes
authors: Jiajie Li, Sihui Shen, Warren J. Gross
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Polar codes with the successive cancellation (SC) de-coder are proven to achieve the channel capacity asymptotically. However, polar codes with SC decoding suffer from performance degradation when decoding short-to-medium length polar codes. The successive cancellation list (SCL) decoder improves the decoding performance of the SC decoder when decoding short-to-medium-length polar codes. The SCL decoder faces many implementation challenges, such as the decoding latency and the area overhead. A recently proposed perturbation-enhanced (PE) SCL decoder with a list size of L reaches the error correction performance of the SCL decoder with a list size of 2L. However, the reason behind this improved decoding performance needs to be investigated. An ablation study on the PE SCL decoder is performed in this work, and we found that the bias added to the received soft information is key for improved error correction performance. Hence, we propose to remove the random noise added to the PE SCL decoder, and we call our simplified decoder the bias-enhanced decoder. From experimental results, our bias-enhanced SCL decoder returns similar decoding performance to the PE SCL decoder under various code lengths and rates.

title: Lineage Diffusion Models
authors: Bahman Moraffah, Raha Moraffah
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Latent variables often depend on directed graph structure. Many existing models impose simple trees or local independence, which limits their ability to represent uncertainty and long-range relationships under noise or missing data. These restrictions reduce the reliability of latent-state inference on general directed acyclic graphs. Lineage Diffusion Models (LDM) define a continuous diffusion process over latent variables placed on the nodes of a known DAG. A graph neural network approximates the reverse-time score so that the model supports structured, uncertainty-aware Bayesian inference on graph-conditioned latent spaces. The method handles multi-parent structure, incomplete observations, and variable graph density. Experiments show that LDM achieves higher inference accuracy and stronger uncertainty calibration than hierarchical Bayesian and graph-VAE baselines.

title: Ziv-Zakai Bound for Near-Field Localization and Sensing
authors: Nicolò Decarli, Davide Dardari
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The increasing carrier frequencies and growing physical dimensions of antenna arrays in modern wireless systems are driving renewed interest in localization and sensing under near-field conditions. In this paper, we analyze the Ziv-Zakai Bound (ZZB) for near-field localization and sensing operated with large antenna arrays, which offers a tighter characterization of estimation accuracy compared to traditional bounds such as the Cramér-Rao Bound (CRB), especially in low signal-to-noise ratio or threshold regions. Leveraging spherical wavefront and array geometry in the signal model, we evaluate the ZZB for distance and angle estimation, investigating the dependence of the accuracy on key signal and system parameters such as array geometry, wavelength, and target position. Our analysis highlights the transition behavior of the ZZB and underscores the fundamental limitations and opportunities for accurate near-field sensing.

title: Ease and Equity of Point of Interest Accessibility via Public Transit in the U.S.
authors: Alexander Li, Aurimas Racas, Junaid Syed, Mengyang Liu, Przemek Zientala, Tejas Santanam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Equitable access to essential Points of Interest (POIs) such as healthcare facilities and grocery stores via public transit is a critical urban planning challenge. This paper introduces an interactive tool that analyzes the ease and equity of such access in major U.S. cities. Using a 2-step floating catchment area (2SFCA) approach, we calculate granular accessibility indices and present them on an interactive, web-based platform. A key contribution of this work is a scenario analysis feature that allows users to simulate the impact of adding or removing POIs and visualize the resulting changes in accessibility equity. Our analysis reveals significant disparities across demographic groups, and we further utilize machine learning models to demonstrate a strong, quantifiable link between accessibility scores and neighborhood racial composition, highlighting systemic inequities. The tool provides a practical framework for policymakers to make more informed and equitable infrastructure decisions.

title: An Innovative Wavelet Neural Network Framework for Glaucoma Detection using Statistical and Graph-Based Feature Analysis of Retinal Images
authors: Monika Khatkar, Naman Pratap Singh Nain, Sandeep Mathur, Nyangam Rime, Jagdeesh Ahirwar, B. Sireesha
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A complete automated system for diagnosing glaucoma is introduced in this study using a Wavelet Neural Network (WNN). First, the method applies a strong retinal image pre-processing to improve strength and clarity using Quantile-based histogram altering and using a Quadratic Rank Transmutation Map on the image's cumulative distribution function. A two-stage image enhancement process is applied next, using multi-scale morphological operations, dynamically produced structuring elements and Dual-Tree Complex Wavelet Transform (DT-CWT) coefficients. After improving the retinal images, a scheme combining different types of features is used. Analysis is performed by applying biorthogonal wavelets to measure first-order, second-order, higher-order cumulant (HoC) and higher-order spectral (HoS) metrics. At the same time, graph features are generated using Local Graph Structures (LGS) and Graph Shortest Paths (GSP) in various directions, so that both nearby and wide-ranging textures of retinal pictures are retained. A combination of statistics and structural characteristics produces the final feature vector which means learning patterns is more reliable. WNN, with Mexican hat wavelet-based activation functions, is put to work for classification as a method. Tests on ORIGA, ACRIMA and DRISHTI datasets prove that our WNN model shows higher accuracy and lower error than MLP, confirming its usefulness for identifying early glaucoma. Overall, the suggested technique gets a 96.36% success rate on the ORIGA dataset. This points to a very dependable glaucoma categorization system.

title:
Structured Grid-Based G
                    <sup>1</sup>
                    Cubic Trigonometric Triangular Spline Surface with Quantitative Error Evaluation
authors: Muhammad Syawal Abd Halim, Normi Abdul Hadi, Rusliza Ahmad, Syaiful Anam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Digitization of an object from visual to data point can help researchers manipulate the data for further use - particularly in reconstructing a surface. The use of trigonometric function in generating a triangular surface offers a smooth surface with flexibility as ensued from its derivation and cyclability advantages. This study used a structured 3-dimensional data of a human head as control points to reconstruct a surface using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G^{1}$</tex> cubic trigonometric triangular spline with rational basis functions. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G^{1}$</tex> continuity was used to generate a smooth surface with rational basis functions that were closer to the control points, as the root mean square error (RMSE) and maximum error were smaller. The generated human head surface was illustrated in this study.

title: An Optimized Multidimensional Cloud Load Migration Method Based on Three-Way Decision
authors: Xinyu Ma, Jianxin Gai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Dynamic changes in cloud load become a significant challenge for cloud resource management today. To address this issue, a three-way decision enhanced strategy for multidimensional cloud load migration control (MLCM-3WD) is proposed in this paper. First, in the virtual machine selection stage, the virtual machine migration control method is combined with the idea of three-way decision. The virtual machine multidimensional resource consumption rate (MRCR) is divided into three parts, and the set of virtual machines with small MRCR is selected. Then, in the VM placement phase, the improved single-step reinforcement learning strategy is used to place the set of VMs with low MRCR onto the physical machine with the highest multidimensional resource balance, completing the VM migration process. Finally, experiments are conducted on the CloudSimPlus platform. The results show that the proposed migration strategy can more effectively reduce energy consumption while controlling the number of virtual machine migrations.

title: An Adaptive Tuning Method for Neutron Spectrum Unfolding Based on Supervised-Autoencoder with Few-Channel Detection
authors: Chenglong Cao, Pei Cao, Qingbo Zhou, Ming Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The neutron spectrum plays a critical role in ensuring reactor operational safety. However, online neutron spectrum measurement methods still face challenges in achieving high accuracy. Although multilayer perceptron (MLP)-based artificial neural networks (ANNs) have been applied in spectrum unfolding, their performance is often limited by insufficient training data and convergence difficulties. To address these issues, this study developed an adaptive tuning method for neutron spectrum unfolding based on Supervised-Autoencoder with few-channel detection. A large dataset of diverse neutron spectra from fast reactors was simulated by a Monte Carlo program. Simulated detector counts were generated using pre-calibrated response functions. An autoencoder was employed to encode and decode spectral data, enabling the trained decoder to rapidly and accurately predict neutron spectra directly from detector counts. The method was validated using a spectral library comprising neutron spectra from the CLEAR-I, RBEC-M, and BN-600 fast reactor cores. Results demonstrated that, compared to conventional MLP-based approaches, the proposed method reduced the mean squared error (MSE) by 67.34%, the average relative deviation (ARD) by 50.37%, and the spectrum quality (Qs) by 42.55%, confirming its improved reliability in neutron spectrum unfolding.

title: Resource-Aware Multiobjective Neural Architecture Search for Low-Light Image Enhancement on Mobile Devices
authors: Bin Cao, Haixuan Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: In the nighttime low-light setting, there is not enough light and the contrast is low. This greatly lowers the accuracy of detecting pedestrians and vehicles in mobile intelligent transportation systems. As a result, it raises the chance of traffic accidents. To address this problem, this paper presents the resource-aware multiobjective neural architecture search for low-light image enhancement (RAMONAS-LIE). This method constructs a lightweight deep curve estimation network in a dynamic way. It improves the visual quality of low-light images by adjusting brightness at the pixel level. In addition, to improve search efficiency and variety in architectures, it introduces a dualcriteria elite selection evolutionary algorithm (NSGAIII-DCES). Experimental results show that RAMONAS-LIE provides the better image enhancement, demonstrating its practicality for mobile applications.

title: Reviewers
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)

title: Deep Learning Approach for Parkinson's Disease Detection Using Voice Feature
authors: Esha, Nagesh U B, Manojrao, Oliva Mary Fernandes, Srajesh Shetty
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Parkinson's disease moves in quietly, progressing over time, so catching it early without invasive tests is crucial. This study presents a detailed framework that uses subtle changes in voice like a faint pitch tremor or a slight shimmer to help identify Parkinson's disease. We compare seven models ranging from classic machine learning classifiers to a 1D Convolutional Neural Network using the standardized Parkinson's Voice Dataset, where each voice clip carries the faint tremor of the condition. Our results show that ensemble methods work best for diagnosing voices, and the XGBoost classifier topped the charts with a 94.87 percentage accuracy rate. Alongside the voice module, the system packs a second unit built to analyze spiral and wave images, tracing their curves as if following ripples in water. This module uses a pre-trained CNN and applies Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to make its predictions easier to understand, lighting up the exact parts of a drawing like a dark corner or a curved line that steer its decision. The study confirms that voice features can predict outcomes with striking accuracy, sets a high bar for performance, and shows that an integrated, multimodal, explainable AI system could realistically guide future clinical decisions much like a clear, confident voice cutting through background noise.

title: Intelligent Watering System for Crop Fields Using IoT-Based Automation and Environmental Sensing
authors: Mohammad Maroof Siddiqui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Optimal use of water is the most critical factor in agricultural productivity, particularly in water-short and erratic-rainfall areas. Conventional irrigation frequently leads to water waste and low crop yield, translate into high operational costs. This paper presents the design and deployment of IWS (Intelligent Watering System) for crop fields using Internet of Things (IoT) technologies and Environmental Sensors. It can sense soil moisture, temperature and humidity and adds intelligence to irrigation process using real-time data analytics. In this system intended to make better use of water and get the highest crop yield out of it, with a goal toward reducing human input as well as time and money. In addition, wireless communication modules are incorporated for remote monitoring and control, and the decision-making algorithms can realize the adaptive response of field status based on changes. The proposed solution is scalable and inexpensive, which can be employed for both small and large farms. Using IoT based platforms and sensor networks, the proposed system has a potential applicability for sustainable farming practices, environmental stress reduction as well as precision agriculture in water stressed areas.

title: Feedback-Assisted Decentralized Q-Learning for Dynamic Spectrum Access
authors: Ghilas Aissou, Salman Habib, Rémi A. Chou, Taejoon Kim
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We present an approach to Decentralized Dynamic Spectrum Access (DDSA) in temporally correlated frequency channels. The problem is formulated as a Decentralized Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (Dec-POMDP) to capture the inherent partial observability and non-stationarity in DDSA. We first propose a novel state representation technique that captures the observation history, providing the temporal context needed to mitigate partial observability. This representation scales linearly with the length of the history window, avoiding the exponential state space growth of traditional history-based methods. The state representation evaluates a score function over the history to achieve a controllable aliasing error, with increasing computational overhead with window size. Building on this representation, we propose a decentralized Q-learning algorithm with a two-phase reward structure driven by individual 1-bit feedback sent by the receiver and a global 1-bit feedback sent by a moderator. The non-stationarity in Dec-POMDPs is addressed by a policy update mechanism that leverages the 1-bit global feedback to align agent behaviors and stabilize learning. The proposed algorithm is then validated through simulation.

title: Brain-DGCNN: A Multi-Scale Dynamic Graph Neural Network for Parkinson's Disease Diagnosis
authors: Ruizhe Zhong, Huan Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Early diagnosis of Parkinson's disease (PD) is an important prerequisite so that effective clinical interventions can be provided timely. Although graph neural network (GNN)-based models have recently demonstrated their potential advantage in analyzing resting-state fMRI, existing GNN methods all take the fixed graphs built upon static threshold as input without considering the adaptive nature characteristics of functional connections. In view of this problem, here we present a new Brain fMRI-Based Dynamic Graph Convolutional Network (Brain-DGCNN) framework for predicting whether the subjects belong to PD and HC, respectively. Different from previous studies based on fixed graph structures, our proposed Brain-DGCNN does not need any predefined adjacency matrices. Instead, we use dynamic edge convolution to rebuild topology structures using k-nearest neighbors in feature spaces layer by layer, which could well discover intrinsic long-distance functional connections. Experiments show that our method outperforms the compared baselines with an accuracy of 95.16%, F1-score of 95.15%, and AUC-ROC of 99%. Our result shows that it is beneficial to learn the topology dynamically rather than fix it in advance when predicting PD from fMRI data, providing a new idea for the accurate diagnosis of PD.

title: Wideband Digital Post-Distortion for Improved Power-Efficiency and Coverage in OFDMA Networks
authors: Huseyin Babaroglu, Lauri Anttila, Oskari Tervo, Esa Tiirola, Mikko Valkama
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper introduces a novel digital post-distortion (DPoD) technique designed to improve the received signal quality in multi-user orthogonal frequency-division multiple access (OFDMA) systems under severe power amplifier (PA) nonlinearities in the involved transmitters. The proposed approach combines memory polynomial-based nonlinear distortion modeling and cancellation with practical parameter estimation using demodulation reference signals (DMRS). Importantly, a new extended DMRS structure and corresponding receiver signal processing solutions are proposed which enable the receiver to mitigate both the ordinary pass-band nonlinearities as well as nonlinear crosstalk or interference between multiple frequency-multiplexed transmitters operating simultaneously within the channel bandwidth. Extensive numerical evaluations conducted in a 5G NR uplink scenario at 28GHz demonstrate significant receiver performance improvements, measured through effective error vector magnitudes (EVMs) for the frequency-multiplexed waveforms. The results validate the method’s effectiveness even under aggressive PA nonlinearities and high-order modulation schemes including 64-QAM and 256-QAM, thus paving the way towards improved power-efficiency and coverage in future OFDMA networks.

title: Wireless Link Scheduling with State-Augmented Graph Neural Networks
authors: Romina Garcia Camargo, Zhiyang Wang, Navid NaderiAlizadeh, Alejandro Ribeiro
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We study a long-term formulation of link scheduling in large-scale wireless networks. Existing approaches maximize the instantaneous sum rate; however, while wireless networks exhibit highly dynamic behavior, end users generally experience performance in expectation. This motivates the optimization of time-averaged performance metrics. We formulate link scheduling as a constrained optimization problem in which the objective is to maximize the sum rate averaged over a series of time slots. Furthermore, we impose a minimum transmission requirement that guarantees each link is scheduled for at least a fixed fraction of the total time. We represent the network by modeling users and links as nodes and edges and construct the corresponding conflict graph to capture interference. We operate in the Lagrangian dual domain and parameterize the scheduling policy with a Graph Neural Network (GNN). To address the challenge of long-term performance optimization, we adopt a state-augmentation technique: by incorporating the Lagrangian dual variables as dynamic inputs to the GNN, the policy can gradually adapt its scheduling decisions to satisfy the time-average constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed policy through numerical simulations.

title: Personalised and Secured Melanoma Diagnosis Using Meta-Learning Driven Decentralised Framework
authors: Sudha D., R. Naresh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Imperative necessity in the healthcare systems is of compelling need to be addressed for earlier detection and diagnosis, where Melanoma. In this dermis-based cancer, early detection saves the patient from invasive treatments. But the stages of melanoma develop at a higher rate, it becomes the deadliest one as melanoma is the most lethal cancer. The growing Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) helps to collect patient data and sends it to the hospital rapidly. To maintain privacy and data integrity, the decentralised system based on a Federated learning system is an efficient way of communicating data in the network. But it lacks in personalisation and is affected by model poisoning attacks. To overcome such issues, the proposed work uses a meta-learningbased Federated learning model that updates the local patient and hospital client based on the meta gradient updates, making the system more dynamic and reducing attacks as the models' updates change continuously. Based on this, the proposed framework was simulated using the PH2 and ISIC 2020 datasets, yielding a 89% Global AUROC. When the proposed work was poisoned by 30%, an 85% Global AUROC was achieved.

title: Detecting Online Hate Using Emotion, Sarcasm, and Visual Cues: A Multimodal Deep Learning Approach
authors: Pratha Shetty, Ravindra B V, Nisha, Adarsh, Ethan Hadley Rodrigues
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Hate speech is racing across social networks, choking the online space with danger, as bitter smoke fills a packed room. Traditional text models often overlook subtle hate, especially when it sneaks in as a sarcastic jab, a clipped, biting tone, or a meme whose grin cuts deeper than the caption. This study introduces a multimodal deep learning framework that brings together text, emotion, sarcasm, and visual signals, such as a quick smile or a sharp tone to boost detection accuracy. The model blends DeBERTa (Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention)'s nuanced understanding of text with DistilBERT (Distilled Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers)'s ability to capture emotional and sarcastic cues, and CNN (Convolutional Neural Network)'s sharp eye for visual details enhanced by OCR (Optical Character Recognition) for embedded text, weaving them together to spot hateful content with precision. The tests revealed that the method beats single-modality baselines, catching hateful language with sharper accuracy and a better grasp of context, such as detecting the sting in an offhand remark.

title: LLM-Assisted Ontology-Based Task-Oriented Data Exchange Paradigm: A Case Study
authors: Shiyao Zhang, Xue Feng, Yuji Dong, Jie Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Smart cities depend on cross-domain data exchange, yet its effectiveness is limited by semantic heterogeneity and privacy-induced information asymmetry, which complicate traditional integration. Although ontology alignment can resolve semantic disparities, it struggles under incomplete knowledge conditions. Leveraging the emergent capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), this paper introduces an LLM-centric, task-oriented paradigm that automates ontology alignment and enables data exchange under realistic constraints. A case study demonstrates the approach's practicality in facilitating semantic interoperability without full information disclosure, highlighting its potential for privacy-aware smart city applications.

title: Intelligent AI-Based Service Provisioning and Task Offloading for Fog-Cloud Environment
authors: Swathi Pacha, Meenu, Prem Ranjan, Amit Kumar Dubey, Vishwa Nath Sharma, Ritu Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In the era of Internet of Things (IoT) and the cumulative request for real-time services, the integration of Fog and Cloud computing has emerged as a promising solution for efficient resource management and task offloading. This paper presents an intelligent AI-based service provisioning and task offloading framework for Fog-Cloud environments. The proposed model leverages machine learning techniques to dynamically allocate resources, optimize task distribution, and reduce latency, thereby enhancing the overall system performance. By intelligently balancing computational loads between the Fog and Cloud layers, the framework ensures low energy consumption and high scalability while maintaining service quality. The system also incorporates context-aware decision-making mechanisms that adapt to varying workloads and user demands, ensuring efficient use of available resources. Simulation results demonstrate significant improvements in response time, energy efficiency, and resource utilization compared to traditional methods, making this approach highly suitable for delay-sensitive and resource-intensive applications.

title: Fairness-Aware Offline Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (Fair-RLHF): Mitigating Bias in Human Preferences
authors: Sheryl Mathew, N Harshit
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Offline Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback aims to train AI agents using predefined human data, however human feedback can be biased which can lead to unfair or sub-optimal decision-making. In this paper, we propose Fairness-Aware Offline RLHF (Fair-RLHF), a framework designed to integrate fairness constraints into Offline Reinforcement Learning. We aim to detect and mitigate bias in Human Feedback. This is done using causal graphs (DAGs), which help in analyzing and identifying where bias originates. This visual representation of the cause-effect relationship determines unfair dependencies and uses fairness techniques to remove them. Furthermore, fairness constraints are applied that use Pareto efficiency to balance fairness with performance, achieving the precision of 46.5% and 84%. It is optimized by ensuring fairness and maximizing performance. This work provides a fair and efficient RLHF model that aims to bridge the gap between reinforcement learning, fairness constraints, and human feedback by developing a framework that ensures AI agents are unbiased and demographic disparities are reduced.

title: Extremum-Based Joint Compression and Detection for Distributed Sensing
authors: Amir Weiss, Alejandro Lancho
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We study joint compression and detection in distributed sensing systems motivated by emerging applications such as IoT-based localization. Two spatially separated sensors observe noisy signals and can exchange only a k-bit message over a reliable one-way low-rate link. One sensor compresses its observation into a k-bit description to help the other decide whether their observations share a common underlying signal or are statistically independent. We propose a simple extremum-based strategy, in which the encoder sends the index of its largest sample and the decoder performs a scalar threshold test. We derive exact nonasymptotic false-alarm and misdetection probabilities and validate the analysis with representative simulations.

title: InSAR-Based Monitoring of Ground Deformation at Grasberg Open-Pit Mine, Indonesia (2017-2025)
authors: Arif Aditiya, Takeo Ito, Agustan Agustan, Moh Fifik Syafiudin, Djoko Nugroho, Ali Nurmoko, Muhammad Iqbal Habibie
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Monitoring ground deformation in large-scale mining areas is critical for operational safety and geohazard mitigation.
