title: Evolutionary Neural Architecture Search for Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Variable-Length Designs_supp1-3676860.pdf
authors: Jiao Liu
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) are a promising approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs), but practical tuning remains largely handcrafted and costly, especially in variable-length search spaces where candidate architectures differ in depth and width. We present Evo-NAS-PINN, an evolutionary neural architecture search framework that addresses both the high per-candidate evaluation cost and the challenges posed by variable-length designs. Evo-NAS-PINN follows a two-stage scheme in which the last layer is cast as a convex regression solved in closed form via a gradient-free, physics-informed pseudo-inverse, while only the representation layers undergo a small number of gradient steps, greatly reducing evaluation cost during search. To explore variable-length designs efficiently, we introduce a self-attention-based surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithm that encodes each architecture as a token sequence, uses attention to summarize sequences of different lengths, and provides uncertainty-aware guidance to the evolutionary loop. Across diverse PDE families, Evo-NAS-PINN delivers consistently better performance with only tens to a few hundred of optimization steps, surpassing strong PINN baselines that require prolonged training. Beyond efficiency, it generalizes robustly to previously unseen settings without additional data or retraining, and extensive experiments demonstrate its effectiveness and scalability for physics-informed architecture discovery.

title: Vision-LLM for Intelligent 6G Vehicular Networks
authors: J. Akilandeswari, Anitha Saravana Kumar, A. Naveen Kumar, Madhan R, Manikandan S, Ramsurath R S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Combining 6G vehicular networks with Visionbased Large Language Models (VLMs) is an innovative solution to the navigation of autonomous vehicles. This paper suggests a new framework that utilizes the superior perception and the ability of Vision LLMs and the lower latency and high bandwidth communication, offered by 6G networks, to improve real-time decision making and navigation in autonomous vehicles. The system detects objects, understands scenes, and predicts intentions using Vision LLMs, and 6G networks facilitate the smooth operation of vehicles to everything (V2X) communication, which guarantees safe and efficient navigation despite the changes in the environment. It is experimentally demonstrated that the object detection accuracy is 96%, the decision-making latency is reduced by 40% and route optimization is enhanced by 30%. This study proves the opportunity of integrating Vision LLMs with 6G networks to have safer, more efficient and scalable autonomous vehicle systems, which will be the basis of the future smart transportation networks.

title: ComboS: Combining State Measurement with DR-Sketch for Adaptive Heavy Hitter Detection
authors: Deyu Zhao, Guang Cheng, Yuyu Zhao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Given that many of the events that lead to the disruption of networks have characteristics of the heavy hitter such as DDoS attack and elephant flows, accurate and flexible heavy hitter detection is indispensable to network operations. However, most of the existing methods for heavy hitter detection suffer from two drawbacks: lack of adaptiveness and imbalance between accuracy and generality. We present ComboS, a flexible network measurement framework for heavy hitter detection. ComboS introduces the network state measurement to guide task transition, and designs DR-Sketch which can adaptively change the measurement contents according to the tasks based on FPGA dynamic reconfigurable pipeline. We further propose a mechanism for filtering and optimizing measurement results based on an accurate table, which helps ComboS improve the measurement accuracy with less memory usage. The implementation of ComboS in FPGA is evaluated in a real network testbed, and the experiments indicate that ComboS can adaptively react to different kinds of heavy hitters rapidly. Besides, we experimentally find the balance between memory usage and state operation time, and verify that ComboS can reduce the detection error rate of heavy hitters to less than 1% with fewer hardware resources and higher throughput.

title: Respiratory Infection Detection Using Cough Pattern Analysis
authors: K. Prithivi, S. Gomathy, K. Nithya Pooja, M. Mohamed Hashim, B. Santhosh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The Early Respiratory Disease Detection System employs voice analysis via deep learning to provide a novel, efficient and accessible way to diagnose respiratory disorders that is non-invasive. To initiate the system, a comprehensive audio dataset is compiled to reflect a variety of respiratory diseases, ages, and demographics. Using the Librosa python library, the dataset is analyzed to extract key acoustic features including Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), spectral contrast and chroma features indicating unique patterns of breath and cough sound. Each audio sample is coupled with a label indicating the corresponding respiratory condition to enable supervised learning. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is subsequently established and trained on the extracted feature set to complicate patterns between sound characteristics and respiratory patterns. The trained model can either classify in real time or batch and can isolate healthy states from diseased respiratory states. An independent test dataset is employed to assess the accuracy of the model and to guide future enhancement for improved reliability and generalization. By taking advantage of CNNs feature-learning advantages, this approach provides a scalable, low cost and reliable diagnostic solution in low-resource or remote healthcare settings. Continuous monitoring, retraining and clinical validation promote consistency and flexibility in a variety of populations and respiratory conditions over time.

title: A QoS-Aware RL-Based Virtual Network Embedding Framework for Wireless Multi-Hop Networks
authors: Yingjie Wang, Liwei Jiang, Chan Wang, Minjian Zhao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Virtual Network Embedding (VNE), which aims to orchestrate the coexistence of multiple virtual networks on shared resources, encounters significant challenges in Wireless Multi-hop Networks (WMNs) due to the open nature of wireless channels and the complexity of interference, resulting in degraded Quality of Service (QoS). To address this, we propose QRL-VNE, a novel prediction-driven, QoS-aware Reinforcement Learningbased VNE framework. This framework integrates RouteNet-W, a specialized QoS prediction model based on a Message-Passing Neural Network, which accurately predicts end-to-end QoS metrics, including latency and packet loss, in WMNs. QRLVNE leverages these precise predictions to guide both node and link embedding, thereby significantly improving the QoS performance of VNE. Extensive packet-level simulation results in various WMNs on OPNET demonstrate that QRL-VNE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in reducing end-toend latency and packet loss, while maintaining high performance in terms of acceptance ratio and resource efficiency.

title: AI-Enabled Orbital Trajectory Optimization for Low-Earth Satellites: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach
authors: Apurva, D.Vetrithangam
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Hybrid deep learning architectures have become effective instruments for simulating nonlinear orbital dynamics in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) systems. This paper suggests a hybrid framework that uses Artificial Intelligence (AI) to combine Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Long-Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for accurate orbital trajectory optimization. The model uses Two-Line Element (TLE) data from the NORAD/CelesTrak General Perturbations (GP) catalogue and the Simplified General Perturbations 4 (SGP4) model to make normalised Cartesian position vectors. The CNN layers find spatial correlations in orbital sequences, and the LSTM layers find long-term temporal dependencies. Experimental evaluation shows that the suggested model has an overall prediction accuracy of 96.4%, with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.021 km and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 0.94%. The model also makes calculations more efficient, with an energy-performance ratio 2.5 times higher than baseline methods and an average inference time of 8.6 milliseconds per prediction step. Combining physics-based orbital parameters with data-driven learning makes it possible to make reliable, understandable, and energy-efficient predictions about trajectories. The results show that the framework can be used for real-time satellite navigation, avoiding collisions, and smart mission planning in future autonomous LEO operations.

title: Coupled Study of Insulation Material Aging Mechanisms and Electric Field Distribution in UHV Converter Transformer Valve-Side Outlet Devices Based on Equivalent Modeling and Deep Learning
authors: Huang Shilei, Zhang Chenchen, Jiao Ruiting, Song Likun
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: During long-term operation, the valve-side oil–paper insulation of ultra-high-voltage (UHV) converter transformers is exposed to combined electrical, thermal, mechanical, and environmental stresses, leading to insulation aging, altered electric field distribution, and potential safety risks. To address this, this paper presents a method based on the Debye-XY model, finite element simulation, and a multi-constraint inversion algorithm, combined with deep learning to process frequency domain spectroscopy (FDS) data. Coupling a genetic algorithm with finite element simulation enables aging state inversion, while a CNN optimized with MPIA-KNN is used to classify 30 aging–moisture states with high accuracy. Experiments show the method achieves 98.5% classification accuracy, significantly improving insulation assessment and electric field prediction, and offering valuable support for UHV equipment operation and maintenance.

title: Application of Deep Learning in Clock Accuracy and Time-Frequency Transfer: Research on Smart Electricity Bill Settlement Algorithms
authors: Keyi Liu, Jiguang Xue, Likun Wang, Bing Bai, Chang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To address the issues of spatiotemporal feature coupling in smart grid time-frequency calibration and the difficulty of traditional models in balancing accuracy and dynamic adaptability, this paper proposes a deep spatiotemporal feature fusion time-frequency calibration algorithm. This algorithm utilizes a three-stage architecture: a graph convolutional network (GCN) characterizes substation node topology to extract spatial features, a bidirectional LSTM network captures the long-term temporal evolution of clock bias, and a spatial-temporal attention mechanism dynamically balances feature weights to achieve end-to-end calibration parameter output. Experiments based on 1.2TB of timefrequency monitoring data from a provincial power company (including 15-dimensional features from 118 substations) show that the proposed algorithm comprehensively outperforms competing algorithms, including IEEE 1588 PTP, NTPv4, and LSTM-Single, in static, dynamic, and interference scenarios. The average clock deviation is as low as 0.021 ms (static), a 92.3% reduction compared to IEEE 1588 PTP. Frequency stability reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.2 \times 10^{-12}/1 \mathrm{s}$</tex>, two orders of magnitude better than LSTM-Single. The monthly cumulative electricity bill error rate is only 0.06%–0.17%, with an average daily growth rate of 0.002%–0.005%. The single calibration time increases linearly with the number of nodes (slope 0.02 ms/node), balancing high precision with large-scale grid adaptability, providing strong support for accurate electricity bill settlement in smart grids.

title: Design of Modular Square Wave Pulse Generator and Multi-Sensor Measurement of Partial Discharge
authors: Donglei Dai, Kai Wang, Dan Zhou, Chongxing Zhang, Ming Ren, Ming Dong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: For the insulation test of power module packaging, a modular square wave pulse voltage output scheme was proposed. The back-to-back modular cascaded Marx circuit is used as the basic circuit framework, the SiC MOSFET is used as the main switching device, and the auxiliary capacitor and auxiliary diode are used to construct a simple gate drive power supply circuit. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 k V}$</tex> pulse voltage generator was fabricated, and its effectiveness was verified by insulation test. In addition, the multi-sensor measurement effect of partial discharge (PD) induced by square wave voltage was preliminarily evaluated.

title: Transformer-Enhanced Contextual Discriminator for Robust Detection of AI-Generated Texts
authors: C. Sushama, Poolakunta Manasa, Muggari Vishnupriya, Srirama Harshitha, Puli Sujith
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The rapid progression of models in large language has made differentiate between human-written and AIgenerated text and it increasingly challenges. This paper proposed a Transformer-Enhanced Contextual Discriminator (TECD) based on a hybrid deep learning framework which integrates with transformer-based contextual embeddings and graph-aware relational learning for sturdy AI-text detection. The suggested model obtains both semantic coherence and global contextual depended by combining Bidirectional Transformer Encoders with a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) layer. The system includes an attention method that helps to model focus on the most important linguistic cues that influence by it decision. This feature also makes the model more understandable, the text led to a particular classification result. Experimental validates on multiple benchmark datasets shows that TECD achieves superior accuracy and generalizes compared to existing Bi-LSTM and attention-based models, while maintaining the computational efficiency. The results signify that influencing from top-down contextual representations substantially improves the reliability of automated systems in identifying AI-generated content overall varied text domains.

title: AI-Driven Framework for Automatic Complexity Reduction and Summarization of Indian Judicial Documents
authors: Taha Malik, Ruban Anburaj A, Lakshmi S.
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Indian judicial documents are quite voluminous, complex, and difficult to comprehend leading to the delay in prosecuting and traiting legal matters. This research suggests the hybrid deep learning system based on AI that is aimed at processing the complexity of text and producing a concise overview of a legal text while maintaining all of the necessary information inside. The system can handle scanned and digitized rulings generating summaries that increase readability and semantic understanding. The extensive analysis of a substantial body of Indian judicial resources proves that the suggested framework can enhance information retention, understanding, and accuracy in summarization in a considerable extent than traditional techniques. This framework can provide an effective and scalable solution to the issue of legal professionals because it simplifies the process of the review and allows accessing the most important and crucial details about cases much faster. The use of it can diminish manual efforts, help in making informed decisions, and decrease the judicial processes, thus reducing the efficiency of the legal practices.

title: An Example-Enhanced and Iteratively Refined Text-to-SQL Framework with Dual-Path Revision
authors: Yanling Chen, Zupei Huang, Zijun Lu, Yaoyao Dai, Yidong Ke, Lin Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Although significant progress has been made in Text-to-SQL technology, existing methods still face challenges: insufficient adaptability between examples and the specific database schema, and inadequate repair capabilities when SQL execution encounters exceptions. To address these challenges, this paper introduces the Example-Enhanced and Iteratively Refined SQL (EEIR-SQL) framework, featuring two key innovations: First, a dynamic example generator produces SQL examples covering varying levels of complexity for each query task. Second, a dual-path intelligent revision agent autonomously selects SQL correction strategies based on execution feedback (error reports or empty results). Experiments on the BIRD benchmark demonstrate that EEIR-SQL's 4-bit (INT4) quantized Qwen332B model achieves an 11.43 percentage points higher execution accuracy than the Qwen3-32B-Q4 baseline, outperforming several GPT-4-based approaches. This result not only validates EEIR-SQL's effectiveness in generating accurate and reliable SQL, but also demonstrates that, despite using a less resourceintensive quantized model, its performance surpasses that of computationally demanding GPT-4. This provides practical guidance for balancing low-resource deployment with high reliability requirements.

title: Comparative Analysis of Naive Bayes and Support Vector Machine Algorithms for Automated Health Status Classification
authors: Chandni Manral, Amit Mittal, Anant Deogaonkar, Kimsy Gulhane, Siddharth Joshi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The use of machine learning in healthcare is really important because it helps doctors find health problems early and manage patients better. In this study, we just tried to find out which model is better from another at knowing if someone's healthy or not. Is it the Naive Bayes (NB) or the Support Vector Machine (SVM). We took data from 800 patients so we can test, which included things like their age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, insulin, and at last their cholesterol levels too. All the job, like making the data, testing the models, and seeing the results, was done in MATLAB. After checking the results, we get to know that the Naive Bayes model did pretty well than the other model which was Support Vector Machine. The Naive Bayes model got about 97.5 % accuracy, and the SVM was close with 96.25%. So yeah, NB worked a bit better as compared to the model. It basically shows that even simple models like Naïve Bayes can be super useful for helping doctors make better and faster choices then the Support Vector Machine.

title: A ResKCN-Based Spatial Prediction Method for Complex Interference in SBFD Networks
authors: Yuhan Zhao, Lingling Chen, Qianqian Si, Xin Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Sub-Band Full Duplex (SBFD) systems enable simultaneous transmission and reception on adjacent frequency bands, offering substantial spectral efficiency gains. However, SBFD introduces complex interference patterns that exhibit nonstationary, heterogeneous characteristics, making traditional interpolation prediction methods inadequate. This paper proposes the Residual Kriging Convolutional Network (ResKCN), which combines Kriging-inspired neighbor weighting with residual graph convolutions to predict complex spatial cross-link interference patterns in SBFD networks. ResKCN addresses the oversmoothing problem of deep graph neural networks while preserving fine-scale interference variations through identity shortcuts. Comprehensive Monte-Carlo simulations demonstrate that ResKCN achieves 13.0 % lower mean squared error compared to state-of-the-art baselines, providing accurate total inband interference estimation for 5G-Advanced network planning.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Gamified E-Marketplace for Sustainable E-Waste Collection
authors: Nurin Damia Sopian, Nor Hasnul Azirah Abdul Hamid, Wan Khairul Anuar Wan Abd Manan, Mazita Mokhtar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Electronic waste (e-waste) disposal remains a global challenge, with limited public participation and fragmented collection systems in Malaysia. This paper presents Recycle Heroes, a gamified e-marketplace designed to enhance e-waste collection and user engagement in Selangor. The platform integrates points, leaderboards, and avatar customization with Jakob Nielsen's usability heuristics to ensure an intuitive user experience. Developed using an Adapted Waterfall System Development Life Cycle and implemented with a PHP-Laravel and MySQL architecture, the system supports consumers, collectors, and administrators through a centralized interface. Usability evaluation using the System Usability Scale produced a score of 69.13, while task-based testing recorded 86% task success and 78% efficiency, indicating generally effective and user-friendly performance. Expert heuristic evaluation highlighted strengths in consistency, aesthetics, and system visibility, alongside major issues in error prevention and recovery. Future work includes mobile deployment, enhanced gamification strategies, and integration of real-time analytics to scale nationwide adoption.

title: Research on REBCO Superconducting Tape Joint Techniques
authors: Liguang Hu, Da Lei, Peng He, Cen Qian, Jian Cheng, Bangzhu Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The Rare-earth barium copper oxide (REBCO) coated conductors are promising due to their high current capacity and reduced cooling requirements. However, current manufacturing limits single-tape lengths to hundreds of meters, while practical applications demand kilometre-scale conductors. Superconducting joints therefore become essential, enabling continuous circuits, and serving as a critical factor in the performance and scalability of HTS systems. This paper reviews major joint fabrication techniques, including soldering, diffusion bonding, and ultrasonic welding, as well as emerging approaches. Despite progress, significant challenges remain. Future directions emphasize integrated electromechanical design, scalable manufacturing processes, and standardized testing. By addressing these challenges, REBCO joint technology can evolve from laboratory validation toward reliable large-scale deployment, enabling transformative applications in energy, research, and medical fields.

title: Explainable AI-Driven Intrusion Detection System Using Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Secure Network Infrastructure
authors: K Sathish, M. Ramkumar Prabhu, V. Suresh Kumar, S. Dinakar Raj
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Threats to network security developments are still in existence and system advanced intrusion detection systems are required which can attain high precision and interpretable decision-making procedures. Conventional IDS solutions tend not to be very transparent and also security analysts may find it hard to make out patterns of attack and have a feel of how the systems react to them. The research studied a novel hybrid deep learning model composed of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks along with the explainable AI methods such as SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) that can be used to maximize the model interpretability. The performance of the system was tested on NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017 datasets by extending the preprocessing, feature selection, and cross-validation elements to 98.7% detection accuracy, 97.3% precision, 96.8% recall, and 97.05 F1-score which was 12-15% better than any conventional machine learning algorithms with 89% explainability confidence scores. Concluding Remarks: The explainable AI and hybrid deep learning models have a great effect on the performance of detection and bring interpretability to the next level, offering security professionals understandable information they can use to mitigate the threat ahead of time.

title: Comparison of Routing Protocols for Energy Efficiency and Network Longevity in Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: G. Brindha, P. Sudha Juliet, M. Umamaheswari, Berakhah. F. Stanley, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Marine exploration, conservational observing, and military surveillance all rely heavily on underwater wireless sensor networks (UWSNs). However, due to the unaltered submerged ecosystem, high energy consumption, and short network lifespan, devising competent routing protocols for UWSNs remains a significant chance. This research grants a relative analysis of numerous routing protocols based on key recital metrics, considering packet delivery ratio (PDR), resources usage, and the quantity of active connections. Energy-Efficient MAC & Routing, Arithmetic Progression-based Routing, and Opportunistic Routing are estimated to assess their effectiveness in enhancing network stability and energy efficiency. According to the findings, Arithmetic Progression-based Routing outperforms other protocols by ensuring a greater number of active sensor nodes over time, maintaining a higher PDR, and reducing energy consumption. Opportunistic routing demonstrated the greatest energy depletion in divergence, resulting in decreased efficiency and concentrated network durability. Although the Energy-Efficient MAC & Routing protocol was welladjusted, the overall efficiency of the 2024 approach was higher. This study emphasizes the significance of energyaware and adaptive routing strategies for enhancing communication reliability and extending the life of UWSN networks. To further enhance underwater communication systems, future research may investigate the incorporation of real-time flexibility, hybrid routing strategies, and machine learning-based optimization. UWSNs can achieve greater scalability, durability, and efficiency by utilizing intellectual routing mechanisms, making it possible for advanced applications in subaquatic surveillance, eco- friendly protection, and deep-sea investigation.

title: Fusion of MFCC Features and CNN Architectures for Multi-Dataset Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Narinder Kaur, Neha Dhiman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Emotion identification from speech is essential for enhancing human-computer interaction, allowing systems to react with contextual empathy. This article presents a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based system that uses Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs) as input characteristics to categorise speech patterns according to emotions. Our approach employs the spatial structure of MFCCs to inherently discern patterns through deep convolutional layers, rather than relying on handcrafted features and basic classifications. We developed and enhanced a CNN architecture, subsequently utilising typical emotional speech datasets to evaluate its efficacy. Our experimental data indicate that our model significantly outperforms standard classification approaches. This demonstrates that deep learning is an effective method for detecting nuanced emotional signals in speech.

title: Research on Retrieval-Augmented Generation Method Based on Dynamic Knowledge Indexing and Semantic Alignment Optimization
authors: Yang Shao, Ziliang Du, Bo Liu, Hao Wen, Xilin Zhang, Chaofan Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) face significant challenges in knowledge-intensive tasks, including knowledge solidification, insufficient timeliness, and hallucination generation. To address these challenges, this study proposes an innovative retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) method based on dynamic knowledge indexing and semantic alignment optimization. However, traditional RAG methods suffer from semantic fragmentation and query-knowledge misalignment. The approach constructs a dynamic multi-granularity knowledge indexing system and integrates an intelligent dual-path routing decision mechanism to achieve efficient collaboration between knowledge retrieval and generation processes. Key innovations include: (1) a dynamic multi-granularity (comprising lexical-level inverted indexing and semantic-level vector indexing) knowledge indexing architecture that employs an adaptive chunking algorithm based on semantic coherence analysis to resolve semantic fragmentation caused by fixed-length segmentation; (2) a prompt engineering-driven semantic alignment optimization mechanism that unifies the semantic space of queries and knowledge bases by generating hypothetical question-answer pairs, significantly improving retrieval accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements: 37.01 EM and 47.53 F1 on HotpotQA, 35.12 EM and 45.69 F1 on SQuAD. This work provides a reliable technical solution for intelligent question answering and advances RAG technology in semantic understanding and retrieval accuracy.

title: Research on Statistical Distribution Characteristics of VHF Over-the-Horizon Communication Channel Resources
authors: Chen Wei, Zhao Yuchao, Shi Jincheng
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Aiming at the limited transmission distance of traditional single-mode in the VHF band, the communication channel resources such as ground wave diffraction and tropospheric scattering in the VHF band were systematically studied. Based on the complexity of VHF over-the-horizon propagation modes, a method for distinguishing different propagation modes was proposed through theoretical analysis and statistical analysis of extensive field channel tests and communication experiment data. The slow fading characteristics such as diurnal and annual variations, as well as the fast fading characteristics including fading depth, fading rate, and fading duration, were analyzed. This provides support for the design of VHF over-the-horizon communication waveforms.

title: Dynamic Model Selection for Energy-Efficient AI Systems in Healthcare
authors: Neha Nahariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The Dynamic Model Selection (DMS) is a method for the energy efficiency of Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems in the healthcare sector. The DMS framework ensures the best trade-off between the precision of the diagnosis and the power consumed. The PTB-XL electrocardiography (ECG) dataset is work for the experiments, three distinct hierarchical models—Tiny, Medium, and Full Networks—are trained, and a reinforcement learning policy is included for adaptive inference. The study of the experiments shows that the suggested framework achieves an overall accuracy of 93.4% while reducing the average power consumption by nearly 60% when compared with the static Deep Learning (DL) models. The technology maintains its clinical sensitivity above 92% and provides real-time processing on both GPU and edge devices. The findings indicate that DMS is a promising path towards green, interpretable, and energy-efficient healthcare AI, and make it possible to achieve reliable diagnostics in different computational and clinical settings.

title: A Super-Resolution MRI Pipeline for Predictive Stroke Risk Evaluation Using ESRGAN and DQN Models
authors: Athim R, Akilan B, Gnanasoundhram J
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Stroke is one of the most common causes of death and lasting disability and the discovery of the factors that are putting people at risk at an early age is important. This project is an implementation of a system in MATLAB that in turn combines the Enhanced Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (ESRGAN) with a Deep QNetwork (DQN) predictive model in stroke-susceptibility analysis based on high-definition MRI scan data. ESRGAN improves the quality of the MRI, and finer elements of structure are sharpened, including the tissues of the brain, blood vessels, and any possible lesions. Attributes extracted around these regions are then fed into the DQN model to be predicted so as to be able to predict stroke probability with accuracy. A realistic Kaggle brain MRI dataset was tested on the system and comparison was made between the classical methods of machine learning and the deep learning models, showing that deep learning models exhibit better precision and earlier detection of a pathology. The solution has practical real-life implementation, which can be identified by at-risk people at earlier stages of age and aid in the execution of preventive and treatment approaches on a personal level. This approach is different to the traditional methods because it uses super-resolution enhancement and reinforcement learning.

title: Enhanced Semi-Global Stereo Matching Via Multi-Feature Cost Fusion
authors: Xinliang Tang, Yixuan He, He Su, Shuyan Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper presents an improved Semi-Global Matching (SGM) algorithm based on binocular stereo vision for high-precision disparity computation. Camera calibration is performed to obtain intrinsic and extrinsic parameters, followed by stereo rectification to align corresponding points on the same horizontal line, simplifying the stereo matching process. In the algorithm design, the Birchfield-Tomasi (BT) cost method is combined with the Census transform through weighted fusion to enhance matching accuracy. Path optimization is also applied by adopting four-path cost aggregation to reduce computational redundancy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms traditional SGM and single-feature methods in both disparity accuracy and robustness, achieving highprecision stereo matching.

title: Research on the Anti Limit Warning Device for Near Electric Operations in Power Transmission and Transformation Projects Based on Laser Fencing
authors: Bin Zhang, Yongjun He, Changyun Shi, Li Chen, Fei Teng, Jiawei Leng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To solve the problem of high risk and low efficiency in manual control of near electric operation construction, this paper proposes a anti limit warning device for near electric operations in power transmission and transformation projects based on laser fencing and designs the software and hardware of the system according to the system functional requirements. The electronic fence is integrated with a scanning laser protection system, and the laser radar is connected to the signal processing module inside the electronic fence equipment through a data cable. Real time collection of distance, orientation and other data information enables comprehensive monitoring and protection of ground and highaltitude areas, solving the problem of electronic fence false alarms caused by materials and power safety fences in near electric operation areas. By vertically installing the LiDAR and utilizing its vertical field of view, the speed of moving invading objects is calculated. Based on collision time, graded warnings are issued, and risks are controlled in advance to effectively prevent accidents from occurring.

title: Cloud-Native Network Slicing in 5G-Enabled Iot Systems: Architectures, Mechanisms, and Applications
authors: Varinder Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Cloud-native network slicing has emerged as a transformative paradigm in advancing 5G-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems. By decomposing traditional monolithic network functions into modular, containerized microservices orchestrated through Software-Defined Networking (SDN), Network Function Virtualization (NFV), and Kubernetes, it enables agile, scalable, and programmable communication infrastructures. This study examines the theoretical foundations and operational mechanisms of cloudnative slicing, emphasizing multi-layered architecture, service mesh integration, and edge-cloud coordination as critical enablers of efficient Quality of Service (QoS) delivery. Artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) play an increasingly vital role in predictive orchestration, adaptive resource management, and intent-driven automation for managing distributed network functions. Despite significant advancements, studies reveal continuing challenges in achieving seamless interoperability, energy-efficient orchestration, and secure isolation within multi-tenant domains. Addressing these complexities requires lightweight, standardized frameworks that support cross-domain adaptability and sustainable operation. The advancement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> architectures thus underscores the importance of intelligent orchestration, distributed automation, and context-aware network management to achieve resilient, efficient, and scalable IoT service delivery across diverse application domains.

title: AI-Driven Customer Service and Its Effect on Customer Loyalty and Trust in E-Commerce
authors: BNS Hemalatha, Srinivas. D
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The development of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has impacted different industries rapidly, and e-commerce is not the exception. Customer service AI which includes the implementation of chatbots, virtual assistants, and automated recommendation engine can provide customers with the best experience in terms of enhancing customer service efficiency and personalization. In this paper, the authors explore how AIbased customer service can convert customer loyalty and trust in the e-commerce industry. The study examines the correlation between the introduction of AI technologies and the two essential elements loyalty and trust that determine customer retention and customer satisfaction in general. A survey and data analysis approaches were employed as a mixture of quantitative and qualitative design to determine the customer perception and behavior toward AI-driven interactions. The results indicate a positivity among the AI-intensive customer service and higher customer trust and loyalty, and can guide the e-commerce corporations in improving their customer service system. The future research directions are also addressed, in particular, with regard to how AI integration significantly influences consumer behavior over time.

title: Causal Inference and Uncertainty Quantification for Counterfactual Analysis of Injury Severity in Multi-Vehicle Collisions
authors: Philip F Balyagati, Abdul Ngereza, Emmanuel Kidando, Ntemi Masanja, Caroline Bikuba
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Road crashes continue to pose significant public health concerns, prompting transportation agencies to prioritize strategies for improving roadway safety and mitigating crash impacts. Traditional approaches to modeling highway safety data often rely on regression-based methods to identify significant factors associated with injury severity. However, these methods primarily capture associations rather than causal effects, limiting their ability to evaluate the effectiveness of interventions. This study introduces a Bayesian causal inference framework to advance safety analysis by capturing underlying causal relationships on crash injuries. Using data from Ohio interstates, the framework assesses the effects of roadway, environmental, and behavioral factors on injury severity, with crash type modeled as a mediating variable. The average causal impacts and their associated uncertainties are estimated using the highest density interval (HDI). The study findings reveal that alcohol involvement and multi-vehicle crashes are key contributors to the increased risk of injuries and fatalities. Road geometry and vehicle speed exhibit both direct and mediated effects through crash type, while lighting conditions and the presence of work zones show uncertain impacts based on HDI. The results offer valuable insights for transportation safety policy, emphasizing the importance of targeting both direct and indirect pathways in crash injury prevention strategies.

title: LMAC-Net: A Lightweight Multimodal Attention Compression Network for Edge-Oriented IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Weizhi Gao, Mingshu He, Miao He
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of IoT devices has increased network heterogeneity and complexity, posing challenges for accurate and real-time intrusion detection under resource constraints. To address this, we propose LMAC-Net, a lightweight multimodal attention compression network designed for edgeoriented IoT intrusion detection. LMAC-Net integrates four complementary modalities-header, temporal, payload, and contextual features-through a gated confidence-uncertainty fusion mechanism that adaptively balances modal contributions and suppresses noise. A bidirectional LSTM captures temporal dependencies, while a structured column pruning algorithm eliminates redundant parameters to reduce computational overhead. Experiments on CICEVSE2024, CIC-IoT 2023, MQTT-IoT-IDS2020, CIC-IDS2017, and a self-collected IoT-AD-Set demonstrate that LMAC-Net achieves high detection accuracy with significantly lower complexity and inference latency, making it well-suited for deployment in intelligent IoT edge environments.

title: Analysis of Prediction Error's Impact of Usable Frequency Window on HF Communications Success Rate
authors: Ke Du, Kun Xu, Yangyang Li, Yangpeng Dan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Usable frequency window (UFW) prediction is of vital importance for high frequency (HF) communications since HF communications will succeed only when operating within the UFW, defined by the maximum usable frequency (MUF) and lowest usable frequency (LUF). In this paper, we investigate the impact of UFW prediction errors on HF communications success rate and try to identify whose (MUF's or LUF's) prediction error has more impact on the success rate. First, closed-form expression for the success rate is derived based on the log-normal distribution assumption for the MUF and LUF. Nevertheless, it is difficult to analyze the prediction error's impact because the derived closedform expression composes of multiple Q functions. To overcome the limitation, we then simplify the closed-form expression for success rate with good accuracy in two cases where the two cases are distinguished by the relationship between the mean values of MUF and LUF. With such simplification, the critical value of the prediction error, identifying MUF's or LUF's prediction error has more impact, is directly obtained. It's shown through theoretic analysis and simulation results that the MUF's prediction error has more impact on success rate for most values of the prediction error.

title: Research on the Model of Dance Personalized Training Path Planning System Based on Data-Driven
authors: Nuo Li, Siyu Liu, Jun Niu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper presents a data-driven computational model for personalized training path planning in dance equipment, integrating reinforcement learning and decision tree algorithms within a computer-based framework. The model leverages multi-dimensional data streams (including motion sensor data, physiological metrics, and historical performance logs) to construct and dynamically optimize training paths through real-time computational adjustments. A core innovation lies in the implementation of an adaptive reinforcement learning mechanism that enables the system to autonomously refine training strategies based on continuous data input, maximizing skill improvement efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to traditional rule-based methods, the proposed computational model enhances training effect by approximately 30% through optimized algorithmic decision-making, reduces computational latency by 25%, and improves real-time adjustment accuracy by 18%. The system architecture emphasizes modular computing components, ensuring scalability for integration with various dance equipment sensor networks and compatibility with IEEE standard communication protocols. This research contributes a novel computational solution for intelligent training systems, highlighting the application of machine learning algorithms in optimizing human-machine interaction scenarios.

title: Automated Visual Analysis of Otolith Morphometry for Indian Ocean Species
authors: Isaac Jeddiah, Mohith Raaj, Subha
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Fishes in water have small calcium-carbonate ear stones in their ear cavity. This is known by the name, otolith structural details useful for species discrimination, examining growth, and fisheries research. Traditional otolith morphometry is manual, time-consuming and filled with variability. This paper explains an automated pipeline with computer vision for extraction of morphometric indices and computing fish length. The length, width, perimeter, area are taken from otolith images of Indian Ocean fish species. This uses adaptive histogram equalization, Otsu thresholding; contours are extracted and calibrated using the scale bar via OCR. Fish length is estimated using published regression models. Comparison with real species-specific length shows strong correlation, proving accuracy and efficiency. This contributes to fisheries science through the digitization, standardization, and large-scale morphometric analyses. The pipeline is expandable to additional species and metrics, and with improvements in biodiversity assessment, can support more informed fisheries and marine resource management.

title: SAP-CORA: Security-Aware PPO for Cooperative Resource Allocation in IoV Networks
authors: Yifei Li, Xiaohan Ge, Liangbin Yang, Jing Bai
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper tackles the challenges of multi-node collaboration, service continuity, and multi-objective optimization in highly dynamic Internet of vehicles (IoV) network resource allocation through the Security-Aware PPO for Cooperative Resource Allocation (SAP-CORA) framework. The framework introduces three core innovations: first, a three-stage service transmission model spanning uplink, processing, and downlink phases to strengthen multi-node coordination; second, a securitylevel mapping system quantifying security enhancement costs between nodes and services; third, a reinforcement learning architecture based on proximal policy optimization (PPO) incorporating a joint latency-security cost reward function. Experimental results confirm the algorithm's superiority over baseline methods in all key metrics including average reward, service success rate, average latency, and security cost.

title: SecureCodeReviewer: An AI-Driven Automated Secure Code Review System with Context-Aware Vulnerability Detection
authors: Dhanush MC, Sandeep Kumar TV, Mohan Krishna G, Keerthika T
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This survey examines the use of AI and machine learning in software vulnerability detection. As software becomes more advanced and complicated over the days cyber-attacks get more sophisticated and advanced, so this has become a problem, this survey examines deep learning methods like CNN, RNN & BiLSTMs, transformers like BERT and all its variations, it also examines graph-based methods like GGNNs and HAGNNs, and conventional ML methods. Key datasets include SARD, NVD, Debian Linus repositories, and Python-specific datasets have been used and examined for their advantages and disadvantages. By comparing performance metrics including accuracy, F1-score, recall, precision, and ROC, reliability is evaluated. To promote secure engineering methods, the study indicates future prospects for multimodal learning, cross-language transfer, federated learning, and integration with software pipelines. It also addresses long-standing challenges such as language reliance, dataset bias, scalability, and explainability.

title: Multi-View Graph Balanced Clustering for Scalable Microservice Decomposition in Cloud Computing
authors: Junjie Teng, Jingyuan Liu, Yi Man
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the rapid adoption of cloud-native systems, monolithic applications are increasingly decomposed into microservice-based architectures to leverage the scalability, elasticity, and parallelism of distributed cloud environments. However, most existing decomposition approaches focus only on structural or functional aspects, often neglecting runtime resource characteristics that significantly impact system efficiency. To address this gap, this paper proposes a Multi-view Graph Balanced Clustering algorithm (MVGBC) for microservice decomposition. It constructs a comprehensive multilayer graph that integrates functional dependencies, interclass communication volumes, and CPU usage to capture both static and dynamic behaviors of the system. By clustering classes with high interaction intensity and balancing CPU loads across microservices, MVGBC reduces inter-service communication and improves resource utilization. Experiments on two open-source Java applications demonstrate that MVGBC outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, improving inter-service communication efficiency by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1 3. 5} {\%}$</tex> and reducing CPU imbalance by up to 29.6%, yielding microservice partitions that are both cohesive and performance-aware.

title: Predictive Maintenance for Industrial Equipment Using IoT and ML
authors: N. R.Sathis Kumar, VigneshKumar M, Supraja Pallem, RupaSree Pothurai, Varshitha Pothureddy, Shaik Karishma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: An IoT-based predictive maintenance system designed to detect abnormal behavior in industrial equipment. The system employs an ESP8266 microcontroller integrated with temperature, humidity, and vibration sensors to continuously monitor machine health parameters. Sensor data is transmitted wirelessly to the Firebase Realtime Database, enabling real time visualization and remote supervision. Any deviation from normal operating conditions can be used to identify early signs of equipment malfunction, reducing downtime and maintenance costs. By combining embedded sensing, cloud connectivity, and data analytics, this system provides a scalable, low-cost solution for intelligent industrial monitoring and predictive maintenance applications.

title: Design and Kinematics Simulation of UAV Landing Gear Supports in Complex Terrain
authors: Haoyu Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Drones are increasingly deployed in complex terrain environments, where traditional rigid landing gears face significant safety risks when landing on uneven ground. To address this issue, this study proposes an optimized landing gear design based on a multidisciplinary simulation approach that integrates dynamics, structure, and mechanics. A dynamic simulation model was developed in MATLAB/Simulink to analyze force variations and motion responses during landing on uneven surfaces. In contrast, a precise 3D geometric model of the landing gear was built in SolidWorks to ensure structural accuracy. Finite element analysis was then conducted to evaluate the stress distribution, deformation behavior, and load-bearing performance of the optimized structure. The integrated research framework combines dynamic simulation, structural optimization, and mechanical verification to achieve multi-objective optimization in stability, strength, and adaptability. Simulation results indicate that the optimized landing gear maintains structural integrity and smooth motion under varying terrain slopes and surface roughness, significantly enhancing landing safety and response efficiency. The study provides theoretical and technical references for the engineering design of UAV landing systems in complex environments. Future work will extend this framework to adaptive and intelligent landing systems with real-time control, contributing to the advancement of safe and reliable UAV operations in the emerging low-altitude economy.

title: Enhanced Air Quality Prediction Using a Two-Stage XGBoost and LSTM Hybrid Architecture
authors: Rinisri R, Ramya K, Revathi M, Jennifer A
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The issue of air pollution forecasting has gained urgency due to the accelerated urbanization, industrial processes and climatic nature of fluctuation in the behavior of pollutants. While traditional machine learning and deep learning approaches demonstrate promising results, their major limitations still lie in either capturing nonlinear feature relationships or long-term temporal dependencies. This work presents an integrated XGBoost-LSTM hybrid for air quality prediction, combining gradient boosting for learning feature interactions with recurrent neural networks for temporal sequence modeling. The approach encompasses data preprocessing, feature engineering, residual learning, and multi-stage prediction. Experimental results show the supremacy of the hybrid model over the respective single models by reducing the RMSE to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.48 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, MAE to 4.82 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> accuracy of 0.96. The system also maintains stable performance over multiple days and can thus be considered for real-time air quality monitoring and decision-support systems.

title: Smart State Feedback Controlled Microgrid Inverter with Seamless Switching and Renewable Prioritization
authors: Palaniappan N, Sanjanaa Shri M, Praveena T, S. Kiruthika
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind, into microgrids presents significant challenges in maintaining stable voltage, regulating frequency, and ensuring continuous operation during both grid-connected and islanded modes. This paper proposes a Next-Generation Smart Microgrid Inverter based on an Adaptive Neuro-Evolutionary Control (ANEC) approach to address these challenges. The ANEC model combines the intelligence of neural networks with evolutionary optimization principles to dynamically tune controller parameters, achieving robust performance under varying loads, grid disturbances, and nonlinear system dynamics. The controller continuously monitors system conditions and generates optimized control signals to reduce total harmonic distortion (THD), stabilize voltage, and enhance frequency response. A hierarchical prioritization mechanism ensures maximum utilization of renewable energy sources, with solar and wind power dispatched before conventional generation units. Additionally, the inverter enables seamless mode transitions with negligible transients, improving system reliability. Simulation studies in MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that the proposed inverter outperforms classical control strategies in terms of dynamic response, power quality, and resilience. The results highlight the ANEC-based smart inverter as a versatile, sustainable, and reliable solution for next-generation microgrids.

title: AirNet: Self-Supervised Anomaly Detection for Aircraft Instrument Screens
authors: Zhenyu Yin, Lijuan Yuan, Siyu Liu, Fulong Xu, Weibin Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional aviation instrument screen anomaly detection methods primarily rely on manual inspection, which is inefficient and susceptible to subjective factors, and it is challenging to meet the requirements of modern aviation maintenance for high reliability and real-time performance. To this end, this paper proposes a lightweight self-supervised anomaly detection framework, AirNet, which is designed for aerial instrument screen image scenes to achieve efficient and accurate anomaly recognition. AirNet utilizes a feature adapter to enhance feature expression ability and injects Gaussian noise into the feature space to generate pseudo-abnormal samples, thereby improving discrimination ability. It combines a lightweight binary classification discriminator to realize anomaly detection and location. Experimental results on the self-built FIS-AD dataset and the public MVTec-AD and VisA datasets demonstrate that AirNet achieves performance comparable to that of state-of-the-art methods, while ensuring a lightweight design and high frame rate, with 98.5% image-level AUROC and 99.1% pixel-level AUROC. It shows good generalization ability and localization accuracy.

title: Design of IoT Based Underground Wire Fault Detection System for Industry 4.0
authors: Shuruti R, Vaishali K, Ponshika M, Vishnu Murthy K
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This method suggests a fault-location model of underground power cables suggesting the use of electronic current-transformer. The system uses the internet hence all fault data is relayed through the online connectivity. The purpose of the invention is to clearly define the precise location of the fault with an underground cable and the distance of the fault in kilometers with regard to the base station. The instrument operates on the fundamental principle of the Ohm law. Whenever a fault sets in like a short circuit, the current varies giving rise to a corresponding variation in voltage drop that varies with the distance to the fault point. A set of resistors are used to model the cable. The system identifies the fault location and the supply end by identifying the changes in the voltage with the help of analogue-to-digital converters. Calculations are then done in a microcontroller and the fault distance is displayed on an LCD screen. The fault information is relayed to any available point through the internet. The control circuit with the IoT-enabled hardware and the electronic current-sensing CT coil are housed in one unit.

title: Research on Second-Hand Car Price Prediction Based on the Integration of Sentiment Analysis and Machine Learning
authors: Geng Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Traditional methods for evaluating second-hand car prices consider fewer factors and some parameters are difficult to quantify, resulting in significant discrepancies between calculated results and actual transaction prices. This article introduces the machine learning method of random forest for predicting second-hand car prices, while integrating text sentiment analysis from the network, emphasizing the influence of market public opinion on changes in second-hand car prices, and further accurately predicting second-hand car prices. The research results indicate that this method has achieved the expected effect.

title: Graph Based Routing Optimisation in 5G/6G Wireless Mesh Networks with Mixed Mobility Nodes
authors: Aarthi D, Kodeeswari. K, Harishchander Anandaram, M.Sri Soundharyaa, S. Sowmiya, F.V. Jayasudha
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The heterogeneity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G} / \mathbf{6 G}$</tex> wireless mesh networks, including UAVs, vehicles, and edge devices, leads to frequent topology changes and intermittent links, both challenging the conventional routing protocols. This work proposes the GROF, Graph-based Routing Optimization Framework, a time-varying graph approach, fusing lightweight graph neural networks (GNN) with reinforcement routing for latencyminimizing, high-reliability path prediction in mixed-mobility meshes. GROF builds dynamic graphs from local link sensing, computes node embeddings by localized message passing, and selects the next hop using a distributed RL policy. Simulation results with 50-200 nodes, static to mobile ratio of 70:30, running on realistic mobility and traffic mixes, show improvement of PDR by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 5-12 \%$</tex> and reduction of end-to-end delay by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-40 \%$</tex>, as compared to AODV, OLSR, HWMP, and a DRL baseline, while reducing routing overhead and energy per delivered packet. GROF's distributed graph learning is designed for deployment at the edge and promises good scalability to denser topologies.

title: Self-Organizing 5G-A Emergency Network with Tethered UAV and Transparent Relay Autonomy
authors: Lingjun Xu, Jing Li, Tao Lv, Hongfa Li, Peng Gu, Xiaoyan Jiang, Qingyu Cai, Wenhui Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative emergency communication system based on a tethered UAV-mounted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 G}$</tex>-A base station, transparent Relay base station, and a Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) platform. The system overcomes the key limitations of traditional emergency communication vehicles through four disruptive breakthroughs. The solution optimizes the radio wave propagation path with a UAV base station that ascends to 200 meters within 5 minutes, achieving a coverage area of over 200 square kilometers, which is 8.4 times the coverage of a traditional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 5}$</tex>-meter ground base station. The transparent Relay base station, based on native <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \mathrm{G}-\mathrm{A}$</tex> air interface, constructs a wireless cascading network without proprietary hardware, providing flexible coverage that simultaneously serves both rescue personnel and trapped civilians. The MEC platform integrates AI modules for fire and flood prediction and facial recognition, offloading local traffic at the BBU level to eliminate the need for core network UPF devices. The multi-path backhaul system dynamically selects transparent Relay, satellite, microwave, or fiber-optic links, reducing satellite bandwidth consumption by over 60 % based on cost-delay optimization strategies. In field testing in 2024, the system demonstrated a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4 8}$</tex>-hour continuous operation capability, achieving a 90 % reduction in coverage cost and an 86 % reduction in personnel requirements, successfully establishing a new paradigm for emergency critical mission networks.

title: Research on Traffic Sign Recognition Under Partial Occlusion Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zhanfei Cao, Ling Xu, Cheng Yao, Xiaoling Cheng, Weiping Zhu, Yu Wang, Dongfei Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of traffic sign recognition under partial occlusion by proposing an occlusion-aware lightweight convolutional neural network, CNN_FV. A self-constructed dataset containing 4,440 images across 15 categories was built, with dynamic occlusion augmentation as well as geometric and photometric transformations applied to enhance generalization. A pyramid-based multi-level feature decoupling mechanism combined with channel attention is introduced to strengthen the extraction of critical non-occluded regions. The model is trained using cross-entropy loss and stochastic gradient descent, with early stopping to optimize convergence. Experimental results demonstrate that CNN_FV achieves an accuracy of 98.5% on a heavily occluded test set while maintaining only about 1.2M parameters, ensuring real-time performance and suitability for in-vehicle deployment. Furthermore, a graphical user interface was developed for visualization, verifying the model's effectiveness on real-world occluded images.

title: Quantifying Macro and Micro-Level Predictors on Consumption Expenditure Using Bayesian Linear Regression Approach
authors: Lee En, Thian Song Ong, Yvonne Lee
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Understanding consumption expenditure is essential for economic policymaking, as it reflect the household well-being from their spending power. Despite its importance, existing studies in Malaysia has primarily focused on using frequentist regression approach to investigate the micro-level predictors. The role of macroeconomic factors such as regional price variations were not included. This study integrates micro-level household variables with state-level Consumer Price Indices (CPI) data using a Bayesian linear regression framework to estimate household head consumption expenditure. Three prior configurations (weak, positive, and negative) are applied to the CPI coefficient to simulate the role of expert-informed beliefs about macro-level price fluctuations. Results show that per capita income is the most influential predictor. Household size is 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nd</sup> influential, and the only variable negatively correlated. The 3<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rd</sup> significant variable, CPI, remains positive under all priors' settings. Education, strata, and age exhibit smaller yet credible positive impacts, whereas ethnicity, marital status, and state display credible intervals encompassing zero. K-fold cross-validation method is used to test model's generalisability. Using 5-fold, the model obtained R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.8641, RMSE of 0.3687, and similar WAIC values were observed across three priors, confirming the model's robustness. These findings emphasise the importance of Bayesian approach in quantifying uncertainty and highlight significant influence of inflation in shaping consumption expenditure. Compared to traditional frequentist linear regression method which requires repeated sampling method to estimate the confidence interval, Bayesian linear regression approach directly inform probabilistic interpretation of parameter uncertainty, known as credible intervals. This characteristic is particularly useful in real-world policy-making field. This insight can guide targeted policies in dealing with economic phenomena such as price volatility. Future research could further extend to incorporate more macroeconomic factors such as GDP and unemployment rate. Integrating real expert opinions into the prior distributions could also further enhance the model's performance and serve as useful information source when observed data is hard to obtain.

title: Shiny Objects: Object-Centric Characterization of Chromium
authors: Saket Upadhyay, Ashish Venkat
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Measurement and Analysis of Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: On Abnormal Execution Timing of Conditional Jump Instructions
authors: Annika Wilde, Samira Briongos, Claudio Soriente, Ghassan Karame
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Measurement and Analysis of Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: Advancing Bayesian Inference with Quantile-Based Likelihoods
authors: Imran Ali Mohammed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper extends Bayesian inference capabilities to accommodate probability distributions defined through their quantile functions. We present an indirect likelihood method for Bayesian models where sampling distributions lack an explicit cumulative form. The equivalence of this quantile-based approach to conventional density-defined likelihoods is demonstrated. Practical considerations for the numerical inversion of quantile functions, including ensuring their validity using polynomial approximations, are addressed, with implementation guidance provided for common statistical platforms. Additionally, an indirect prior definition is proposed, offering new avenues for robust Bayesian analysis in complex distributional settings.

title: AI-SEFAS: An AI-Driven Framework for Spectral Efficiency Optimization in Fluid Antenna Systems for 6G Networks
authors: El Miloud Ar-Reyouchi, Asmaa Kharouaa, Ayoub Hadj Sadek, Kamal Ghoumid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper introduces AI-SEFAS (Artificial Intelligence-powered Spectral Efficiency Fluid Antenna System), an artificial intelligence-driven framework designed to optimize spectral efficiency (SE) in sixth-generation (6 G) wireless networks utilizing Fluid Antenna Systems (FAS). By integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for fast beamforming weight prediction and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for adaptive antenna repositioning, the proposed approach jointly addresses the challenges of dynamic user mobility, channel variations, and multi-user coordination. Unlike conventional methods that treat beamforming and antenna placement as separate problems, AI-SEFAS performs end-to-end optimization to maximize spectral utilization (bps/Hz) while effectively mitigating inter-cell interference in ultra-dense deployments. Extensive simulations under realistic 6 G scenarios demonstrate that AI-SEFAS achieves substantial gains in spectral efficiency, adaptability, and scalability compared to state-of-the-art Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) and Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS)assisted systems. These results highlight the potential of AI-SEFAS as a key enabler for intelligent and flexible next-generation wireless networks.

title: Assessing Semantic Consistency of Court Judgments Using Domain-Specific Language Models and Multivariate Statistical Tests
authors: Lohitha Kanisettypalli, Jiya Borikar, Harsda Shrivastava, Priyanka Prabhakar, Peeta Basa Pati
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: We recognize that guaranteeing the fair enforcement of law across regional courts is critical to judicial consistency and public confidence. In our paper, we present a statistical approach to quantitatively evaluate the structural, linguistic, and sectional uniformity of legal judgments across various High Court jurisdictions in India. We represent legal judgments from civil and criminal domains as high-dimensional vectors using domain-specific transformer embeddings—specifically, InLegalBERT and a fine-tuned Custom-InLawBERT. We then run two strong multivariate statistical tests, ANOSIM (Analysis of Similarities) and PERMANOVA (Permutational Multivariate Analysis of Variance), on these embeddings to test for strong semantic differences between High Court regions (North, South, East, West). Across both embedding types and domains, we consistently observe low <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> statistics and non-significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex>-values, indicating no significant regional bias in judgment semantics. Our results corroborate the hypothesis that Indian High Courts enforce law equally, and we further establish the utility of embedding-based statistical testing in legal AI systems.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)

title: Heterogeneous IoT Traffic in Data Center Networks: Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Ze Zhang, Chongxiang Zhang, Shaohan Wu, Liye Zhu, Qian Dong, Limin Yu, Gordon Owusu Boateng, Junjie Zhang, Long Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: With the increasing demand for reliable computing power and large-scale storage, the Internet of Things (IoT) has been rapidly expanding, interconnecting a vast number of heterogeneous devices and enabling data-intensive applications. Data Center Networks (DCNs) play a critical role in processing massive volumes of sensor-generated data while ensuring high throughput and low latency. However, IoT traffic often combines continuous high-volume flows from devices such as cameras with short and bursty transmissions from low-power sensors. This heterogeneity often results in congestion, packet loss, and elevated latency within DCNs, thereby undermining overall network efficiency. Currently, most DCNs rely on the conventional TCP transport protocol, which is ill-suited to manage such diverse traffic dynamics and frequently exacerbates performance issues. This paper reviews existing research on IoT traffic characteristics, summarizes key performance requirements, and examines the limitations of traditional transport protocols. Finally, it discusses emerging approaches and outlines future directions for optimizing IoT traffic management in DCNs.

title: Norm-Q: Effective Compression Method for Hidden Markov Models in Neuro-Symbolic Applications
authors: Hanyuan Gao, Xiaoxuan Yang
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Hidden Markov models (HMM) are commonly used in generation tasks and have demonstrated strong capabilities in neuro-symbolic applications for the Markov property. These applications leverage the strengths of neural networks and symbolic reasoning to create robust and interpretable AI systems. However, they may inherit and amplify the shortcomings of both approaches. Both components require dense computation and data transfer, and their communication further hinders performance. This paper proposes Norm-Q, a normalized linear quantization approach for compressing probabilistic symbolic models, such as HMMs. We reduce the bit width of the data with minimal impact, thereby alleviating memory and bandwidth stress and enabling deployment on potential custom hardware. Our method introduces a normalized quantization-aware expectation maximization process for probabilistic model training. The experimental results show that Norm-Q achieves a higher compression rate with reasonable score loss compared to traditional quantization methods. In the case of the constrained generation task of large language models, we successfully quantize an HMM of 4096 hidden states to 8 bits without loss and, at most, 3 bits with acceptable loss. Notably, the Norm-Q method can achieve a compression rate of 99% for the weights of the HMM. The code is open source at https://github.com/superstarghy/Norm-Q.

title: Smart Reconstruction: AI-Driven Image Super-Resolution via CNN
authors: Nihar Shetty S, Ankitaa Pupneja, M J Akshaya, Anisha, Siddharth Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The demand for high-resolution (HR) digital imagery is ever-increasing across various domains, yet images are often limited to a low-resolution (LR) format due to hardware or bandwidth constraints. While state-of-the-art deep learning models achieve remarkable reconstruction quality, their immense computational cost often limits their practical deployment. This paper introduces EffiSR-Net, a computationally efficient Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) designed to address this performance-efficiency trade-off. Our approach involves modifying the established ESRGAN architecture by reducing its network depth from 23 to 10 residual blocks, thereby creating a lightweight variant. We conduct a systematic performance analysis, comparing EffiSRNet against its parent model, ESRGAN, the Transformerbased SwinIR, and a traditional bicubic baseline. Quantitative evaluation demonstrates that EffiSR-Net achieves a significant reduction in model parameters and inference latency—nearly halving the size and doubling the speed of ESRGAN—while maintaining high-fidelity image reconstruction with only a marginal decrease in PSNR and SSIM scores. The results validate EffiSR-Net as a compelling alternative for applications where computational resources are limited, offering a practical balance between speed and quality for real-world superresolution tasks.

title: AI-Powered Smart Product Recommendation and Price Prediction System for Electronics
authors: Deepan S B, Ashwin Karthik S M, Priya S B
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The fast growth of the electronic appliances especially mobile phones and laptops has made consumers have too many options to choose. Although recommendation systems can give recommendations on a particular product based on their preferences, features, they seldom look at the economic aspect of the product belonging, including resale value and possible repair costs. The current study introduces a combined intelligent system, which fills this gap with a combination of personalized product recommendation and predictive cost analytics.
