A hybrid reference tracking strategy with adaptive weighting is designed to enable fast and smooth trajectory transitions during cut-in maneuvers. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of maneuver execution quality and convergence performance under representative cut-in scenarios.

title: Understanding the Mechanism of Discretionary Lane-Changing Behavior Based on Cumulative Prospect Theory and Decision Tree Model
authors: Wenbin Yao, Waner Li, Sheng Jin, Xinyi Shen, Jiaqi Zeng, Chunqin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Discretionary lane changing is one of the most important behaviors in vehicle dynamics. The analysis of discretionary lane changing can provide support for human-like autonomous driving system and microscopic traffic simulation. The rule based discretionary lane change decision-making model has the advantages of good interpretability and being in line with human intuitiveness, but the performance of the rule based models are relatively poor. The learning-based discretionary lane changing decision-making models perform better in lane changing analysis than that of the rule based models, while they require a large amount of training data and have poor interpretability. This study proposes a framework for discretionary lane changing decision-making based on cumulative prospect theory and decision tree model, and uses genetic algorithm to calibrate the parameters of the framework. The framework proposed in this study has good interpretability for discretionary lane changing behavior similar to rule-based models, and it can achieve good lane changing prediction performance. The Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) dataset is used to validate the framework proposed in this study. The results show that the framework proposed in this study can achieve better performance of discretionary lane change prediction than the analysis model based on decision tree and the analysis model based on cumulative prospect theory. When predicting discretionary lane changing behavior 2–0 seconds in advance, the accuracy of the framework in the test set can reach 85.6%.

title: CondFusionSAR: A Bidirectional Multi-Conditional Fusion Network for Generating High-Resolution SAR Images with Complex Backgrounds
authors: Moran Ju, Mulin Li, Tengkai Mao, Sinian Jin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Currently, data-driven deep learning models advance slowly in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship image processing, largely because acquiring SAR images is quite challenging. Though SAR generation models offer a solution, their practical application value is often limited due to inadequate modeling of scene elements, poor controllability of ship positions, and ship offset or loss issues in complex scenes, To tackle the above challenges, we design a bidirectional multi conditional fusion network named CondFusionSAR with the location and class of targets as multi-conditional inputs to controllably generate SAR ship images with complex backgrounds. We decouple SAR synthesis into bidirectional branches, namely a ship synthesis branch and a background synthesis branch. The former adopts anencoder-decoder architecture with the proposed Semantic Injection Module (SIM), which injects mask priors to address the issue of ships target loss in complex scene. The latter employs a mask-based diffusion model enhanced by Mask-Aware Cross-Attention (MaCA) to guide feature generation with the mask, and Multi-Scale Aggregation (MSA) to enhance background modeling capabilities. Moreover, a ship-background fuser with the Spectral Attention Module (SAM) is designed to integrate outputs by applying learnable attention in both spatial and frequency domains, generating complex SAR image. Extensive experiments conducted on improved dataset show that CondFusionSAR can generate high-fidelity SAR ship images. Particularly, with the provided target annotation, the synthesized images can be directly used in other SAR ship tasks, which saves the time required for manual annotation.

title: Multi-Terabit Coherent Free-Space Optical Communication Over a 4.6 km Urban Channel
authors: Vincent van Vliet, Menno van den Hout, Kadir Gümü¸s, Eduward Tangdiongga, Chigo Okonkwo
abstract: We established Reid Photonloop, a permanent 4.6 km testbed for free-space optical (FSO) communications, in Eindhoven, The Netherlands, to characterize the urban FSO channel in detail and validate the implementations of the FSO communication system. In this paper, we present an experimental field demonstration of a coherent wavelength-division multiplexed (WDM) system capable of transmitting data with a multi terabit-per-second net rate over the 4.6 km terrestrial urban FSO link, measured continuously for 12 days. By employing 22 WDM channels with 50-GHz channel spacing and using modulation formats 4-, 8-, and 16-QAM, we measure low density parity-check-decoded data rates of up to 7.73Tb/s. Additionally, we investigate the concurrent channel conditions by analyzing the fiber-coupled optical received power, as well as the impact of rainfall and visibility information collected by a weather station on the FSO data transmission. The 12-day measurements enable a detailed analysis of channel attenuation, scintillation, and outage events under varying weather conditions, revealing the impact on data transmission performance. We further quantify the outage probability of the link and its corresponding availability for different atmospheric conditions and modulation formats, highlighting the trade-off between availability and decoded data rate. The results advance understanding of the FSO communication channel dynamics in urban environments, demonstrate its multi-terabit-per-second potential, and can inform future designs of high-capacity FSO networks in urban areas.

title: Hela: A System Call Restriction Framework for Protecting the Entire Containers Lifecycle
authors: Shaohu Li, Jin Zhou, Xinxin Li, Weizhi Meng, Bei Gong, Jun Hu
abstract: Limiting the number of system calls used by container processes can effectively reduce the kernel attack surface. Existing container system call restriction schemes only focus on the minimum system call set of applications in containers, and lack restrictions on the container runtime runc and other container components that create containers. To solve these problems, this paper proposes Hela, a system call restriction framework that can limit container runtimes and container applications. Hela introduces the Attack Surface Exposure Score (ASES), defined as the dot product of a container's system call usage vector and a risk-weight vector, to quantify exposure. Hela calculates and compares the ASES indicators of various partitioning schemes and selects the best partitioning boundary in the common hook nodes of runc. Hela divides the container creation phase into two phases and generates a minimum set of system calls for each phase. The advantage of Hela is that it combines seccomp with eBPF to achieve accurate parameter checking and efficient system call whitelist switching. Experimental results show that Hela can reduce the kernel attack surface of runc in container runtime compared to traditional schemes. Security experiments prove that our method can mitigate vulnerabilities involving runc and system call parameters.

title: Cheaper Clicks from Boxes: Cyclic Querying for Interactive Small Object Detection
authors: Xiang Yuan, Ruixiang Yao, Junwei Han, Gong Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Interactive object detection paves a promising way to alleviate the annotation burden, particularly for size-limited instances in remote sensing images. While early studies emphasize fortifying object representations through click-based interactions, the alternative strategy of harnessing image as a prior to derive informative clicks remains largely under-explored—despite its substantial potential in advancing interactive understanding. To address this limitation, we devise a CycLIc Querying (CLIQ) paradigm tailored for interactive small object detection in remote sensing imagery. The core lies in an Interactive Semantic Querying framework, where user clicks and visual representations interact in a mutually reinforcing fashion: the click prior first shepherds feature modulation and correlates with a set of class-wise queries to elicit rich semantic responses; the resulting heatmap, in turn, dynamically functions as a spatial activator to highlight small-instance regions. Furthermore, we devise a simple yet effective Informative Click Querying strategy to pinpoint potential click candidates, thereby completing the closed image-to-click interaction loop and ameliorating the performance under few-click scenarios. Thanks to its lightweight cyclic querying design, the proposed CLIQ framework achieves competitive results on the AITOD-R and Tiny-DOTA benchmarks with negligible computational overhead, significantly outperforming pioneering attempts in small object detection. More importantly, the superior performance of our interactive paradigm under low-data regimes underscores its strong potential as a cost-effective and adaptive alternative for data-efficient detection workflows. Code is available at https://github.com/shaunyuan22/CLIQ.

title: Diffusion Bridge Generation of Visible Light to Infrared Images Based on Multi-Scale Enhancement from Aerial Photography Perspective
authors: Xin Wang, Wei Cai, Yao Ding, Xingyu Di, Zhongjie Yin, Haoran Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: To address the challenges of imbalanced multi-scale feature rhythm, network representation defects, and insufficient loss constraints in the generation of infrared images converted from aerial visible light images, a diffusion bridge generation model focusing on multi-scale enhancement is proposed. Starting from the full-link design of multi-scale generation, the model, in terms of diffusion process scheduling, designs a multi-scale scheduling optimized differential equation, adjusts multi-scale features by combining with a gated polynomial enhancement term, and realizes time-varying variance scheduling with a thermodynamic factor, so as to adapt to the logic of first constructing large-scale structures and then refining small-scale thermal details. In terms of network structure enhancement, it builds a time-modulated multi-scale enhancement network, which regulates the intensity of infrared thermal features at each scale according to time steps—ensuring the thermal distribution of large-scale structures in the early stage and improving the authenticity of small-scale details in the later stage; it also proposes a frequency decomposition and fusion module, which decouples high-frequency and low-frequency features through discrete wavelet transform, repairs high-frequency details by virtue of discrete Fourier transform, and realizes dynamic fusion by combining time parameters. In terms of the loss function, it uses high-, medium-, and low-frequency losses to constrain pixel accuracy, and matches LPPIS perceptual loss to optimize semantic consistency, thus constructing an information closed loop. In the comprehensive scene test on the DroneVehicle dataset, the model outperforms 14 mainstream Generative Adversarial Network(GAN) and diffusion models, with core indicators as follows: PSNR(Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio) of 15.06, SSIM(Structural Similarity Index Measure) of 49.78, LPIPS(Learning-Perceived Image Block Similarity) of 30.71, and FID(Frechet Inception Distance) of 43.88; it also shows outstanding performance in sub-scenarios—for single-target scenarios, PSNR is 16.78 and SSIM is 59.48; for dense-target scenarios, FID is 34.67; for mixed-target scenarios, compared with the second-best method, PSNR increases by 0.58% and FID decreases by 15.36%. The model successfully distinguishes cross-category multi-scale features and realizes the generation of multi-scale infrared images in complex aerial scenarios.

title: MIRAGE-DoH: Metamorphic Intelligence and Resilient AI Grid for Autonomous Governance of Encrypted DNS
authors: Basharat Ali, Guihai Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Existing DNS over HTTPS defenses have demonstrated limited resilience against polymorphic traffic shaping, staged tunneling, and adaptive mimicry, largely because they rely on static learning pipelines and rigid cryptographic configurations. MIRAGE-DoH was designed to examine whether adaptive inference, persistent structural encoding, and calibrated cryptographic agility could be integrated into a deployable and measurable encrypted DNS control architecture. The framework combined flow-level Cognitive MetaAgents capable of internal reconfiguration, Topological Memory Networks that preserved stable geometric irregularities across temporal windows, and Metamorphic Cryptographic Shards that adjusted key encapsulation policies according to empirically calibrated threat severity. A Causal Counterfactual Environment modeled constrained attacker decision pathways, while Spectral Game Intelligence analyzed flow interaction graphs to anticipate structural attack transitions.Evaluation on extended CIC-DoH2023 and Gen-C-DDD-2022 datasets was conducted under fixed flow-level decision intervals with explicit accounting for synchronization overhead, spectral graph construction cost, and cryptographic rotation latency. Cross-dataset experiments yielded a mean detection accuracy of 97.8% with a 0.41% false positive rate, sustaining median inference latency of 62μs and cryptographic morph latency of 3.7 ms under load. Quantum-assisted inference was assessed through bounded simulations, indicating constrained information gain within the adopted lattice-based configuration, without asserting unconditional post-quantum immunity. These results demonstrated that adaptive encrypted DNS governance can be empirically grounded, operationally bounded, and stress-evaluated without reliance on unqualified claims of perfect security.

title: Secure and Efficient Keyword Search over Encrypted Graphs with Trusted Hardware
authors: Qiuhao Wang, Xu Yang, Saiyu Qi, Yiwei Liu, Hongguang Zhao, Ke Li, Wenjia Zhao
abstract: Graph databases are increasingly outsourced to cloud servers for efficient management of entity relationships, yet this raises significant privacy concerns. Although PeGraph, the state-of-the-art scheme, supports encrypted conjunctive search over the graph, it suffers from limitations such as heavy client burden (requiring data users to maintain secret keys and generate extensive search tokens securely), high computational cost, result pattern leakage, and an inability to handle updates and verification. To address these issues, we propose SecGraph, a system that leverages an SGX-enabled cloud server to offload key protection and computation into a trusted enclave. We further design an SGX-aware data structure, LDCF-encoded XSet, based on a Logarithmic Dynamic Cuckoo Filter, to enable efficient plaintext computation within constrained trusted memory while mitigating pattern leakage. Additionally, we introduce Twin-TSet to support dynamic graph updates while ensuring forward and backward privacy. To support verifiable search, we propose VSec-Graph, which employs a procedure-oriented verification method to efficiently validate all data structures inside the enclave, avoiding costly local verification. Security analyses demonstrate the security properties of both schemes. Experimental results show that SecGraph achieves up to 208× faster search than PeGraph, while VSecGraph reduces verification time by 103× compared to VCQ.

title: Predictive Human Trajectory Classification in Industrial Settings with Transfer Learning and Data Augmentation
authors: Even Falkenberg Langås, Atle Aalerud, Svein Olav Nyberg, Muhammad Hamza Zafar, Daniel Hagen, Filippo Sanfilippo
abstract: The successful realisation of Industry 5.0 relies on seamless collaboration between humans and robots in shared workspaces. This paper investigates predictive human trajectory classification (HTC) in a factory setting to enhance safety, productivity, and adaptability in human-machine environments. Using a state-of-the-art light detection and ranging (LiDAR) system with reflective mirrors to extend the field of view, motion data are collected across a 26 m × 20 m indoor industrial floor. Human trajectories are classified to predict workstation transitions using a stacked long-short term memory (LSTM) network. To address the challenges of limited real-world data, transfer learning is applied by first training the model on synthetically generated trajectories. The paper compares a traditional stochastic method, specifically the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) process, with a modern learning based method, represented by a timeseries generative adversarial network (TimeGAN). Further on, the algorithm is fine-tuned on the real-world dataset. The comparison shows that in data-scarce scenarios, OU outperforms TimeGAN in most metrics. The LSTM model trained on OU-based data and real-world data shows an overall accuracy gain of 4.8 percentage points compared to training on real-world data only. It is able to classify the goal workstation three seconds in advance with an accuracy of 95.6%, demonstrating the potential for predictive models to improve workplace operations by reducing stall time, optimising workstation readiness, and reducing the need for traditional safety cages.

title: FEM-Based Quantification of Eddy-Current Losses for MV Cables in Trefoil Formation with Non-Magnetic Screens
authors: Marko Šućurović, Dardan Klimenta, Nikolay Hinov, Dragan Tasić, Mladen Banjanin, Darius Andriukaitis
abstract: Circulating- and eddy-current losses occur in non-magnetic screens and armors of three medium-voltage (MV) single-core cables installed in trefoil formation, where they are bonded and earthed at both ends. According to IEC 60287-1-1, eddy-current losses in non-magnetic screens and armors should be neglected, whereas the technical brochure CIGRÉ TB 880 recommends that these losses should always be taken into account. For the considered screen bonding design, a reliable quantitative separation of circulating- and eddy-current losses is therefore essential. Such a separation cannot be performed accurately while simultaneously using experimental methods alone, but it can be achieved through a combination of analytical modeling and finite element method (FEM)-based electro–magneto–thermal simulations. This paper proposes and applies a new hybrid FEM-based procedure for the quantitative determination of circulating- and eddy-current losses in MV cables. The method is applied to Cu/XLPE/CTS/PVC/AWA/PVC 1/C 19/33 kV (BS 6622) cables with twelve different conductor cross-sections. The accuracy of the proposed procedure is verified by comparisons with IEC-based analytical calculations that account for eddy-current losses in accordance with the technical brochures CIGRÉ TB 272 and CIGRÉ TB 880. The FEM-based electro–magneto–thermal models are calibrated and validated using experimental data already published for PL 1×300 RM 6/10 kV cables. The developed calibration strategy represents another novelty. The results demonstrated that, for the BS 6622 cables with conductor cross-sections ranging from 70 to 1000 mm², the screen and armor eddy-current loss factors lie within the ranges 0.00094–0.03625 and 0.00719–0.1951, respectively. The deviations between the FEM- and IEC-based eddy-current loss factors range from −6.398% to 5.302%, demonstrating a very good agreement.

title: Applicability of DIN ISO 14505-2 in short-term Neutral Thermal Comfort Assessment for Highly Automated Vehicles
authors: Manuel Kipp, Chira Beji, Klaus Bengler
abstract: Highly automated vehicles enable non-driving-related activities (NDRAs) such as working, reading, or listening to music which modify posture and local boundary conditions relevant for thermal comfort. DIN EN ISO 14505-2 provides an equivalent-temperature framework for vehicle comfort assessment but its application has primarily focused on conventional driver postures. This study evaluates the applicability of DIN EN ISO 14505-2 and the Nilsson comfort-zone concept to NDRA-relevant postures under stationary near-neutral conditions. A two-step workflow is applied in which a segmented equivalent-temperature dummy first identifies near-neutral combinations of heated-panel surface temperature and outlet air temperature under controlled radiant and convective conditions in a climatic-chamber vehicle mock-up. The resulting setpoints are then transferred to a subject study with 33 participants performing working, reading, and listening to music for 12 min each. Two counterbalanced sequence groups (1–2–3 vs. 3–2–1) are used to assess order effects under identical boundary conditions. The protocol targets short-term near-neutral exposures and is not designed to establish thermophysiological steady state. Global and local sensation and comfort are collected with standardized scales and a core-temperature proxy is recorded. Across activities, sensation remains between slightly cool and neutral and no significant activity-related comfort differences are observed within the short exposures, while localized effects occur in selected regions. Core-temperature proxy tendencies are small and interpreted within measurement uncertainty. The results support DIN EN ISO 14505-2 as a practical near-neutral target framework for NDRA-relevant postures and motivate longer and more thermally challenging scenarios for future work.

title: UAV Trajectory Planning for Computational Offloading and Resource Allocation Optimizing in UAV-assisted MEC with Unavailable Base Station
authors: Xiao Zeng, Guofeng Yan, Hengliang Tan, Jiao Du, Xia Deng, Heng Wu
abstract: The task offloading and resource allocation strategies aim to provide efficient edge computing services for ground mobile devices in mobile edge computing (MEC) environments. In this paper, we propose a UAV-assisted dynamic deployment and decision-making framework (dDDM) to address the issue of insufficient base station coverage in remote areas or emergency scenarios, which leverages the flexibility and computational capabilities of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) to supplement terrestrial cellular networks. We focus on optimizing the UAV trajectory to support and enhance the effectiveness of task offloading and resource allocation strategies under conditions of user mobility and stochastic task arrivals. Therefore, a joint optimization problem is formulated with the objective of minimizing the weighted sum of the total latency of ground mobile devices and the overall energy consumption of UAV. To solve this problem, the original optimization problem is decomposed into two subproblems: UAV trajectory planning, and decision-making for task offloading & computing resource allocation. For the UAV trajectory planning subproblem, we propose an improved particle swarm optimization(PSO) algorithm integrated with K-means clustering, i.e., KMPSO algorithm, enabling dynamic UAV deployment in response to user mobility. For the task offloading and resource allocation decision-making, an improved black-winged kite algorithm (IBKA) is developed to determine optimal offloading and allocation strategies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms existing benchmark algorithms in terms of both latency and energy consumption.

title: Robust Beamforming for Practical RIS-Aided RSMA Systems with Imperfect SIC under Transceiver Hardware Impairments
authors: Xuejun Cheng, Qian Zhang, Yunnuo Xu, Zheng Dong, Ju Liu, Bruno Clerckx
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-aided rate splitting multiple access (RSMA) systems have demonstrated remarkable potential in enhancing spectral efficiency. However, most existing works rely on ideal hardware, which is unrealistic. In practical deployments, RIS elements suffer from amplitude–phase coupling, where transceivers are subject to hardware impairments (HWI), and successive interference cancellation (SIC) in RSMA networks cannot achieve perfect interference elimination for decoded signals. To address these limitations, we investigate a robust beamforming design for RIS-aided RSMA systems under practical hardware imperfections. We first characterize the asymptotic signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of practical RIS systems when the beamformer is designed based on ideal RIS model, thereby theoretically quantifying the resulting performance degradation. We then derive a closed form expression for the distortion noise power induced by transceiver HWI, while also accounting for residual interference due to imperfect SIC. Building on these insights, we establish a comprehensive system model that jointly incorporates all hardware-induced impairments and formulate a multiuser sum rate maximization problem. To solve the resulting non-convex optimization problem, we develop an efficient block variable relaxation algorithm. Simulation results verify that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms conventional non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) approaches, and achieves superior robustness compared with benchmark schemes neglecting HWI, imperfect SIC, or amplitude–phase coupling.

title: A Phase-Noise-Based Method for Calculating Random Jitter Amplification in Clock Channels
authors: Tao Wei, Xiuqin Chu, Yuhao Huang, Haiyue Yuan, Yajun Lv, Jun Wang, Yushan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: As data transmission rates escalate across successive generations, the impact of timing jitter on the performance of high-speed systems becomes increasingly significant. Moreover, jitter is exacerbated by frequency-dependent channel characteristics as signals propagate through the interconnects. In this paper, an analytical methodology based on phase noise theory is proposed to accurately quantify the random jitter amplification factor in clock signals. To ensure the high accuracy, the proposed method incorporates the higher-order spectral components of the clock waveform rather than relying on sinusoidal approximations. Extensive simulations and empirical measurements conducted on transmission lines and 5th-generation double data rate (DDR5) channels validate the effectiveness and precision of the proposed approach. This methodology provides essential insights into jitter mitigation strategies pertinent to high-speed channel design.

title: Doubly Minimized Petz & Sandwiched Rényi Mutual Information: Properties
authors: Laura Burri
abstract: The doubly minimized Petz Rényi mutual information of order α is defined as the minimization of the Petz divergence of order α of a fixed bipartite quantum state relative to any product state. The doubly minimized sandwiched Rényi mutual information is defined analogously using the sandwiched divergence in place of the Petz divergence. In this work, we establish several properties of these two types of Rényi mutual information. In particular, for the Petz case, we prove additivity for α ∈ [1/2, 2]. For the sandwiched case, we establish a novel duality relation for α ∈ [2/3,∞] via Sion’s minimax theorem, and we subsequently use this duality relation to prove additivity for the same range of α. Previously, additivity for the sandwiched case was known only for α ∈ [1,∞], but it had been conjectured to hold for α ∈ [1/2,∞].

title: Privacy-Preserving Multi-Modal Object Fusion for Connected Autonomous Vehicles: Resilience Against Malicious Third-Party Attacks
authors: Renwan Bi, Jinbo Xiong, Xu Yang, Yuanyuan Zhang, Zhiqiang Ruan, Junfeng Tian, Xun Yi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs) utilize multi-modal sensors, such as LiDAR and high-definition cameras, to collect diverse types of sensing data. Fusing object detection information from these two modalities facilitates more accurate environmental perception. In this context, lightweight secret sharing techniques are employed to protect information privacy, enabling further calculation while effectively alleviating the computational resource constraints of CAVs. Meanwhile, such techniques require an additional third-party to generate some necessary random numbers. Addressing the challenges of privacy disclosure of multi-modal object information and the reliability of random numbers, we propose a malicious third-party-resistant privacy-preserving multi-modal object fusion model, termed MPOF. First, we develop a series of secure computation protocols that do not rely on time-consuming cryptographic primitives, including secure multiplication, secure sharing conversion, and secure comparison. Leveraging the idea of sacrificial verification, we can effectively detect malicious behavior by the third-party during the random number generation process. Second, we construct a secure object bounding-box matching module based on arithmetic secret sharing (ASS), enabling similarity calculation and matching of bounding-boxes between point cloud and image modalities. Additionally, we design a secure object score fusion module that achieves fusion and updating through secure implementations of convolution, ReLU, and Maxout operations. Detailed theoretical analysis and experimental results demonstrate that, compared to secure computation protocols using homomorphic encryption for random number generation, the proposed protocols reduce computational overhead by five orders of magnitude. Furthermore, the MPOF model constructed by integrating these protocols is secure, accurate, and efficient.

title: Adaptive AI ECC Hybrid Burst Error Correction using Deep Learning for Next Generation Communication and Memory Devices
authors: Shiv Prasad Lingampally, Chakradhar Adupa, Gyanendra Kumar Maurya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Burst errors—extended runs of consecutive bit corruptions—pose a fundamental challenge for next-generation systems such as 6G wireless networks and QLC NAND flash memories. Conventional error correction codes (ECC), such as LDPC and BCH, offer strong protection against random noise but deteriorate markedly when faced with structured burst patterns. Conversely, AI-based detectors can localize burst-prone regions but lack mathematically grounded correction. To bridge this gap, we introduce a hybrid error-control framework that couples deep learning models (CNNs, LSTMs, and Transformers) for burst-region detection with LDPC decoding to ensure reliable correction. Evaluation was carried out using synthetic datasets together with real-world traces covering wireless, optical, and memory use cases. Experimental evaluations confirm consistent gains in bit error rate (BER), burst coverage ratio (BCR), and decoding latency, outperforming both ECC-only and AI-only baselines. Beyond performance gains, the design emphasizes explainability, scalability, and hardware readiness, thereby positioning it as a strong candidate for real-time deployment in future communication networks and high-density storage systems.

title: MiniV-Bat: A Micro Bi-Copter UAV With Bias-CoG Stability and Optimized Flight Endurance
authors: Yinglun Liu, Yubing Bao, Yang Guo, Zhulin Wang, Xiang He
abstract: Bi-copter UAVs offer greater endurance potential compared to quadrotors but suffer from nonminimum phase (NMP) dynamics and center of gravity (CoG) sensitivity. This article presents MiniV-Bat, a 233.7 g bi-copter with a novel rotor disk-based control framework that simultaneously addresses both challenges. The controller eliminates NMP behavior by redefining the control objective to the rotor-disk plane while incorporating feedforward compensation for unknown CoG biases. Co-optimization of mechanical design and control enables exceptional performance: maximum 74-minute hover endurance–a record for sub-250 g bi-copters–with demonstrated stability under 45<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> body tilts from CoG displacement and robust disturbance rejection.

title: A Robustness-Enhanced Dual-Loop Control Strategy Based on SMC for Vienna Rectifier
authors: Hao Ding, Jinrui Xu, Jing Yuan, Shouxiang Li, Zhenbin Zhang
abstract: Although the Vienna rectifier is a promising rectifier topology for broad applications, its high-performance control is susceptible to disturbances such as parameter mismatches and load variations. To enhance the robustness against disturbances while maintaining a low chattering level, this article proposes a novel observer-based terminal sliding mode controller (TSMC) approach for current-loop control. First, after analyzing the inherent chattering and inaccurate voltage vector selection associated with conventional TSMC, we develop an error-based fixed-time supertwisting extended state observer (FTST-EESO) and integrated within the TSMC framework. This integration effectively improves the dynamic response, suppresses chattering, eliminates zero-crossing current distortion, and ensures balanced neutral-point voltage. Second, recognizing the limited robustness of conventional proportional-integral (PI) controller in voltage-loop control, we propose a hybrid control strategy termed the barrier function-based adaptive-gain supertwisting algorithm (BAGSTA). By constructing adaptive control gains based on positive-definite barrier function (PBF) and synthesizing the merits of PI controller and the supertwisting algorithm (STA), BAGSTA enables the system to flexibly configure the controller under diverse operating conditions, exhibiting high performance and enhanced the robustness in both steady-state and transient operations. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed scheme is validated through experimental results.

title: RAG SLIITbot: Sri Lankan Universities Develop AI Tutor Tailored to Unique Teaching Styles
authors: Kithusshand Raveendran, Gowrisha Karuneswaran, Vasavan Parthipan, Kanagasabai Thiruthanigesan, Uthpala Samarakoon, Nuwan Kodagoda, Roshan G. Ragel
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has enabled personalized learning, but general-purpose chatbots like ChatGPT often lack curriculum alignment. This paper introduces RAG SLIITbot, a specialized AI tutor for Sri Lankan higher education, built on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to deliver accurate, subject-specific responses grounded solely in lecturer-uploaded materials. The system processes PDF, DOCX, and TXT files through a multimodal pipeline that extracts text and visuals using AI transcription, then generates semantic embeddings via OpenAI models stored in a Pinecone vector database. Leveraging LangChain for orchestration, Pinecone for indexing, and GPT-4 for generation, RAG SLIITbot delivers responses through a Streamlit interface. Evaluated on ten software engineering queries, it achieved 92% accuracy and 90% curriculum specificity, significantly outperforming ChatGPT (70% accuracy, 60% specificity), with differences confirmed by the Wilcoxon signed-rank test (p < 0.01). A rubric-based assessment showed high inter-rater reliability (Cohen’s Kappa k = 0.87). User surveys of 50 students and 10 lecturers reported 88% overall satisfaction, with 92% rating ease of use and 89% curriculum relevance positively. RAG SLIITbot reduces hallucinations, ensures content fidelity, and supports student-centered learning in resource-constrained settings, demonstrating how domain-specific AI tutors can be effectively tailored to local educational needs.

title: Exploring Geo-demographic and Digital Drivers of Consumer Behavior in Telangana: Urban and Rural Perspectives on Brand Choice
authors: Padma. A, N. Suman Kumar, D. Srinivas, Ramesh G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper will examine the relationship between consumer preference on fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) products in both urban and rural areas in Telangana, India, and the brand attributes and geo-demographic variables. This has been as the consumers are taking health maintenance more into consideration in their consumer behavior, and brand choice has become a factor of significant influence in the consumer buying behavior. This study examines the influence of variables, brand consciousness, brand loyalty, price, and emotional connection on consumer choices. The study uses SPSS to support the testing of appropriate hypotheses based on statistical results in relation to the applied qualitative and quantitative research method design, and primary data gathered using Likert 5point scale structured questionnaire through 230 respondents. Results indicate that demographic factor, which includes age, gender, income, marital status, and education, plays a significant part on the decision of consumers to buy products or services. These findings demonstrate the significance of brand attributes and the effect they have on consumer behavior and creation of branding strategies to create customer relationships with various consumer segments.

title: Comparative Analysis of Transformer and CNN Architectures for OCT-Based Detection of Circular Leaf Spot Disease in Diospyros kaki
authors: Lakshan Madhushanka Bandara, Ovini Seneviratne, Deshan Kalupahana, Bhagya Nathali Silva, Udaya Wijenayake, Ruchire Eranga Wijesinghe
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Circular Leaf Spot (CLS) disease caused by Mycosphaerella nawae poses a significant threat to the cultivation of Diospyros kaki (persimmon). Early and accurate identification is essential to reduce yield loss, yet conventional diagnostic methods are often invasive, subjective, or time-consuming. Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) offers a non-destructive imaging technique capable of revealing detailed leaf structures, allowing the use of deep learning models for automated disease classification. This study investigates the performance of transformer-based architectures, including ViT, Swin, CoaT, ConViT, LeViT, and MobileViT, for the classification of CLS disease OCT images. The same dataset as in previous CNN-based research was used, applying identical preprocessing techniques for fair comparison. Research was conducted using a 5-fold cross-validation, and transformer models were compared with traditional CNNs from the previous research, such as DenseNet121, VGG16, and Xception. The results show that Transformer architectures, particularly the hybrid CoaT model, achieve superior overall accuracy (87.6%) and micro-averaged AUC (0.974), surpassing the best CNN-based models. The findings highlight the strong potential of transformer architectures in the diagnosis of plant diseases using OCT, especially for early-stage detection. This research underscores the suitability of advanced vision models for high-resolution non-destructive classification tasks in agriculture.

title: On the security of Sequential Multi-signer Ring Signature for Secure Medical Data Sharing in IoMT
authors: Jianhong Zhang, Chuming Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: In the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), transmitted medical data contain sensitive patient information. To protect patient identity privacy and address the adaptability limitations of traditional ring signatures, Xu et al. proposed the anonymous sequential multi-signature ring signature (ASMR) to enable secure medical data sharing in the IoMT (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security, DOI 10.1109/TIFS.2025.3574959). Although Xu et al. claim that the ASMR scheme is secure, our analysis demonstrates that it is insecure, as it is existentially and universally forgeable, allowing any entity to generate a valid ring signature for arbitrary messages. Following the presentation of the attack, we analyze the causes of these vulnerabilities and propose corresponding countermeasures.

title: Eclipse Refactoring Preconditions
authors: N/A
abstract: None

title: Internet of Medical Things Through Explainable AI at the Edge: A Practical Case Study
authors: Arcangela Rago, Alessio Fascista, Giuseppe Piro, Gennaro Boggia, Luigi Alfredo Grieco
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is emerging as an enabler of trustworthy and human-aligned decision making in next-generation IoT ecosystems. As 6G envisions a highly distributed Global Internet of Things paradigm, embedding explainability of AI models at the network edge will be essential for supporting critical emerging domains such as the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). In this paper, we explore a practical integration of XAI methods at the edge of 6G IoT architectures and propose novel explainable feature selection and adaptive sample selection strategies that allow low-cost IoT devices to improve data acquisition, on-device processing, and communication efficiency in large-scale deployments. Through a practical case study using an experimental IoMT dataset and comparing different learning models, we show how SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) can be used to interpret patient-related data, improve clinical insight, and significantly boost key performance and value indicators such as learning accuracy, energy consumption, and bandwidth utilization, outperforming traditional dimensionality-reduction techniques.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation

title: Self-repair environmentally friendly insulation material based on disulfide bond and DA reaction
authors: Bo Zhu, Bo Yang, Yu Feng, Wenliang Xu, Zhiqing Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) is widely used in high-voltage cable insulation for its good insulation and mechanical properties. However, long-term use leads to damage, breakdown, and environmental pollution due to non-recyclability and high thermal degradation. This study designs an S-S-DA cross-linker with reversible Diels-Alder (DA) and disulfide bonds using isocyanate reactions. Graft cross-linking forms XLPE-S-S-DA, and doping silane-modified SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> creates XLPE-S-S-DA/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> composite for self-repair. Tests show elongation at break increases by 17.89%, tensile yield stress by 17.26%, and breakdown field strength by 34.4% compared to XLPE. It self-repairs mechanical/electrical damages (three mechanical repairs restore strain to 91.26%, yield stress to 82.65%, strength to 92.37%; three breakdowns restore field strength to 76.04%). Three recycling retains 68.89% strain, 71.59% strength (yield stress increases 6.46%), and 79.57% breakdown strength. Lower thermal degradation helps recycling. Dynamic cross-linking and doping create self-repair, recyclable XLPE composites, improving service life and eco-friendliness for high-voltage cable insulation, providing a new design strategy.

title: Scalable and Generalizable Cross-Domain Invariant Network Analysis and Intrusion Detection System Using Deep Learning
authors: George Thamer Francis, Abdulrahman Gashut, Mohanad Alayedi, Malek Malkawi
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Modern networks are increasingly complex and diverse—especially with the rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT)—so intrusion detection (ID) models that are tightly coupled to a single dataset or environment often fail when deployed elsewhere. This work studies cross-domain generalization within network-traffic domains and proposes a scalable neural classifier trained with quasi-Newton optimization: NN–BFGS in WEKA (MLPClassifier) and its low-memory variant NN–LBFGS in Python (scikit-learn). We evaluate on three widely used corpora: CICIDS_2017 (general network traffic), MQTT_IDS (protocol-specific), and ToN_IoT (system/host telemetry). Across MQTT scenarios, the proposed model achieves >99.90% accuracy with near-perfect recall and minimal false positives; on CICIDS_2017 it is robust across seven attack groups. We also assess scalability on merged scenarios, where performance is retained. From a feature perspective, we identify a compact set of 3 feature types (packet transmission, transmission pattern, and flags) that are invariant between the network-traffic datasets and can guide future model design and dataset construction. Finally, we present a reusable workflow architecture to facilitate building similar scalable ID systems.

title: On the Effectiveness of Radio Environmental Map Prediction Through Image-Based Deep Learning
authors: Mohammad Hossein Zadeh, Marina Barbiroli, Enrico Maria Vitucci, Vittorio Degli-Esposti, Franco Fuschini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper investigates the use of deep learning techniques for predicting 2D radio environment maps in urban microcellular scenarios. Starting from the common framework established by prior works, a comprehensive and systematic analysis is conducted to evaluate the impact of multiple factors—including neural network architecture, auxiliary input data, data augmentation strategies, and training dataset fidelity—on the prediction performance. A modified U-Net architecture, referred to as DeepUNet, is proposed, incorporating hybrid loss functions and deep supervision to enhance learning effectiveness. Extensive experiments are carried out by training the model via synthetically generated data using standard ray tracing, as well as multiple types of auxiliary data, including empirical path loss predictions, line-of-sight predictions, and sparsified or interpolated radio environment maps. Results demonstrate that, by properly combining the aforementioned techniques, the proposed deep learning model achieves high prediction accuracy, even in challenging configurations. Furthermore, generalization capabilities are evaluated by training and testing on different ray tracing datasets. A detailed performance analysis is provided to offer practical insights and design guidelines for future radio environment map prediction tasks.

title: MetaMLIR: Meta-Learning With Multilevel Integration for Motion Pattern Recognition of Maneuvering Targets
authors: Zhenzhen Ma, Wei An, Yuan Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Pattern recognition of maneuvering target is challenging due to its rapidly changing patterns and uncertainties. Short-term trajectories are essential for capturing dynamic motion changes. However, existing deep-learning based methods rely on long-term trajectories to extract more comprehensive information, making them less effective with limited data. In this paper, we focus on recognizing short-term trajectories and propose a multilevel integrated framework that narrows the latent search space. Our approach employs a two-level classification with meta-learning (MetaMLIR) to recognize target motion patterns by learning multivariate features from noisy observations. Specifically, the first-level classification generates preliminary scores derived from multiple heterogeneous classifiers with a balanced integration strategy. The second-level classification uses logistic regression with meta-learning to share knowledge from the first-level outputs and provide the final classification result for each motion behavior. Additionally, we develop a target motion trajectory dataset (namely, TMT) to conduct comprehensive performance evaluation. Experimental results on our self-developed TMT dataset and the real-world NCLT dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves enhanced motion-pattern recognition performance across both non-pattern-switching and pattern-switching scenarios.

title: Movable Antenna-Enabled MIMO Integrated Sensing and Communication: A Unified Mutual Information Framework
authors: Guangyi Chen, Ruoyu Zhang, Xinrong Guan, Qingqing Wu, Boyu Ning, Yu Zhang, Wen Wu, Rui Zhang
abstract: Movable antenna (MA)-enabled multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems offer a promising enhancement for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) applications. Unlike conventional MIMO systems with fixed-position antenna (FPA) arrays, MAs can flexibly adjust their positions within a given region, enabling reconfiguration of both communication and sensing channels with additional spatial degrees of freedom. In this paper, we propose a unified mutual information (MI) framework for MA-enabled MIMO ISAC systems, where MI characterizes communication performance as reliably conveyable information and sensing performance as extractable target information in cluttered environments. We formulate an optimization problem to maximize the weighted sum of communication and sensing MI by jointly optimizing the transmit beamforming matrix under a transmit power constraint and the MA positions under practical constraints, with a weighting coefficient characterizing their trade-off. To tackle the non-convexity arising from the log-det objective, position constraints, and the nonlinear coupling between optimization variables, we develop an alternating optimization-based algorithm that iteratively updates the transmit beamforming matrix and the MA positions. Specifically, with the fixed MA positions, we optimize the beamforming by approximating the objective function using weighted mean square error and majorization-minimization methods, yielding a closed-form solution. Moreover, with fixed beamforming, the MA positions are sequentially refined by decomposing the position optimization into simpler subproblems, resulting in an efficient suboptimal solution. Numerical results show that the unified MI framework with MAs significantly outperforms conventional FPA systems in both communication and sensing. Channel amplitude heatmap visualizations further illustrate how MA positioning strategies exploit spatial flexibility in array geometry to enhance overall system performance.

title: Safe-DRL-Based Resource Allocation for Traffic Matching Between Transmission and Computation in QoS-Guaranteed MEC-IoT Networks
authors: Guang Chen, Yueyun Chen, Zhen Wang, Liping Du
abstract: In internet of things (IoT) networks, resource-constrained devices can offload data to mobile edge computing (MEC) servers for processing. Nevertheless, offloading incurs additional energy consumption, making energy-efficient offloading a critical issue. Furthermore, the mismatch of transmission and computation traffic rates further exacerbates energy inefficiency. Additionally, ensuring quality of service (QoS) requires not only meeting the per-slot processed data volume thresholds of devices but also satisfying their long-term energy consumption limits imposed by limited battery capacity. The coexistence of these heterogeneous constraints renders algorithm design more challenging. In this paper, we propose a safe deep reinforcement learning (Safe-DRL)-based resource allocation strategy for traffic matching of transmission and computation in QoS-guaranteed MEC-IoT networks, aiming to maximize energy efficiency. Specifically, we formulate an energy efficiency maximization problem that captures the interdependence among transmission, buffering, and computation traffic, subject to both data processing and energy consumption constraints. To reduce complexity, closed-form solutions for a subset of variables are derived via mathematical analysis. Subsequently, we develop a Safe-DRL algorithm, termed action projection augmented Lagrangian soft actor-critic (APAL-SAC), which integrates a penalty-based action projection mechanism to enforce per-slot constraints and a Lagrangian dual method to ensure long-term constraint satisfaction. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of APAL-SAC.

title: Enhancing Resilience of Complex Networks Against Node Attack: A Constraint Reinforcement Learning Approach for Edge Addition
authors: Yangyang Li, Yunjun Lu, Kebin Chen, Kaishen Zeng, Jian Cheng, Wenlu Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Ensuring the resilience of complex networks against structural failures and targeted attacks is a critical challenge in modern network science. Adding edges between nodes enhances redundancy and alternative pathways in the network, thereby improving its ability to withstand and recover from structural disruptions. Traditional edge addition strategies often fail to adaptively optimize multiple resilience metrics simultaneously, leading to suboptimal network performance. In this paper, we propose a Constraint Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (CMORL) method for enhancing network resilience through strategic edge addition. Our approach incorporates multiple objectives—maximizing the size of the largest connected component, improving algebraic connectivity, and preserving node degree distributions—into a unified reinforcement learning paradigm with constraint-based action selection and constraintbased reward function. The designed composite reward functions guide the algorithm to learn balanced and effective restoration policies under various attack scenarios. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world network topologies demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing baseline strategies.

title: Profit-Driven Multi-Item Data Dissemination Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning in Internet of Vehicles
authors: Guangxiong Jin, Guangchao Chen, Shujuan Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Data dissemination plays a vital role in providing value-added services which enhance traffic safety and efficiency in the Internet of Vehicles (IoVs). However, major obstacles exist due to the limited communication resources and harsh transmission environment in IoVs. Moreover, existing work often assume the data/service providers offer their data/service for free, which weaken the practicability of these work. This paper focuses on the profit-driven multi-item data dissemination in IoVs. An incentive mechanism is established between RoadSide Units (RSUs) and vehicles, where vehicles are allowed to compete for receiving better service from RSUs, through a bidding strategy which dynamically adjusts a vehicle's bidding price for a certain data item, based on multiple characteristics that affect the transmission performance. RSUs obtain the payments from vehicles as a compensation for the resource consumed in responding them. The efficient multi-item data dissemination problem is modeled as the optimization of RSU's long-term revenue. A Double Deep Q Network (DDQN) based scheduling algorithm is proposed which implements intelligent data scheduling decisions. Simulation results show that the proposed strategy achieves significant performance improvement in terms of RSU's revenue, successful response ratio and completion delay under varied environment, which justifies the effectiveness and efficiency of this work.

title: Research on Credit Card Risk Prediction Using an LSTM-TCN Hybrid Model
authors: Shiyao Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To tackle the extreme class imbalance and dynamic temporal dependencies inherent in credit card transaction data, this study introduces a hybrid architecture integrating Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The proposed framework employs TCN to extract localized temporal features and capture long-range dependencies within transaction sequences. It further incorporates the gating mechanisms of LSTM to strengthen the modeling of temporal dynamics, while an attention layer is embedded to adaptively weight critical risk-related features. Evaluations conducted on benchmark credit card fraud datasets demonstrate that the model attains near-optimal performance across evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, substantially outperforming conventional logistic regression baselines. These results confirm the framework's robust fraud detection capability and strong generalizability in complex financial time-series scenarios.

title: Joint CFO and Channel Estimation for OTFS System in LEO Satellite Communications
authors: Tingguang Zhang, Kegang Pan, Aijun Liu, Xiaohu Liang, Xin Lin, Chen Han
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication systems, previous research usually overlooks the impact of carrier frequency offset (CFO) during the channel estimation process of the orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation. In this letter, we investigate the joint CFO and channel estimation for OTFS modulation in LEO satellite communication scenarios. Firstly, the joint estimation model for the OTFS system is formulated. Based on this model, we can obtain the joint CFO and channel estimation expression from the joint probability density function of the received samples. Then, we design the objective function for the joint estimation of OTFS. By maximizing the objective function, a joint CFO and channel estimator is proposed and its computational complexity is analyzed. To improve the channel estimation accuracy, we further design the joint estimator architecture enhanced by deep learning. Under the LEO channel, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed joint estimator outperforms the conventional approaches in both CFO estimation performance and channel estimation performance for the OTFS system.

title: A Differentiable Bi-Objective Framework for Risk-Aware Distribution-System Repair Scheduling Using IEEE-33 Feeder and NOAA Storm Data
authors: Ling-Feng Chiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: We propose SoftSchedule, a differentiable dualobjective distribution-system repair scheduling framework that integrates real disaster data with standard feeder test systems. Using the IEEE-33 bus feeder as the base network and NOAA Storm Events 2022 as the hazard source, county-level records are mapped to buses and converted into temporal hazard images via edge-midpoint rasterization, providing localized environmental features for candidate faulted components. Service supply is approximated by reachability-weighted connectivity; subtree load sums are used as estimates, with exact recomputed for the top-ranked edges to improve label quality. Training jointly minimizes the area ∫ ( 1− Q(t))  and T95 (time to reach 95% service). The former is optimized via a Gumbel–Sinkhorn– based differentiable sorting that produces a continuous surrogate of execution order, combined with time-discount weights to approximate area; the latter uses a smooth hinge proxy to encourage early attainment, plus a Q(t) smoothness regularizer to suppress curve jitter. A pairwise rank-distillation term based on v/t strengthens learning of relative order. At inference, instead of computationally heavy MPC, we introduce a risk-aware beam search targeting both area and T95, which fuses standardized signals from dQ, job durations, and neural scores to produce robust, real-time repair sequences. Experiments show that SoftSchedule achieves low area while significantly improving the early part of the service curve and T95, outperforming a greedy v/baseline in time efficiency and risk suppression. Overall, SoftSchedule organically combines real external hazard data, standard test systems, differentiable sorting, and lightweight inference to deliver a reproducible pipeline—from data-driven scenario generation and end-to-end learning to online decision-making—providing a concrete baseline and extensible framework for distribution-grid resilience restoration research and practice.

title: Entropy based Intuitionistic Fuzzy Weighted Least Square Twin Support Vector Machine
authors: Nauman Ansary, Niher Ranjan Das, Q. M. Danish Lohani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Imbalanced datasets are difficult to handle in classification problems, where some classes have significantly fewer samples than others. Support Vector Machines (SVMs) are known for their strong performance in many areas, but they struggle with imbalanced and noisy data as they treat all training samples equally, which causes the model to favour the majority class when forming the decision boundary. Although fuzzy SVM variants like entropy based fuzzy SVM (EFSVM) and intuitionistic fuzzy twin SVM (IFTSVM) reduce the impact of noise and outliers by assigning fuzzy membership values, they rely heavily on distance-based assumptions and assume uniform uncertainty across the data. These methods compute membership and non-membership values based on the centres of the data. In this paper, we propose a new entropy based IFW-LSTSVM (EIFW-LSTSVM) that combines intuitionistic fuzzy theory with local entropy to handle class imbalance better. It assigns full membership to minority class samples and reduces it for the uncertain majority class. This dual approach enhances the model performance by focusing more on the minority class while reducing the influence of noisy majority samples, leading to a more balanced and reliable decision boundary. Moreover, to validate the performance of the proposed method, experiments were conducted on 14 imbalanced datasets from the KEEL repos-itory, comparing it with several recent and widely recognized SVM variants. For statistical validation of these findings, the Friedman test followed by the Dunn-Bonferroni post-hoc test was applied at a 95% confidence level. The results indicate that the proposed method exhibits significantly superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Research on Efficient Path Planning Algorithm for UAV in Power Equipment Inspection
authors: Bei Li, Zhicong Dong, Yulin Zhu, Lingbo Liu, Yujie Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper proposes a path planning method that integrates reinforcement learning and improved ant colony algorithm. By constructing a mathematical model of inspection scenes that includes factors such as terrain, weather, and equipment distribution, accurate path planning in complex environments is achieved. In the reinforcement learning framework, the state space is defined by the position of the UAV, the equipment number, etc., the flight direction adjustment, etc. is used as the action space, and the reward function is constructed in combination with the path length, inspection efficiency, etc.; at the same time, the pheromone update and search strategy of the ant colony algorithm are improved to achieve complementary advantages. On the MATLAB simulation platform, multiple groups of scenes with terrains such as mountains and plains, high, medium, and low density equipment distribution, and meteorological conditions such as sunny days and strong winds are set up, and compared with Dijkstra algorithm, traditional ant colony algorithm, etc. The results show that in mountainous terrain, the path length of the proposed algorithm is 25.3% shorter than that of the Dijkstra algorithm, and the inspection time is reduced by 27.8%; in the scenario of high-density distribution of equipment, the path planning success rate reaches 96.1%, which is 21.3% higher than that of the traditional ant colony algorithm; the average convergence speed of the algorithm is 35.6% faster than that of the traditional algorithm, which effectively verifies the efficiency and reliability of the algorithm in power inspection path planning.

title: Federated Multimodal Deep Learning Framework with Adaptive Feature Selection for Early Diagnosis and Personalized Treatment of Cardiac Diseases
authors: G Mahender, Kabita Thaoroijam
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Early identification is key for efficient treatment and management of heart disease, which remains to be a running cause of mortality globally. Unfortunately, the clinical use of present diagnostic methods is limited by their often-low accuracy, which is a result of the misintegration of diverse health data. Federated learning (FL) provides a structure for building local models across organizations while protecting sensitive information. This study presents a new method for predicting the occurrence of cardiovascular illness by utilizing the advanced deep learning (DL) model, which integrates the advantages of both deep-neural-networks and tree-based models. We use above architecture to improve data processing in federated systems and use the UCI-Heart-Disease datasets in our study. The federated averaging approach was used to securely aggregate model modernizes among contestants in horizontal federated learning. With the use of smart contracts, blockchain technology was able to automate governance while simultaneously increasing transparency and accountability. After 50 epochs, The proposed approach successfully balanced privacy and performance, according to the experimental data, with a score of $\mathbf{1. 5 9 4}$ for balanced metrics, an accuracy(Acc) of 0.932, and an epsilon value of 6.755. A strong demonstration of accuracy was achieved by the model after only 10 iterations on aggregated data, demonstrating the advantages of integrating data from several sources. By integrating deep learning approach with blockchain, this study offers a scalable, privacy-preserving method for anticipating cardiac problems, which helps to solve important problems in healthcare while also guaranteeing the integrity of data.

title: Annual Power Flow Scenario Generation for Hybrid AC/DC Receiving-End Provincial Grids Based on Production Simulation
authors: Yifei Pan, Shuolin Zhang, Yue Yu, Changgang Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: In response to the challenges faced in annual power flow scenario generation for AC/DC hybrid receiving-end grids with high-penetration renewable energy integration— such as the difficulty in quantifying dynamic interactions of cross-regional power transmission, low efficiency in long-term data processing, and insufficient convergence stability of power flow—this paper proposes a simulation method based on hierarchical modeling and dynamic per-unit curve mapping. First, a hierarchical model based on balanced partitions and tie-line snapshots is constructed to achieve coordinated representation of external AC power, DC interconnections, and provincial power sources. Second, a dynamic per-unit curve scaling method for load/renewable energy and a unit output adjustment algorithm are designed to establish an 8,760-hour continuous power flow snapshot generation framework. Finally, a dynamic compensation strategy for external power is proposed to optimize power flow convergence. Using Simulation Toolkit for Electrical Power Systems (STEPS) and Power Energy Balance Software (PEBL), the proposed method is validated on an ultra-high-voltage (UHV) AC/DC hybrid receiving-end provincial grid. The results demonstrate that the proposed method improves the efficiency of power flow scenario generation in AC/DC hybrid receiving-end grids, and the generated power flow snapshot data supports the verification of provincial power grid planning schemes.

title: Research on an Ecological Benefit Calculation Model for Mine Restoration Based on Deep Learning and DEA
authors: Cen Wang, Zhiyu Liu, Xue Li, Linlin Yang, Shichang Wang, Jiwen Ge, Xinwen Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Traditional evaluations of mine restoration benefits often rely on qualitative methods, which makes it difficult to achieve quantitative and dynamic comparisons across projects. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an ecological benefit calculation model for mine restoration that integrates deep learning with Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA). First, deep learning techniques are employed to automatically extract and predict ecological indicators from multi-source data such as remote sensing imagery, soil monitoring records, and biodiversity surveys. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are applied to obtain refined vegetation coverage indices, while recurrent neural networks (RNNs) are used to capture temporal variations in soil physicochemical properties and ecosystem service values. These outputs, together with traditional inputs (funding, labor, land reclamation area, and technical equipment), form a high-dimensional input-output dataset. Next, CCR and BCC DEA models are introduced to compute the relative efficiency of each restoration unit, identify input redundancies and output shortfalls, and provide targeted improvement directions. An empirical study of the Hashotan open-pit coal mine restoration project in Inner Mongolia demonstrates that the deep learning-enhanced DEA model achieves more accurate and discriminative efficiency evaluations compared to traditional DEA, while effectively handling data gaps and uncertainties. The results highlight that deep learning significantly improves the quality of ecological indicators, thereby strengthening DEA's ability to differentiate restoration performance. Finally, sensitivity and robustness analyses confirm the model's stability. This study offers a novel methodological framework that combines artificial intelligence with efficiency analysis, providing scientific evidence and practical references for refined mine restoration management and ecological decision-making.

title: Balancing Interpretability and Performance in Credit Card Fraud Detection Using Machine Learning Techniques with SMOTE and Logistic Regression
authors: Modina Jahan Priya, Hasan Ahammad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Credit card fraud detection is a critical issue in modern financial systems, with challenges arising from the imbalanced nature of transaction datasets, where fraudulent activities account for only a small fraction. This paper proposes an enhanced fraud detection system by combining Logistic Regression with Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) to address class imbalance, improve model performance, and detect fraudulent transactions more effectively. SMOTE, which generates synthetic samples from the minority class, helps to improve the recall and precision of the Logistic Regression model. The results are evaluated using standard metrics - accuracy, precision, recall and AUC. The results show a significant improvement in detecting fraudulent transactions. Similar works like those of IEEE have also shown the benefits of SMOTE in fraud detection, thus providing further justification for the use of this technique. The results show that the combination of logistic regression and SMOTE makes it possible to successfully detect fraud in imbalanced datasets, thus providing a solution to the requirements needed in the monetary organizations in the real world.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Correlation and GAN Based Preprocessing Scheme for Missing Data in Short-Term Power Load Forecasting Systems
authors: Leiming Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a two-stage data restoration scheme that integrates spatiotemporal correlation analysis (STCA) and conditional generative adversarial network (CGAN) to address the problem of data loss caused by sensor failures and communication interruptions in short-term power load forecasting. Firstly, explore the collaborative fluctuation patterns of adjacent regional load data through spatiotemporal correlation matrices; secondly, design a CGAN model to generate fill values that comply with physical constraints, ensuring that the statistical properties of the repaired data are consistent with the true distribution. Based on actual load data from 10 regions of a certain power grid, verification shows that in the scenario of random missing rate of 40% and continuous missing for 72 hours, the prediction error (MAE=10.9MW) of the repaired data in this scheme is significantly lower than that of traditional methods (KNN: MAE=16.7MW; MF: MAE=16.5MW), and the prediction accuracy is improved by 22.7% after repairing. This physical constraint module can ensure that the repaired data conforms to the operating rules of the power grid and avoid scheduling risks caused by "pseudo repair".

title: A Novel Blockchain-Based Secure Voting System with End-to-End Verifiability and Privacy
authors: Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Current electronic voting infrastructure continues to be plagued by security, transparency and voter privacy concerns, in both large and remote elections. In order to cope with these issues, this paper introduces a blockchain-based voting system which fulfills end-to-end verifiability and maintains ballot secrecy. The system proposed uses a permissioned blockchain system along with Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) consensus protocol to guarantee data integrity and resilience to faults when facing partially adversarial conditions. The combination of homomorphic encryption of tallying encrypted votes and zero-knowledge proofs of voter eligibility and validation of ballots, without disclosing the content of the vote, results in vote confidentiality and auditability. Smart contracts facilitate the process of vote validation and aggregation making it publically auditable without trusting third parties. Simulated workload performance evaluation suggests that, under regular operating conditions, the system has a verification accuracy greater than 98 % and has an average processing time and computational overhead that are lower than those of the corresponding blockchain-based voting systems under realistic operating conditions. The given framework is planned to assist with the real-time auditing and ensure privacy assurances. Besides this, the paper also addresses the practicability of post-quantum cryptographic primitives and cross-chain mechanisms as further improvements to ensure enhancement of long-term security and scalability.

title: Fast Switching Quasi-Inversion-Channel β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFET
authors: Huidong Yao, Xuanze Zhou, Qi Liu, Jingbo Zhou, Man Hoi Wong, Guangwei Xu, Shu Yang, Shibing Long
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive investigation into the dynamic switching characteristics of a vertical β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFET. A 400-mA vertical quasi-inversion-channel β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFET fabricated using a self-aligned process was demonstrated with a specific on-resistance of 13.2 mΩ·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and a threshold voltage of 2.9 V. Through double-pulse testing at bus voltages up to 200 V, the device presents excellent switching speed, achieving an outstanding turn-on time of 10.6 ns and a turn-off time of 73.4 ns. In addition, inter-terminal capacitances are characterized and used to calculate the switching charges, including total gate charge of 1.37 nC and Miller charge of 0.26 nC, which can account for the high switching speed. Device figure of merits (FOMs) and switching frequency were then derived. Our β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFETs exhibit superior switching FOMs when compared with commercial Si power transistors of similar current rating. These results offer the critical insights into the dynamic performance of β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFETs, confirming their significant potential for next generation power electronics.

title: Optimization and Performance Improvement of Multisensor Piezoelectric Balance Assembly Method Based on a Complex Super-Statical Determinate Structure_supp1-3667884.pdf
authors: Yuzhe Tian
abstract: Multisensor piezoelectric balances are increasingly used in wind tunnel tests requiring high dynamic performance, due to their flexible structure, easy fabrication, high stiffness, and excellent dynamic performance. However, superimposed manufacturing and assembly errors degrade their force-measuring performance. To address this issue, this article proposes a novel assembly method for such balances based on a complex statically indeterminate structure. The method controls the preload of connecting bolts using a digital torque wrench and optimizes sensor height via three approaches, leveraging coordinate measuring machine data and copper sheets’ high plasticity. Mechanical and mathematical models were established, followed by simulations to analyze error impacts, and a six-component force calibration system was built for static/dynamic tests. Results show that placing copper sheets on sensors’ top/bottom (with uniform bolt preload) significantly reduces force imbalance; top-placed sheets improve static performance. Natural frequency tests confirm no negative impact on dynamic performance, verifying the method’s effectiveness and feasibility.
