publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: The circulating currents present in Modular Multilevel Converter (MMC) adversely affect the waveform quality of the arm currents. A novel Vector Resonance (VR) control approach is developed in this paper to suppress the second-harmonic circulating current in MMC. Firstly, the topology of the MMC is introduced. Secondly, the mathematical expression for circulating currents is derived and a detailed analysis of its components is conducted. Subsequently, the circulating current suppression strategy utilizing the VR controller is elaborated in detail, accompanied by the presentation of the overall MMC control diagram. Finally, the MMC simulation model based on VR controller is established by using MATLAB/Simulink. Before adding the VR controller, the bridge-arm current contains significant even harmonics, with a Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) as high as 19.81%. After adding the circulating current suppressor, harmonic components are significantly reduced, and the THD is lowered to 1.48%.

title: IPO-ResNet: Improved Parrot Optimization Algorithm-Based Optimization of Residual Network for Myocardial Infarction Classification
authors: Chang Qu, Xin Zhang, Yansong Lu, Yi Wang, Chengzhi Su
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Myocardial infarction (MI) causes permanent cardiac damage, making rapid and accurate diagnosis critical. Electrocardiography (ECG) serves as a key diagnostic tool for MI, and integrating computer-aided diagnosis technology enhances diagnostic efficiency and accuracy. This paper proposes an automated MI classification method based on an improved parrot algorithm (IPO) to optimize residual networks (ResNet). The core approach involves enhancing the parrot optimization algorithm through two strategies: adaptive switching factors and hybrid Cauchy-Gaussian mutation. The improved parrot algorithm is used to find optimal hyperparameter configurations for the ResNet network, thereby constructing a high-performance MI classification model. Experiments on the PTB-XL dataset demonstrate that this method achieves an accuracy of 88.18% and a ROC-AUC of 0.9647, outperforming existing methods and exhibiting high accuracy and practicality in MI classification.

title: Efficient Wireless Power Transfer Considering Superdirectivity in Transmit and Receive Arrays
authors: Brian McMinn, Thomas L. Marzetta
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We investigate wireless power transfer (WPT) systems using jointly optimized superdirective transmit and receive arrays as a possible solution to the numerically large currents of superdirective arrays. Modeling the system as a ported network and applying circuit theory and scalar Green’s function analysis, we propose and demonstrate that the optimal array gain scales approximately as M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, where M and N are the number of elements in the transmit and receive arrays, respectively. Notably, we find that current magnitudes within each array depend only on the local array size and are largely independent of the configuration of the other array. We further show that systems achieving the same array gain with paired superdirective arrays (e.g., M → M) require significantly lower currents than traditional configurations (e.g., M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> → 1).

title: President's Message
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)

title: Optimizing Waste Classification with Ensemble Learning: Fine-Tuned CNNs for Organic and Recyclable Waste Sorting
authors: Ratnesh Kumar Choudhary, Ansh Devendra Dulewale, Dishant Sevake, Riya Ramteke, Shweta Rewatka, Bharti Mathanka, Prachi Borade
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Waste management research deals with the urgent need to implement sustainable waste management strategies and considers the effectiveness of ensemble learning and refined CNNs in the classification of waste. This research experiment would be associated with the waste-classificationdata dataset (more than 25,000 images) with the help of which the models are trained and tested according to paramount performance indicators (accuracy, precision, and recall). The optimized transfer learning pre-trained architectures (VGG16, ResNet50, and InceptionV3) achieved test accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.50,96.00$</tex> and 97.00 percent respectively. To improve classification, a majority voting ensemble design was applied that combines predictions of these optimized models and achieves a final test accuracy of 98.00 which is better than the individual model. Transfer learning with ensemble methods has shown to be effective in attaining accurate classification of waste and provides a sorting solution that is both automated and scalable. The findings of this study conclude that carefully trained CNNs are highly resilient to real-life waste management issues and make an important contribution to developing sustainable recycling systems.

title: Smart System to Monitor Noise Using Machine Learning
authors: M. Bindu Madhavi, R. Swathika
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Urban sound environments present complex challenges for modern city management systems, requiring sophisticated approaches to understand and classify diverse acoustic patterns. This research proposes a comprehensive machine learning framework that combines supervised and unsupervised learning techniques to automatically recognize, classify, and analyze urban sound patterns. The system integrates deep neural networks, ensemble methods, and feature learning algorithms to process real-time acoustic data from multiple urban locations. Our approach utilizes convolutional neural networks for spectral analysis, recurrent networks for temporal pattern detection, and clustering algorithms for automated sound categorization. The methodology incorporates adaptive learning strategies that continuously improve classification accuracy through online learning mechanisms. Experimental validation across multiple urban districts demonstrates significant improvements in sound classification accuracy, achieving 94.7% precision in identifying different acoustic events and 89.3% accuracy in temporal pattern prediction. The system provides real-time processing capabilities with response times under 300 milliseconds, making it suitable for practical deployment in smart city infrastructure.

title: Development of an Off-line Visual Detection Device for Nozzle Atomization Effect
authors: Jiamin Lei, Jianfeng Chen, Jiacheng Ou, Airong Ling, Yinru He, Liming Lin, Weitao Zhou, Zhenye Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In this paper, a nozzle atomization effect detection device is developed to solve the problems of low detection efficiency and long adjustment time in tobacco shred process. It has the following features: offline detection, visual analysis and intelligent control. Through the integration of modular design, image processing technology and intelligent PID control system, accurate quantification and rapid adjustment of atomization parameters are achieved. Practical application shows that the device shortens the detection and adjustment time from the original 131.18 minutes to 21.09 minutes, improving efficiency by 522%, providing an innovative solution for process quality control in the tobacco industry.

title: Construction of General Representation Model and Cross-domain Migration Method Based on Multi-modal Comparative Learning
authors: Qianhui Yu, Guihua Lin, Zhe Zhang, Xudong Zhang, Zhiming Zhang, Jintao Cui, Xiaoxi Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In order to solve the core problems of modal heterogeneity, semantic gap and model performance degradation in multi-modal data applications, this paper proposes a framework of Dynamic Domain-aware Multimodal Contrastive Learning (DD MCL), aiming at constructing a general multi-modal representation model and realizing efficient cross-domain migration. The framework is optimized through three stages: pre-training, self-adaptation and joint optimization. In the pre-training stage, Modality Attention Weight Network (MAWN) is introduced to dynamically fuse modal features, and the modes are initially aligned with standard comparison loss. In the adaptive phase, Domain-Aware Contrastive Loss (DACL) is activated for unlabeled target domain data, balancing the contrastive learning intensity between the source and target domains through dynamic weight coefficients to alleviate distribution differences between domains; In the joint optimization stage, a Memory Replay Mechanism (MRM) is introduced to construct a source domain sample "memory bank" to avoid catastrophic forgetting of the model when adapting to the target domain. At the same time, the weighted sum of source domain contrastive loss, target domain contrastive loss, and memory replay loss is used as the overall loss function to optimize the model. In the experiment, MS-COCO is used as the source domain data set, cartoon illustration data set CIBR and medical image report data set MIMIC-CXR are used as the target domain data set, ViT-B/16 and RoBERTa-base are used as encoders, and Recall@1, Recall@5 and Recall@10 of cross-modal retrieval tasks are used as evaluation indexes. The results show that the Recall@1 index of image-to-text (I2T) and text-to-image (T2I) retrieval tasks of DDMCL on CIBR and MIMIC-CXR reaches 45.7 and 43.9, respectively, which is significantly better than the baseline models such as CLIP and ALBEF. Ablation experiments verify that the core components of MAWN, DACL and MRM contribute significantly to the performance, and the model performance is the best when the domain sensing weight β is in the range of 0.6-0.8. The research shows that DDMCL can effectively realize cross-modal semantic fusion and efficient cross-domain migration without target domain labeling, and improve the generalization ability and application efficiency of the model in complex and data-scarce scenarios.

title: Multi-Task Pulmonary Nodule Detection and Segmentation in CT Scans Using Hybrid 2D-3D Deep Learning with Size-Aware Attention
authors: Harshan, K Kiran, Upasana Singh, Hemant Kumar Yadav, Bhavik Kuchipudi, Jolly Sneh Kapoor
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The early identification of pulmonary nodules within CT scans serves as a crucial element for enhancing lung cancer patient outcomes. This research introduces an innovative dual deep learning system which combines two-dimensional (2D) and three-dimensional (3D) convolutional neural networks to detect and segment nodules simultaneously. The proposed architecture divides into parallel 2D and 3D feature extraction streams which merge through a size-aware attention mechanism that adjusts weights according to nodule features. The approach uses multi-task learning to optimize classification and segmentation jointly which results in better accuracy alongside improved computational efficiency. Our approach demonstrates superior performance on the LIDC-IDRI dataset by achieving an AUC of 0.92 for malignancy classification and a Dice coefficient of 0.89 for segmentation than all other state-of-the-art 2D, 3D and hybrid models. The obtained results show how adaptive hybrid architectures can be used to create robust systems for clinical lung nodule analysis.

title: Voicyx: An Intelligent Voice Assistant Driven by the Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B-Instruct AI Model
authors: Aswathy PB, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Vineeta Singh, Basanta Mahato, Bhavik Kuchipudi, Rohit Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) has driven the creation of intelligent voice assistants capable of deep contextual understanding and adaptive interaction. Voicyx, an advanced AI-powered voice assistant built on Meta's Llama-3.1 70B-Instruct model and optimized using NVIDIA NIM, marks a significant leap in real-time, context-aware voice communication. This paper provides an in-depth analysis of Voicyx's architecture, core features, applications, challenges, and future prospects. By integrating the Web Speech API for precise speech recognition, Django for robust backend operations, and AI-based text generation for natural, human-like conversations, Voicyx delivers an intuitive and seamless user experience. Its adaptability allows implementation across diverse fields such as personal assistance, education, healthcare, customer support, and smart home automation, establishing Voicyx as a key milestone in the advancement of intelligent, voice-driven human computer interaction systems.

title: Pinpointing Radio Devices: Robust Fingerprint Localization in Industrial Environments
authors: Anton Schösser, Friedrich Burmeister, Joschua Bogner, Zhongju Li, Philipp Schulz, Gerhard Fettweis, Norman Franchi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The massive availability of wireless links will shape the factory of the future. Many envisioned wireless use cases rely on ultra-reliable low latency communication (URLLC). While providing single devices with URLLC is trivial these days, scaling URLLC networks up to many users remains a challenge. Channel-aware radio resource allocation is a method to face this challenge efficiently. However, fine-granular channel awareness is required for channel-aware radio resource allocation. A recently proposed approach to address this requirement is to reuse the channel state information (CSI) stored in a radio environment map (REM). To successfully apply the information from the REM, very accurate localization of the radio device(s) is required, which is the focus of this paper. Based on measurement data and spatially extremely fine-grained fingerprint sequences, it is demonstrated that localizing an automated guided vehicle (AGV) as a radio device is possible with sub-centimeter accuracy on its trajectory. The performance is achieved with the k-nearest neighbor (kNN) approach, and its robustness, even in a time-varying environment, is highlighted. As an enabler for channel-aware resource allocation, the presented work represents a step towards scaling up URLLC networks in industrial use cases.

title: An Intelligent Captioning System for Deaf Users Using Speech Recognition and NLP
authors: Patan Amrulla Khan, Gayatri Ketepalli, Srikanth Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Effective communication requires real-time access to spoken content, especially for those who are deaf or hard of hearing (DHH). For dynamic, real-time settings like classrooms, meetings, or conferences, conventional solutions like post-event transcription or human interpreters are inadequate. This study suggests an AI-Based Real-Time Captioning System that uses cutting-edge Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models, such as Mozilla's Deep Speech and OpenAI's Whisper. The system's features include multilingual support, high accuracy, and low latency while transcribing spoken speech into written captions. It is deployable on a variety of platforms, including as computers, tablets, and smartphones, and incorporates a user-centered interface. With the goal of offering a communication solution that is inclusive, scalable, and economical, the article describes the system design, methodology, evaluation findings, and major problems.

title: Integrated Multi Stage Transformer (IMST) model for Crop Yield Prediction
authors: M. Vamshi Reddy, M. Ananya Reddy, M. Akhil Kumar, M. Rama Chandra Rao, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Predicting crop yield is a crucial factor in precision agriculture, especially under the conditions of increasing environmental uncertainty and climatic variability. This interest in predicting crop yield increased the necessity to develop advanced machine learning and dl models to refine the capabilities of accurate and efficient crop yield prediction. Existing models fail to handle real-time data and multiple types of data like weather conditions and IoT sensor readings. So it is crucial for us to implement a better crop yield prediction framework that can work well in all scenarios. In this paper, an Integrated Multi Stage Transformer (IMST) framework is discussed that uses an advanced Deep Learning (DL) approach. This IMST model uses a self-attention mechanism that captures complex spatial and temporal dependencies. The dataset contains different data like weather conditions, IoT sensor readings. The model is implemented and their performance is assessed using several evaluation metrics like accuracy, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and F1 score. The transformer model results with an accuracy of 94.3%. It addresses the difficulties commonly found in real-world applications and exhibits good performance with real-time data.

title: An Intelligent IDS/IPS Framework to Fortify Industry 4.0 Applications
authors: Samir Qurbani, Athirah Mohd Ramly, Pardha Saradhi Chakolthi, Mhd Saeed Sharif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The deployment of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) devices has been crucial to enable new business processes that convert traditional manufacturing to Smart Factory ecosystems, with higher levels of automation and operational efficiency. This digital transformation, however, also opens additional cybersecurity vulnerabilities to the system, including emerging threat types, such as advanced persistent threats (APTs), zero-day attacks, and insider threats. In this paper, a deep learning-based IDS/IPS based on BiLSTM with an attention mechanism is proposed. The BiLSTM model is trained on the KDDTrain+ dataset and validated on the KDDTest+ dataset, with class balancing addressed using the SMOTE technique. Hyperparameters are optimized using Bayesian Optimization with Keras Tuner. The model achieved 89.2% validation accuracy and a weighted F1-score of 0.91. Performance metrics, including confusion matrices and ROC-AUC scores, suggest effective detection across both common and rare attack classes, with a low false positive rate. Additionally, the system was integrated into a real-time environment using a Flask-based API, showcasing the practical feasibility of the proposed solution for Smart Factory cybersecurity.

title: Deep Learning-Based Fraud Detection in Digital Trade and Logistics Systems
authors: Trapty Agarwal, Madhur Grover, Madhusmita Sahu, Varun Ojha, S M Jahirul Islam, M. Saravanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The flood in digital trade and cross-supply chain logistics systems has become a challenge now where the supplying entities require the ability to detect frauds. It is hard to miss security and compliance issues that would be much less likely to occur in rule-based systems, since those cannot adapt to evolving fraud patterns. The proposed application of this study is the use of deep learning in detecting fraudulent activities based on transactional and behavioural logistic network data of high dimensions. The model applies the attention-based LSTM architecture and graph the attention-based LSTM architecture. They capture sequential patterns and relations between objects with the purpose of enhancing the accuracy of detection. With real-time processing, it is feasible to perform dynamic threat assessment and the method of feature embedding shows a superior detection of anomalies of structured and unstructured data. The system has good generalization to the various logistics forms such as the e-commerce, B2B, as well as maritime dealings. It has been confirmed in experimental outcome that better performance is achieved against fraud prediction metric compared to the traditional models in terms of the amount of load and noise present. This will greatly minimize the occurrence of the false positive and enhance early detection of frauds, enhancing confidence in digital logistics. The proposed model of a deep learning solution is scalable, has real-time adaptability and is more than viable as well as future-proof in terms of digital fraud defence logistics operations. The results of the proposed system were better in comparison with those of other methods with the highest ACR of 93.1% and LLR of 88.7%.

title: Integrating CBC Data with Artificial Intelligence for Early Detection of Dengue Fever
authors: Ashish Reddy Kotla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Dengue fever is a major health issue that affects the world community with early detection being the way forward. The current paper presents the newest framework that integrates complete blood count data and a hybrid artificial intelligence model to identify dengue early in its progression. The system combines a set of algorithms based on the principle of the Random Forest, XG Boost and neural networks, whose work has been optimized with specific focus on features selection. Another unique Query Fragment Caching Algorithm enhances further computing performance. The accuracy of clinical validation (94.99) was high, and the sensitivity (92.35) and specificity (95.78) were higher than the traditional diagnostic methods and machine-learning methods that exist. The results obtained prove that CBC-based AI screening is a cost-efficient, scalable method of early dengue disease detection, which is especially beneficial in cost-limited settings with high disease rates.

title: Transitive Indexing for Main-Memory: Adaptive Performance under Massive Interleaved Queries and Updates
authors: Imran Ali Mohammed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper introduces Transitive Indexing, a novel paradigm designed for efficient main-memory indexing in modern applications that demand seamless handling of massive interleaved reads and writes. Unlike traditional static indexing methods, which require high initialization costs and prior workload knowledge, or adaptive indexing methods, which struggle to maintain performance under continuous query and update workloads, transitive indexing evolves dynamically to optimize performance across varying workload stages. We present TRIMMER, a concrete implementation of transitive indexing, which begins as a collection of unordered vectors and incrementally adapts its structure in response to workload patterns. Over time, TRIMMER morphs into a trie for high-access data regions, balancing fast lookups and update absorption without any upfront initialization. Detailed experimental evaluations on synthetic and real-world datasets from the astronomy domain demonstrate that TRIMMER overcomes the limitations of traditional adaptive and static indexing, providing an efficient and resilient solution for interactive data exploration under massive update scenarios.

title: Hybrid Model ARIMA-Random Forest for Energy Forecasting of Smart Energy Monitoring System
authors: Norith Chealy, Channareth Srun, Sros Nhek, Virbora Ny, Chivon Choeung, Mengseu Pheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The current contribution is a hybrid model interfacing ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average) with a Random Forest for energy consumption forecasting with a proposed smart energy monitoring system. The ARIMA is used as a predictor to catch periodic time series data in periods, while the random forest would finalize these periodic patterns in energy consumption trends. The embedding of these models within a smart energy monitoring system creates a chance of improving the forecasting accuracy and enables the facilities consumption behaviors effectively, leading to support in energy optimization as a result. We validate our methodology by applying it to actual household data and find that the performance was significantly better than standalone ARIMA or Random Forest. When the dataset size increases, the hybrid model, as discussed, gives an accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) value of 0.98 and the lowest MSE from 0.34 to 0.03. ARIMA yields fairly steady <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}(0.56-0.61)$</tex> and an increase in MSE being relatively slight (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 4 6 - 0. 4 8}$</tex>). MSE drops from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 4 4}$</tex> to 0.16, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> improves from 0.68 to 0.90 for Random Forest as sample size increases. This indicates the feasibility of how much this work is an applicable and reliable alternative that enhances the efficiency of energy administration, especially in domestic units.

title: Efficient Fog-Cloud Computing Networks: Task Placement and Binary Offloading Optimization
authors: Shweta Bansal, Pankaj Rahi, D Nagesh, Merajul Islam, Aakanksha Upadhyay, S. Naveen Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The increase in low latency and high resource end applications in the global IoT, computing through the fog-cloud network model is becoming the most emerging one. The methodology for solving APTPO and binary process offloading proposed in this paper focuses on fog + cloud solution in cloudcloud computing networks. However, when task characteristics, resource available, and connection band width are integrated into the model, it is possible to minimize computing latency and overall energy utilized. To identify jobs that can be effectively offloaded and hence improve the effectiveness of computing, simple binary decision-making procedure is employed. From the results of all the experiments, it can be determined that the anticipated technique can be used in Internet of Things applications in real world environments which lead to an increased coverage area of the system, longer time consumed in all operations, cost and total overall network throughput as well. The specificity about the technique that has been described can be advantageously applied to a variety of IoT applications that will improve the credential of the network.

title: SmartTicket: Chatbot-Based Ticketing System for Museums
authors: Shanmugam Sundaramurthy, Meheraab Chothia, Arshaan Iqbal, Jothi Kumar C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Conversational AI is becoming a widely used tool in end-user applications. Through advances in technology, it is now capable of interpreting intent and extracting structured information from users, making it ideal for systems such as ticket booking, where accuracy and clarity of information are essential. This paper presents the development of an LLM-powered chatbot designed to handle user interactions concerning booking, updating, or deleting museum tickets, along with general museumrelated queries. Since the application's performance depends on the accuracy of the LLM model being used, a comparison of different models is conducted in this paper. We primarily compare three instruction-tuned variants of Meta's Llama model family: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct, Llama-3.2-90B-Vision-Instruct, and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct. Additionally, we compare results from two smaller models in the same family: Llama-3.1-8BInstruct and Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct. This comparison provides insight into how smaller models perform relative to larger models on the same task. Results indicate that the larger models (70 B and 90B) naturally outperform the smaller models, demonstrating higher accuracy in entity extraction. However, in classifying user intent, returning responses in valid JSON format, and identifying missing elements, the smaller models' performance closely matches that of the larger models. Among the larger models, Llama-3.3 narrowly outperforms Llama-3.1 and Llama3.2, despite Llama-3.2 having 90 billion parameters compared to Llama-3.3's 70 billion.

title: Monotonic Learning: Guaranteed Improvement in Neural Network Training
authors: Sa'eed Serwan Abdulsattar, Fayzeh Abdulkareem Jaber, Hani Al-Balasmeh, Rahmeh Abdulkareem Jaber, Ahmad AbdulKareem Jaber, Nour Abdulkareem Jaber
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents Monotonic Neural Training, a novel approach to neural network optimization that eliminates performance regressions while improving model quality. Unlike conventional methods that accept all parameter updates, our method enforces strict improvement criteria, ensuring each step advances validation performance. Traditional training often suffers from unpredictable dynamics, with sudden deteriorations requiring constant monitoring and manual intervention. Monotonic Neural Training addresses this via an “improve-or-reject” mechanism: updates are only committed if they demonstrate measurable improvement. The approach treats optimization as a quality-controlled process rather than blind descent. Candidate updates are validated, and failing updates trigger automatic retries with varied batch sampling and intelligent perturbations, forming a self-correcting training loop that guarantees monotonic progress. Experimental results on both regression and classification tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed monotonic training framework. On the diabetes regression benchmark, the method achieved a mean coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.569 compared to 0.450 for conventional training, reflecting a 26 % improvement. On the breast cancer classification task, it reached an accuracy of 0.991 versus 0.947, yielding a 4.6 % gain. While the approach requires greater computational effort due to its selective update process—accepting less than 1 % of candidate steps—it ensures strictly non-degrading performance and stable, deterministic convergence across all runs. Beyond performance gains, Monotonic Neural Training navigates complex loss landscapes autonomously, adapting to training difficulties without human supervision. Even steps requiring over thousands of retries ultimately succeed, demonstrating that patient, qualityfocused optimization outperforms rapid but unstable approaches. Ultimately, the fastest path to superior models is not accelerating updates but ensuring that every update genuinely improves performance.

title: Enhanced Optical Character Recognition Through Statistical and Neural Network-Based Handwriting Character Recognition Framework
authors: Shruthi S, Rashmi V, Megha A, Vijaya Kumar H R, Swetha G, Raghu N
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Handwritten character recognition (HCR) has numerous applications, including postal address interpretation, bank check processing, and document digitization. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for handwritten character recognition using statistical methods and artificial neural networks (ANNs) to address challenges such as nonlinearity and stylistic variations. The study evaluates traditional methods, including template matching, structural pattern recognition, and graphical approaches, against the proposed Support Vector Machine (SVM)-based model integrated with machine learning techniques. The proposed framework demonstrates high recognition accuracy, achieving 91% on a diverse dataset of handwritten characters, including stylistic letters and digits. Extensive testing with datasets such as MNIST and IAM highlights the robustness and adaptability of the approach. Future research directions are discussed, emphasizing improvements in dataset diversity and scalability for broader real-world applications.

title: Artificial Intelligence in the Detection of Knee Joint Injuries: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Baswarani Sandila, Rama Valupadasu, Manjula Sri Rayudu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Knee joint injuries are a leading cause of musculoskeletal disorders, affecting millions worldwide. Traditional diagnostic methods, such as clinical examination and radiographic imaging, often have constraints in terms of performance parameters. Past one-decade, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has shown immense potential in enhancing the performing of diagnosis of knee joint injuries. The Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models are applied to medical images from modalities viz. X-ray, MRI, and CT scans for the detection of knee disorders. Common knee injuries include knee osteoarthritis (KOA), Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) tears, meniscal injuries, and fractures. This paper reviews the current state of AI applications in knee joint injury detection, AI models used, their advantages, challenges, and prospects. The findings suggest that AI can significantly enhance diagnostic performance, decrease detection time, speedup clinical decision-making, despite challenges related to data quality, interpretability, and generalization.

title: Spatiotemporal Analysis of Drought Trends in Key Towns across the Philippines using Machine Learning
authors: Dessa Louise Clemenia, Jane Charmaine Wong, Jazzie R. Jao, Edgar A. Vallar, Ibrahim Hameed
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This study investigates spatiotemporal climate variability and drought dynamics in four Philippine munici-palities-Coron, Kabugao, Virac and Tagum-using a 20 -year record (2003-2023) of key meteorological variables, including Precipitable Water (PW), 2-meter Temperature (T2M), Wind Speed (WS2M), Relative Humidity (RH2M), and Surface Soil Wetness (GWETTOP, GWETPROF). Analysis reveals distinct seasonal patterns, with increasing rainfall during the wet season and declining precipitation in the dry season, alongside a consistent upward trend in surface temperatures. Vegetation decline was detected in Virac, while Coron, Kabugao and Tagum showed relative stability. The study highlights the intensifying impacts of climate change and extreme events such as El Niño on drought frequency and severity, posing significant risks to agriculture and rural livelihoods. Drought characterization was conducted using the Standardized Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI), and predictive modelling was performed using both machine learning (Linear Regression, Support Vector Machine) and deep learning (Long Short-Term Memory, Gated Recurrent Units) approaches. Working within a limited data resource, linear regression, demonstrated best predictive performance, achieving an $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ of $\mathbf{7 2. 0 \%}$, RMSE of $\mathbf{0. 4 3 0}$, and MAE of $\mathbf{0. 4 4 0}$ for a stacked dataset with all four locations, having a 6 -months look-back period.

title: Comparative Analysis of Shading Strategies on Solar Heat Gain Through Glass for Energy-Efficient Buildings
authors: Jay Prakash Mathiya, Ankit Goyal, Sashi Kumar Jain, Priyavrat Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of different shading strategies—no shading, fixed shading, and louver shading—on solar heat gain through glass surfaces. Solar radiation, comprising direct and diffuse components, significantly contributes to indoor heat buildup and cooling loads in buildings. Without shading, daily heat gain values reach approximately 4.4-4.5 kWh, indicating maximum solar penetration. The application of fixed shading reduces this value to about 3.3 kWh, demonstrating its ability to block a portion of incident radiation, though with limited adaptability to variations in solar angle. In contrast, louver shading proves to be the most effective strategy, lowering daily heat gain to around 2.1-2.2 kWh. This reduction, nearly 50% compared to unshaded conditions, highlights the superior performance of louvers in minimizing direct and diffuse solar transmission while maintaining controlled daylight entry. Overall, the results emphasize that dynamic shading systems such as louvers are critical for improving energy efficiency, reducing cooling demand, and enhancing thermal comfort in sustainable building design.

title: SMAP and Sentinel-2 Based Vegetation Dynamics and Soil Moisture Analysis for Evaluating impact of farm Pond interventions
authors: Veeramallu Satya Sahithi, Suneetha Manne
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Water conservation activities like farm ponds and check dams are crucial for improving the agricultural resilience in drought prone regions. Developmental activities in India like Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) and IWMP aim at constructing a wide variety of water conservation activities with focus on improving the soil moisture and crop productivity. Remote sensing data from optical and radar sensors can help in analyzing the impact of these activities on surrounding. The present study reports an integrated analysis using SMAP’s L band soil moisture data and Sentinel-2 optical data-based vegetation indices for evaluating the impact of farm ponds in Prakasam District, Andhra Pradesh. The SMAP’s all-weather, high temporal soil moisture data from 2016 to 2024 complements high-resolution Sentinel-2 imagery to track the surface soil moisture along with vegetation dynamics in a period before and after the construction of farm ponds. Results show a consistent improvement in soil moisture with an increase of $0.02-0.04 \mathrm{~m}^{3} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$ and an increase in NDVI $\mathbf{1 2}-\mathbf{1 8 \%}$ in areas influenced by new pond interventions. Field visits and farmer interactions corroborate the enhanced water retention in the study area. Correlation with annual mean rainfall of the area also indicated strongly that the positive agricultural changes were related to new farm pond construction but not due to rainfall. This study demonstrates the utility of combining microwave and optical data for scalable, seasonal impact analysis of water conservation efforts, particularly where cloud cover limits conventional remote sensing.

title: A Novel Data-Driven Framework Based on K-NN and PCA: Evolutionary Calibration of Game Parameters in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Xin Wu, Kai Zou, Jianyi Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The validity of conventional game models frequently finds itself constrained by their reliance on theoretical assumptions for parameters, which are devoid of empirical validation. In order to address this fundamental limitation, this paper puts forward a data-driven framework that employs an innovative and robust integration of the K-Nearest Neighbours (K-NN) algorithm and Principal Component Analysis (PCA), with a view to achieving empirical parameter calibration. The parameter estimation problem is transformed into a multiple regression task, which involves predicting payoff matrix parameters for a four-agent evolutionary game. These parameters are based on feature similarity between target scenarios and historical cases. A comprehensive data set was constructed, comprising 120 real-world event records across 28 dimensions. PCA facilitates the reduction of dimensionality and enhancement of features, thereby ensuring computational efficiency while concomitantly mitigating multicollinearity. The core framework utilises a distance-weighted K-nearest neighbour (KNN) regression model in order to calibrate cost, payoff, and incentive parameters. It is posited that parameters derived through data mining processes are to be integrated into replicator dynamic equations, with the aim of simulating strategic interactions among finitely rational agents. The efficacy of the proposed model has been substantiated by a substantial body of numerical simulations. In comparison to conventional parameterised models, the proposed model has been shown to demonstrate a 45% reduction in oscillation amplitude and an 18% enhancement in convergence rate. In addition, a sensitivity analysis of the data-derived parameters has exposed critical system thresholds and counterintuitive optimal policy ratios. These are conclusions that are unattainable through theoretical deductions alone. The central tenet of the present research lies in the establishment of a rigorous, transparent, and reproducible methodological framework. The employment of fundamental data mining techniques has been demonstrated to bridge the gap between abstract game theory and empirical reality. This enhancement of the predictive capability and practical value of evolutionary game models has been shown to facilitate more efficient analysis of complex multi-agent systems.

title: Optimizing Organizational Performance through Machine Learning Enhanced Management Practices
authors: Ashish Mishra, Nyangam Rime, Somil Vasal, T Aswani, Pankaj Agarwal, Sandeep Tomar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The digital sphere is seeing progressive application of machine learning (ML) to change managerial decision-making and operational performance of organizations. The paper discusses the ways to streamline organizational performance by using ML to improve management using predictive analytics, adaptive process automation, and data-driven leadership approaches to management. As opposed to traditional methods, ML makes it possible to conduct real-time analysis of mega-organizational data and contribute to evidence-based decision-making regarding resource allocation and workforce productivity or customer engagement. The study underlines the importance of the intelligent models in locating the performance bottlenecks, business predictions, and the improved collaboration of employees via dynamic task management. Also, an ethical study is examined, this encompasses transparency and fairness under responsible use of AI in the management environment. This paper proved how companies may gain resilience and sustainable competitive advantage even in fast-changing environments by developing an overall framework to enable machine learning skills to support managerial functions.

title: An Ai Powered Information Assistant for Government Schemes - Development and Comparative Analysis
authors: Nagesh H R, Ravinarayana B, Prakhyath Rai
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: An AI-powered information assistant -Abhiyan is developed to streamline access to government schemes, especially for rural users who may lack technical knowledge. Its core purpose is to simplify data retrieval, enhancing information accessibility and bridging the digital divide. Utilizing advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Large Language Models (LLMs), the developed chatbot can understand user queries, including those in regional languages, to provide relevant, userfriendly responses. Abhiyan leverages a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework, enabling it to pull real-time information from structured government notices and documents. By preprocessing and organizing data on various schemes, the system ensures accurate and efficient response delivery, addressing common inquiries like eligibility criteria and application processes. This work integrates LLMs for natural language understanding with RAG for data retrieval, creating a platform that promotes digital inclusion. Abhiyan's scalable infrastructure and ability to offer real-time updates make it a powerful tool for citizens and government agencies alike, empowering users with timely access to essential information on government schemes.

title: Enhancing Financial Protection using Advanced Fraud Detection Methods
authors: Krishnaveni V, V Sumalatha, Mridul Dixit, Ajay Kuamr Sahu, Deep Biswas, Jolly Sneh Kapoor
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Financial fraud continues to pose a significant threat to financial institutions and individual consumers alike. Traditional rule-based systems struggle to keep pace with the evolving nature of fraudulent activities. This paper explores the potential of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) with a specific focus on binary classification for identifying fraudulent transactions in real-time. We propose a novel approach utilizing a powerful ANN architecture trained on historical labeled data, enabling effective and efficient detection of anomalous patterns indicative of fraud. Furthermore, the paper introduces the development of a comprehensive and user-friendly dashboard designed to seamlessly integrate with the ANN model. This interactive dashboard empowers stakeholders with real-time visualization of transaction activity, allowing for dynamic monitoring and management of flagged transactions. By combining the strengths of an advanced ANN model and a user-centric dashboard, this research proposes a comprehensive solution aimed at enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of financial fraud detection, ultimately safeguarding financial institutions and consumers from potential losses. This research signifies a valuable contribution to the field of financial fraud detection by demonstrating the effectiveness of ANNs coupled with an insightful dashboard, offering a more robust and comprehensive approach to combatting financial crime.

title: Fostering Engagement Through a Latency-Optimized LLM-Based Dialogue System for Multimodal ECA Responses
authors: Konstantin W. Kühlem, Jonathan Ehret, Torsten W. Kuhlen, Andrea Bönsch
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Interactions with Embodied Conversational Agents (ECAs) are an integral part of many social Virtual Reality (VR) applications, increasing the need for free, context-sensitive conversations characterized by latency-optimized and multimodal ECA responses. Our presented methodology consists of three interdependent steps: We first present a holistic framework driven by a Large Language Model (LLM), which integrates existing technologies into a modular and extendable system that is developer-friendly and suitable for diverse use-cases. Building on this foundation, our second step comprises streaming-based optimizations that effectively reduce measured response latency, thereby facilitating real-time conversations. Finally, we conduct a comparative analysis between our latency optimized LLM-driven ECA and a conventional button-based Wizard-of-Oz (WoZ) system to evaluate performance differences in user engagement. Our insights reveal that users perceive our LLM-driven ECA as significantly more natural, competent, and trustworthy than their WoZ counterparts, despite objective measures indicating slightly higher latency in technical performance. These findings underscore the potential of LLMs to enhance engagement in ECAs within VR environments.

title: Design and Analysis of a Power-Efficient 4-Bit ALU with XNOR Logic for Enhanced Performance
authors: K. Padmavathi, Venkatarao Tirumalasetty, K.Swetha, D.Prathyusha, B.Kaveri, M.Jhansi, K.Sridevi, A. Jayasree
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper focuses on designing a 4-bit ALU that prioritizes low power consumption and high speed of operation by utilizing XNOR logic. The ALU supports a range of arithmetic functions—such as addition, subtraction, pass-through, and two’s complement—as well as logic operations including AND, OR, XOR, and XNOR. Since the full adder is a fundamental component of the ALU, optimizing its power usage significantly affects the overall power efficiency. The designed ALU exhibits a power consumption of 2.26 mW, a propagation delay of 1.58 ns, and a power-delay product (PDP) of 3.5210 pJ. By adopting an XNOR-based full adder design, the system achieved a 50% reduction in power consumption compared to the traditional Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) full adder. The entire design was simulated using the Cadence Virtuoso platform at the 90 nm technology node, operating at a supply voltage of 1.8 V.

title: MedXAI: A Retrieval-Augmented and Self-Verifying Framework for Knowledge-Guided Medical Image Analysis
authors: Midhat Urooj, Ayan Banerjee, Farhat Shaikh, Kuntal Thakur, Sandeep Gupta
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Accurate and interpretable image-based diagnosis remains a fundamental challenge in medical AI, particularly under domain shifts and rare-class conditions. Deep learning models often struggle with real-world distribution changes, exhibit bias against infrequent pathologies, and lack the transparency required for deployment in safety-critical clinical environments. We introduce MedXAI (An Explainable Framework for Medical Imaging Classification), a unified expert knowledge based framework that integrates deep vision models with clinician-derived expert knowledge to improve generalization, reduce rareclass bias, and provide human-understandable explanations by localizing the relevant diagnostic features rather than relying on technical post-hoc methods (e.g., Saliency Maps, LIME).We evaluate MedXAI across heterogeneous modalities on two challenging tasks: (i) Seizure Onset Zone localization from resting-state fMRI, and (ii) Diabetic Retinopathy grading. Experiments on ten multicenter datasets show consistent gains, including a 3% improvement in cross-domain generalization and a 10% improvmnet in F1 score of rare class, substantially outperforming strong deep learning baselines. Ablations confirm that the symbolic components act as effective clinical priors and regularizers, improving robustness under distribution shift. MedXAI delivers clinically aligned explanations while achieving superior in-domain and cross-domain performance, particularly for rare diseases in multimodal medical AI.

title:
